# SM70/V100 Migration Control

Date: 2026-05-30

## Objective

Target tree:

- Latest upstream vLLM: `/home/ymzx/desktop/1cat-vllm/vllm`
- Actual local path: `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm`
- Current HEAD: `4ff865c38 [Bugfix] Disable allreduce_rms_fusion when pipeline_parallel_size > 1 (#43616)`

Source optimization tree:

- `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm`
- Current HEAD: `530ac4db5 [Doc] Refocus README on project overview`

Goal:

- Move as many safe SM70/V100 optimizations as possible from the source tree
  into latest upstream vLLM.
- Decouple them into maintainable SM70-specific kernel/backend/facade pieces.
- Preserve latest vLLM's upper-layer model, scheduler, serving, KV cache,
  sampling, and normal MoE architecture wherever possible.
- Do not carry unsafe quality-regressing routes into the main path.

Main implementation priority:

1. Add a decoupled SM70 TurboMind backend for AWQ and FP8 base GEMM.
2. Wire AWQ and FP8 quantized linear paths to the SM70 backend on V100.
3. Wire only safe/basic FP8 MoE GEMM support; do not import unsafe compact
   single-token experiments as mainline.
4. Merge or minimally port the V100 attention fast path.
5. Inventory and evaluate the remaining SM70 assets: LM head/top1, logits,
   custom allreduce, CUDA graph, worker, Qwen/GDN hooks, and validation tools.

## Hard Rules

- No output-quality tradeoff is allowed.
- Deterministic token output is tracked and exact match is preferred, but it is
  not the sole hard gate for proven Type B fp16/fp8 paths. If the accepted
  baseline itself can flip greedy tokens at low margin, default-enable decisions
  must use classified numeric bounds plus fixed-text logprob/perplexity or
  dataset-level quality evidence rather than treating every token mismatch as a
  structural kernel bug.
- Numerical acceptance is classified per path before judging the result. Do not
  blindly require `max_diff == 0` for every path, and do not wave away nonzero
  diff as "fp16 noise" without proof.
- This classified numeric policy is part of the active final goal, not an
  optional benchmark note. All later quality rules, default-enable decisions,
  and performance claims inherit it.
- Type A layout/equivalent paths require strict `torch.equal` and
  `max_diff == 0`. This includes dense, paged prefill, paged decode, varlen,
  causal, and non-causal paths when the algorithm is the same and only memory
  access/layout changes. Any stable nonzero diff at or above `1e-6` is treated
  as an indexing, mask, KV-source, block-table, or `cu_seqlens` bug until root
  caused and fixed.
- Type B reduction-order/cross-implementation paths may accept bounded small
  noise only after proof. This includes multi-partition/block online softmax vs
  single-pass reference, or Flash vs Triton comparisons where arithmetic order
  genuinely differs. Acceptance requires all of:
  - input alignment is proven first: KV source, `cu_seqlens`, block table, mask,
    and raw-vs-cache data are not the source of the diff;
  - the diff source is proven to be reduction order or different fp16 rounding;
  - the dtype-specific bound is satisfied: fp32 comparison `<= 1e-5`, fp16
    output around `<= 2e-3`, bf16 output around `<= 1e-2`. The bound is chosen
    from path class plus dtype, never reverse-fit from the observed result;
  - the diff does not grow monotonically with sequence length.
- The mechanical classifier is: rerun the reference with the same
  block/partition tiling as the tested path. If the diff goes to zero, classify
  the original nonzero as Type B reduction-order noise. If it remains nonzero,
  classify it as a bug and fix it.
- For real-model or cross-kernel nonzero diffs, input alignment is a hard
  prerequisite before any Type B discussion. KV source, raw-vs-cache KV,
  `cu_seqlens`, block table, mask, dtype, scale, and shape must be proven
  aligned first. Until then, the result is treated as suspected bug /
  `B-pending`, not acceptable noise.
- Every nonzero path must be labeled explicitly as `A-bug`, `B-accept`, or
  `B-pending`. `B-accept` must include evidence for reduction-order source,
  dtype threshold, and non-divergence with sequence length.
- Mainline/default enablement requires all op-level Type A bugs to be cleared.
  After that, default enablement is decided by model-level behavior:
  deterministic greedy token equivalence, fixed-text perplexity stability, and
  bounded logprob/logit drift. A proven Type B op-level noise does not have to
  be forced to bitwise zero if doing so destroys the intended speed path.
- The two forbidden shortcuts are symmetric: do not pass a Type A nonzero as
  noise, and do not slow down or replace a proven Type B fast path merely to
  chase bitwise equality below the dtype noise floor.
- Routes with unclassified nonzero diff remain experimental and cannot count
  toward accepted/default performance.
- FlashAttention2/V100 is a required migration target. If the current
  FA2/V100 path has numerical mismatch, classify it first by the rules above,
  then either fix it as `A-bug`, keep it as `B-pending`, or accept it only with
  complete `B-accept` evidence. Mismatch is not permission to drop the route.
- Latest-TRITON schedule tuning can be used as a safe interim branch only when
  it satisfies the same numerical classification gate; it does not replace the
  required FlashAttention2/V100 migration/fix track.
- Benchmark modes must not be mixed: no-MTP, MTP, greedy, and official sampling
  results are separate evidence.
- Accepted 35B-A3B speed evidence must run on the latest vLLM tree with the
  exact requested criterion: model, quantization, TP size, GPU set, max length,
  input/output length, sampling/MTP state, attention backend, and CUDA graph
  state all recorded. When GPUs 0/1 are reserved, validation uses only
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`.
- For long-context decode benchmarks such as 35B-AWQ `input_len=16324` /
  `output_len=256`, the first request is warmup only and is not counted in the
  reported result. Report TTFT/prefill and pure decode separately from overall
  output throughput.
- Formal speed runs must enable every already-mainlined, quality-accepted SM70
  acceleration that is applicable to the route. Do not include diagnostic
  disable flags such as `--disable-custom-all-reduce` in final speed evidence
  unless the route being measured explicitly requires that as part of its
  accepted configuration.
- This document must be updated as each route is migrated, paused, rejected, or
  replaced by upstream code.

Active continuation update, 2026-06-01:

- This is not a goal restart. All previous migration states remain valid unless
  current evidence contradicts them.
- The classified numerical policy above replaces the older one-size-fits-all
  `max_diff == 0` wording wherever it appears in quality or acceptance
  discussions.
- Quality rule #2 is now: a route can enter the main/default path only after
  the classified gate passes. Type A paths need strict `max_diff == 0`; Type B
  paths need complete `B-accept` evidence plus model-level quality evidence.
  Token equivalence remains strong evidence, but low-margin token flips may be
  recorded as warnings when bounded logit/logprob/perplexity or dataset gates
  pass.
- Quality rule #4 is now: nonzero paths labeled `A-bug` or `B-pending` stay
  experimental and do not count toward accepted performance. A `B-accept`
  route may enter the mainline even when op-level bitwise equality is not
  true, provided the required proof and model-level gates pass.
- Acceptance condition #7 is now: this migration document must record each
  direction's source files, status, quality label, performance evidence, and
  non-migration reason. Known unsafe routes must stay out of the default path,
  while `B-pending` routes must stay explicit and uncounted until promoted or
  fixed.
- Attention route-policy update: the earlier split route of Triton prefill plus
  Flash-V100 decode is superseded by item 133. It may still be used as an
  explicit diagnostic/fallback with `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1`, but
  selecting `FLASH_ATTN_V100` now defaults to full Flash-V100 prefill and
  decode. Mixed prefill/decode results cannot be counted as final SM70
  Flash-V100 performance.

## Current State Summary

Latest upstream vLLM currently has no local SM70 TurboMind backend:

- No `lmdeploy/` vendor tree.
- No `csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`.
- No `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8_sm70_moe.py`.
- No `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py`.
- No `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`.
- No `VLLM_SM70_*` env gates found in the target tree during the initial scan.

Important upstream target differences:

- Latest `AWQConfig.get_min_capability()` is `75`; source tree changed this to
  `70` for SM70 TurboMind.
- Latest AWQ MoE uses the newer `RoutedExperts` path and Marlin/MoeWNA16
  fallback logic.
- Latest `Fp8Config.get_min_capability()` is `75`; source tree adds SM70
  TurboMind/dequant fallback behavior.
- Latest FP8 MoE selection uses the modular fused-MoE oracle/backend system.
- Latest attention priority list does not include `FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
- Latest vLLM has newer Qwen3.5/Qwen3Next/MTP/GDN code; old Qwen model files
  should not be copied over wholesale.

Latest build integration anchors:

- Generic CUDA extension sources are accumulated in `VLLM_EXT_SRC` and built
  into `_C`.
- Stable ABI sources are accumulated in `VLLM_STABLE_EXT_SRC` and built into
  `_C_stable_libtorch`.
- MoE-specific CUDA sources are built into `_moe_C`.
- Existing arch-specific kernels use `cuda_archs_loose_intersection(...)` and
  `set_gencode_flags_for_srcs(...)` before appending sources.
- First SM70 TurboMind port should start in `_C`, matching the source tree's
  current op registration style. Revisit `_C_stable_libtorch` only after the
  base AWQ/FP8 path is exact and stable.

## Migration Status Legend

- `todo`: not migrated yet.
- `in-progress`: actively being ported.
- `migrated`: code migrated into latest tree.
- `upstream-rebase`: latest upstream has a newer owner/interface; rebase the
  idea rather than copy old code.
- `upstream-replaced`: latest upstream already covers the need.
- `paused-unsafe`: known or suspected output drift; do not enter main path.
- `rejected`: not worth migrating; requires evidence and reason.
- `validation-needed`: code can move only after exactness/speed proof.

- `lmdeploy/src/turbomind/kernels/gemm/kernel/sm70_884_8.cu`
- `lmdeploy/src/turbomind/kernels/gemm/kernel/sm70_884_16.cu`

Current behavior:

- Source `CMakeLists.txt` detects `lmdeploy/src/turbomind`, restricts this
  block to CUDA arch `7.0`, and appends TurboMind sources to `VLLM_EXT_SRC`.
- `tm_registry_sm70.cu` registers `sm70_884_4`, `sm70_884_8`, and
  `sm70_884_16`.

Target design:

- Do not restore the full `lmdeploy/` tree at repo root.
- Create an isolated target such as `csrc/sm70_turbomind/` with only the needed
  TurboMind GEMM/core subset.
- Keep SM70 TurboMind build conditional on arch `7.0`.
- Keep all public op entrypoints behind one SM70 facade and capability checks.
- Initial build hook should append SM70 sources to `_C`'s `VLLM_EXT_SRC` only
  when `cuda_archs_loose_intersection(SM70_TURBOMIND_ARCHS "7.0" "${CUDA_ARCHS}")`
  is non-empty.

Target implementation update, 2026-05-30:

- Added isolated source subset under `csrc/sm70_turbomind/`.
- Kept the vendor include root under
  `csrc/sm70_turbomind/lmdeploy` so existing TurboMind includes such as
  `src/turbomind/...` remain local to the SM70 component.
- Pruned obvious non-runtime noise from the copied subset: TurboMind tests,
  CMake fragments, and non-SM70 kernel `.cu` files.
- Added latest-tree `CMakeLists.txt` hook:
  - computes `SM70_TURBOMIND_ARCHS` with arch `7.0`;
  - appends TurboMind sources to `_C` only when that intersection is non-empty;
  - adds `csrc/sm70_turbomind/lmdeploy` to `_C` include dirs;
  - defines `ENABLE_SM70_TURBOMIND=1` only for SM70 builds.
- Follow-up build fix: `VLLM_GPU_FLAGS` alone did not make
  `ENABLE_SM70_TURBOMIND` visible to `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp`, so the C++
  binding registration block was compiled out even though the CUDA symbols were
  present in `_C.abi3.so`. The CMake hook now adds a source-level compile
  definition for `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp`.
- Wrapped the copied MoE compact/router/single-token block in
  `VLLM_ENABLE_SM70_TURBOMIND_EXPERIMENTAL_MOE`; it is not compiled into the
  default/main route.
- Added two indirect header dependencies found by static include-closure
  check: `src/turbomind/macro.h` and
  `src/turbomind/kernels/attention/quantization.h`.
- Current file count in the isolated subset: 139 files.

Validation required:

- Build target with `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`. Done for Python/CUDA
  extensions in conda env `vllm-0.0.5-t210`; Rust server remains optional and
  failed only because `protoc` is missing.
- Unit-level AWQ/FP8 GEMM exactness against current safe baseline.
- Confirm no non-SM70 build regression.
