### 3. AWQ Dense Linear GEMM

Status: `in-progress`

Source files:

- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq.py`
- `csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`
- `vllm/_custom_ops.py`

Source behavior:

- Lowers AWQ min capability from `75` to `70`.
- On SM70 and group size 32/64/128, prepares TurboMind weight/scales at load
  time via `ops.awq_sm70_prepare`.
- Replaces original AWQ resident tensors with empty tensors after successful
  TurboMind preparation to reduce memory residency.
- At apply time, if `_awq_sm70_prepared` is set, calls `ops.awq_gemm_sm70`.

Latest target state:

- Latest `AWQConfig.get_min_capability()` remains `75`.
- Latest dense AWQ path uses dequant matmul for large token counts or
  `ops.awq_gemm` otherwise.
- No SM70 preparation or TurboMind call exists.

Target design:

- Add an SM70-specific AWQ linear method or backend selector.
- Keep latest AWQ method as source of truth and insert a minimal SM70 hook.
- Avoid changing generic AWQ behavior on SM75+.

Target implementation update, 2026-05-30:

- Added default-off env gate `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND`.
- `AWQConfig.get_min_capability()` returns `70` only when that gate is set;
  otherwise latest upstream default remains `75`.
- `AWQLinearMethod.process_weights_after_loading()` prepares TurboMind weights
  only when all of these are true:
  - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`;
  - parameter is already on CUDA;
  - active device capability is exactly `(7, 0)`;
  - `awq_sm70_prepare` exists in `_C`;
  - group size is 32, 64, or 128.
- `AWQLinearMethod.apply()` dispatches to `sm70_ops.awq_gemm_sm70` only when
  the layer was prepared successfully.
- AWQ dense GEMM now uses `select_awq_dense_dispatch_policy()`, whose
  small-shape tuning is default-off and opt-in via
  `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1`. The generic dense selector remains
  default-on so FP8/F16 preserve old 0.0.3 behavior.
- Unlike the old 0.0.3 code, this first latest-tree hook does not replace
  original AWQ tensors with empty tensors. That memory optimization is paused
  until the classified numeric gate and fallback behavior are proven.

Validation required:

- Qwen3.6-AWQ dense layers load and use SM70 TurboMind.
- Layer-level AWQ output exactness under the classified numeric gate: Type A
  self-repeat/layout checks require `max_diff == 0`; any cross-implementation
  nonzero must be labeled `B-pending` or promoted to `B-accept` only with the
  required source, dtype-bound, and non-divergence evidence.
- Memory residency checked before/after optional source-tensor release.
- Decode speed compared against 0.0.3 AWQ safe baseline.

### 4. AWQ MoE GEMM

Status: `validation-needed`

Source files:

- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq.py`
- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py`
- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/compressed_tensors/compressed_tensors_moe.py`
- `csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`

Source behavior:

- Uses `AWQSM70MoEMethod` on SM70 when dimensions are compatible.
- Prepares per-expert TurboMind weights.
- Defaults to a per-expert dense TurboMind GEMM bridge for exactness.
- Keeps grouped/batched GEMM via strided pointer arrays behind
  `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`.
- Has single-token compact flags and compare flags.

Latest-tree observation, 2026-05-31:

- Ran a minimal latest-tree Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP4 smoke with
  `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`, `--quantization awq`, `--max-model-len 4096`,
  and one greedy output token.
- Result: startup failed before generation. The latest `AWQConfig` still routed
  `RoutedExperts` through `AWQMarlinConfig` / `MoeWNA16` instead of a SM70
  TurboMind MoE backend.
- Root error:
  `ValueError: Marlin does not support weight_bits = uint4. Only types = [] are supported (for group_size = 128, device_capability = 70, zp = True).`
- Source-level gap found after the failure: the target tree has the C++
  implementation for `awq_moe_gemm_sm70_out`, but only
  `awq_moe_build_strided_ptrs` and `fp8_moe_gemm_sm70_out` are currently
  declared/registered in `csrc/ops.h`, `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp`, and
  `vllm/_sm70_ops.py`.
- Conclusion: AWQ MoE cannot be accepted by falling through Marlin on V100.
  It needs a decoupled SM70 AWQ MoE method wired into latest `RoutedExperts`.
  The first migration step should expose only the safe base batched GEMM path
  and keep compact single-token and gated-SiLU epilogue variants out of the
  mainline until they pass the classified numeric gate.

Implementation checkpoint, 2026-05-31:

- Added `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70_out` declaration, torch registration, and
  `vllm/_sm70_ops.py` facade.
- Added `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py` with a
  latest `RoutedExperts` method for V100. The method intentionally uses:
  per-expert TurboMind prepare, separate `torch.ops._C.silu_and_mul`, and
  standard `moe_unpermute`.
- Excluded the old single-token compact path and excluded the AWQ gated-SiLU
  GEMM epilogue from this first mainline candidate.
- Added a `RoutedExperts` skip check in `AWQConfig.get_quant_method()` so
  modules listed in `modules_to_not_convert` still use
  `UnquantizedFusedMoEMethod`. This was required because Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ
  stores layer 0 experts unquantized (`*.weight`) while later layers store
  AWQ tensors (`*.qweight`, `*.qzeros`, `*.scales`).
- Build:
  `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0 MAX_JOBS=6 NVCC_THREADS=1 CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=6 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m pip install -e . --no-build-isolation --no-deps`
  completed successfully.
- Op visibility check after build:
  `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70_out=True` and
  `_C::awq_moe_build_strided_ptrs=True`.
- First rerun after adding the hook failed at weight loading with
  `KeyError: 'layers.0.mlp.experts.w2_weight'`. This was not a kernel failure;
  it exposed the missing skip handling for unquantized layer 0.
- After the skip fix, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP4 latest-tree smoke passed:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sm70_tp4_greedy1_smoke_after_awq_moe_skipfix.json`.
  Evidence:
  - four visible GPUs all reported capability `[7, 0]`;
  - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`;
  - log confirmed `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled (256 experts)`;
  - model loaded and generated one greedy token (`token_id=271`);
  - `load_seconds=709.44`, dominated by first-run torch/Triton compile and
    CUDA graph capture; `generate_seconds=5.02`.
- Acceptance status: this is only a load/generate smoke. It does not satisfy
  the quality gate yet. AWQ MoE still needs an op-level strict diff harness and
  a model-level deterministic token comparison before it can count as accepted.

Strict AWQ MoE batched-GEMM check, 2026-05-31:

- Added `--mode awq_moe` to
  `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py`. It loads real
  Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ expert weights, prepares TurboMind layouts, and compares
  grouped/batched AWQ MoE GEMM against per-expert dense TurboMind GEMM for
  `w13`, `silu_and_mul`, and `w2`.
- Strict command:
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py --mode awq_moe --awq-moe-model /home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ --awq-moe-layer model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts --m 1 2 --group-size 128 --device cuda:0 --json-out bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_moe_batched_vs_dense_strict_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260531.json`
- Result failed strict equality:
  - `m=1 w13_gate_up`: `max_diff=0.000244140625`;
  - `m=1 silu_and_mul`: `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`;
  - `m=1 w2_sorted_output`: `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`;
  - `m=2 w13_gate_up`: `max_diff=0.000244140625`;
  - `m=2 silu_and_mul`: `max_diff=0.0001220703125`;
  - `m=2 w2_sorted_output`: `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`.
- Setting `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0` did not remove the difference,
  so this is not only the small-shape autotuner choosing a different first
  algorithm.
- Safety change: added default-off
  `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`. The latest-tree AWQ MoE method now defaults
  to per-expert dense TurboMind GEMM as the exactness bridge, while the
  grouped/batched MoE GEMM remains available only as an explicit experimental
  path until it reaches `torch.equal` and `max_diff == 0`.
- Default bridge strict command:
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py --mode awq_moe --awq-moe-actual dense_out --awq-moe-model /home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ --awq-moe-layer model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts --m 1 2 --group-size 128 --device cuda:0 --json-out bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_moe_dense_out_vs_dense_strict_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260531.json`
- Default bridge result passed strict equality:
  `w13_gate_up`, `silu_and_mul`, and `w2_sorted_output` are all
  `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`, `mean_diff=0.0` for `m=1` and `m=2`.
- Full-model default-bridge smoke without eager failed during CUDA graph
  memory profiling/capture:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sm70_tp4_greedy1_default_dense_bridge.json`
  was not written because startup aborted. Root cause:
  `_dense_moe_gemm_out()` copied `expert_offsets64` to CPU with
  `expert_offsets64.detach().cpu().tolist()`, which is illegal while CUDA graph
  capture is active (`cudaErrorStreamCaptureUnsupported`, followed by
  `cudaErrorStreamCaptureInvalidated`).
- Full-model default-bridge eager smoke passed:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sm70_tp4_greedy1_default_dense_bridge_eager.json`.
  Evidence:
  - `--enforce-eager`, `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`, TP4, four SM70 GPUs;
  - log confirmed
    `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled (256 experts)`;
  - model loaded and generated one greedy token (`token_id=271`);
  - `load_seconds=112.81`, `generate_seconds=4.41`.
- Tried `@eager_break_during_capture` plus
  `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` for the same default dense bridge. This did
  not fix non-eager startup because latest vLLM runs this section under
  `CUDAGraphMode.FULL`, where the breakable-cudagraph wrapper deliberately
  returns the original function and does not break capture. The same CPU read
  of `expert_offsets64` invalidated capture, so the JSON result file
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sm70_tp4_greedy1_default_dense_bridge_breakable_cg.json`
  was not written.
- Added JSON parsing for `--engine-arg` in
  `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`, then reran with
  `--engine-arg 'compilation_config={"cudagraph_mode":"NONE"}'`.
  Result passed load/generate:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sm70_tp4_greedy1_default_dense_bridge_cg_none.json`.
  Evidence:
  - latest vLLM parsed `cudagraph_mode` as `CUDAGraphMode.NONE` and explicitly
    skipped CUDA graph capture;
  - log again confirmed
    `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled (256 experts)`;
  - model generated one greedy token;
  - `load_seconds=660.18`, with `Initial profiling/warmup run took 470.57 s`;
  - `generate_seconds=8.86`;
  - inference still triggered Triton JIT for `_zero_kv_blocks_kernel`,
    `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`, `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel`,
    `_fused_post_conv_kernel`, `fused_moe_kernel`, and
    `kernel_unified_attention`.
- Engineering status: the default dense bridge is strict at the op stage and
  functional under eager and with CUDA graph disabled, but it is not
  CUDA-graph safe and the no-graph route has unacceptable startup/warmup cost.
  It cannot be accepted as a production default until either a capture-safe C++
  per-expert bridge is added, or the grouped/batched kernel is repaired to
  strict equality.
- Interpretation: the grouped/batched AWQ MoE kernel is not an acceptable
  mainline optimization yet. It is a repair target. The likely next
  kernel-level work is to make grouped/batched AWQ MoE use the same
  arithmetic/order as the dense TurboMind path, or to add a strict grouped
  implementation whose output is bitwise identical.

Target design:

- Rebase onto latest `RoutedExperts` and modular fused-MoE interfaces.
- Migrate only the safe base GEMM/prepare path first.
- Keep the exact per-expert dense bridge as the default until the grouped
  batched kernel proves strict equality.
- Do not treat `cudagraph_mode=NONE` as final architecture. It is only a
  temporary debug/bring-up mode for strict output validation.
- Single-token compact AWQ path is not accepted until classified exactness is
  proven.

Validation required:

- Qwen3.6-AWQ MoE path loads and runs.
- Default AWQ MoE exactness: per-expert dense bridge must pass the Type A
  `max_diff == 0` check against the chosen migration oracle.
- Batched AWQ MoE exactness: `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` must be
  classified and accepted under the A/B rules before it can become a
  mainline/default speed path. Current grouped/batched nonzero evidence stays
  `B-pending` until the source is proven.
- Single-token compact route must stay off until separate proof.

### 5. FP8 Dense Linear GEMM

Status: `in-progress`

Source files:

- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8.py`
- `csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`
- `vllm/_custom_ops.py`

Source behavior:

- Adds `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND`.
- Adds `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK`.
- Uses `ops.fp8_sm70_prepare` and TurboMind W8A16-style SM70 kernels for block
  FP8 weights.
- Provides dequant fallback when TurboMind path is not used.

Latest target state:

- Latest FP8 min capability is `75`.
- Latest FP8 linear goes through modern kernel selection such as Cutlass and
  Marlin FP8 scaled-mm kernels.

Target design:

- Add an SM70 FP8 linear backend that slots into latest FP8 kernel selection.
- Keep latest `Fp8Config` and weight-loading semantics.
- Only enable on SM70 and supported FP8 block/layout shapes.

Target implementation update, 2026-05-30:

- Added default-off env gate `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND`.
- `Fp8Config.get_min_capability()` returns `70` only when that gate is set;
  otherwise latest upstream default remains `75`.
- Latest FP8 linear `create_weights()` normally selects a kernel before weight
  processing. On SM70 this can fail before we reach TurboMind preparation, so
  the hook intercepts earlier:
  - only if `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND=1`;
  - only on exact SM70;
  - only for serialized block FP8 with `weight_block_size=[128, 128]`;
  - otherwise raises a clear error instead of silently falling back to an
    unverified path.
- `process_weights_after_loading()` prepares local TurboMind layout with
  `sm70_ops.fp8_sm70_prepare`.
- `apply()` dispatches to `sm70_ops.fp8_gemm_sm70_out_meta` only when the layer
  was prepared successfully.
- Gated-SiLU/shared-gate and FP8 MoE single-token variants were not migrated.

Validation required:

- Qwen3.6-FP8 dense layers load and use SM70 TurboMind.
- Layer-level FP8 exactness under the classified numeric gate: Type A
  self-repeat/layout checks require `max_diff == 0`; any cross-implementation
  nonzero must be labeled `B-pending` or promoted to `B-accept` only with the
  required source, dtype-bound, and non-divergence evidence.
- Decode speed compared against 0.0.3 FP8 safe baseline.

### 6. FP8 MoE Base GEMM

Status: `validation-needed`

Source files:

- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8.py`
- `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8_sm70_moe.py`
- `csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`

Safe candidate:

- Basic `fp8_moe_gemm_sm70_out` path.
- Per-expert TurboMind weight preparation.
- Existing strict compare infrastructure.

Paused unsafe candidates:

- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_CPP`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_GATED_SILU_EPILOGUE`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BROADCAST_INPUT`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BROADCAST_INPUT_IDXS`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_ROUTER_TOPK_CPP`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_W2_DIRECT_REDUCE`
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_WEIGHTED_REDUCE_EPILOGUE`

Known evidence:

- Previous strict checks found nonzero diffs on compact/gated variants.
- Exact-layout/reduce debug experiments showed exactness but no speed gain.

Target design:

- Implement only a latest-modular-FusedMoE-compatible SM70 FP8 base backend.
- Keep compact/gated/router variants documented as `paused-unsafe`.
- Use latest upstream oracle/backend selection rather than copying old
  `fp8.py` wholesale.

Validation required:

- Qwen3.6-FP8 MoE path loads and runs.
- Base path must pass the classified numeric gate. The per-expert dense bridge
  remains the Type A strict oracle; grouped/batched nonzero stays `B-pending`
  until proven as acceptable Type B or repaired to exactness.
- Unsafe flags remain default off and not part of accepted performance.

FP8 MoE route recovery update, 2026-06-13:

- Native SM70 FP8 MoE is now the default route for the FP8 checkpoints under the
  latest vLLM tree: `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK` defaults to false and
  the runtime logs `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind batched path enabled (256 experts)`.
  This replaces the dequantized fp16 expert fallback for the main FP8 speed
  lane.
- `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_PERMUTE_WITH_SCRATCH` is default-on. Direct low-level
  comparison of `moe_permute` and `moe_permute_with_scratch` for representative
  `hidden=2048`, `top_k=8`, `num_experts=256`, and `m in {1,2,8,32}` was exact:
  permuted tensor, offsets, inverse indices, and permuted indices all matched
  with `max_diff=0`.
- FP8 batched W13/W2 per-expert dispatch has op-level evidence but is still not
  default-on. The 2026-06-13 exactness runs for
  `batched_per_expert_dispatch`, `batched_w13_per_expert_dispatch`, and
  `batched_w2_per_expert_dispatch` on
  `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`,
  `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`, `m in {1,2,8}`, and expert
  patterns `round_robin` / `single:0` all stayed within fp16 output bound:
  `max_diff=0.000244140625`, no NaNs. Label remains `B-pending` until
  model-level numeric or dataset-quality evidence is added.
- FP8 legacy single-token compact has been wired as
  `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`, default-off. The op-level
  exactness run for layer 1, `m=1`, `top_k=8` is fp16-bound:
  W13 max diff `0.000244140625`, SiLU max diff `4.57763671875e-05`, W2 max diff
  `1.52587890625e-05`, no NaNs. The C++ helper was adjusted to match
  `_C.silu_and_mul`'s fp16 rounding before multiply.
- Model-level token comparison is no longer a reliable sole gate for this lane,
  and greedy-token divergence must be attributed dialectically. In one 4K/1024
  greedy run, no-compact vs compact first diverged at output token index 52
  (`364` vs `440`) while compact improved decode speed from roughly `104 tok/s`
  to `114 tok/s`. A follow-up 4K/64 no-compact run with the same prompt and
  route diverged from the earlier no-compact run at token index 44, while the
  compact 4K/64 prefix exactly matched the earlier compact prefix. This proves
  the current FP8 graph decode baseline can already flip greedy tokens without
  the new compact route. Therefore a compact/per-expert route is not proven bad
  merely because it disagrees with one no-compact graph artifact. The valid
  attribution rule is: first establish baseline self-stability for the same
  graph policy, then compare only the common-prefix sampler/logit/logprob
  evidence before the first context split, and finally use fixed-text
  perplexity/dataset quality evidence before defaulting the route.
- Superseding root-cause update, 2026-06-14: the no-compact FP8 graph baseline
  self-instability above was traced to `FLASH_ATTN_V100` dense prefill shared
  memory initialization, not to custom all-reduce, CUDA graph metadata, GDN/QLA,
  or FP8 MoE compact itself. The stable-baseline evidence below now replaces
  the old "baseline can already flip" artifact for future compact attribution.
  Compact remains default-off until it is retested against this repaired
  baseline with common-prefix logits/logprobs and model-level quality evidence.
- Speed value of the compact route is real and decode-specific. On the same
  Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 TP2 4K/1024 no-logprobs graph path, default no-compact
  measured prefill `8615.87 tok/s` and steady decode `109.43 tok/s`; compact
  measured prefill `8542.75 tok/s` and steady decode `119.85 tok/s`. This is
  roughly a `+10.42 tok/s` / `+9.5%` decode gain with no prefill gain, so it is
  worth repairing for the 35B-FP8 decode target, but it remains default-off
  until the common-prefix logprob/model-quality issue is fixed.
- Post Flash-V100 shared-memory fix compact retest, 2026-06-14:
  - Repaired no-compact baseline artifact:
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/after_flash_v100_smem_fix_long_20260614/decode_qwen36_35b_fp8_default_long1024_i4096_tp2_r2_20260614.json`.
    Warmup and both repeats produced identical 1024-token hashes; steady decode
    was `108.89`/`108.91 tok/s`.
  - Compact artifact:
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/compact_after_flash_v100_smem_fix_20260614/decode_qwen36_35b_fp8_compact_long1024_i4096_tp2_r2_20260614.json`.
    Compact was self-stable across warmup/repeats, but disagreed with
    no-compact at token index `46` in that decode-script run. In that repaired
    long run compact did not show a speed win (`~105 tok/s` vs no-compact
    `~109 tok/s`), so keep the older `119.85 tok/s` speed artifact as
    non-current until rerun under the exact same post-fix baseline.
  - Same-prompt top-token-margin diagnostic using
    `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` moved the first no-compact vs compact
    divergence to token index `35` (`13` vs `364`), which is expected because
    the harness differs. The compact run at the first divergence had
    top1 `364 = 16.5`, top2 `13 = 16.296875`, margin `0.203125`:
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/compact_top_margin_after_smem_fix_20260614/compact_firstdiff_margin/`.
    This is a low-margin greedy flip, not a large sampler-logit failure.
  - Compact internal reference compare was attempted with
    `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT_COMPARE=1` and printed
    exact reports for the compact-vs-batched-reference MoE layers before CUDA
    graph capture rejected the diagnostic `.item()` call:
    `perm=0`, offsets equal, inverse indices equal, `w13=0`, `silu=0`,
    `w2=0`, `out=0` through the reported layers in
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/compact_top_margin_after_smem_fix_20260614/compact_firstdiff/run.log`.
    Do not rerun this compare inside CUDA graph capture without first making
    the diagnostic capture-safe.
  - Current decision: FP8 legacy compact is not proven to be a kernel-correctness
    bug. The strict exact-layout runtime can match no-compact greedy tokens for
    a 4K/1024 prompt, but it still has not recovered the old compact speed.
    The useful next implementation work is to recover the faster top-k compact
    descriptor / monolithic two-GEMM shape while keeping the strict exact-layout
    quality behavior or proving the remaining diff is bounded FP16 model-safe
    numeric noise. Do not default the divergent old compact route by speed
    alone.
  - Same-command post-fix speed rerun, 2026-06-14:
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/compact_speed_recheck_20260614/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_no_compact_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260614.json`
    measured no-compact steady decode `108.252 tok/s`; compact artifact
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/compact_speed_recheck_20260614/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_compact_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260614.json`
    measured `104.914 tok/s`. This confirms the earlier compact `119.852 tok/s`
    was not retained after the Flash-V100 prefill correctness fix, even under
    the same `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` single-generate 4K/1024口径.
    Compact route still hit (`fp8_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_effective=true`
    and runtime route log present), so the speed loss is not a route miss. The
    old compact output and current compact output first differ at token index
    `42` (`6707` vs `3300`). The older statement that repaired current
    no-compact and repaired current compact also differed at index `42` is
    superseded by the later strict exact-layout compare below: those two can be
    token-identical for 1024 generated tokens. Treat the old `119.852 tok/s`
    artifact as pre-repair trajectory evidence only, not an accepted current
    speed target.
  - Superseding fixed-route interpretation for the missing compact speed value:
    the post-fix compact/no-compact runs have matching attention, graph,
    custom all-reduce, FP8 MoE native, scratch-permute, and GDN policy fields
    except for `fp8_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_effective`. The compact
    route also logs its runtime hit, so the loss is not a missing env/default.
    In the current implementation compact only swaps the single-token MoE
    layout/permute preparation for
    `awq_moe_single_token_exact_layout_prepare`; both paths still use the same
    `fp8_moe_gemm_sm70_out` W13/W2 kernels and the same unpermute stage.
    Do not interpret the Flash-V100 shared-memory fix as a direct mechanical
    slowdown of the compact MoE kernel. It only makes the old end-to-end
    compact `119.852 tok/s` artifact non-comparable as a same-work benchmark,
    because the repaired run follows a different greedy/router trajectory.
    The compact kernel-speed question must be answered with a fixed
    hidden/top-k replay or a fixed-route MoE microbenchmark, not by comparing
    two different generated token traces.
  - Fixed-route FP8 MoE layer-1 microbenchmark on 2026-06-14, GPU0, 35B-FP8
    `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`, `m=1`, `top_k=8`,
    `group_size=128`, fixed hidden/top-k:
    - `single:0`: no-compact `0.1446 ms`, current exact-layout compact
      `0.0836 ms`, monolithic old compact family `0.0637 ms`.
    - `round_robin`: no-compact `0.1352 ms`, current exact-layout compact
      `0.0867 ms`, monolithic old compact family `0.0640 ms`.
    - `random_unique:0`: no-compact `0.1374 ms`, current exact-layout compact
      `0.0982 ms`, monolithic old compact family `0.0646 ms`.
    - `random_unique:7`: no-compact `0.1370 ms`, current exact-layout compact
      `0.0994 ms`, monolithic old compact family `0.0655 ms`.
    This proves the current exact-layout compact path still has real
    fixed-work MoE speed value versus no-compact. The old `119.852 tok/s`
    artifact is better explained as the faster monolithic old compact family
    being active before the later exact-layout quality hardening, not as a
    Flash-V100 prefill effect. Do not discard FP8 compact quality work solely
    because the divergent end-to-end generated-token run showed `104.914` vs
    `108.252 tok/s`; first compare with a common token/router replay or a
    same-token teacher-forced decode path.
  - Strict-quality exact-layout compact end-to-end speed, 2026-06-14:
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/strict_compact_e2e_speed_20260614/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_strict_compact_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260614.json`
    ran Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8, TP2, 4K input, 1024 greedy output, FP8 KV,
    Flash-V100, compile/FULL graph, custom all-reduce, native FP8 MoE
    batched GEMM, and `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=1`.
    Runtime logs hit both `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind batched path enabled` and
    `SM70 FP8 MoE legacy single-token exact-layout batched path enabled`.
    Request metrics: prefill `0.451776 s` = `9066.44 tok/s`; decode
    `9.762155 s` for `1023` steady tokens = `104.792 tok/s`; total output
    throughput `100.255 tok/s`.
  - Strict compact vs the prior no-compact 4K/1024 artifact
    (`compact_speed_recheck_20260614/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_no_compact_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260614.json`)
    has equal prompt and all `1024` output token IDs:
    `strict_compact_e2e_speed_20260614/compare_no_compact_vs_strict_compact_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260614.json`.
    This establishes token-level model quality for this prompt, but not a
    full default quality gate because logprob/sampler-logit/perplexity evidence
    was intentionally not collected. Speed remains below the prior no-compact
    steady decode `108.252 tok/s` on this generated trace, so the exact-layout
    compact implementation is quality-safe for this prompt but not yet an
    accepted default speed win. The next useful work is to recover more of the
    monolithic compact speed while keeping this token-equal behavior, not to
    rerun more proof that the current exact-layout route is only ~105 tok/s.
  - No-compact 4K/1024 recheck, 2026-06-14:
    `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/no_compact_e2e_recheck_20260614/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_no_compact_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260614.json`
    measured prefill `0.444015 s` = `9224.92 tok/s`; decode `9.431384 s`
    for `1023` steady tokens = `108.468 tok/s`. This confirms no-compact is
    stable near `108 tok/s`; the current strict exact-layout compact `104.792`
    is a real end-to-end regression on same-token output, not just trajectory
    noise.
  - 4K/128 Nsight/profile-speed check, 2026-06-14:
    no-compact
    `compact_rootcause_nsys_20260614/no_compact_i4096_o128_tp2.json`
    measured prefill `0.466437 s` = `8781.46 tok/s`, decode `1.249953 s`
    for `127` steady tokens = `101.604 tok/s`; strict exact-layout compact
    `compact_rootcause_nsys_20260614/strict_compact_i4096_o128_tp2.json`
    measured prefill `0.453354 s` = `9034.88 tok/s`, decode `1.284905 s`
    for `127` steady tokens = `98.840 tok/s`. The 128-token run reproduces
    the compact decode slowdown, so this is not a 1024-token measurement fluke.
    The exported no-compact nsys kernel summary missed most graph GEMM kernels,
    so do not use that CSV as kernel-count proof; use it only as a speed
    reproduction artifact.
  - Rejected attempted fix, 2026-06-14: wiring the runtime strict compact branch
    to C++ `fp8_moe_single_token_sm70_out(... exact_per_route=True)` produced
    op-level exact output for layer1/single:0
    (`fp8_moe_legacy_compact_runtime_monolithic_exactroute_opdiff_layer1_m1_single0_20260614.json`,
    `max_diff=0` for W13, SiLU, W2, and final weighted output), but end-to-end
    speed collapsed:
    `monolithic_compact_runtime_20260614/tokens_i4096_o128_tp2.json` measured
    decode `1.838187 s` for `127` steady tokens = `69.090 tok/s`. Root cause:
    `exact_per_route=True` is bitwise-safe because it loops over active routes
    and launches per-route W13/W2 dense stages, but that expands the full-model
    graph to many tiny GEMM kernels per MoE layer. It is faster in isolated
    fixed-route layer microbenchmarks but loses badly in end-to-end decode.
    Do not use per-route exact as the production compact fix.
  - Root-cause direction after the rejected fix: the old fast compact family
    likely got its speed from the monolithic top-k compact descriptor path:
    prepare/copy only the active expert pointer rows, run W13 as one
    top_k-expert GEMM, run W2 as one top_k-expert GEMM, then weighted reduce.
    The current strict exact-layout runtime instead keeps the quality-safe
    256-expert descriptor for W13/W2, so it only saves some permute/layout work
    and cannot recover the old `~119 tok/s` decode. The production fix should
    target a top-k descriptor/two-GEMM compact path whose numeric output is
    either token-equal to strict exact-layout or proven within the accepted FP16
    op/model quality gate.
- Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 is not an A3B MoE checkpoint (`Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration`),
  so the FP8 MoE compact route does not apply. Current TP2 4K/256 FP8-KV
  compile/FULL-graph speed evidence measured prefill time `2.8829 s`
  (`4096 / 2.8829 = 1420.8 tok/s`) and steady decode `46.98 tok/s`; route logs
  hit `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path`, FlashQLA GDN, Flash-V100, FP8 KV,
  and custom TP all-reduce. The 27B-FP8 slowdown is therefore a dense/GDN/attention
  path issue, not evidence for or against FP8 MoE compact.
- Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 MTP4 check, 2026-06-14, TP2 GPUs 2/3, code prompt,
  512 input / 512 output, official sampling (`temperature=1.0`, `top_k=20`,
  `top_p=0.95`), Flash-V100, compile/FULL graph, SM70 FP8 dense/GDN gates on:
  FP8-KV no-MTP baselines completed at steady decode `46.4775 tok/s`
  (`kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`) and `46.7690 tok/s` (`kv_cache_dtype=fp8`).
  FP8-KV MTP4 is not usable yet: `fp8_e5m2` fails MTP attention initialization
  because the MTP attention assert accepts only `{"fp8", "fp8_e4m3", "nvfp4"}`;
  `fp8` fails during MTP startup in TorchInductor autotune with
  `ValueError("type fp8e4nv not supported in this architecture. The supported fp8 dtypes are ('fp8e4b15', 'fp8e5')")`.
  The usable route is FP8 weights with default/non-FP8 KV cache. Artifact
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260614/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_defaultkv_mtp4_flash_code_i512_o512_w1_r2_tp2_20260614.json`
  ran MTP4, target Flash-V100, drafter `TRITON_ATTN`, compile/FULL graph,
  `gpu_memory_utilization=0.80`, and produced steady decode mean
  `84.4215 tok/s` (`83.0007`, `85.8423`) and output mean `80.0914 tok/s`
  (`78.8095`, `81.3733`). Acceptance length was `3.42` and `3.5517`;
  overall acceptance rate was `0.6050` and `0.6379`. Relative to the existing
  no-MTP ~`46.8-47 tok/s` 27B-FP8 baseline, this is about `1.80x` steady
  decode speedup. This formal 512/512 MTP4 run is the route smoke as well; do
  not spend a separate model load on a standalone smoke or a no-MTP rerun when
  the current question is MTP4 speed. The default-KV route has much lower KV
  capacity during
  target+drafter residency (`~20.6k tokens` in this run), so it is a speed
  workaround for MTP4 rather than the final FP8-KV-cache answer.

### 7. FlashAttention V100

Status: `in-progress`

Source files:

- `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`
- `vllm/v1/attention/backends/registry.py`
- `vllm/platforms/cuda.py`
- `vllm/engine/arg_utils.py`
- `vllm/v1/spec_decode/eagle.py`

Source behavior:

- Adds `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
- On SM70, selects `FLASH_ATTN_V100` before Triton attention.
- Uses lazy imports from external `flash_attn_v100`:
  `flash_attn_decode_paged` and `flash_attn_prefill_paged`.
- The external package is not a normal vLLM `_C` CMake source. It lives under
  source-tree `flash-attention-v100/` and builds two Python/CUDA extension
  modules:
  - `flash_attn_v100_cuda`;
  - `paged_kv_utils`.
- Decode path reads vLLM paged KV cache directly.
- Prefill has direct and fallback paths, including paged KV gather fallback.

Latest target state:

- No `FLASH_ATTN_V100` enum.
- No `flash-attention-v100/` external extension package.
- No `flash_attn_v100_cuda` or `paged_kv_utils` module in the latest target
  tree.
- Standard priorities are `FLASH_ATTN`, `FLASHINFER`, `TRITON_ATTN`,
  `FLEX_ATTENTION`, and `TURBOQUANT`.

Investigation update, 2026-05-30:

- Source extension build file:
  `1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/flash-attention-v100/setup.py`.
- Source extension CUDA files:
  `kernel/fused_mha_api.cpp`, `kernel/fused_mha_forward.cu`,
  `kernel/fused_mha_forward_paged.cu`, `kernel/flash_decode_paged.cu`,
  `kernel/fused_mha_backward.cu`, and `kernel/paged_to_contiguous.cu`.
- The backend Python file alone is insufficient for speed. Without the
  external CUDA modules, the old backend either cannot import its V100 ops or
  falls back to slower Triton/Python gather behavior.
- Next attention step must therefore include either:
  - packaging/installing `flash-attention-v100/` as an explicit optional SM70
    subpackage; or
  - migrating those kernels into a latest-tree optional extension target.
- Quality gate remains unresolved. Attention can only become default on SM70
  after Type A layout paths prove strict `max_diff == 0`, all remaining
  nonzero paths are either fixed or promoted from `B-pending` to `B-accept`,
  and the model-level gate passes.

Flash-V100 dense prefill quality fix, 2026-06-14:

- Root cause: `flash-attention-v100/kernel/fused_mha_forward.cu` initialized
  shared memory with `lane_id = threadIdx.x & 31` and `__syncwarp()`. Every warp
  redundantly cleared the full shared-memory region, but only synchronized
  within the warp. Other warps could begin writing Q/row state while a later
  warp was still clearing the same shared-memory buffer. This is an `A-bug`
  race: same input can produce small nonzero attention-core output drift.
- The same unsafe helper pattern was also fixed in the forward paged prefill
  sources: `kernel/fused_mha_forward_paged.cu` and
  `kernel/fused_mha_forward_paged_full.cu`. Current compiled extension includes
  dense forward and paged forward.
- Fix: make shared-memory clearing block-cooperative by iterating from
  `threadIdx.x` with stride `Config::THREADS_PER_BLOCK`, then use
  `__syncthreads()` before the kernel reuses shared memory.
- Attribution evidence before the fix, with Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 TP2 4K/6,
  `--enforce-eager`, `--disable-custom-all-reduce`, no compile and no CUDA
  graph:
  - layer0-2 were exact;
  - layer3 is the first full-attention layer;
  - layer3 `input_norm_out`, qkv, rotary, v, and gate were exact;
  - rank1 `full_attn_core_out` first drifted (`max_diff=6.103515625e-05`);
  - row-parallel `o_proj`/all-reduce then propagated the drift to both ranks
    (`attn_out max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`);
  - layer4/GDN received already-different hidden states, so GDN/QLA was a
    downstream amplifier, not the root.
- Post-fix focused evidence:
  `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/qwen_layer3_full_attn_after_smem_fix_20260614/decode_qwen36_35b_fp8_layer3_full_attn_after_smem_fix_float16_i4096_o6_tp2_r1_20260614.json`.
  Same eager/no-graph/no-custom-reduce isolation, layer3 full-attention dumps
  for step1 vs step7: both ranks have `max=0, nz=0` for `input_norm_out`,
  qkv, rotary q/k, v, `full_attn_core_out`, gate, gated output, `o_proj`, and
  `attn_out`. Warmup and repeat tokens both equal
  `[16401, 18100, 13, 220, 1061, 8097]`.
- Post-fix default-path long-output evidence:
  `bench_results/fp8_moe_route_recovery_20260613/after_flash_v100_smem_fix_long_20260614/decode_qwen36_35b_fp8_default_long1024_i4096_tp2_r2_20260614.json`.
  Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8, TP2 on GPUs 0/1, input 4096, output 1024, warmup 1,
  repeat 2, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, FP8 KV cache, default compile/graph enabled,
  custom TP all-reduce enabled. Warmup and both repeats produced the same
  token hash `ec591061118a5e02ca5c78124c1366a4bcbcc0c647ec6e435f5855f5eb40efc6`
  and token lists were identical (`first_diff=None`). Counted repeat speeds:
  prefill `9583.95` and `9519.87 tok/s`, steady decode `108.89` and
  `108.91 tok/s`.
- Status: dense Flash-V100 prefill self-stability for this 35B-FP8 default
  route is repaired. Future quality failures in compact/TurboQuant/MoE routes
  must be compared against this repaired baseline; do not continue attributing
  them to the old unstable no-compact graph artifact.

TurboQuant KV-cache update, 2026-06-04:

- Latest-tree upstream TurboQuant KV-cache code is present under
  `vllm/v1/attention/backends/turboquant_attn.py`,
  `vllm/v1/attention/ops/triton_turboquant_store.py`,
  `vllm/v1/attention/ops/triton_turboquant_decode.py`, and
  `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/turboquant/`.
- Added `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turboquant_quality.py` for lossy KV-cache
  validation. The gate does not require greedy token equality as a hard rule.
  It checks:
  - identical-context next-token top-logprob distribution drift;
  - baseline top1 NLL delta and missing rate;
  - high-margin baseline top1 mismatch rate;
  - natural-prompt long-output health against baseline.
- Route blocker fixed: on SM70, TurboQuant prefill no longer treats an
  importable FA2 package as sufficient evidence that FA varlen kernels can run.
  V100 now skips FA varlen in TurboQuant prefill and can use either the
  Flash-V100 dense raw-QKV first-chunk prefill bridge or the streaming Triton
  fallback instead of PyTorch SDPA while preserving TurboQuant store/decode.
- Long-context route blocker fixed: 35B-AWQ TP2 `input_len=16324` /
  `output_len=256` with `turboquant_4bit_nc` originally failed because the
  SM70 no-FA prefill fallback called `scaled_dot_product_attention`, which
  attempted to allocate a dense attention matrix and OOMed. Switching the
  first-chunk path to `context_attention_fwd` removed that OOM. The first
  Triton attempt then failed because head_dim=256 with the generic SM70
  `BLOCK=64` prefill tile required 128KB shared memory, above V100's 96KB
  limit; `context_attention_fwd` now selects `BLOCK=32` for CUDA-alike
  head_dim>=256 on non-Ampere devices as a fallback.
- TurboQuant/Flash-V100 decoupling update: added a module-level
  `flash_v100_dense_prefill(...)` helper so TurboQuant can reuse Flash-V100
  dense raw-QKV prefill without depending on the Flash-V100 backend class,
  metadata builder, or normal KV-cache layout. The runtime route log is
  `TURBOQUANT prefill using Flash-V100 dense raw-QKV path`.
- TurboQuant/Flash-V100 decode decoupling update: added a TQ packed-cache
  decode op in the optional Flash-V100 extension:
  `flash-attention-v100/kernel/flash_decode_turboquant.cu`, exposed through
  `decode_turboquant_paged_fwd`, `flash_attn_turboquant_decode_paged(...)`,
  and the vLLM helper `flash_v100_turboquant_decode(...)`. TurboQuant remains
  responsible for `q @ Pi.T`; the Flash-V100 extension receives only rotated
  query, packed TQ cache, block table, seq lens, and centroids. This avoids
  coupling TurboQuant into the standard Flash-V100 backend class or normal
  KV-cache layout.
- Quality-investigation rule: while CUDA graph output quality is being fixed
  separately, TurboQuant/Flash-V100 quality probes on this branch must run with
  CUDA graph disabled, e.g. `--enforce-eager`. CUDA-graph speed artifacts can
  be recorded as route/speed evidence only; they do not satisfy output-quality
  acceptance until the CUDA graph issue is closed.
- Diagnostic switch: `VLLM_SM70_TURBOQUANT_FLASH_V100_DECODE=0` forces the
  TurboQuant backend back to the old Triton TQ packed-cache decode while
  retaining the new code. Default remains enabled so speed work is not
  discarded.
- 35B-AWQ TP2 long-context speed evidence with all applicable accepted SM70
  routes enabled and first request treated as warmup:
  - Flash-V100, `kv_cache_dtype=auto`, artifact
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_flashv100_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_fullaccel_20260604.json`:
    prefill `4.43775s` (`3678.44 tok/s`), pure decode `84.5963 tok/s`,
    output throughput `34.3252 tok/s`.
  - TurboQuant `turboquant_4bit_nc` with Triton prefill fallback, artifact
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_turboquant4bit_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_fullaccel_tqprefill_b32_20260604.json`:
    prefill `9.11379s` (`1791.13 tok/s`), pure decode `69.1938 tok/s`,
    output throughput `19.9940 tok/s`.
  - TurboQuant `turboquant_4bit_nc` with Flash-V100 dense raw-QKV prefill
    bridge, artifact
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_turboquant4bit_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_flashv100prefill_20260604.json`:
    prefill `4.44126s` (`3675.65 tok/s`), pure decode `69.1680 tok/s`,
    output throughput `31.4771 tok/s`.
  - TurboQuant `turboquant_4bit_nc` with Flash-V100 dense prefill plus
    Flash-V100 TQ packed-cache decode, CUDA graph enabled, artifact
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_turboquant4bit_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_flashv100_tqdecode_20260604.json`:
    route logs include `TURBOQUANT prefill using Flash-V100 dense raw-QKV
    path` and `TURBOQUANT decode using Flash-V100 packed-cache path`; prefill
    `4.44342s`, pure decode `76.3165 tok/s`, output throughput
    `32.8640 tok/s`. Not quality-accepted: the two counted repeats produced
    different token hashes (`9e574c...` vs `83754b...`, first mismatch at token
    index 76). Treat as speed/route evidence only until CUDA graph quality is
    resolved.
  - TurboQuant `turboquant_4bit_nc` with Flash-V100 dense prefill plus
    Flash-V100 TQ packed-cache decode, CUDA graph disabled via
    `--enforce-eager`, artifact
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_turboquant4bit_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_flashv100_tqdecode_enforce_eager_20260604.json`:
    route logs include eager/no-cudagraph messages plus both TQ Flash-V100
    prefill/decode logs. Not quality-accepted: warmup and counted repeat0
    produced `fe57b0...`, counted repeat1 produced `6d4025...`, first counted
    mismatch at token index 123. Decode speed under eager is only
    ~`9.12 tok/s`, so this artifact is quality isolation only, not a speed
    baseline.
  - Speed interpretation: the Flash-V100 prefill bridge fully recovers the
    TurboQuant prefill regression for this shape. The first Flash-V100
    TQ-cache-aware decode op improves packed-cache decode from ~`69.17 tok/s`
    to ~`76.32 tok/s`, but normal Flash-V100 paged-KV still reaches
    ~`84.60 tok/s`; remaining decode work is inside the TQ packed-cache
    Flash-V100 op, not disabling TurboQuant or falling back to slow prefill.
- Kernel-level TQ decode parity, no model load:
  - Random D=256/GQA short-cache smoke: Flash-V100 TQ decode vs Triton TQ
    decode `max_diff=3.05176e-05`, no NaN.
  - Random D=256, `seq_len=16324`, `block_size=4096`, Hq=32/Hk=4 smoke:
    Flash-V100 TQ decode vs Triton TQ decode `max_diff=1.90735e-06`,
    mean diff `1.29963e-07`, no NaN.
  - CUDA graph micro replay of the new op matched eager exactly for changed
    static query buffers, so the model-level CUDA graph drift is not yet
    attributed to the TQ packed-cache CUDA op itself.
- Kernel-level smoke on GPU 2:
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 pytest -q
  tests/quantization/test_turboquant.py::TestStoreDecodeRoundTrip::test_single_token_roundtrip[turboquant_k8v4]
  tests/quantization/test_turboquant.py::TestStoreDecodeRoundTrip::test_single_token_roundtrip[turboquant_4bit_nc]`
  passed: `2 passed, 2 warnings in 3.18s`.
- `turboquant_k8v4` Qwen3-8B SM70 quality smoke did not pass:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/turboquant_qwen3_8b_k8v4_quality_smoke_20260604.json`.
  Evidence: one high-margin 512-token probe flipped top1
  (`token_mismatch_rate=0.125`, `high_margin_mismatch_rate=0.125`,
  `mean_top_logprob_jsd=0.0778106`,
  `mean_baseline_top1_nll_delta=0.610964`). Label: `B-pending` / not default.
- `turboquant_4bit_nc` Qwen3-8B SM70 quality smoke passed:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/turboquant_qwen3_8b_4bit_nc_quality_smoke_20260604.json`.
  Evidence: `token_mismatch_rate=0`, `high_margin_mismatch_rate=0`,
  `mean_top_logprob_jsd=0.000204823`, `p95_top_logprob_jsd=0.00106469`,
  `mean_baseline_top1_nll_delta=0.00322235`, and no 128-token canary
  degradation.
- `turboquant_4bit_nc` Qwen3-8B natural-prompt 1024-token long-output gate
  passed:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/turboquant_qwen3_8b_4bit_nc_quality_long1024_natural_20260604.json`.
  Evidence: `token_mismatch_rate=0`, `high_margin_mismatch_rate=0`,
  `mean_top_logprob_jsd=0.000163125`, `p95_top_logprob_jsd=0.000848044`,
  `mean_baseline_top1_nll_delta=0.00250975`, and
  `severe_candidate_degradation_rate=0`.
- `turboquant_4bit_nc` Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ TP2 short target gate passed on GPU
  2/3 with `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`,
  `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`, `quantization=awq`,
  `disable_custom_all_reduce=true`, 1024-token probe context, and 128-token
  natural canary:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/turboquant_qwen36_27b_awq_4bit_nc_quality_probe1024_canary128_20260604.json`.
  Evidence: `token_mismatch_rate=0`, `high_margin_mismatch_rate=0`,
  `mean_top_logprob_jsd=0.00138263`, `p95_top_logprob_jsd=0.00464523`,
  `mean_baseline_top1_nll_delta=0.0174407`, and
  `severe_candidate_degradation_rate=0`.
- `turboquant_4bit_nc` Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP2 short target gate passed on
  GPU 2/3 with the same environment. This run hit the SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind
  batched path and legacy single-token compact path during load/warmup:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/turboquant_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_4bit_nc_quality_probe1024_canary128_20260604.json`.
  Evidence: `token_mismatch_rate=0`, `high_margin_mismatch_rate=0`,
  `mean_top_logprob_jsd=0.000237086`, `p95_top_logprob_jsd=0.000601372`,
  `mean_baseline_top1_nll_delta=-0.00012644`, and
  `severe_candidate_degradation_rate=0`.
- Current recommendation: on SM70, do not default `turboquant_k8v4`.
  `turboquant_4bit_nc` is the first TurboQuant preset with model-level V100
  quality evidence on Qwen3-8B plus target AWQ 27B/35B short gates. Before
  production/default claims, still run the same gate on the target FP8 routes
  and, for 27B/35B, add a non-reused natural-prompt 1024-token output canary
  when runtime budget allows.

Target implementation update, 2026-05-30:

- Added source copy under latest-tree `flash-attention-v100/`, excluding old
  build artifacts, `.so` files, egg-info, and Python caches.
- Added latest-tree backend file:
  `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`.
- Added `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
- Added default-off selector gate `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
- When `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=1` and device capability is exactly SM70,
  latest `vllm/platforms/cuda.py` prioritizes:
  `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `TRITON_ATTN`, `FLEX_ATTENTION`, `TURBOQUANT`.
- Built and installed the extension as an independent editable package:
  `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0 MAX_JOBS=8 NVCC_THREADS=1 conda run --live-stream
  -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m pip install -e ./flash-attention-v100
  --no-build-isolation --no-deps`.
- Build completed successfully. Host memory stayed safe; sampled available
  memory stayed around 26-29 GiB and peak sampled CUDA compiler RSS was about
  2 GiB for one process.
- Import/backend checks passed:
  - `flash_attn_v100_cuda` imported;
  - `paged_kv_utils` imported;
  - `flash_attn_decode_paged` available;
  - `flash_attn_prefill_paged` available;
  - `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100.get_class()` resolves;
  - gated SM70 priority list selects `FLASH_ATTN_V100` first.
- Strict dense-prefill sample against a simple PyTorch softmax reference is not
  bitwise exact:
  - evidence file:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/flash_attn_v100_import_and_prefill_exactness.json`;
  - shape `[1, 16, 4, 64]`: `equal=false`, `max_diff=0.0009765625`,
    `mean_diff=0.0001001286`.
- Kernel regression:
  - first attempt to run old test files directly failed because pytest loaded
    old-tree `tests/conftest.py`, which imported the old `_C.abi3.so` and hit
    a Torch ABI symbol mismatch;
  - rerun with
    `--confcutdir=/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/tests/kernels/attention`
    isolated the kernel tests from the old vLLM package;
  - result: `20 passed in 0.63s` for
    `test_flash_attn_v100_smallq_decode.py` and
    `test_flash_attn_v100_dense_short_prefill.py`.
- Conclusion: the extension/backend are migrated and usable for explicit
  experiments, but `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100` remains default-off. It cannot
  become the automatic/default SM70 route until the classified numeric gate and
  model-level quality gate pass against the latest baseline.

Strict prefill/decode exactness expansion, 2026-05-30:

- Added a model-free strict checker:
  `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`.
- Command:
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py --mode all --json-out bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_strict_exactness.json --allow-nonzero`.
- The checker directly calls `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_func`,
  `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_prefill_paged`, and
  `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_decode_paged`, then compares with a simple
  PyTorch FP32-softmax GQA reference cast back to FP16. It records strict
  `torch.equal`, `max_diff`, `mean_diff`, NaN counts, shape, seed, and kernel
  mode.
- Result on V100/SM70: `passed=false`.
  - dense prefill cases: nonzero `max_diff` from `0.00048828125` to
    `0.0009765625`;
  - paged prefill cases: nonzero `max_diff` from `0.00006103515625` to
    `0.001953125`;
  - paged decode cases: nonzero `max_diff` from `0.0000152587890625` to
    `0.000244140625`;
  - all cases had zero NaNs.
- Interpretation: the V100 attention kernels appear numerically close but not
  bitwise identical to the latest strict reference. Under the current
  `max_diff == 0` rule, this confirms `FLASH_ATTN_V100` cannot be selected by
  default or counted toward accepted mainline performance. It stays
  default-off/experimental unless a future implementation can produce exact
  prefill and decode output.

Triangle exactness update, 2026-05-30:

- User constraint update: FlashAttention2/V100 is a required migration target,
  not a discardable optimization. If `max_diff != 0`, the path must be fixed
  or isolated behind a non-default debug/experimental gate rather than removed
  from the migration plan.
- User reaffirmed on 2026-05-30: the old `0.0.3` tree already carries a
  modified FlashAttention path, so latest-tree FA2/V100 is a mandatory
  migration/fix track. Numerical mismatch, token mismatch, or `max_diff != 0`
  is a defect to debug. It can block default enablement, but it cannot be used
  as a reason to drop FlashAttention2/V100 from the SM70 migration scope.
- Source diff check found that old `0.0.3` and latest-tree
  `flash-attention-v100/` sources are effectively the same, excluding build
  outputs, caches, egg-info, and local metadata. The current exactness problem
  is therefore an inherited kernel/numeric-order issue, not merely a bad port.
- Added a stricter triangle comparison to
  `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`:
  - `dense_prefill` vs `torch_float32`;
  - `paged_prefill` vs `torch_float32`;
  - `paged_prefill` vs `dense_flash_v100`;
  - `paged_decode` vs `torch_float32`;
  - `paged_decode` vs `dense_flash_v100`.
- Evidence file:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_triangle_exactness_20260530.json`.
- Result:
  - all `paged_prefill` vs `dense_flash_v100` cases passed strict equality
    with `max_diff=0.0` and `num_different=0`;
  - all `dense_prefill` vs `torch_float32` cases still had small nonzero
    diffs (`0.00048828125` to `0.0009765625`);
  - `paged_decode` vs `dense_flash_v100` still had nonzero diffs
    (`0.00006103515625` to `0.0009765625` in the tested cases);
  - no NaNs were observed.
- Interpretation:
  - the paged prefill kernel is internally aligned with dense Flash-V100 and
    is the closest candidate for future acceptance once the canonical latest
    vLLM attention oracle is selected;
  - the paged decode kernel is the immediate fix target because it disagrees
    even with dense Flash-V100;
  - dense Flash-V100 itself does not match the simple PyTorch FP32-softmax
    oracle bitwise, so final acceptance must compare against the agreed
    latest-vLLM canonical attention path with strict `max_diff == 0`, not only
    against this diagnostic PyTorch oracle.
- Added an additional decode-as-prefill diagnostic to the same checker and ran:
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python
  benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py --mode paged-decode
  --json-out
  bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_decode_as_prefill_exactness_20260530.json
  --allow-nonzero`.
- Decode-as-prefill result:
  - `paged_decode_as_prefill` vs `dense_flash_v100_causal` passed strict
    equality on all tested decode shapes, including `query_len=1`, `4`, `8`
    and `seq_len=9`, `69`, `181`, `4096`;
  - every such case had `max_diff=0.0` and `num_different=0`;
  - `dense_flash_v100_causal` also matched `dense_flash_v100_decode_steps`
    with `max_diff=0.0`.
- Interpretation update:
  - the `max_diff != 0` issue is not caused by paged KV layout, block table,
    GQA head mapping, or causal offset;
  - the mismatch is localized to the dedicated scalar/partitioned paged decode
    kernel in `flash-attention-v100/kernel/flash_decode_paged.cu`;
  - a quality-safe bridge exists: run decode through the WMMA paged prefill
    kernel, then optimize from that exact compute order instead of accepting
    the scalar decode mismatch.
- Op-level timing probe, `seq_len=4096`, `query_len=1`, `q_heads=6`,
  `kv_heads=1`, `head_dim=256`, `block_size=16`, 200 iterations:
  - scalar `flash_attn_decode_paged`: `0.0492 ms`, but
    `max_diff=6.103515625e-05` vs dense Flash-V100;
  - `decode-as-paged-prefill`: `0.7976 ms`, with `max_diff=0.0` vs dense
    Flash-V100;
  - dense Flash-V100 over contiguous KV: `0.5312 ms`.
- Performance interpretation:
  - the exact decode-as-prefill bridge is too slow to be the final decode
    fast path;
  - it is still important because it proves the exactness issue is solvable
    without changing KV layout or causal semantics;
  - the final FA2/V100 decode optimization should be a new or rewritten paged
    decode kernel that preserves the WMMA/paged-prefill compute order while
    avoiding the full prefill kernel's wasted work for one-token decode.
- Reproducible timing harness update:
  `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` now supports
  `--benchmark-decode`, `--benchmark-warmup`, and `--benchmark-iters`. This
  records timing and exactness for scalar paged decode, decode-as-paged-prefill,
  and dense Flash-V100 in the same JSON file.
- Command:
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python
  benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py --mode paged-decode
  --benchmark-decode --benchmark-warmup 20 --benchmark-iters 80 --json-out
  bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_decode_timing_exactness_20260530.json
  --allow-nonzero`.
- Timing result:
  - `seq_len=4096`, `query_len=1`, `block_size=16`:
    scalar paged decode `0.04928 ms`, `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`;
    decode-as-paged-prefill `0.79987 ms`, `max_diff=0.0`; dense Flash
    `0.54482 ms`;
  - `seq_len=4096`, `query_len=8`, `block_size=832`:
    scalar paged decode `0.17274 ms`, `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`;
    decode-as-paged-prefill `0.59288 ms`, `max_diff=0.0`; dense Flash
    `0.49381 ms`.
- Source-level cause hypothesis, strengthened by this probe:
  `flash_decode_paged.cu` computes decode through scalar/half2 dot products,
  per-partition softmax, fp16 partial outputs, and a second reduce kernel.
  `fused_mha_forward_paged.cu` computes through the WMMA prefill path. The
  latter matches dense Flash-V100 bitwise, so the next implementation should
  preserve WMMA reduction order while specializing away the full prefill
  overhead for decode shapes.
- Partition-size elimination test:
  - `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_PARTITION_SIZE=512` evidence file:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_decode_partition512_exactness_20260530.json`;
  - `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_PARTITION_SIZE=1024` evidence file:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_decode_partition1024_exactness_20260530.json`;
  - both runs still failed `paged_decode` vs `dense_flash_v100`;
  - representative results stayed nonzero:
    `seq_len=9/query_len=1 max_diff=0.0009765625`,
    `seq_len=69/query_len=1 max_diff=0.00048828125`,
    `seq_len=181/query_len=4 max_diff=0.000244140625`,
    `seq_len=4096/query_len=8 max_diff=6.103515625e-05`.
- Conclusion from the partition test: changing partition size is not a safe
  fix. The exactness gap is deeper than the cross-partition reduce setting and
  should be treated as a compute-order mismatch between scalar decode and WMMA
  prefill.
- Added default-off dense-cache bridge path
  `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_DENSE_CACHE=1` in
  `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`.
  - Scope: single-sequence no-MTP decode experiments.
  - Behavior: maintain an incremental contiguous K/V cache after the first
    step and call dense Flash-V100, preserving the WMMA compute order.
  - Quality: the op-level simulation is bitwise equal to dense Flash-V100.
  - Limitation: not CUDA-graph capture safe, not multi-sequence safe, and not
    a final paged decode fast path.
- Dense-cache timing evidence:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_flash_attn_v100_decode_dense_cache_timing_20260530.json`.
  - `seq_len=4096`, `query_len=1`, `block_size=16`:
    scalar paged decode `0.04925 ms`, `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`;
    decode-as-paged-prefill `0.78830 ms`, `max_diff=0.0`;
    dense-cache `0.53059 ms`, `max_diff=0.0`; dense Flash `0.52594 ms`.
  - `seq_len=4096`, `query_len=8`, `block_size=832`:
    scalar paged decode `0.17294 ms`, `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`;
    decode-as-paged-prefill `0.57490 ms`, `max_diff=0.0`;
    dense-cache `0.47237 ms`, `max_diff=0.0`; dense Flash `0.46717 ms`.
- Dense-cache interpretation:
  - this is a better strict bridge than decode-as-paged-prefill for
    single-concurrency experiments because it avoids full paged prefill
    overhead;
  - it still does not recover the scalar paged decode speed, so it cannot be
    the final attention performance route;
  - it gives a useful intermediate acceptance probe for no-MTP single request
    quality while the real exact paged decode kernel is implemented.
- Full-model minimal token gate for dense-cache bridge:
  - baseline command:
    `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1,2,3,4 CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID
    VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND=1 conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210
    python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --model
    /home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 --out
    bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_triton_eager_greedy8_recheck.json
    --tensor-parallel-size 4 --quantization fp8 --dtype auto
    --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.88 --max-tokens 8
    --ignore-eos --disable-custom-all-reduce --enforce-eager
    --attention-backend TRITON_ATTN --prompt "Write one sentence about
    deterministic validation."`;
  - dense-cache command:
    `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1,2,3,4 CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID
    VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND=1 VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=1
    VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_DENSE_CACHE=1 conda run --live-stream -n
    vllm-0.0.5-t210 python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --model
    /home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 --out
    bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_greedy8.json
    --tensor-parallel-size 4 --quantization fp8 --dtype auto
    --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.88 --max-tokens 8
    --ignore-eos --disable-custom-all-reduce --enforce-eager
    --attention-backend FLASH_ATTN_V100 --prompt "Write one sentence about
    deterministic validation."`;
  - compare command:
    `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python
    benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --compare
    bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_triton_eager_greedy8_recheck.json
    bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_greedy8.json
    --json-out
    bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_vs_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_greedy8.json`.
- Full-model minimal token result:
  - `equal=true`;
  - prompt token IDs matched;
  - output token IDs matched for the 8-token greedy sample;
  - logs confirmed `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`, no-prefix prefill
    path, and `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode dense-cache path active`;
  - both runs used `--enforce-eager`, so CUDA graph capture was disabled and
    the Python debug bridge could actually execute.
- Interpretation of the full-model token gate:
  - this is the first full-model evidence that a strict FA2/V100 bridge can
    preserve deterministic tokens against the latest TRITON eager baseline for
    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8;
  - it is not sufficient for default enablement: coverage is only one prompt,
    8 greedy tokens, `max_model_len=4096`, eager mode, and no speed acceptance;
  - next required gates are broader prompt/token coverage, official sampling
    semantics, 256k startup, and either an exact graph-safe paged decode kernel
    or an explicitly documented eager-only/debug scope.
- Added default-off debug path
  `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_DENSE_REFERENCE=1` in
  `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`. It gathers paged KV and
  runs decode through dense Flash-V100 per sequence. This is intentionally a
  slow oracle/debug path, not a speed path. Its purpose is to keep FA2/V100
  work moving while the paged decode CUDA kernel is brought to bitwise parity.
- Added default-off bridge path
  `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_PAGED_PREFILL=1`. It keeps paged KV storage but
  routes decode through `flash_attn_prefill_paged`, which the model-free
  checker proved bitwise aligned with dense Flash-V100 for the tested decode
  shapes. This is the current candidate for quality-safe FA2/V100 decode
  experiments, pending backend-level/full-model token checks and speed
  measurement.
- Broader full-model token gate, batched small-prompt mode:
  - baseline output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_triton_eager_small_prompts_greedy16.json`;
  - dense-cache output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_small_prompts_greedy16.json`;
  - compare output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_vs_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_small_prompts_greedy16.json`.
- Batched small-prompt result:
  - `equal=false`;
  - prompt 0 first output-token mismatch: index `3`, TRITON token `90700`,
    FA2/V100 dense-cache run token `8160`;
  - prompt 1 first output-token mismatch: index `5`, TRITON token `13955`,
    FA2/V100 dense-cache run token `1652`;
  - prompt 2 matched;
  - console logs showed the backend selected `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, ran the
    prefill path, then activated dense-cache and also dense-reference fallback.
    This is expected from the current bridge because dense-cache only supports
    `num_seqs == 1`; batched decode falls back to the slow dense-reference
    oracle instead of the single-sequence cache bridge.
- Added `--sequential-prompts` to
  `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` to isolate single-request
  quality from batched scheduling/fallback behavior.
- Broader full-model token gate, sequential small-prompt mode:
  - TRITON output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_triton_eager_small_prompts_seq_greedy16.json`;
  - FA2/V100 dense-cache output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_small_prompts_seq_greedy16.json`;
  - compare output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_vs_flash_v100_dense_cache_eager_small_prompts_seq_greedy16.json`.
- Sequential small-prompt result:
  - `equal=true`;
  - all three prompt token ID sequences matched;
  - all three 16-token greedy output sequences matched exactly;
  - FA2/V100 logs confirmed the prefill path and
    `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode dense-cache path active`.
- Cross-checks:
  - TRITON batched vs TRITON sequential compare:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_batched_vs_seq_small_prompts_greedy16.json`,

    comparison.
- Fix applied:
  - in `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`, the
    `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_DENSE_CACHE=1` path now routes unsupported
    dense-cache cases through `_flash_v100_decode_as_paged_prefill` when the
    paged prefill kernel is available;
  - this covers multi-sequence decode and FP8 KV-cache storage cases, where
    the previous fallback went to dense-reference and produced batched token
    divergence.
- Fix validation:
  - direct `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_PAGED_PREFILL=1` batched output:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_tp4_flash_v100_decode_as_paged_prefill_eager_small_prompts_greedy16.json`;
