  prefill, and paged decode. All three families produced nonzero `max_diff`
  on V100, so the route is explicitly default-off/experimental.

Compile safety note:

- A first build attempt was started with `uv pip install -e .` and
  `MAX_JOBS=16 NVCC_THREADS=4`. This was stopped before CUDA compilation
  because that concurrency is too aggressive for this workstation.
- User requested conda-based builds and explicit memory control.
- A `vllm-sm70` conda build probe (`Python 3.12`, `torch 2.9.1+cu128`) was
  run with `MAX_JOBS=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`, `--no-deps`. It configured CMake
  and started compiling, but failed in upstream `_C_stable_libtorch`, not in
  the SM70 TurboMind code:
  `torch::headeronly::IntHeaderOnlyArrayRef` and `TORCH_BOX` are missing from
  the Torch 2.9 headers.
- The `vllm-sm70` probe confirmed the latest tree expects newer Torch headers
  for stable-libtorch. Do not keep retrying Torch 2.9 for full latest-vLLM
  builds.
- A `vllm-0.0.5-t210` conda build probe (`Python 3.12`,
  `torch 2.10.0+cu128`, nvcc 12.8) passed the previous stable-libtorch header
  failure point, but then failed in upstream
  `csrc/libtorch_stable/quantization/gptq/q_gemm.cu`.
- That second failure exposed a build-configuration bug: latest vLLM filtered
  `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0` out of `CUDA_SUPPORTED_ARCHS` for CUDA 12.8, so
  per-source `-gencode sm_70` flags were never added and nvcc fell back to its
  default `compute_52`. GPTQ uses `__hfma/__hfma2`, which is not valid under
  that default target.
- Fix: add `7.0` back to `CUDA_SUPPORTED_ARCHS` only for the CUDA 12.8 branch.
  Do not add it to the CUDA 13 branch unless a separate CUDA 13 SM70 proof
  exists.
- Current build policy for the next probe: use the existing
  `vllm-0.0.5-t210` conda environment (`Python 3.12`, `torch 2.10.0+cu128`,
  nvcc 12.8) and compile with moderate parallelism (`MAX_JOBS=8`,
  `NVCC_THREADS=1`) while monitoring memory. Drop back to `MAX_JOBS=4` if
  available host memory falls below a safe margin.
- During long builds, monitor `free -h`, top RSS processes, and `nvidia-smi`;
  reduce to `MAX_JOBS=1` if host memory pressure rises.

### 2026-05-30 Conda SM70 Build and Op Registration

Build command:

```bash
TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="7.0" MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 \
  CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=4 \
  conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 \
  python -m pip install -e . --no-build-isolation --no-deps -v
```

Result:

- Build exited successfully and installed editable latest vLLM.
- CMake reported `CUDA target architectures: 7.0`.
- CMake reported `Building SM70 TurboMind GEMM backend for archs: 7.0`.
- `_C`, `_C_stable_libtorch`, `_moe_C`, FA2, and FA3 shared objects linked.
- Rust `vllm-rs` failed because `protoc` was missing, but the build treats this
  as an optional Rust extension failure and still produced/installed the wheel.
- Host memory stayed safe with `MAX_JOBS=4`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`; sampled memory
  stayed around 26-28 GiB available.
- A later rebuild for the AWQ-only dispatch split used
  `MAX_JOBS=6`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`, and
  `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=6` in the same conda env. It also completed and
  installed successfully. Sampled host memory stayed safe; the highest sampled
  compiler-process RSS sum was about 6.4 GiB and available host memory stayed
  above 18 GiB.
- A later rebuild for the safe FP8 MoE base path used
  `MAX_JOBS=8`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`, and
  `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=8` in the same conda env. It completed and
  installed successfully. Sampled host memory stayed safe; available host
  memory stayed around 20 GiB during the higher-RSS compiler phases.

Important build debt:

- `pip install -e .` creates a fresh temporary CMake build directory each run,
  so even small CMake fixes trigger a full rebuild. For the next long build
  iteration, use a persistent CMake/Ninja build directory or a documented wheel
  build command to avoid paying the full FA2/FA3 rebuild cost repeatedly.
- The nested `vllm-flash-attn` subproject reports no supported arch for `7.0`
  but still compiles many FA2/FA3 sources. This is build-time waste and should
  be decoupled from the V100-only migration path.
- The later `MAX_JOBS=6` rebuild again compiled large FA2/FA3 SM80/SM90
  source sets even though this migration target is SM70-only; this should be a
  build-system cleanup item before repeated benchmark iteration.
- The later `MAX_JOBS=8` rebuild repeated the same FA2/FA3 overhead and spent
  most wall time there. This confirms a persistent build-system cleanup need,
  not an SM70 TurboMind compile bottleneck.

Op registration:

- First successful build had SM70 symbols in `_C.abi3.so`, but
  `torch.ops._C.awq_sm70_prepare` and related schemas were absent.
- Root cause: the `ENABLE_SM70_TURBOMIND` binding macro was not visible to the
  C++ `torch_bindings.cpp` compile.
- After adding a source-level compile definition for `torch_bindings.cpp`, the
  following ops were visible through `torch.ops._C`:
  `awq_sm70_prepare`, `awq_gemm_sm70`, `awq_gemm_sm70_out`,
  `fp8_sm70_prepare`, `fp8_gemm_sm70_out`, `fp8_gemm_sm70_out_meta`,
  `sm70_f16_prepare`, `sm70_f16_gemm`, `sm70_f16_gemm_out`,
  `sm70_gemm_import_cache`, and `sm70_gemm_export_cache`.
- The Python-facing entrypoint in the latest tree is `vllm._sm70_ops`; these
  SM70 ops are not re-exported through latest `vllm._custom_ops`.

Strict exactness:

- Ran `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` on GPU 1, a V100.
- The strict check failed. This blocks default/mainline enablement of the
  current dense TurboMind hooks.
- Evidence file:
  `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_turbomind_exactness_strict.json`.
- After repairing the AWQ oracle, strict checks still fail but now fail with
  finite diffs rather than AWQ reference NaNs. Evidence files:
  - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_turbomind_exactness_strict_manual_awq_ref.json`;
  - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_turbomind_awq_real_qwen36_27b_out_proj_strict.json`;
  - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260530/sm70_turbomind_fp8_real_qwen36_35a3b_out_proj_strict.json`.

layout/equivalent paths must be bitwise exact, while Type B paths require
source proof, dtype-bound proof, non-divergence proof, and model-level quality
evidence before they can count as accepted/default.

non-packed recurrent forward harness added and BV-only gate is Type A strict-pass for B=1/T=16 with and without single-segment `cu_seqlens`; fused sigmoid gating schedule envs are registered default-off/no-op by default, `SCHED=1` with no overrides strict-passes B=1/T=16 with and without single-segment `cu_seqlens`, but explicit `BV=8` changes `final_state` by `7.6294e-06` and remains experimental/not accepted; normal production graph and non-smoke throughput remain pending |

## Next Implementation Steps

1. Done: extract a minimal SM70 TurboMind source subset into the latest vLLM
   target tree under an isolated directory.
2. Done: add build integration for CUDA arch 7.0 without affecting other arch
   builds.
3. Done: add a minimal SM70 op facade and register only the base
   AWQ/FP8/F16 prepare/GEMM ops first.
4. Done: run an SM70 conda build probe in `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
5. In progress: wire AWQ dense linear to the SM70 backend behind an explicit
   exactness gate.
6. Done: add op-level AWQ exactness check script.
7. Done: add op-level FP8 exactness check script.
8. Done: run `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` on V100.
9. Done: build an old-0.0.3-vs-latest parity oracle for the copied TurboMind
   dense ops. The dequant-plus-`torch.matmul` oracle remains a diagnostic
   oracle, not the migration acceptance oracle.
10. Next: decide whether to install/provide `protoc` for the optional Rust
    server component, or explicitly document Rust-free SM70 wheel builds.
11. Next: reduce build cost by avoiding repeated full FA2/FA3 rebuilds during
    SM70-only iteration.
12. Done for minimal smoke: run full Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ and
    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 model startup/generation after the parity oracle was
    defined. Next coverage needs more prompts and same-criterion speed.
13. Done: rebuild the latest tree so the AWQ-only small-shape tuning
    default-off source change is active in `_C`, then rerun AWQ parity without
    setting `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0` in the latest tree.
14. Done for minimal greedy smoke: run full-model deterministic token equality
    for Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ and Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 with SM70 TurboMind gates
    enabled. Both passed old-vs-latest equality for the recorded 8-token
    prompt. Broader prompt coverage is still required before default enablement.
15. Done for first FP8 smoke: add `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
    run latest Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 no-MTP single-request decode at 1024 input /
    32 output. Next speed work must use the real 512-output acceptance length
    and same-criterion old-vs-latest comparison.
16. Done for short old-tree comparison smoke: old 0.0.3 reaches higher short
    decode TPS, but the output token hash differs from the latest safe route
    and the old attempted-aligned run still selects `FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
17. Done: add and run a strict `FLASH_ATTN_V100` attention exactness checker
    for dense prefill, paged prefill, and paged decode. Follow-up triangle
    comparison shows paged prefill is exactly equal to dense Flash-V100, while
    paged decode still differs from dense Flash-V100 and must be fixed rather
    than discarded. A decode-as-paged-prefill bridge also passes strict
    equality against dense Flash-V100 for tested decode shapes.
18. Done: expose `--attention-backend` in the minimal token/decode harnesses
    and rerun old 0.0.3 with `TRITON_ATTN` actually forced through `LLM`.
    This old short safe baseline matches latest safe tokens and reaches
    `72.20 tok/s`.
19. Next: continue the V100 attention migration as a required default-off
    fix path until strict prefill/decode `max_diff == 0` is proven. The
    immediate batched dense-cache fallback failure is fixed by routing
    multi-sequence/FP8-KV unsupported dense-cache cases through the exact
    paged-prefill bridge, and sequential official sampling now matches TRITON.
    The paged-prefill strict bridge now batches uniform-query decode requests
    into one WMMA paged-prefill launch and still passes the three-prompt
    greedy16 TRITON token gate. Continue with lower-overhead batched
    official-sampling logits/probability diagnostics that do not perturb
    scheduling, broader speed and token validation for any
    `async_scheduling=false` SM70 quality policy, and a rewritten scalar paged
    decode kernel that matches the exact WMMA path bitwise, then compare
    against the agreed latest-vLLM canonical attention oracle. The old
    dense/LM-head FP16 fast path is paused because direct V100 op-level
    comparison showed `max_diff != 0`.
20. Done: profile why latest safe short decode is only about `79.8%` of the
    old forced-TRITON/no-LM-head short safe baseline. Latest repeat-range
    profile shows GDN/FLA `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_kernel_h_blockdim64` is
    the largest kernel-time bucket, but the old profiler run is not quality
    evidence because `cudaProfilerStart/Stop` changed old output tokens.
21. Latest explicit-TRITON/gmu0.70 run is recorded as a non-acceptance speed
    probe: speed is unchanged, but deterministic tokens differ from the latest
    auto-selector safe route.
22. Latest no-packed GDN decode probe is recorded as another non-acceptance
    route: speed remains `57.75 tok/s`, and deterministic output diverges at
    output index 16. Do not pursue this as a main-route switch.
23. Next: compare latest-vs-old safe route with profiler evidence rather than
    more blind parameter probes. Focus candidates are latest scheduler and
    async scheduling overhead, compile/cudagraph capture/replay behavior,
    GDN/FLA Triton schedule differences, and safe TurboMind/MoE dispatch
    overhead.
24. Done: add `--cuda-profile-repeat` to the decode harness so long Nsight
    runs can capture only the measured decode repeat. Next profile runs should
    use conda environments and moderate compile/build parallelism
    (`MAX_JOBS`/`CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL` around 8-10, lower if memory
    pressure appears) and should be polled infrequently to avoid wasting
    context during long initialization.
25. Done and rejected for main route: temporary SM70 `chunk_delta_h` schedule
    transplant reached `68.76 tok/s` but failed deterministic token equality.
    The code was fully reverted and latest baseline rechecked back to
    `a11e...` at `57.61 tok/s`.
26. Done: add and run an op-level strict equality harness for
    `chunk_gated_delta_rule` outputs and final state. latest self-repeat and
    old 0.0.3 self-repeat are bitwise stable, but old-vs-latest same-input
    comparison has nonzero `out` and `final_state` diffs, so direct old GDN
    schedule migration is not accepted.
27. Next: isolate GDN/FLA sub-ops and only test schedule changes inside one
    implementation at a time. Candidate sub-ops are `chunk_local_cumsum`,
    `chunk_scaled_dot_kkt`, `solve_tril`, `recompute_w_u`, `chunk_delta_h`,
    and `chunk_fwd_o`. A candidate can move to full-model benchmarking only
    after op-level `max_diff == 0`.
28. Done for first sub-op split: old-vs-latest first diverges at
    `chunk_scaled_dot_kkt_fwd`, while `chunk_local_cumsum` is bitwise equal.
    Next safe experiment should target KKT inside the latest implementation:
    separate "math-source change" from "autotune/schedule search-space change"
    and accept only variants that keep `stage_A_kkt max_diff == 0`.
29. Done for KKT schedule-only experiment: added default-off
    `VLLM_SM70_GDN_KKT_SCHEDULE=1` gate in latest KKT. The gate preserves
    latest math/cast expression and only reduces the SM70 autotune config
    space. Strict compare against default-off passed with
    `stage_A_kkt/out/final_state max_diff=0`.
30. Done: add repeat timing to the GDN harness and classify the KKT schedule
    gate. It is strict-safe but runtime-neutral on the measured shape, so it is
    not a decode-speed recovery path.
31. Next: move the same op-level strict method to `chunk_delta_h`, because it
    is the largest latest Nsight kernel-time bucket and the prior full-model
    schedule transplant showed speed but failed token equality.
32. Done for first delta_h proof: added default-off
    `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DELTA_H_SCHEDULE=1`. On the Qwen-like 1024-token prefill
    shape it is strict-safe (`stage_h/v_new/final_state/out max_diff=0`) and
    faster (`4.331 ms` to `1.384 ms`). One full-model Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8
    short decode smoke also passed deterministic token equality and improved
    from `57.61` to `69.24 tok/s`. Next required gate is broader
    prompt/length coverage before default-on.
33. Done for broader delta_h check: multicase full-model run shows speed
    improvements, but token equality is inconclusive because latest
    default-off itself is cross-process token-unstable on 3 of 4 prompts.
    The op-level delta_h config matrix is still strict-pass for all 12 tested
    configs, with fastest `BV=16, warps=8, stages=1` at `1.437 ms` versus
    default `4.433 ms`. Next required gate is to freeze/canonicalize full GDN
    autotune choices or capture real model GDN/logit tensors and enforce
    `max_diff == 0`; do not default-enable delta_h before that.
34. Done for AWQ MoE first strict split: added a real-weight AWQ MoE
    batched-vs-dense strict harness. The grouped/batched AWQ MoE GEMM failed
    strict equality with `max_diff` up to `0.000244140625`, even with
    `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`. The default AWQ MoE route is now the
    per-expert dense TurboMind exactness bridge; that bridge passed the same
    real-weight stage-wise strict check with `max_diff=0.0`, and the
    grouped/batched kernel is isolated behind
    `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` until repaired. Full-model eager smoke
    passed, but non-eager startup fails in CUDA graph capture because the
    Python bridge reads GPU expert offsets on CPU. `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`
    does not fix this in `CUDAGraphMode.FULL`. A rerun with
    `compilation_config={"cudagraph_mode":"NONE"}` passes load/generate, but
    takes `660.18 s` to load with `470.57 s` profiling/warmup and remains a
    debug route only. Next AWQ MoE work is a capture-safe C++ dense bridge or
    strict-equal grouped/batched kernel repair.
35. Done for one additional FA2/V100 exactness repair probe: replacing
    Flash-V100 softmax `__expf(...)` with `expf(...)` in forward,
    paged-prefill, and paged-decode did not improve strict equality against
    latest TRITON (`max_diff` stayed at `0.000244140625` for the smallest
    head-dim-256 case and `6.103515625e-05` for the long case). The source and
    installed extension were restored. This route is recorded as exhausted;
    next FA2/V100 work must instrument or rewrite the lower-level QK/PV MMA
    and reduction order, or build on the strict-equal latest-TRITON SM70
    schedule path while FA2 remains default-off.
36. Done for split latest-TRITON SM70 attention schedule gate: added separate
    default-off prefill/decode warp envs so a safe prefill schedule does not
    force the same warp count on decode. In the Qwen-like
    `q_heads=6/head_dim=256/block_size=528/seq_len=4096` harness,
    `prefill_num_warps=4/decode_num_warps=8` passed strict equality for both
    phases (`max_diff=0.0`) and measured `1.149x` prefill plus `1.175x`
    decode speedup. Full-model Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 TP4 eager greedy8 token
    comparison against default TRITON also passed (`equal=true`) in
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/model_tokens_compare_triton_default_vs_split_p4_d8_eager_greedy8.json`.
    This is a safe candidate branch but still needs broader prompts, official
    sampling, and same-criterion decode benchmark before default enable.
37. Done and rejected for FA2/V100 exactness: temporarily allowing Triton
    attention `num_warps=16` did not make latest TRITON match the 16-warp
    Flash-V100 WMMA kernels. The probe still had nonzero `max_diff`
    (`0.000244140625` on the smallest head-dim-256 case and
    `6.103515625e-05` on the long 4096-token case). The validator was restored
    to `1/2/4/8`. Next FA2/V100 work remains source-level arithmetic/order
    alignment, not more launch-warp probing.
38. Done and rejected for FA2/V100 exactness: changing Flash-V100 final
    normalization from reciprocal-multiply to direct division did not change
    the Flash-vs-TRITON strict mismatch. The probe result is
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_div_epilogue_probe_20260531.json`.
    Source and installed extension were restored. Next source-level work should
    inspect QK/PV MMA accumulation and row reduction ordering.
39. Done and rejected for FA2/V100 exactness: a dense-only serial row-softmax
    probe did not fix the smallest single-tile mismatch and destabilized
    multi-tile dense outputs, so it was reverted. Evidence:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_dense_serial_row_softmax_probe_20260531.json`.
    This narrows the next attempt to preserving Flash's online-softmax tiling
    while aligning the exact QK/PV MMA and reduction tree with latest TRITON.
40. Done for FA2/V100 basis-value diagnostic: added
    `--mode basis-value-diagnostic` to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` and ran it on V100:
    `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID timeout 300s conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py --mode basis-value-diagnostic --allow-nonzero --json-out bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_basis_value_diagnostic_20260531.json`.
    The result keeps the FA2/V100 route in mandatory-fix status, not
    accepted/default-on status. In every tested case `paged_flash_v100` is
    still bitwise equal to `dense_flash_v100` (`max_diff=0.0`), so paged KV
    layout/paged prefill is not the source. `ones` values also pass strict
    equality against latest TRITON for every tested case (`max_diff=0.0`), so
    plain output normalization/store is not the source. But probability-probe
    values already diverge: `basis_prefix` fails for
    `seq_len=9/q_heads=6` (`max_diff=6.103515625e-05`), `seq_len=69`
    (`6.103515625e-05`), `seq_len=181` (`3.0517578125e-05`), and
    `seq_len=4096` (`1.9073486328125e-06`); `single_key_0` also fails for
    `seq_len=69/181/4096`. This means the Flash-V100-vs-latest-TRITON
    mismatch is already present in the QK/softmax probability path, before
    random-value P@V accumulation can be blamed as the sole cause. Next repair
    target is exact QK dot/reduction + online-softmax update order alignment
    with latest TRITON, while keeping FA2/V100 default-off until
    `max_diff == 0`.
41. Done and rejected for FA2/V100 exactness: temporarily removed Flash-V100's
    softmax exp lower clamp (`fmaxf(score - new_max, -80.0f)`) from dense and
    paged prefill to match latest TRITON's unclamped `tl.exp(S - m_j)` shape.
    Rebuilt only `flash-attention-v100` with
    `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0` and reran the
    basis-value diagnostic:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_no_exp_clamp_probe_20260531.json`.
    The result was unchanged: random values still failed up to
    `max_diff=0.00048828125`, `basis_prefix` still failed for the same
    multi-head/long cases, and `single_key_0` still failed for
    `seq_len=69/181/4096`. The source and installed extension were restored.
    This rules out the exp clamp as the primary cause and leaves QK WMMA dot
    accumulation order, row max/sum reduction order, and exact Triton tile
    traversal as the next source-level suspects.
42. Done for FA2/V100 QK-pattern diagnostic: added
    `--mode qk-pattern-diagnostic` to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` and ran it twice on
    V100:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_ramp_diagnostic_20260531.json`
    and
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_ramp_repeat_20260531.json`.
    This diagnostic varies Q/K while keeping basis/single-key V probes, so it
    can distinguish uniform-mask/softmax issues from QK dot arithmetic. Both
    runs show:
    - `zero_scores` and `constant_single_dim` pass strict probability probes
      for all tested shapes (`max_diff=0.0`), so causal masking, basic
      normalization, and exact uniform-score softmax are not the root cause.
    - `exact_key_ramp_single_dim` also passes `basis_prefix` and
      `single_key_0` for all tested shapes (`max_diff=0.0`). This constructs
      non-uniform scores from exact FP16 values (`q=1`, key ramp, scale
      `1/16` for head_dim 256), so arbitrary non-uniform softmax/reduction
      alone is not enough to explain the mismatch.
    - `random_first_1_dims` fails strict probability probes on longer shapes:
      `seq_len=69` has `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`,
      `seq_len=181` has `max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`, and
      `seq_len=4096` has `basis_prefix max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07`.
      Full random QK still fails as before. This points the next repair at
      Flash-V100 QK dot input/WMMA/tl.dot rounding and accumulation semantics,
      not at paged KV layout or the softmax epilogue.
    - `exact_key_ramp_single_dim` with random V still differs from TRITON on
      longer shapes (`max_diff` up to `6.103515625e-05`), while its probability
      probes pass. This separately confirms a P@V accumulation-order difference
      for random values, but the mandatory probability mismatch route must be
      fixed first.
    - `paged_flash_v100` vs `dense_flash_v100` is generally still strict-equal,
      but each repeat had one synthetic partial-QK/random-V case with nonzero
      diff. That is recorded as a diagnostic instability and is not acceptable
      for any default path; future repair should keep dense/paged triangle
      exactness in the gate.
    Next FA2/V100 source work should inspect and align the QK dot path:
    Flash's `wmma::mma_sync` fragments/load layout/scale placement versus
    latest TRITON `tl.dot` input precision and accumulation, then separately
    revisit P@V accumulation once probability probes reach `max_diff == 0`.
43. Done and rejected for FA2/V100/TRITON `tl.dot` input-precision alignment:
    added default-off diagnostic gates
    `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_QK_INPUT_PRECISION` and
    `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_PV_INPUT_PRECISION` in latest TRITON unified
    attention. Allowed values are empty/default, `ieee`, `tf32`, and `tf32x3`.
    This is a diagnostic gate only; default behavior is unchanged. V100 runs:
    - QK `ieee`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_qk_ieee_20260531.json`.
    - QK `tf32`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_qk_tf32_20260531.json`.
    - QK `tf32x3`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_qk_tf32x3_20260531.json`.
    - PV `ieee`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_pv_ieee_20260531.json`.
    - PV `tf32`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_pv_tf32_20260531.json`.
    - PV `tf32x3`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_pv_tf32x3_20260531.json`.
    All QK precision variants preserved the same probability-probe failures as
    default: for `random_first_1_dims`, `seq_len=69` still has
    `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`, `seq_len=181` still has
    `max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`, and `seq_len=4096` still has
    `basis_prefix max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07`. PV `ieee` and `tf32` also
    kept the exact-key-ramp/random-V differences unchanged; PV `tf32x3`
    worsened one short `q_heads=6` exact-key-ramp/random-V case to
    `max_diff=0.0009765625`. Conclusion: Triton's exposed
    `input_precision` knob does not align latest TRITON with Flash-V100 on
    SM70 FP16 attention. Next work remains lower-level source alignment of the
    Flash QK/PV compute order or a strict-equal SM70 Triton schedule; do not
    count any of these precision variants as a default/mainline route.
44. Done for FA2/V100 QK reference diagnostic: added
    `--mode qk-reference-diagnostic` to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` and ran:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_reference_diagnostic_20260531.json`.
    This compares Flash-V100 and latest TRITON against two explicit PyTorch
    first-dimension score references: FP32 product and half product. Result:
    - `exact_key_ramp_single_dim` probability probes still have
      Flash-V100 vs TRITON `max_diff=0.0` for `basis_prefix` and
      `single_key_0`, confirming again that exact non-uniform scores can flow
      through both kernels identically.
    - The same exact-ramp probability probes do not match either simple
      PyTorch reference bitwise (`max_diff` up to `1.52587890625e-05`), while
      FP32-product and half-product references are equal to each other for
      this exact-ramp input. Therefore PyTorch softmax/matmul reference is not
      a valid bitwise oracle for the kernel schedule.
    - `random_first_1_dims` probability probes still differ between
      Flash-V100 and TRITON (`seq_len=69 max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`,
      `seq_len=181 max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`,
      `seq_len=4096 basis_prefix max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07`).
      FP32-product and half-product references also differ from each other on
      this random single-dim input, which supports the conclusion that input
      half multiply/rounding is involved, but neither reference exactly matches
      both kernels.
    - `exact_key_ramp_single_dim` with random V still has Flash-V100 vs TRITON
      P@V differences (`max_diff` from `1.52587890625e-05` to
      `6.103515625e-05`), so P@V remains a separate follow-up after QK
      probability reaches `max_diff == 0`.
    Conclusion: do not replace the exactness oracle with a simple PyTorch
    reference. The next useful source-level path is a direct kernel-side QK
    score dump/compare or an intentional rewrite of Flash-V100 QK/PV to mimic
    latest TRITON's tile/update semantics exactly.
45. Direction lock and new FA2/V100 factor probes after the migration-scope
    correction: the total goal remains "migrate all safe 0.0.3 SM70/V100
    optimizations into latest vLLM, decoupled, with latest vLLM upper layers".
    `FLASH_ATTN_V100` is still a required migration item, not a discarded item,
    but any Flash/V100 branch must remain default-off until the optimized output
    has strict `max_diff == 0` against the latest vLLM baseline. If a probe has
    nonzero diff, the required action is source-level repair or a strict-equal
    replacement schedule, not accepting tolerance.

    Added more QK pattern probes to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`:
    `random_q_exact_key_ramp_single_dim`, `unit_q_random_key_single_dim`, and
    `half_q_random_key_single_dim`, then added explicit `query_len=1` cases for
    decode-shaped FA2-vs-TRITON evidence. V100 evidence files:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_factor_probe_20260531.json`;
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_pattern_factor_probe_q1_v2_20260531.json`;
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_q1_triton_compare_20260531.json`.

    Findings:
    - `query_len=1` is not automatically safe. In the all/compare run, latest
      TRITON vs `dense_flash_v100` and `paged_prefill` still has nonzero diff:
      `seq_len=69/q=1 max_diff=0.000244140625` and
      `seq_len=4096/q=1 max_diff=0.000030517578125`.
    - For `seq_len=69/q=1`, exact-ramp probability probes stay strict-equal, and
      `unit_q_random_key_single_dim` plus `half_q_random_key_single_dim` also
      stay strict-equal. But `random_first_1_dims` fails the probability probe:
      `basis_prefix max_diff=1.52587890625e-05` and random-V output
      `max_diff=0.000244140625`. This shows the single-token decode shape is
      still not strict-equal; item 46 below refines the source to softmax/update
      rather than raw single-dim QK scores.
    - For `seq_len=4096/q=1`, random-key-only probes already expose a tiny
      probability diff (`basis_prefix max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07`), and
      `random_first_1_dims` has `basis_prefix max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07`,
      `single_key_0 max_diff=1.1920928955078125e-07`, and random-V
      `max_diff=0.000030517578125`.

    Conclusion: the FA2/V100 migration must continue, but the current Flash-V100
    implementation is not a strict-equal drop-in for latest TRITON even for
    single-token decode. Until that reaches `max_diff == 0`, the
    production/default SM70 attention route remains latest TRITON plus only
    strict-equal schedule knobs.
46. Done for kernel-side FA2/V100 QK score dump: added a debug-only
    `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_qk_scores` binding and
    `--mode qk-score-diagnostic` in
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`. Rebuilt only
    `flash-attention-v100` with
    `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0` in conda env
    `vllm-0.0.5-t210`. V100 evidence:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_score_diagnostic_20260531.json`.

    Result:
    - `random_first_1_dims` raw QK scores are strict-equal between Flash-V100
      WMMA and the diagnostic Triton `tl.dot` kernel for all tested shapes:
      `seq_len=69/q=1`, `seq_len=69/q=16`, and `seq_len=4096/q=1` all have
      `max_diff=0.0`.
    - The same shapes previously had nonzero probability/output diffs, so for
      the single-dim random case the first root cause is not raw QK score
      multiplication. It is Flash-V100's softmax/update path: row max, exp,
      row-sum reduction, online accumulator scaling, or the exact order around
      those operations.
    - `exact_key_ramp_single_dim`, `random_q_exact_key_ramp_single_dim`,
      `unit_q_random_key_single_dim`, and `half_q_random_key_single_dim` also
      have raw QK `max_diff=0.0`.
    - `random_first_2_dims` already shows tiny raw score diffs
      (`max_diff=2.9802322387695312e-08`), and `random_full` shows raw score
      diffs up to `5.7220458984375e-06`. Those are second-layer QK accumulation
      differences that still must be solved after the softmax/update mismatch is
      fixed.

    Updated repair order: first align Flash-V100 softmax/update semantics with
    latest TRITON for the raw-QK-equal single-dim case until probability probes
    reach `max_diff == 0`; then address multi-dim/full-random raw QK accumulation
    and P@V accumulation. No FA2/V100 route is default-eligible before all three
    layers are strict-equal.
47. Done for FA2/V100 softmax-LSE diagnostic: added a debug-only Python wrapper
    `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_lse` that calls the existing Volta C++ forward
    with `return_softmax=False` and returns the already-produced
    `softmax_lse`. The first attempted diagnostic used
    `return_attn_probs=True` and failed because `flash-attention-v100` rejects
    `return_softmax`; this failed route is recorded here to avoid repeating it.
    Added `--mode softmax-lse-diagnostic` in
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`, comparing Flash LSE
    against diagnostic Triton online-softmax LSE with tile16/tile32 and a
    Torch FP32 `logsumexp` reference. V100 evidence:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_softmax_lse_diagnostic_20260531.json`;
    failed first-run log:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_softmax_lse_diagnostic_20260531.log`.

    Findings:
    - For `random_first_1_dims`, where item 46 proved raw QK scores are
      strict-equal, Flash LSE is still not bitwise equal to Triton/Torch-style
      LSE: `seq_len=69/q=1 max_diff=4.76837158203125e-07`,
      `seq_len=69/q=16 max_diff=4.76837158203125e-07`, and
      `seq_len=4096/q=1 max_diff=9.5367431640625e-07`.
    - This supports the current root-cause order: the first nonzero probability
      path is in softmax/update denominator semantics, not raw single-dim QK
      multiplication.
    - However this diagnostic is not by itself a complete oracle. Some
      exact-ramp cases have small Flash-vs-diagnostic-LSE diffs while their
      probability probes are strict-equal against latest TRITON. Therefore we
      should not "fix" only LSE against the custom diagnostic and assume the
      backend is safe.

    Next source-level diagnostic: dump or reconstruct the unnormalized
    single-key numerator (`exp(score - row_max)` after Flash's float-to-half
    conversion) and final row sum for the raw-QK-equal `random_first_1_dims`
    case. The acceptance target remains strict probability/output
    `max_diff == 0`, not merely matching an approximate LSE reference.
48. Done for FA2/V100 softmax tile simulation and `BLOCK_N_256=16` source
    experiment. Added `--mode softmax-tile-sim-diagnostic` to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`; it uses Flash's dumped
    raw QK scores, simulates online softmax with tile16/tile32/tile64,
    FP16-cast probabilities, optional Flash clamp80, and compares simulated
    single-key probabilities with real `dense_flash_v100` and latest TRITON
    outputs. V100 evidence:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_softmax_tile_sim_diagnostic_20260531.json`.

    Important result on `seq_len=69/q=1/random_first_1_dims`:
    - key 28 is the previous `basis_prefix` max-diff location.
    - Real Flash vs TRITON single-key output differs by
      `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`.
    - `sim_tile16_p_half` and `sim_tile32_p_half` match latest TRITON exactly
      (`max_diff=0.0`) for key 28.
    - `sim_tile64_p_half_clamp80` matches Flash exactly (`max_diff=0.0`) for
      key 28.

    This proves the single-dim probability split is caused by online-softmax
    tile/update order interacting with FP16 probability materialization before
    later alpha rescale. It is not caused by raw single-dim QK score mismatch.

    Source experiment: changed Flash-V100 D=256 `BLOCK_N_256` from `64` to `16`
    in dense/paged traits, rebuilt with
    `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`, and ran:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_blockn16_qk_pattern_20260531.json`;
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_blockn16_compare_triton_20260531.json`.

    Rejected result:
    - q-pattern subset improved some selected single-dim locations but did not
      reach strict equality.
    - It introduced/expanded exact-ramp probability diffs, for example
      `seq_len=69/q=1/exact_key_ramp_single_dim basis_prefix
      max_diff=7.62939453125e-06`.
    - Full Flash-vs-TRITON compare still failed and maxed at
      `0.0009765625`.

    The `BLOCK_N_256=16` source experiment was restored to `BLOCK_N_256=64` in
    `fused_mha_forward.cu`, `fused_mha_forward_paged.cu`,
    `fused_mha_forward_paged_full.cu`, and `flash_v100_traits.cuh`, then
    `flash-attention-v100` was rebuilt. A restored smoke rechecked key 28 and
    reproduced the known baseline diff (`1.52587890625e-05`, Flash
    `0.0160675048828125`, TRITON `0.01605224609375`). Conclusion: a naive
    global `BLOCK_N` change is not acceptable. The repair needs a more exact
    schedule alignment that preserves exact-ramp equality while matching latest
    TRITON's single-dim probability behavior.
49. Done for the first default-eligible latest-vLLM SM70 attention fast path:
    keep the latest TRITON `unified_attention` backend selected and only change
    SM70 launch meta-parameters. Added
    `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_SAFE_DEFAULTS`, defaulting to `1` on SM70. When this
    gate is on and no explicit warp override is supplied, latest TRITON uses
    `prefill_num_warps=4` and `decode_num_warps=8`. Set
    `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_SAFE_DEFAULTS=0` to recover upstream default launch
    parameters for A/B tests.

    Implementation files:
    - `vllm/envs.py`: registered the safe-default gate and existing schedule
      knobs.
    - `vllm/v1/attention/ops/triton_unified_attention.py`: applies SM70-only
      safe defaults after explicit overrides and before launching the Triton
      kernel.

    V100 strict evidence against upstream defaults disabled:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_triton_prefill4_decode8_vs_upstream_disabled_20260531.json`.
    Command used `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1`, conda env `vllm-0.0.5-t210`,
    `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_SAFE_DEFAULTS=0`, `seq_len=4096`,
    `prefill_query_len=128`, `decode_query_len=1`, `q_heads=6`,
    `kv_heads=1`, `head_dim=256`, `block_size=528`, `warmup=10`,
    `repeats=20`.

    Results:
    - `prefill4_decode8` prefill: `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`,
      `num_different=0`, median `2.737152 ms` vs upstream default
      reference `3.156992 ms`, speedup `1.153x`.
    - `prefill4_decode8` decode: `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`,
      `num_different=0`, median `2.221056 ms` vs upstream default
      reference `2.624512 ms`, speedup `1.182x`.
    - `warps=8` is not acceptable for prefill despite speed because it produced
      `max_diff=7.62939453125e-06`.
    - Tile overrides are not acceptable: `tile16` produced
      `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`, and `tile64` also produced nonzero diffs.

    A first run was accidentally executed without disabling safe defaults and
    was renamed to
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_triton_safe_defaults_on_full_sweep_20260531.json`.
    It should only be read as a safe-default-on full sweep, not as an upstream
    disabled reference.

    Policy decision: this is currently the only attention fast path that is
    default-eligible in latest vLLM because it preserves strict output equality.
    The FA2/Flash-V100 migration remains required, but it stays behind
    `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=1` until its probability/output path reaches
    `max_diff == 0`. The next FA2 repair target is still the online-softmax
    update/materialization semantics identified in items 46-48.
50. Done for a more precise Flash-V100 vs latest-TRITON softmax-update trace.
    Added `--mode softmax-update-trace-diagnostic` to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`. It uses Flash-V100's
    dumped raw QK scores, single-key value probes, and per-tile scalar traces
    for the online softmax state (`old_max`, `new_max`, `alpha`, `tile_sum`,
    `row_sum`, `numerator`) under both latest-TRITON-like tile sizes and the
    current Flash-V100 D=256 tile64/clamp80 path. V100 evidence:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_softmax_update_trace_diagnostic_20260531.json`;
    log:
    `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_softmax_update_trace_diagnostic_20260531.log`.

    Key result for `seq_len=69`, `query_len=1`,
    `random_first_1_dims`, `key_idx=28`, output index `[0,0,2]`:
    - Raw QK score compare is strict-equal:
      `flash_v100_qk_scores` vs diagnostic Triton `tl.dot` has
      `max_diff=0.0`.
    - Real Flash-V100 output differs from latest TRITON:
      Flash `0.0160675048828125` vs TRITON `0.01605224609375`,
      `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`.
    - The TRITON-like simulation with tile16 and half P matches latest TRITON
      exactly (`max_diff=0.0`) and differs from Flash by the same amount.
    - The Flash-like simulation with tile64, half P, and clamp80 matches real
      Flash exactly (`max_diff=0.0`) and differs from TRITON by the same amount.

    The scalar trace explains the first divergence:
    - Flash tile64 covers keys `[0,64)`. For key 28, `exp(score-new_max)` is
      `0.9749495387077332`; Flash immediately materializes it as half
      `0.97509765625`, keeps that rounded numerator, then divides by final
      `row_sum=60.713111877441406`, producing final half
      `0.0160675048828125`.
    - Latest TRITON decode tile16 first sees key 28 in tile `[16,32)`, where it
      is the local tile max and is materialized as exactly `1.0`; later tiles
      rescale the accumulated numerator with float `alpha`
      (`0.9789527654647827`, then `0.9959107041358948`), producing final half
      `0.01605224609375`.

    This answers the current root-cause question: for single-dim QK where raw
    scores are equal, nonzero output diff is not inevitable to FlashAttention
    as an algorithm. It is caused by this implementation's D=256 `BLOCK_N=64`
    softmax/PV update schedule and the timing of FP16 probability
    materialization. A viable repair direction is a strict mode that keeps the
    fast Flash-V100 QK tile but performs softmax/PV updates in TRITON-compatible
    virtual subtiles (`16` for decode, `32` for prefill), so the half-P cast
    and float `alpha` rescale happen at the same boundaries as latest TRITON.

    The trace also records the next unsolved layer: multi-dim/full-random raw
    QK is already not bitwise equal before softmax. In the same diagnostic:
    - `random_first_2_dims`: raw QK `max_diff=2.9802322387695312e-08`.
    - `random_full`: raw QK `max_diff=2.6226043701171875e-06` for
      `seq_len=69/q=1`, `3.814697265625e-06` for `seq_len=69/q=16`, and
      `5.7220458984375e-06` for `seq_len=4096/q=1`.

    Therefore the Flash-V100 strict-equality migration has two ordered repair
    tasks:
    1. Align online-softmax/PV sub-tile update and half-P cast boundaries with
       latest TRITON. This should fix the raw-QK-equal single-dim class and is
       not conceptually impossible.
    2. After task 1, align QK accumulation order/rounding for multi-dim/full
       QK. Until this also reaches `max_diff == 0`, Flash-V100 remains
       experimental and cannot be default-enabled.
51. Done for the first source-level FA2/V100 repair experiment. Modified only
    dense `flash-attention-v100/kernel/fused_mha_forward.cu` to add a D=256
    strict-softmax experiment: keep the existing Flash QK tile, but allocate a
    separate small half-P buffer and perform softmax/PV updates in virtual
    TRITON-compatible subtiles (`16` for `M<=1` decode, `32` otherwise). D!=256
    falls back to the original one-block behavior after a follow-up fix to avoid
    breaking head_dim 128. Rebuilt `flash-attention-v100` with
    `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0` in conda env
    `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.

    Evidence after the dense subtile experiment:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_dense_subtile_softmax_trace_20260531.json`
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_dense_subtile_softmax_trace_v2_20260531.json`
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_dense_subtile_full_compare_v2_20260531.json`
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_dense_subtile_qk_pattern_20260531.json`
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_dense_subtile_basis_value_20260531.json`

    What improved:
    - The exact previously explained single-key mismatch
      `seq_len=69/q=1/random_first_1_dims/key=28` is fixed for dense Flash:
      Flash dense now matches latest TRITON (`max_diff=0.0`) and matches the
      TRITON-like tile16/half-P simulation, while the old tile64 simulation now
      differs by the old `1.52587890625e-05`.
    - Similar selected key28 probes for `seq_len=69/q=16` and
      `seq_len=4096/q=1` also moved to the TRITON-like path. This proves the
      tile/update/cast-boundary repair direction is real and not just a
      theoretical explanation.
    - The temporary D!=256 breakage was caught by full compare
      (`head_dim=128` showed large diffs), then fixed by using the separate
      p-buffer and subtile path only for D=256. The v2 full compare restores
      head_dim 128 to normal small-diff behavior.

    What is still not solved:
    - Full dense Flash is still not strict-equal to latest TRITON. In v2 full
      compare, dense-vs-TRITON still has:
      `seq_len=69/q=1/head_dim=256 max_diff=0.00048828125`,
      `seq_len=69/q=16/head_dim=256 max_diff=0.000244140625`,
      `seq_len=4096/q=1/head_dim=256 max_diff=0.000030517578125`, and
      `seq_len=4096/q=8/head_dim=256 max_diff=0.00006103515625`.
    - Basis/single-key probes show a second softmax arithmetic gap even after
      virtual subtiles. Example:
      `seq_len=69/q=1/random_first_1_dims/key=2` still differs:
      Flash `0.01264190673828125` vs TRITON `0.0126495361328125`
      (`max_diff=7.62939453125e-06`). The new trace shows the D=256 dense
      kernel now matches the Python TRITON-like tile16 simulation, but not the
      actual latest TRITON kernel. That points at lower-level arithmetic:
      CUDA C++ `__expf` plus warp reductions are not bitwise identical to
      Triton's `tl.exp` plus `tl.sum` lowering, and the difference crosses a
      half rounding boundary.
    - Multi-dim/full-random QK remains non-bitwise-equal before softmax, as
      recorded in item 50.

    Current conclusion:
    - FlashAttention as an algorithm is not inherently incompatible with
      `max_diff==0`; the first tile/cast mismatch can be repaired.
    - The current C++ Flash-V100 implementation is still not default-eligible.
      To make it strict-equal, the next repair must reproduce latest TRITON's
      exp/reduction arithmetic and then QK accumulation order. If that forces
      Flash to become a near-clone of the Triton kernel, the safer engineering
      answer may be to keep latest TRITON as the default SM70 attention fast
      path and keep Flash-V100 as experimental until a strict arithmetic clone
      is proven.
52. Done for the requested FlashAttention boundary coverage and long-context
    speed pass. Added `--mode edge-cases` to
    `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` and expanded decode
    timing to record latest-TRITON timing alongside Flash dense/paged paths.
    V100 commands used `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1`, conda env
    `vllm-0.0.5-t210`, and `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_SAFE_DEFAULTS=1`.

    Evidence:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_edge_cases_20260531.json`
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_decode_timing_long_20260531.json`

    Source correction relative to item 51: after checking the real latest-vLLM
    TRITON reference path, q=1 in this direct benchmark is still using the 2D
    unified-attention path, so the dense D=256 source experiment now uses a
    fixed virtual softmax/PV subtile size of `32` (`P_SUB_TILE=32`) rather than
    `16` for q=1. The corrected trace was:
    `sm70_flash_v100_tile32_triton_debug_prob_trace_20260531.json`, where
    `seq_len=69/q=1/random_first_1_dims` selected keys 0, 1, 2, 3, and 28 all
    matched latest TRITON exactly. This only fixes that narrow probability
    class; it does not make full random attention strict-equal.

    Boundary-case results:
    - Causal mask semantics are correct in a deterministic mask probe:
      `seq_len=17/query_len=5` causal and non-causal Flash both match the
      PyTorch reference with `max_diff=0.0`, while causal vs non-causal differs
      by `0.125`. This verifies the mask is actually active and not ignored.
    - Random mask cases still have arithmetic diffs. At `seq_len=69/query_len=16`
      causal dense Flash vs latest TRITON had `max_diff=0.0009765625`, and
      paged-prefill had `max_diff=0.00048828125`. At
      `seq_len=4096/query_len=8`, both dense and paged-prefill were
      `max_diff=0.00006103515625`.
    - Non-causal dense/paged cases compare only against PyTorch because latest
      vLLM `unified_attention` asserts causal-only. They also show expected
      FP16 arithmetic diffs, not strict equality.
    - Varlen is not strict-equal. Uniform query varlen
      `seq_lens=[69,181,4096]`, `query_lens=[8,8,8]`, block16 had
      `max_diff=0.00048828125`; ragged query varlen
      `query_lens=[1,3,8]` had `max_diff=0.000244140625`.
    - Non-contiguous inputs are safe for the tested supported layouts:
      dense q/k/v, paged-prefill q/key_cache/value_cache/block_table, and
      paged-decode q/key_cache/value_cache/block_table/seq_lens all matched
      their contiguous Flash references with `max_diff=0.0`.
    - Long-context diffs do not disappear. With block16, q_heads=6, kv_heads=1,
      head_dim=256:
      `4096/q1` dense `3.05e-5`, paged-prefill `3.05e-5`, paged-decode
      `6.10e-5`;
      `8192/q1` all around `3.05e-5`;
      `16384/q1` dense/paged-prefill `1.53e-5`, paged-decode `3.05e-5`;
      `32768/q1` dense and paged-decode `1.53e-5`.
    - A real long-context resource boundary was found:
      `seq_len=32768/block_size=16/head_dim=256` paged-prefill fails with
      `Shared memory exceeds 96KB: 102144 bytes`. The same limit hits the
      decode-as-paged-prefill/WMMA bridge. Scalar paged decode does not hit
      this limit because it uses the partitioned decode kernel and switches to
      partition size `512` past the 20480-token threshold.

    Speed results against latest TRITON unified attention:
    - `4096/q1/block16`: scalar paged decode `0.0426 ms` vs TRITON
      `2.2147 ms` (`~52.0x`), but `max_diff=6.10e-5`.
    - `4096/q8/block16`: scalar paged decode `0.1626 ms` vs TRITON
      `2.6382 ms` (`~16.2x`), but `max_diff=6.10e-5`.
    - `16384/q1/block16`: scalar paged decode `0.1148 ms` vs TRITON
      `8.2546 ms` (`~71.9x`), but `max_diff=3.05e-5`.
    - `32768/q1/block16`: scalar paged decode `0.2155 ms` vs TRITON
      `16.2992 ms` (`~75.6x`), but `max_diff=1.53e-5`; the strict
      paged-prefill bridge is unavailable here due to shared-memory pressure.
    - Dense Flash remains faster than TRITON in these shapes (`~2.8x-4.4x`) but
      still has `1.53e-5` to `3.05e-5` diffs vs TRITON in long decode-like
      cases.

    Current policy after this pass:
    - Latest TRITON with SM70 safe defaults remains the only default-eligible
      attention path because it is strict-equal to upstream TRITON and gives
      `~15%-18%` speedup vs the unmodified launch parameters.
    - Flash-V100 scalar paged decode is the important speed path and should not
      be discarded: it is the only path that recovers very large long-context
      decode speed. However it cannot be default-enabled yet because edge-case
      coverage still shows nonzero diffs up to `0.0009765625` in short/random
      cases and `6.10e-5` in normal 4k decode-like cases.
    - If accepting `~1.5e-5` is allowed, the next required gate is not another
      short kernel smoke. It must be a model-level amplification test across
      many layers and token steps, plus focused repair of the larger
      `6.10e-5`/`0.000244`/`0.000488`/`0.000976` classes. Only after those are
      bounded can Flash-V100 move from experimental opt-in to a runtime fast
      path.
53. Done for the first AWQ/FP8 TurboMind dense migration parity split.
    Updated `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_parity.py` so each dump records
    SHA256 digests for the deterministic input, raw model tensors, prepared
    TurboMind tensors, and meta values. This makes future old-vs-latest
    comparisons distinguish packing/prepare drift from GEMM execution drift.

    Evidence:
    - FP8 dense old 0.0.3 vs latest, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8
      `model.language_model.layers.1.linear_attn.out_proj`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_fp8_dense_old003_vs_latest_qwen36_35a3b_outproj_m1.json`
      and
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_fp8_dense_old003_vs_latest_qwen36_35a3b_outproj_m8.json`.
      Both passed strict equality (`max_diff=0.0`).
    - AWQ dense old 0.0.3 vs latest, Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ
      `model.language_model.layers.1.linear_attn.out_proj`, `m=1`:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_dense_old003_vs_latest_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1_with_details.json`.
      This failed strict equality (`max_diff=0.00048828125`).
    - The details in that JSON show input, qweight, qzeros, scales, tm_weight,
      tm_scales, and meta SHA256 all match between old and latest. Therefore
      the AWQ diff is not caused by model loading, packing, zero/scales
      handling, or `awq_sm70_prepare`; it starts inside the GEMM execution
      strategy/runtime.
    - Old 0.0.3 AWQ dense repeated in a new process with the same input and
      same prepared tensors also failed strict equality:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_dense_old003_repeat_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1.json`,
      `max_diff=0.00048828125`. This means the old default small-M dynamic
      measurement path is not a valid bitwise-stable oracle.
    - Latest AWQ dense default repeated cleanly:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_dense_latest_repeat_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1.json`
      and
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_dense_latest_repeat_qwen36_27b_outproj_m8.json`
      both passed strict equality (`max_diff=0.0`).

    Source finding:
    - The migrated AWQ dense entry point is effectively the old 0.0.3 source
      plus a split dispatch gate. In 0.0.3, `select_dense_dispatch_policy`
      treated `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` as enabled by default
      (`raw == nullptr || atoi(raw) != 0`), so small `m` can run `kMeasure`.
      In latest, `select_awq_dense_dispatch_policy` treats the same tuning as
      opt-in only (`raw != nullptr && atoi(raw) != 0`) and therefore defaults
      to `kDefault`.
    - Forcing latest with `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1` did not restore
      strict old-vs-latest equality:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_dense_old003_vs_latest_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1_tune1.json`
      still had `max_diff=0.00048828125`.

    Current policy:
    - Do not restore the old AWQ small-M `kMeasure` behavior as a default just
      to claim parity. It is already proven not bitwise-stable across old 0.0.3
      process repeats.
    - Latest AWQ dense default is currently the safer strict route because it
      is repeatable (`max_diff=0.0`) for `m=1` and `m=8` on a real Qwen3.6 AWQ
      layer. To recover any speed lost by disabling dynamic measurement, the
      next step should be a deterministic fixed dispatch/plan benchmark rather
      than runtime measurement that can select different kernels.
    - FP8 dense migration is in a better state: old-vs-latest real-model dense
      parity is already strict for `m=1` and `m=8`.
54. Done for AWQ/FP8 dense latest-tree repeat stability and pure GEMM timing.
    Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_parity.py` with `--bench`
    mode. It times only the prepared TurboMind dense GEMM call on a real model
    layer using CUDA events and records output SHA256, so it does not mix in
    model scheduling, attention, sampling, or serving overhead.

    Evidence:
    - AWQ latest tune1 repeat check:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_awq_dense_latest_tune1_repeat_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1.json`.
      Result: failed strict equality with `max_diff=0.00048828125`.
      This proves `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1` is not default-eligible
      under the current strict quality rule even inside the latest tree.
    - AWQ latest default fixed-dispatch timing, Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ
      `model.language_model.layers.1.linear_attn.out_proj`:
      `sm70_awq_dense_latest_default_timing_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1.json`
      reported mean `0.056033 ms`;
      `sm70_awq_dense_latest_default_timing_qwen36_27b_outproj_m8.json`
      reported mean `0.051779 ms`.
    - AWQ latest tune1 timing for the same layer:
      `sm70_awq_dense_latest_tune1_timing_qwen36_27b_outproj_m1.json`
      reported mean `0.044006 ms` (`1.273x` faster than default);
      `sm70_awq_dense_latest_tune1_timing_qwen36_27b_outproj_m8.json`
      reported mean `0.049034 ms` (`1.056x` faster than default).
      Because tune1 is not bitwise-stable, this speed cannot count toward the
      mainline accepted performance route yet.
    - FP8 latest repeat checks:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_fp8_dense_latest_repeat_qwen36_35a3b_outproj_m1.json`
      and
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_fp8_dense_latest_repeat_qwen36_35a3b_outproj_m8.json`.
      Both passed strict equality with `max_diff=0.0`.
    - FP8 latest timing, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8
      `model.language_model.layers.1.linear_attn.out_proj`:
      `sm70_fp8_dense_latest_timing_qwen36_35a3b_outproj_m1.json`
      reported mean `0.037622 ms`;
      `sm70_fp8_dense_latest_timing_qwen36_35a3b_outproj_m8.json`
      reported mean `0.038303 ms`.

    Current policy after this pass:
    - Keep AWQ dense default on the fixed-dispatch strict route. Do not enable
      `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1` by default.
    - The speed gap from AWQ tune1 is real, especially for `m=1`, so the next
      performance-recovery route is to identify and pin a deterministic
      fixed-dispatch plan that is both repeat-stable (`max_diff=0.0`) and close
      to the measured tune1 speed. Runtime `kMeasure` remains an experimental
      non-mainline path until that is proven.
    - FP8 dense is now stronger than AWQ dense on this narrow gate: it has
      old-vs-latest strict parity, latest self-repeat strict parity, and a
      pure-GEMM timing baseline for real Qwen3.6 FP8 weights.
55. Done for the first FlashAttention-V100 edge-case repair round under the
    stricter A/B acceptance taxonomy.

    User requirement for this round:
    - Type A layout-equivalent paths must reach `max_diff=0.0`; do not loosen
      tolerances.
    - Type B long-sequence reduction-order paths may only be accepted after
      proof that the diff comes from arithmetic order, stays within dtype
      bounds, and does not grow with sequence length.
    - Crash/OOM/shared-memory overflow is unconditional failure.
    - Scalar paged decode speed must not regress.

    Source changes:
    - Added `--mode varlen-layout-diagnostic` to
      `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`. This is an additive
      diagnostic only; it does not replace the original strict edge-case
      reference.
    - Changed `flash-attention-v100/kernel/fused_mha_forward_paged.cu` so
      paged-prefill D=256 uses the same strict 32-key softmax/PV sub-tile
      materialization already used by dense Flash. This fixes the paged-vs-dense
      half-P cast boundary mismatch.
    - Removed shared-memory staging of the whole `block_table` row in paged
      prefill and switched to read-only global loads for page ids. This frees
      enough shared memory for the D=256 strict P buffer and removes the
      `max_num_blocks * sizeof(int)` shared-memory growth that caused the
      32k/block16/head_dim256 96KB overflow.

    Build:
    - Rebuilt only `flash-attention-v100` in conda env `vllm-0.0.5-t210` with
      `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`.

    Evidence:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_varlen_layout_diagnostic_after_paged_subtile_20260531.json`
      passed the new Type-A layout gate:
      - uniform varlen:
        `flash_v100_batched_paged_prefill` vs looped paged `max_diff=0.0`;
        looped full-cache paged vs standalone paged `max_diff=0.0`;
        looped paged vs dense Flash `max_diff=0.0`;
        Triton varlen vs Triton looped `max_diff=0.0`.
      - ragged varlen:
        looped full-cache paged vs standalone paged `max_diff=0.0`;
        looped paged vs dense Flash `max_diff=0.0`;
        Triton varlen vs Triton looped `max_diff=0.0`.
      This proves the old varlen `4.88e-4/2.44e-4` signal was not caused by
      `cu_seqlens`, batch-row indexing, block-table row selection, or
      cross-sequence leakage.
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_paged_prefill_after_paged_subtile_20260531.json`
      showed all `paged_prefill` vs `dense_flash_v100` cases now pass strict
      equality with `max_diff=0.0`, including D=256 and D=128 cases.
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_edge_cases_after_paged_subtile_20260531.json`
      still fails the original edge-case gate because Flash-V100 and latest
      Triton are not yet bitwise-identical arithmetic families. The varlen
      comparisons against Triton improved but remain nonzero:
      uniform `max_diff=0.000244140625`; ragged
      `max_diff=0.000244140625`.
    - The same edge-case run no longer crashes at
      `seq_len=32768/block_size=16/head_dim=256`; 32k long sequence completed.
      Its Flash/Triton diff was `1.52587890625e-05`.
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_decode_timing_after_paged_subtile_20260531.json`
      confirms scalar paged decode speed did not regress:
      `4096/q=1` decode `0.04235 ms` vs Triton `2.30039 ms` (`54.3x`);
      `16384/q=1` decode `0.11490 ms` vs Triton `8.25483 ms` (`71.8x`);
      `32768/q=1` decode `0.21524 ms` vs Triton `16.27342 ms` (`75.6x`).
      The `4096/q=8` case remains much smaller speedup (`15.9x`), consistent
      with previous multi-query timing behavior.

    Current classification:
    - Type A varlen layout: repaired for Flash-internal layout equivalence
      (`max_diff=0.0`). The original edge JSON still reports nonzero against
      latest Triton because that comparison includes Flash-vs-Triton arithmetic
      differences, not only varlen layout.
    - P2 WMMA/shared-memory overflow: repaired for the tested
      32k/block16/head_dim256 paged-prefill edge; no crash in the edge run.
    - P1 long decode: still pending final acceptance. The observed fp16 output
      diffs are bounded and do not grow with seqlen, but the path is not
      default-eligible until the matched-tiling proof is completed against the
      latest Triton arithmetic family.

    Policy after this round:
    - Do not default-enable FlashAttention-V100 yet. The safe mainline remains
      latest Triton SM70 safe-default scheduling for strict `max_diff=0.0`.
    - Keep the paged-prefill strict-subtile and no-shared-block-table changes;
      they recover Type-A Flash layout equality and remove the long-context
      smem overflow without sacrificing scalar paged decode speed.
    - Next Flash repair target is dense Flash vs latest Triton arithmetic:
      first finish the matched-tiling classification for long decode, then
      decide whether to reproduce Triton's exp/reduction/QK accumulation
      semantics or keep Flash-V100 experimental if that would erase its speed
      advantage.
56. Done for P1 long-decode matched-tiling diagnosis.

    Source changes:
    - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` with
      `--mode long-decode-matched-diagnostic`.
    - Added a diagnostic Flash-family partition reference: dense Flash is run
      independently for each decode partition, per-partition LSE is used to
      combine the partitions, and the intermediate per-partition outputs remain
      FP16 like scalar paged decode.
    - Added a second workspace-level diagnostic by reading scalar decode's
      private `tmp_out`, `max_logits`, and `exp_sums` buffers from
      `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_interface._get_decode_workspace`, then
      replaying the final reduce in Python. This does not replace the strict
      edge reference; it only localizes the source of the diff.

    Evidence:
    - First matched diagnostic:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_long_decode_matched_diagnostic_20260531.json`.
    - Workspace diagnostic:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_long_decode_matched_workspace_diagnostic_v2_20260531.json`.

    Result summary:
    - Scalar decode final reduce is not the main source of the long-decode
      mismatch. Replaying the reduce from the actual scalar workspace matched
      scalar output exactly for `8192` and `32768`, and differed only by
      `7.62939453125e-06` at `4096` and `1.1920928955078125e-07` at `16384`.
      The `4096` case has only 3 differing elements in the final output.
    - The main source is inside the scalar partition kernel before the final
      reduce. `scalar_decode_partition_tmp_out` vs
      `dense_flash_partition_outputs` had stable
      `max_diff=0.000244140625` at `4096`, `8192`, `16384`, and `32768`.
      This points to the scalar partition kernel's QK/PV/softmax arithmetic
      and accumulation order, not the cross-partition reduce.
    - `wmma_decode` still matches dense Flash exactly (`max_diff=0.0`) for all
      tested long-decode cases. It therefore remains the strict Flash-family
      route, but it is much slower than scalar paged decode.
    - Scalar paged decode vs latest Triton stayed bounded and did not grow with
      sequence length:
      `4096 max_diff=6.103515625e-05`,
      `8192 max_diff=3.0517578125e-05`,
      `16384 max_diff=3.0517578125e-05`,
      `32768 max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`.

    P1 classification after this proof:
    - The long scalar paged decode diff is not accepted as pure Type-B
      reduction-order noise. The matched diagnostics localize a real
      arithmetic-family mismatch inside scalar partition compute, so it remains
      experimental under the current `max_diff == 0` default-path rule.
    - The bounded trend satisfies the "not linearly growing with seqlen"
      observation, but that alone is insufficient because the matched partition
      proof did not reduce scalar decode to `max_diff=0.0`.
    - For any default Flash-V100 decode route, the candidate must either use
      the strict WMMA/dense-family path and recover its speed, or repair scalar
      partition QK/PV/softmax arithmetic until `scalar_decode_partition_tmp_out`
      matches the chosen strict oracle.

    Policy after P1:
    - Do not default-enable scalar paged Flash decode even though it remains
      `54x-75x` faster than latest Triton in microbenchmarks. It is fast but
      fails strict quality.
    - Keep latest Triton SM70 safe-default scheduling as the default attention
      route for now.
    - Keep Flash-V100 code and diagnostics in the migration tree because the
      Type-A layout issues and 32k smem overflow have been repaired, and the
      remaining blocker is now narrowed to scalar partition arithmetic.
57. Done for scalar decode partition QK localization.

    Source changes:
    - Added debug-only `flash_attention_decode_qk_scores` in
      `flash-attention-v100/kernel/flash_decode_paged.cu`, exposed through
      `flash-attention-v100/include/fused_mha.h`,
      `flash-attention-v100/kernel/fused_mha_api.cpp`, and
      `flash-attention-v100/flash_attn_v100/flash_attn_interface.py` as
      `flash_attn_v100.flash_attn_decode_qk_scores`.
    - Extended `--mode long-decode-matched-diagnostic` to compare scalar decode
      partition QK scores against dense Flash partition QK scores and the
      diagnostic Triton QK kernel.
    - Rebuilt only `flash-attention-v100` with
      `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0` in conda env
      `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.

    Evidence:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_long_decode_partition_qk_diagnostic_20260531.json`.

    Result summary:
    - Scalar partition QK scores are not bitwise equal to dense Flash or Triton,
      but the raw score differences are small:
      - `scalar_decode_partition_qk_scores` vs
        `dense_flash_partition_qk_scores`: `max_diff` ranged from
        `4.5299530029296875e-06` to `6.67572021484375e-06`.
      - `scalar_decode_partition_qk_scores` vs `triton_partition_qk_scores`:
        `max_diff` ranged from `2.384185791015625e-06` to
        `2.86102294921875e-06`.
      - dense Flash partition QK vs Triton partition QK also differed:
        `max_diff` ranged from `5.4836273193359375e-06` to
        `6.198883056640625e-06`.
    - The partition output remains much farther apart:
      `scalar_decode_partition_tmp_out` vs `dense_flash_partition_outputs`
      stayed at `max_diff=0.000244140625` for `4096`, `8192`, `16384`, and
      `32768`.
    - The final scalar reduce remains mostly explained by the workspace:
      scalar output vs workspace Python reduce was `0.0` for `8192` and
      `32768`, `1.1920928955078125e-07` for `16384`, and
      `7.62939453125e-06` for `4096`.

    Interpretation:
    - The scalar partition kernel's first divergence is already visible at QK
      score accumulation, but the QK delta is only a few e-6. The larger
      `2.44e-4` partition-output delta appears after softmax/PV half-output
      materialization, where small QK changes can cross FP16 rounding
      boundaries.
    - This makes a safe scalar repair more involved than changing the final
      reduce. The repair target is now specifically
      `flash_decode_paged.cu` partition compute: align QK accumulation and
      softmax/PV materialization with a strict oracle while preserving the
      scalar path's speed.
    - Until that partition `tmp_out` comparison reaches `max_diff=0.0`,
      scalar paged Flash decode remains experimental and cannot be the default
      SM70 attention decode path.
58. Done for the first post-gate FlashAttention-V100 strictness pass.

    User acceptance gate for this pass:
    - Type-A layout-equivalent paths must have `max_diff=0.0`; stable nonzero
      diffs are treated as bugs, not threshold issues.
    - Type-B arithmetic/reduction-order paths can only be accepted after a
      matched-tiling proof, bounded dtype-appropriate diff, and no monotonic
      growth with sequence length.
    - No crash/OOM/shared-memory overflow is acceptable. If a configuration
      cannot run in Flash-V100 it must gracefully fall back.
    - Scalar paged decode speed must not regress while investigating quality.

    Source changes kept:
    - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` with
      `_partition_outputs_from_qk_scores`, then added comparisons inside
      `--mode long-decode-matched-diagnostic`:
      `scalar_qk_python_partition_outputs` vs scalar `tmp_out`,
      scalar-QK Python output vs dense-QK Python output, and both Python
      replays vs dense Flash partition output. This is diagnostic only and does
      not replace the strict edge reference.

    Experiment tried and rejected:
    - In `flash-attention-v100/kernel/flash_decode_paged.cu`, scalar decode was
      temporarily changed so the PV numerator used `P` rounded through FP16,
      matching dense/WMMA's stored `sP` precision more closely:
      `scores_shared[i] = __half2float(__float2half_rn(p))`.
    - Rebuilt `flash-attention-v100` with
      `MAX_JOBS=4 NVCC_THREADS=1 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0` in conda env
      `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
    - Evidence:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_long_decode_p_half_scalar_experiment_20260531.json`.
    - Result: rejected. `scalar_decode_partition_tmp_out` vs
      `dense_flash_partition_outputs` still had `max_diff=0.000244140625` at
      `4096`, `8192`, `16384`, and `32768`. The scalar-QK Python replay vs
      CUDA scalar `tmp_out` got worse, reaching `0.000244140625` in multiple
      cases. This proves that simply matching dense/WMMA's FP16 probability
      storage does not recover exactness.
    - The temporary kernel edit was reverted, and `flash-attention-v100` was
      rebuilt back to the previous scalar path.

    New softmax/PV diagnostic evidence:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_long_decode_partition_softmax_pv_diagnostic_20260531.json`.
    - `scalar_decode_partition_tmp_out` vs `dense_flash_partition_outputs`
      stayed at `max_diff=0.000244140625` for `4096`, `8192`, `16384`, and
      `32768`.
    - `scalar_qk_python_partition_outputs` vs scalar CUDA `tmp_out` was smaller
      but nonzero (`6.103515625e-05`, `0.0001220703125`,
      `0.000244140625`, `0.0001220703125`). This means the Python replay is
      not a strict CUDA oracle, but it does show that QK perturbations can cross
      FP16 output rounding boundaries.
    - `dense_qk_python_partition_outputs` vs `dense_flash_partition_outputs`
      was also `0.000244140625`, so the Python replay cannot be used as the
      dense/WMMA oracle either; dense Flash's WMMA PV path is materially
      different from a PyTorch `bmm` replay.

    P0 varlen status under the latest gate:
    - Strict edge comparison against Triton still fails:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_edge_cases_after_softmax_pv_diag_20260531.json`.
      Uniform varlen max diff is `0.000244140625`; ragged varlen max diff is
      `0.000244140625`.
    - Matched layout diagnostics pass:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_varlen_layout_after_softmax_pv_diag_20260531.json`.
      Flash batched paged prefill vs Flash full-cache looped paged prefill is
      `0.0`; Flash full-cache looped paged prefill vs standalone paged prefill
      is `0.0`; Flash full-cache looped paged prefill vs Flash dense looped
      prefill is `0.0`; Triton varlen vs Triton looped is `0.0`.
    - Interpretation: the previously suspected varlen `cu_seqlens`/segment
      layout bug is not present in the current Flash family implementation.
      The remaining nonzero edge diff is Flash-family arithmetic vs Triton
      arithmetic. Under the user's strict default gate, this still cannot be
      called passed unless the default reference family is explicitly Flash, or
      the Flash arithmetic is changed to match Triton exactly.

    Restored decode timing evidence:
    - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_paged_decode_timing_restored_after_p_half_fail_20260531.json`.
    - Scalar paged decode speed did not regress after reverting the failed
      experiment:
      `4096/q1` is `0.0450764799118042 ms` vs Triton
      `2.526904296875 ms` (`56.06x`);
      `4096/q8` is `0.16312320709228514 ms` vs Triton
      `2.641387634277344 ms` (`16.19x`);
      `16384/q1` is `0.11505663871765137 ms` vs Triton
      `8.2271435546875 ms` (`71.51x`);
      `32768/q1` is `0.21479423522949218 ms` vs Triton
      `16.259461669921876 ms` (`75.70x`).

    Current default-enable decision after this pass:
    - Do not default-enable scalar paged Flash decode yet. It keeps the desired
      speed, but it is still nonzero against dense/WMMA and Triton references.
    - `paged_decode_wmma` and `decode_as_prefill` remain exact against
      dense Flash (`max_diff=0.0`) but are much slower than scalar paged decode;
      they are correctness references, not acceptable replacements for the
      scalar fast path.
    - The next repair direction is not "round P to FP16"; it is to design a
      scalar-speed partition kernel whose QK/softmax/PV arithmetic has a strict
      oracle. Either align scalar partition compute to Triton exactly, or build
      a specialized q=1 WMMA/tensor-core decode path that preserves most scalar
      speed while matching dense Flash exactly.
59. Done for Flash-vs-Triton arithmetic classification on the latest gate.

    Evidence:
    - Arithmetic family diagnostic:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_arithmetic_diagnostic_latest_gate_20260531.json`.
    - QK score diagnostic:
      `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260531/sm70_flash_v100_qk_score_latest_gate_20260531.json`.

    Result summary:
    - In Flash's own family, paged prefill can be exact against dense Flash:
      several paged-prefill cases report `max_diff=0.0` vs `dense_flash_v100`.
      This supports the conclusion that paged/varlen layout is not leaking or
      indexing the wrong KV segments.
    - Dense Flash vs Triton is already nonzero before varlen enters the
      picture. Examples:
      `seq_len=9/query_len=9/q_heads=1` has `max_diff=0.000244140625`;
      `seq_len=9/query_len=9/q_heads=6` has `max_diff=0.0009765625`;
      `seq_len=69/query_len=16/q_heads=6` has `max_diff=0.000244140625`;
      `seq_len=4096/query_len=8/q_heads=6` has `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`.
    - QK-score diagnostics show the first nonzero difference begins in the
      multi-term dot product:
      one-dimensional patterns are exactly equal (`max_diff=0.0`);
      two-dimensional random patterns are only around
      `2.9802322387695312e-08`;
      full 256-dimensional random QK reaches roughly
      `2.6226043701171875e-06` to `5.7220458984375e-06`.

    Interpretation:
    - The varlen edge failure against Triton is not a varlen-specific Type-A
      layout bug. It is the same Flash-vs-Triton arithmetic-family difference
      seen in dense Flash and paged Flash.
    - If the acceptance reference is "latest vLLM Triton output must be
      bitwise identical", then Flash dense/paged/varlen cannot be defaulted
      without either using Triton for those paths or reimplementing Flash's QK
      and PV arithmetic to match Triton bit-for-bit. The current data shows the
      first divergence is in dot-product accumulation order, so changing
      masks, `cu_seqlens`, or layout will not fix this class of diff.
    - If the acceptance reference is Flash-family strictness, then paged
      prefill/varlen layout has passed, but scalar paged decode still fails
      because it is not exact against dense/WMMA Flash. That leaves q=1 decode
      as the remaining blocker for a fast Flash-family default.

    Current engineering conclusion:
    - Do not weaken thresholds.
    - Keep Triton as the quality default when bitwise parity to latest vLLM
      Triton is required.
    - Keep Flash-V100 default paths restricted to strict bridges unless a new
      q=1 tensor-core decode kernel can match dense Flash exactly while
      recovering most of scalar paged decode speed.
