       --selfnoise-compare-json
       bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_selfnoise_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_candidate_bounds_20260601.json
       --json-out
       bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/flash_v100_current_classification_summary_qk_online_candidate_bounds_20260601.json
       --allow-nonzero`.

     Result:
     - Summary:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/flash_v100_current_classification_summary_qk_online_candidate_bounds_20260601.json`.
     - Overall label remains `B-pending`; default acceptance remains
       `not default-accepted`.
     - Current input/source/mask A-bug component is cleared:
       `all_raw_kv_cache_aligned=true`,
       `num_raw_key_cache_mismatch=0`,
       `num_raw_value_cache_mismatch=0`,
       `num_qk_max_invalid_causal=0`,
       `num_flash_replay_mismatch=0`,
       `num_triton_replay_mismatch=0`.
     - Current bug/noise split:
       `a_bug_suspect_count=0`,
       `input_or_layout_bug_suspect_count=0`,
       `causal_mask_bug_suspect_count=0`,
       `rounding_noise_candidate_count=40`,
       `within_fp16_bound_nonzero_count=37`,
       `above_fp16_bound_half_ulp_count=3`,
       `above_fp16_bound_not_half_ulp_count=0`.
     - Residual attention values:
       `max_output_diff=0.0078125`,
       `max_output_probability_diff=7.987022399902344e-06`,
       `max_output_score_diff=3.62396240234375e-05`,
       `max_qk_diff=0.0001125335693359375`.
     - The matched reconstructions are still mixed rather than proving Type B:
       best precomputed-QK Flash-side match is
       `qk_output.flash_qk_scores_online_tile32_p_half_output=26/40`;
       best Triton-side match is
       `qk_output.triton_qk_scores_p_float_output=23/40`;
       best PV/final-cast Flash-side match is
       `flash_row_reduce.p_half_v_half_sum=20/40`;
       best Triton-side match is
       `triton_row_reduce.pv_fp32_as_half=23/40`.
     - Model-level bounded replay is separated from the op-level label:
       - Flash-vs-Triton model compare is `model-pass` under the candidate
         bounds, with exact token equality and
         `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=true`;
       - observed model diffs:
         `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007842063903808594`,
         `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007416248321533203`,
         `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.046875`,
         `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.042518155516034994`,
         `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.087890625`;
       - Triton self-noise under the same bounds is also `model-pass`, with
         `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.010903716087341309`,
         `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007416367530822754`,
         `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.039061546325683594`,
         `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.37222371810287314`,
         `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.12109375`.

     Classification:
     - Historical raw-vs-cache K/V mismatch: not an active production-path
       `A-bug` in the synchronized replay set. Keep the old record, but do not
       spend more time retesting it unless new evidence reappears.
     - Current Flash-V100 real-model attention output residual:
       `B-pending`, not `B-accept`. The active A-bug component is zero, but no
       matched-tiling/block reference has made the full nonzero diff collapse to
       zero; therefore the mechanical Type-B proof is incomplete.
     - Model-level logprob/logit drift: `model-pass` under the explicit
       candidate bounds and comparable to Triton self-noise, but this does not
       promote the underlying attention op by itself.

     Decision:
     - Do not modify Flash-V100 kernel code just to chase bitwise equality here;
       current evidence does not identify a concrete A-bug.
     - Do not count Flash-V100 prefill as accepted/default performance either;
       it remains non-default behind `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1`.
     - Continue using the split production route while moving development back
       to speed restoration: Triton prefill plus scalar Flash-V100 decode.

125. Greedy token scheduling fast path re-plumbed into latest tree,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - The short AWQ no-MTP decode baseline gap should be attacked in code, not
       by repeating broad quality tests that already have evidence.
     - Static comparison against old `0.0.3` showed that latest-tree
       `gpu_model_runner.py` always computed full logits before sampling, while
       the old SM70 tree had a pure-greedy shortcut that could skip the full
       logits all-gather/sampler path and ask the model for top tokens directly.
     - This path is a scheduling/communication optimization. It does not change
       attention, MoE, AWQ, FP8, or GDN arithmetic.

     Source changes:
     - Added default-on `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH=1` and trace gate
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH_TRACE=0`.
     - Added default-on `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=1` for the pure-greedy
       shortcut only. The full LM-head GEMM fast path remains default-off
       through `VLLM_SM70_ENABLE_LM_HEAD_FASTPATH=0`, because its full-logits
       output previously failed strict bitwise equality against torch.
     - Added default-off `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=0`; the custom allreduce
       over top1 pairs remains experimental and is not part of the default
       route.
     - Added `get_top_tokens()` to `Qwen3_5ForCausalLMBase` and
       `Qwen3MoeForCausalLM`.
     - Follow-up fix: Qwen3.6-AWQ uses the outer
       `Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration` architecture, so exposing only the
       inner language-model method would still make the runner reject the fast
       path with `missing_get_top_tokens`. Added the outer wrapper delegate, and
       restored the same `get_top_tokens` interface on Qwen3Next and the Qwen3.5
       / Qwen3Next MTP modules to match the old tree.
     - Added vocab-parallel top1 reduction to `LogitsProcessor`: each rank
       computes local `(value, token_id)`, then only gathers the tiny top1
       pairs instead of the full vocab logits. If the optional custom AR is
       disabled or unavailable, it falls back to the normal TP all-gather over
       those pairs.
     - Updated `v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` so pure greedy batches can set
       `logits=None` before sampling and call the top-token route directly.
     - Split LM-head preparation from LM-head full-logits execution:
       `VocabParallelEmbedding` may prepare the SM70 layout for top1, but
       `_maybe_sm70_lm_head_forward()` now still requires
       `VLLM_SM70_ENABLE_LM_HEAD_FASTPATH=1`. This prevents
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=1` from accidentally changing official sampling
       or any normal full-logits path.
     - Added trace-only LM-head skip diagnostics. With
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH_TRACE=1` or
       `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`, an eligible `lm_head` that cannot prepare
       now reports the first skip reason, such as dtype, missing op, non-CUDA
       weight, or unsupported shape. This is for route debugging only.

     Safety gates in code:
     - Fast path only applies when all of the following are true:
       CUDA SM70, no spec decode, not pooling, not PP broadcast, no structured
       output/grammar, model exposes `get_top_tokens`, all requests are greedy,
       no requested logprobs, no logprob token IDs, no penalties, no allowed-token
       mask, no bad words, and no non-argmax logits processors.
     - If any gate fails, latest vLLM computes logits and samples through the
       normal route.
     - Grammar handling now forces logits materialization if grammar output is
       present, so structured-output requests cannot accidentally use the
       shortcut.
     - The optional custom AR remains default-off until it has same-criterion
       model evidence. The default pair-gather route is the safe scheduling
       restore.
     - LM-head top1 uses the existing standalone-strict-equal op result from
       item 7: Qwen-sized synthetic `m=1,n=151936,k=5120` had
       `index_equal=true`, `value_equal=true`, and `value_max_diff=0.0`
       against `torch.mm(...).max(...)`.

     Checks run:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/envs.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_moe.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - After the outer-wrapper fix:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5_mtp.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next_mtp.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/envs.py vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_moe.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - After the outer-wrapper fix:
       `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5_mtp.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next_mtp.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - After the LM-head top1/full-GEMM split:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/envs.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/vocab_parallel_embedding.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5_mtp.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next_mtp.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_moe.py`
       passed.
     - Matching `py_compile` over the same files passed in
       `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - After adding the skip diagnostics, `ruff check` and `py_compile` passed
       again for `vllm/model_executor/layers/vocab_parallel_embedding.py`,
       `vllm/envs.py`, `vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py`, and
       `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`.
     - Import/source sanity in `vllm-0.0.5-t210` confirmed that Python imports
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm/vllm/__init__.py` and that
       `envs.VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1` resolves to `True`.

     Aborted route-hit attempt:
     - Command shape was latest tree, Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, TP4, CUDA graph,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`, repeat 1,
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH_TRACE=1`, output path
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_flash_v100_graph_i1024_o256_repeat1_max4608_greedy_top1_20260601.json`.
     - It was manually terminated before generation because it stayed in
       Triton/ptxas cold compilation and repeatedly emitted shared-memory
       wait messages. No JSON was produced, so it is not a performance data
       point and must not be compared with the old `76.6-76.9 tok/s` baseline.
     - Useful partial evidence from the log:
       `Resolved architecture: Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration`,
       `Using AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100 backend`, and
       `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`.
     - No top1 route conclusion was drawn from this attempt because it never
       reached measured decode. The next route-hit run should use the new skip
       diagnostics and should avoid cold-compile confounds where possible.

     Classification:
     - This is not an op-level numeric kernel change, so it should not be mixed
       with the Flash-V100 prefill `B-pending` issue or the paused MoE compact
       exactness issue.
     - It is an argmax-equivalent pure-greedy scheduling path. The next useful
       evidence is a route-hit/speed check on the already-used AWQ `1k/256`
       pure-decode口径, plus one greedy token equality check only if route-hit
       shows the shortcut is actually active.
     - Do not rerun the broad Flash/MoE quality matrix for this item.
     - Expected route-hit log for the safe default greedy path is:
       `SM70 LM head top1 layout prepared` during load, then
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled` during greedy decode. It should
       not log `SM70 dense fp16 fast path enabled for LM head` unless the
       explicitly unsafe full-GEMM gate is enabled.

126. Active-goal numeric-policy audit for Flash-V100 real-model residual,
     2026-06-01.

     Scope:
     - This is the continuation update required by the active goal, not a goal
       restart.
     - No new benchmark or model run was performed. This audit reuses the
       current source-of-truth evidence from item 124:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/flash_v100_current_classification_summary_qk_online_candidate_bounds_20260601.json`,
       the synchronized QK-online tensor-dump replay JSON, and the AWQ
       Flash-vs-Triton / Triton-selfnoise model compare JSON files.

     Rule status:
     - The classified numerical policy is already written in this document's
       Hard Rules section. It supersedes any older blanket
       "`max_diff == 0` for every path" wording.
     - Quality rule #2 is now classified-gate based: Type A needs strict
       equality; Type B needs complete `B-accept` proof plus model-level
       deterministic behavior.
     - Quality rule #4 is now: `A-bug` and `B-pending` remain experimental and
       uncounted; only `B-accept` may enter the accepted/default performance
       path.
     - Acceptance condition #7 is now document-evidence based: every direction
       must carry source files, status, quality label, performance evidence,
       and non-migration reason.

     Current Flash-V100 split:
     - Historical raw-vs-cache K/V mismatch:
       fixed or not active in the synchronized replay set; do not rerun unless
       new evidence reappears.
     - Current input/source/mask bug component:
       `A-pass` for the synchronized replay evidence. Exact fields:
       `all_raw_kv_cache_aligned=true`,
       `num_raw_key_cache_mismatch=0`,
       `num_raw_value_cache_mismatch=0`,
       `num_qk_max_invalid_causal=0`,
       `num_flash_replay_mismatch=0`,
       `num_triton_replay_mismatch=0`,
       `a_bug_suspect_count=0`,
       `input_or_layout_bug_suspect_count=0`,
       `causal_mask_bug_suspect_count=0`.
     - Remaining attention arithmetic/output component:
       `B-pending`, not `B-accept`. Exact fields:
       `max_output_diff=0.0078125`,
       `max_output_probability_diff=7.987022399902344e-06`,
       `max_output_score_diff=3.62396240234375e-05`,
       `max_qk_diff=0.0001125335693359375`,
       `rounding_noise_candidate_count=40`,
       `within_fp16_bound_nonzero_count=37`,
       `above_fp16_bound_half_ulp_count=3`,
       `above_fp16_bound_not_half_ulp_count=0`.
     - Reason it is still `B-pending`:
       the active input/layout/mask bug part is cleared, but the mechanical
       Type-B proof is incomplete. The matched-tiling/block reconstructions are
       mixed rather than collapsing the full nonzero diff to zero; therefore
       the diff is not yet proven pure reduction-order/fp16-rounding noise.
       Also, `3/40` outputs are adjacent-half-ULP cases above the nominal fp16
       output bound, so this cannot be accepted by threshold alone.
     - Model-level signal:
       Flash-vs-Triton AWQ graph compare is `model-pass` under the explicit
       candidate bounds, and Triton selfnoise also passes. Flash-vs-Triton
       values were:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007842063903808594`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007416248321533203`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.046875`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.042518155516034994`,
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.087890625`,
       `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=true`.
       Triton selfnoise was comparable or larger on several metrics:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.010903716087341309`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007416367530822754`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.039061546325683594`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.37222371810287314`,
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.12109375`.
       This supports keeping the route alive, but it does not promote the
       op-level attention residual to `B-accept`.

     Current decision:
     - Do not chase bitwise equality for this residual without a concrete
       `A-bug`; that would violate the new rule against grinding proven or
       near-proven Type-B noise below the dtype floor.
     - Do not default-enable or count Flash-V100 prefill performance either;
       it is still `B-pending`.
     - Keep the production split route:
       Triton prefill via `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1`, and scalar
       Flash-V100 decode for the graph decode speed path.
     - Next useful Flash work, if revisited, is not another broad quality
       rerun. It is a targeted matched-tiling/reference proof for the remaining
       `0.00390625` / `0.0078125` output residual. If that proof collapses the
       diff, promote toward `B-accept`; if it remains after matched tiling,
       relabel as `A-bug` and repair.

127. Greedy LM-head top1 route-hit bug fixed, 2026-06-01.

     Root cause:
     - The latest-tree LM-head top1 preparation checked `layer.prefix` to decide
       whether a `VocabParallelEmbedding` instance was the real `lm_head`.
     - `VocabParallelEmbedding` / `ParallelLMHead` did not preserve the `prefix`
       argument on the module, so `_is_sm70_lm_head_fastpath_eligible()` returned
       false before preparing the SM70 layout. This silently forced the greedy
       fast path to fall back to the full local logits path.
     - This explains the earlier route-hit gap: `FLASH_ATTN_V100` decode and AWQ
       TurboMind could be active while the LM-head/top1 shortcut was still not
       active.

     Code change:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/vocab_parallel_embedding.py` now stores
       `self.prefix = prefix`.
     - LM-head eligibility now accepts either a preserved prefix ending in
       `lm_head` or the concrete `ParallelLMHead` class name. This keeps tied
       embedding layers out of the shortcut while making normal LM-head modules
       eligible.
     - Added a trace reason for `top1_not_prepared` so future route misses fail
       visibly when `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH_TRACE=1`.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/vocab_parallel_embedding.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/vocab_parallel_embedding.py` passed.

     Route-hit evidence:
     - Command shape: Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, TP4, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `enforce_eager=True`, `input_len=64`, `output_len=4`, greedy,
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH_TRACE=1`.
     - Files:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_eager_routehit_greedy_top1_i64_o4_prefixfix_20260601.log`
       and matching `.json`.
     - Same run logged:
       `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 LM head top1 layout prepared`,
       `SM70 greedy token fastpath trace: enabled`,
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`, and
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`.
     - Token hash matched the previous short route-hit run:
       `eb8f2dedc874bebf0afe4f78234e10384a294cbdf065e3d57f76afbb234c3263`.
       Output tokens were `[369, 1429, 310, 9640]`.

     Interpretation:
     - This is a route-connectivity repair, not a new numerical kernel change.
       It does not relax any diff threshold and does not promote Flash prefill.
     - The short run is not a real speed benchmark; it only proves that the
       intended default greedy decode chain is now reachable:
       AWQ TurboMind dense layers + Triton prefill split + Flash-V100 scalar
       decode + greedy LM-head top1.
     - Production CUDA-graph follow-up used the old AWQ pure-decode口径:
       Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, TP4, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `input_len=1024`,
       `output_len=256`, `warmup=1`, `repeat=1`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1`, and
       `disable_log_stats=false` to expose request timing.
       Files:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_flash_v100_graph_greedy_top1_i1024_o256_prefixfix_20260601.log`
       and matching `.json`.
     - The graph run logged `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`, captured
       mixed prefill-decode and full decode CUDA graphs, then logged
       `SM70 greedy token fastpath trace: enabled` and
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`.
     - Measured repeat metrics:
       `prefill_time=0.4856969399988884 s`,
       `first_token_latency=0.48780179023742676 s`,
       `decode_time=3.307110259000183 s` for `255` steady decode tokens,
       `steady_decode_tps=77.10659156465273`,
       `tpot_seconds=0.012969059839216403`, and total
       `output_tps=67.45824632318673`.
     - Interpretation: the latest-tree AWQ no-MTP production graph route is
       back in the user's expected old-tree `~80 tok/s` pure-decode band after
       the LM-head/top1 route-connectivity fix. Do not compare the warmup
       result; it includes first-run compile/capture costs.

128. Old-tree custom top1 allreduce source path ported, 2026-06-01.

     Scope:
     - This is a development port, not a default-enabled performance claim.
     - The stable production route from item 127 still uses the normal tiny
       `tensor_model_parallel_all_gather(local_pair)` reduction over top1
       pairs. It remains the accepted path until the custom path is rebuilt and
       validated.

     Ported from 0.0.3:
     - `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cuh`: added `cross_device_top1_argmax` and
       `CustomAllreduce::top1_argmax`.
     - `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cu`: added the `top1_argmax` binding body.
     - `csrc/ops.h` and `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp`: declared and registered
       `_C_custom_ar::top1_argmax`.
     - `vllm/_custom_ops.py`: added the Python wrapper.
     - `vllm/distributed/device_communicators/custom_all_reduce.py`: restored
       `top1_argmax()` and `custom_top1_argmax()`.

     Semantics:
     - Input is the local rank's `(max_value, token_id)` float32 pair from the
       greedy LM-head top1 path.
     - The kernel chooses the largest value and breaks exact ties by the
       smallest token id, matching the existing all-gather reduction semantics
       for sharded vocab ranges.
     - It does not change LM-head arithmetic or sampling policy; it only
       replaces the TP communication/reduction for pure greedy top1.
     - Safety repair after the initial port: during CUDA-graph warmup,
       `custom_top1_argmax()` must not return an uninitialized placeholder
       token. Warmup still consumes the sampled token while constructing the
       dummy decode sequence, so the latest-tree port now returns `None` in
       non-capturing graph warmup and lets the existing all-gather top1 path
       produce the real token. The custom kernel is used only outside graph
       capture or during the real CUDA stream capture.

     Current status:
     - Source checks passed:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/_custom_ops.py
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/custom_all_reduce.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py`
       and matching `py_compile` in `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - After the CUDA-graph warmup safety repair, targeted checks also passed:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/custom_all_reduce.py` and
       `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/custom_all_reduce.py`.
     - Build note: a full `pip install -e . --no-build-isolation` pulled in
       unrelated `vllm-flash-attn` FA2/FA3 targets and was stopped after
       spending time in FA3. For this item, use a narrow CMake build of `_C`
       only:
       configure with `-S . -B /tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260601`, then
       `cmake --build /tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260601 -j=4 --target=_C`,
       then install component `_C` into the source tree. Logs:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/cmake_configure_sm70_C_only_20260601.log`,
       `cmake_build_sm70_C_only_20260601.log`,
       and `cmake_install_sm70_C_only_20260601.log`.
     - Runtime symbol check after the narrow install passed in
       `vllm-0.0.5-t210`: `_C_custom_ar.top1_argmax`,
       `_C_custom_ar.all_reduce`, `_C.sm70_f16_lm_head_top1_out`, and
       `_C.awq_sm70_prepare` were all present.
     - Minimal eager TP4 route-hit/equality check passed:
       Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `input_len=64`, `output_len=4`,
       `enforce_eager=True`, `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=1`.
       File:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_eager_routehit_custom_top1_ar_i64_o4_20260601.log`
       and matching `.json`.
       Logs showed `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`,
       `SM70 custom top1 argmax path enabled`, and
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`. Output tokens were
       `[369, 1429, 310, 9640]`, matching the accepted all-gather top1 hash
       `eb8f2dedc874bebf0afe4f78234e10384a294cbdf065e3d57f76afbb234c3263`.
     - CUDA-graph route-hit with the same short shape was attempted in
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_graph_routehit_custom_top1_ar_i64_o4_20260601.log`.
       It reached model load, AWQ TurboMind, LM-head layout prep, and
       torch.compile, then stalled with repeated shared-memory broadcast wait
       messages before producing tokens. The run was stopped to avoid spending
       more time on test plumbing.
     - Keep `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=0` by default. The eager route is
       semantically equivalent, but the production graph route is still
       `pending-debug`; the accepted production route remains the normal tiny
       all-gather top1 from item 127.

129. Flash-V100 numeric-gate summary made explicit, 2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - The active goal update asks for the current Flash-V100 nonzero residual
       to be reviewed under the classified max-diff policy, not under either
       "all max_diff must be zero" or "fp16 noise is fine" shortcuts.
     - Item 124 already contained the key evidence, but the JSON summary did
       not make the per-path labels mechanically obvious enough for future
       review.

     Code/tool change:
     - Updated `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py`.
     - `classification-summary` now emits a `numeric_gate` block with:
       current input/source/mask label, Flash-vs-Triton attention residual
       label, dtype-bound status, matched-tiling status, B-accept blockers,
       model-level status, and the default route decision.
     - This is diagnostic reporting only. It does not change a threshold,
       reference implementation, kernel, or benchmark pass/fail condition.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_attention_exactness.py` passed.

     Offline replay:
     - Reused existing JSON only; no model was loaded:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/sm70_flash_v100_tensor_dump_replay_dir_prefill_qkv_sync_qk_online_20260601.json`,
       `model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_vs_flash_v100_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_candidate_bounds_20260601.json`,
       and the Triton self-noise compare.
     - New summary:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/flash_v100_current_classification_summary_qk_online_candidate_bounds_numeric_gate_20260601.json`.

     Current classified result:
     - `numeric_gate.current_input_source_mask.label=A-pass`.
       Evidence remains clean:
       `all_raw_kv_cache_aligned=true`,
       `num_raw_key_cache_mismatch=0`,
       `num_raw_value_cache_mismatch=0`,
       `num_qk_max_invalid_causal=0`,
       `num_flash_replay_mismatch=0`,
       `num_triton_replay_mismatch=0`, and
       `a_bug_suspect_count=0`.
     - `numeric_gate.flash_vs_triton_attention_residual.label=B-pending`.
       The residual is not an active input/layout/mask `A-bug`, but it is not
       `B-accept` either:
       `max_output_diff=0.0078125`,
       fp16 nominal output bound is `0.002`,
       `dtype_bound_satisfied=false`, and
       `matched_tiling_proven=false`.
     - B-accept blockers now appear explicitly in the JSON:
       matched block/partition replay has not collapsed the diff to zero, and
       the maximum output diff exceeds the configured fp16 output bound. The
       adjacent half-ULP evidence remains useful as a source clue, but it does
       not by itself promote the path to `B-accept`.
     - Model-level evidence remains `model-pass` and comparable to Triton
       self-noise, but the JSON now states that model-pass is supporting
       evidence only and cannot promote an op-level `B-pending` residual.

     Decision:
     - Keep Flash-V100 prefill non-default and uncounted.
     - Current accepted/default attention route remains split:
       Triton prefill plus scalar Flash-V100 decode.
     - Do not rerun broad Flash quality matrices for this same evidence. The
     next Flash prefill development step requires a new concrete source
     hypothesis that can either make matched replay collapse or explain why
     the dtype bound should be changed by policy, not by this result.

130. AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense bridge and active-expert decode path,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - The accepted AWQ MoE default bridge was the Python per-expert dense
       TurboMind path because it is Type A exact, but it read
       `expert_offsets64.detach().cpu().tolist()` inside `forward()`. That
       makes the route eager-only and invalid under CUDA graph capture.
     - Switching to the existing grouped/batched AWQ MoE GEMM is not acceptable:
       item 34 measured stable nonzero diffs against the per-expert dense
       oracle, so that route remains experimental behind
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`.

     Source changes:
     - Added `_C::awq_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`, exposed through
       `vllm/_sm70_ops.py`.
     - The multi-token bridge loops over experts in C++ and selects real
       expert pointer rows with CUDA-resident `dense_expert_ids`, avoiding any
       CPU read of offsets during capture.
     - Added active-expert single-token ops:
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out` and
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`.
       This route prepares only the top-k active experts, keeps SiLU on the
       existing `torch.ops._C.silu_and_mul`, and does not use the gated-SiLU
       epilogue or the grouped/batched all-expert MoE route.
     - `AWQSM70MoEMethod.apply()` now uses the active-expert single-token path
       for decode (`num_tokens == 1`, non-batched), and the graph-safe
       dense-stage bridge for other non-batched shapes. The experimental
       batched route is still gated by `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`.
     - Added `dense_graphsafe` and `single_token_dense` modes to
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` so this path is
       checked against the old per-expert dense oracle, not against a relaxed
       threshold.

     Build/checks:
     - Python checks passed:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py
       vllm/_sm70_ops.py`
       and matching `py_compile` in `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - Narrow `_C` build only, not full FA2/FA3 build:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/cmake_build_sm70_C_only_awq_moe_single_token_dense_20260601.log`.
     - Runtime symbol check passed for
       `_C.awq_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`,
       `_C.awq_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`, and
       `_C.awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`.

     Strict exactness:
     - Multi-token graph-safe dense stage, real
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ` layer
       `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`, `m=1/2`, top-k 8:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/sm70_awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_vs_dense_strict_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260601.json`.
       Result: `A-pass`, six reports, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0` for
       `w13_gate_up`, `silu_and_mul`, and `w2_sorted_output`.
     - Single-token active-expert route, same real layer, `m=1`, top-k 8:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/sm70_awq_moe_single_token_dense_vs_dense_strict_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260601.json`.
       Result: `A-pass`, three reports, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0` for
       the same three stages.
     - CUDA graph capture/replay of the single-token active-expert op chain:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/sm70_awq_moe_single_token_dense_graph_capture_qwen36_35a3b_20260601.json`.
       Result: `capture_ok=true`, `all_equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`.

     Op-level speed sanity:
     - Micro timing file:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/sm70_awq_moe_single_token_dense_micro_timing_qwen36_35a3b_20260601.json`.
     - Same real layer, single-token top-k 8:
       all-expert graph-safe dense bridge stage chain: `6.109 ms`;
       active-expert single-token stage chain: `0.253 ms`;
       op-level speedup: `24.14x`.
     - This is not a model decode TPS number. It only proves the decode path
       should use the active-expert route, not the all-expert graph-safe bridge.

     Current status:
     - AWQ MoE default bridge is no longer blocked by the previous CPU-offset
       CUDA graph capture issue at the op level.
     - Next useful validation is a small production-route model check for
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ or the chosen AWQ MoE target: route logs must show
       the AWQ single-token dense path under CUDA graph, deterministic tokens
       must match the accepted reference, and then a same-口径 decode speed run
       can count. Do not repeat the old eager-only dense bridge tests unless a
       new source change invalidates this evidence.

131. AWQ MoE production route-hit follow-up and compile-safe route logging,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - After item 130, the useful remaining question was not another op-level
       strict test. It was whether the latest production path can reach decode
       with CUDA graph enabled while using both `FLASH_ATTN_V100` and the new
       AWQ MoE single-token active-expert route.
     - A prior route-hit attempt accidentally passed
       `--attention-backend TRITON_ATTN`, so it could not prove the Flash-V100
       production route even though it loaded the AWQ MoE SM70 backend.

     Source change:
     - Added explicit AWQ MoE runtime route-hit log messages for:
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled` and
       `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`.
     - Immediately made those logs compile-safe: they now skip while
       `torch.compiler.is_compiling()` is true, so route observability does not
       become part of the compiled forward graph. This is diagnostic logging
       only; it does not change AWQ MoE arithmetic or routing.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py` passed.

     Route-hit attempts:
     - First attempt:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_graph_single_token_dense_routehit_o4_20260601.log`.
       It selected `TRITON_ATTN`, loaded
       `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`, then stalled in
       shared-memory broadcast waits after first torch.compile. It produced no
       JSON and no decode route evidence. This run is recorded as the wrong
       attention口径 for Flash validation.
     - Cleaned stale `/dev/shm/psm_*` files after confirming no vLLM process was
       using them.
     - Second attempt:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_flash_v100_graph_single_token_dense_routehit_o4_capture1_20260601.log`.
       Command used `--attention-backend FLASH_ATTN_V100`, TP4,
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, CUDA graph enabled, max output 4, and constrained
       cudagraph capture sizes to `[1]`.
       Logs confirmed all four TP workers selected
       `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100` and rank 0 loaded the AWQ MoE
       SM70 backend. It did not reach generation: after model load it spent
       over 10 minutes in first Triton/Inductor compile/worker initialization
       with repeated shared-memory broadcast waits and active `ptxas`/launcher
       compilation. The run was stopped rather than waiting further; no JSON
       was produced and no single-token decode route log was reached.

     Current interpretation:
     - Flash selection under CUDA graph is no longer the blocker for this
       attempt: the backend was explicitly `FLASH_ATTN_V100` on all workers.
     - The remaining production-route blocker for this 35B-A3B smoke is latest
       vLLM first-compile/startup cost, not an AWQ MoE exactness failure and not
       a Flash-V100 selector fallback.
     - Do not repeat the same 35B-A3B graph route-hit with
       `max_num_batched_tokens=4096` as a validation loop. The next source-level
       work should reduce or bypass this first-compile path for route-hit and
       speed work, or reuse a warmed/cached compile path, then run one minimal
       production decode route check.

132. FP8 MoE route observability aligned with the safe single-token path,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - A source pass confirmed that latest-tree `Fp8SM70MoEMethod.apply()` is
       already wired symmetrically to AWQ for the safe decode route:
       `num_tokens == 1` and non-batched uses
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out` plus
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`.
     - Multi-token non-batched graph shapes use
       `_C::fp8_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`.
     - This means the next FP8 speed work should not reimplement the unsafe old
       compact/gated path. It should validate and count the already-wired
       active-expert route, while the grouped/batched route stays gated behind
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`.

     Source change:
     - Added compile-safe runtime route logs for FP8 MoE:
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled` and
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`.
     - The helper skips while `torch.compiler.is_compiling()` is true, matching
       the AWQ logging fix from item 131. It does not change routing or
       arithmetic.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8_sm70_moe.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8_sm70_moe.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py` passed.

133. Flash-V100 split route demoted from default to explicit diagnostic fallback,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - User rule update: if the operator selects `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, the backend
       must not silently run a mixed route of Triton prefill plus Flash-V100
       decode. That route was useful for isolating decode speed and reducing
       quality risk while Flash prefill was still under diagnosis, but it is a
       diagnostic/fallback route, not the final migration target.
     - The final target is a complete SM70 Flash-V100 attention backend:
       Flash-V100 prefill, Flash-V100 paged decode, CUDA-graph decode support,
       and classified numeric/model-quality evidence. If full Flash prefill
       still has `B-pending` residuals, the next step is root-cause and repair
       or prove a valid `B-accept`; the solution is not to hide it behind Triton
       prefill and count the speed as accepted.

     Source change:
     - `vllm/envs.py` now defaults
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON` to `0`.
     - `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py` now defaults
       `self.use_triton_prefill` to false. Triton prefill is available only
       when `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1` is set explicitly.
     - The backend log now says this is an explicit Triton diagnostic fallback
       and that the mixed route is not a final performance path.

     Current classification:
     - This source change is route-policy alignment, not numeric acceptance.
     - Already repaired Type A layout paths, including varlen, remain
       `max_diff=0.0`.
     - The real-model Flash-vs-Triton prefill residual from earlier evidence
       remains `B-pending` until a matched-tiling/reference diagnosis collapses
       it, repairs it, or proves a bounded `B-accept`.
     - Future Flash-V100 speed reports must say whether they are full Flash or
       explicit Triton diagnostic fallback. Only full Flash can count toward
       final SM70 Flash-V100 migration performance.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/envs.py
       vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/envs.py vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py` passed.

134. Flash-V100 CUDA-graph prefill no longer silently falls back to Triton,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - After item 133 changed the default policy to full Flash-V100, a source
       pass found one remaining default-path contradiction in
       `FlashAttnV100Impl.forward()`: if `is_prefill` was true during CUDA
       graph capture and the shape was not the small-query decode verifier,
       the backend still returned `super().forward(...)` and recorded
       `prefill_capture_triton_fallback`.
     - That made a selected `FLASH_ATTN_V100` backend capable of silently
       becoming Triton prefill during CUDA graph capture even when
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`. This violates the active route
       rule: mixed Triton-prefill plus Flash-decode is allowed only as an
       explicit diagnostic fallback, not as the production/default route.

     Source change:
     - Updated `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py`.
     - The module and forward docstrings now state the active policy:
       selected Flash-V100 means Flash prefill plus Flash decode by default;
       Triton prefill requires explicit
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1`.
     - Removed the implicit CUDA-graph prefill return to Triton. During capture,
       the small-query verifier still goes through
       `prefill_capture_smallq`; other prefill capture shapes now continue into
       the normal Flash prefill branches and trace
       `prefill_capture_full_flash` before selecting
       `prefill_prefix_flash`, `prefill_no_prefix_paged_cache_flash`, or
       `prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash`.

     Classification:
     - This is a route-policy/control-flow fix, not a numerical promotion.
     - The real-model Flash-vs-Triton prefill residual remains `B-pending` and
       still needs matched-tiling/source proof before full Flash-V100 can be
       default-accepted.
     - Future CUDA-graph route-hit tests must confirm no
       `prefill_capture_triton_fallback` route appears unless
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1` was explicitly set.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py vllm/envs.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py vllm/envs.py` passed.

135. Flash-V100 implicit Triton fallback paths are gated behind an explicit env,
     2026-06-01.

     Reason:
     - A follow-up source scan after item 134 found three remaining ways for a
       selected `FLASH_ATTN_V100` backend to run Triton without the operator
       explicitly selecting Triton:
       unsupported attention features or missing Flash extension ops,
       unavailable paged decode op, and `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=0`
       when no other Flash decode bridge can run.
     - Those fallbacks are useful for emergency debugging, but they must not be
       the default behavior. Otherwise route-hit logs and benchmarks can again
       report `FLASH_ATTN_V100` selection while measuring Triton work.

     Source change:
     - Added `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK`, default `0`, in
       `vllm/envs.py`.
     - `FlashAttnV100Impl` now reads that gate and raises a clear
       `RuntimeError` instead of returning to `super().forward(...)` for:
       `unsupported_triton_fallback`, `decode_triton_no_flash_decode`, and
       `decode_triton_scalar_disabled`.
     - If the gate is explicitly set to `1`, the old fallback behavior remains
       available and the route summary still records the corresponding Triton
       fallback route. This keeps diagnostics possible without letting fallback
       results count as final Flash-V100 performance.
     - `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1` remains the separate explicit
       Triton prefill diagnostic switch from item 133.

     Classification:
     - This is a route-semantics hardening change. It does not change Flash
       attention arithmetic and does not promote the real-model Flash prefill
       residual from `B-pending`.
     - It makes future failures more honest: if the selected Flash route cannot
       run, the run fails loudly unless the command line explicitly opts into
       diagnostic Triton fallback.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py vllm/envs.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py vllm/envs.py` passed.

136. Full-Flash AWQ graph route-hit attempt after fallback hardening,
     2026-06-01.

     Purpose:
     - Try the smallest useful CUDA-graph route-hit after items 133-135:
       Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, TP4, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `input_len=64`,
       `output_len=4`, `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`, and
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`.
     - This was a route-hit check only, not a speed baseline and not a quality
       promotion for the Flash prefill `B-pending` residual.

     Benchmark harness fix:
     - First attempt failed before engine construction:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_full_flash_graph_routehit_i64_o4_no_triton_fallback_20260601.log`.
     - Root cause: `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` did not parse JSON
       values in `--engine-arg`, so `compilation_config={...}` was passed as a
       string and pydantic rejected it.
     - Fixed `_parse_scalar()` in `benchmark_sm70_decode.py` to match
       `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`: values beginning with `{` or `[` are
       parsed with `json.loads`.

     Retry result:
     - Retry log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_full_flash_graph_routehit_i64_o4_no_triton_fallback_retry_20260601.log`.
     - No JSON was produced. The run was manually terminated after it remained
       in first compile / shared-memory broadcast waits for roughly nine
       minutes, then leftover worker processes were killed and all four GPUs
       were released.
     - Useful route evidence before the stall:
       all four workers selected `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`;
       rank 0 logged `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`;
       rank 0 logged `SM70 LM head top1 layout prepared`;
       no `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK` RuntimeError appeared before
       the compile stall.
     - Compile/startup evidence:
       rank 0 compiled ranges `(1, 1024)` and `(1025, 8192)`, saved an AOT
       compiled function, and logged `torch.compile took 107.06 s in total`;
       the engine then repeatedly logged
       `No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds`;
       active `ptxas` processes remained when the run was stopped.

     Classification:
     - This attempt is not a Flash arithmetic failure and not a performance
       result. It did not reach prefill/decode route summary or token
       generation.
     - It reconfirms the current practical blocker for graph route-hit work:
       latest vLLM first-compile / worker synchronization cost. This is the
       same class as the earlier 35B-A3B graph route-hit blocker, now observed
       even on a small 27B-AWQ route-hit with constrained capture sizes.
     - Next useful source work is to reduce or bypass first-compile for
       route-hit validation, or to run a warmed/cached graph route check. Do
       not compare this aborted run with the `1k/256` decode baselines.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`
       passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.

190. Benchmark environment trace now records low-memory compile and MQ knobs,
     2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - The migration goal now explicitly requires conda builds and controlled
       compile parallelism to avoid host OOM or machine lockup. Future decode
       and model-quality JSON artifacts must show whether a run used the safe
       compile envelope, otherwise the result is not reproducible enough for
       this long goal.
     - Item 189 added `VLLM_MQ_MAX_CHUNKS` and
       `VLLM_MQ_BROADCASTER_MAX_CHUNKS` for shared-memory broadcast pressure
       during compile/warmup bursts. Those values also need to appear in the
       benchmark artifacts that will be used to judge route-hit, speed, and
       startup behavior.

     Source change:
     - Extended `_tracked_env()` in `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` to record:
       `VLLM_MQ_*`, `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING`, `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST`,
       `MAX_JOBS`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL`, `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE`,
       `NVCC_THREADS`, and `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX`.
     - Existing SM70/Flash/TorchInductor/Triton/threading entries are kept.

     Rule carried forward:
     - Unless the user explicitly asks for a higher-concurrency build, compile
       and compile-adjacent validation should use the safe envelope:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, and `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`.
     - This item is not a performance optimization. It is traceability and
       safety plumbing so future benchmarks cannot silently omit the compile
       and message-queue settings that affected startup, CUDA graph capture,
       or first-run behavior.

     Checks:
     - `env MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 NVCC_THREADS=1
       TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1 OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1

       NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2
       conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile

167. DFlash multi-KV-cache-group metadata plumbing migrated to latest tree,
     2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Continue items 139-143 instead of re-walking the DFlash diagnostic path.
       The remaining 0.0.3 delta here was functional metadata plumbing: DFlash
       draft attention layers can span multiple KV cache groups, so context
       slot mappings, query slot mappings, block tables, and per-layer attention
       metadata must all be selected by KV-cache group. A single `cad`/single
       slot mapping is not safe for mixed-group draft layouts.

     Source comparison:
     - Latest `vllm/v1/spec_decode/dflash.py` still had a single
       `_context_slot_mapping_buffer`, single `_slot_mapping_buffer`, one
       `copy_and_expand_dflash_inputs_kernel` launch using `cad.block_table`,
       and `build_per_group_and_layer_attn_metadata()` delegated to the base
       single-`CommonAttentionMetadata` implementation.
     - 0.0.3 kept query/context slot buffers by KV group, stored
       `draft_layer_to_kv_cache_gid`, built `new_cad_by_gid`, passed a
       `dict[layer_name -> slot_mapping]` into Qwen3 DFlash context-KV cache
       writes, and had the runner register per-group common metadata back into
       the DFlash proposer.

     Changes migrated:
     - `vllm/v1/spec_decode/dflash.py` now has DFlash-local multi-group state:
       `draft_layer_to_kv_cache_gid`, per-gid query/context slot buffers,
       per-gid common metadata, per-gid block sizes, and DFlash-only overrides
       for `validate_same_kv_cache_group()`, `initialize_attn_backend()`,
       `_get_slot_mapping()`, `set_inputs_first_pass()`,
       `build_model_inputs_first_pass()`, and
       `build_per_group_and_layer_attn_metadata()`.
     - The single-group behavior still reuses the original buffers. Extra
       buffers are allocated only for additional DFlash KV cache groups.
     - `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_dflash.py` now accepts
       `context_slot_mapping: Tensor | dict[str, Tensor] | None` and, when a
       dict is supplied, uses the actual attention layer name to select the
       correct slot mapping before `do_kv_cache_update()`. It also preserves the
       0.0.3 guard for list-style `kv_cache`.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` now collects
       `CommonAttentionMetadata` for DFlash's involved KV groups and calls
       `DFlashProposer.set_common_attn_metadata_by_kv_cache_group()` after
       unpadding, matching the old-tree data flow. This is DFlash-gated and
       does not change the returned main-model attention metadata.

     Safety classification:
     - This is a correctness plumbing migration, not a speed optimization.
       Without it, multi-KV-group DFlash can use a block table/slot mapping from
       the wrong group, which is a potential A-class KV-source/indexing bug.
     - This patch does not relax any quality threshold and does not add a
       fallback that hides bad metadata. It makes the group identity explicit so
       later route-hit and no-leak tests can prove the path.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory guard used for validation:
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`, `MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/v1/spec_decode/dflash.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_dflash.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` passed.
     - `python -m py_compile vllm/v1/spec_decode/dflash.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_dflash.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` passed.

     Remaining gates:
     - No CUDA build, route-hit, or model-quality run was executed in this
       checkpoint.
     - Next DFlash/GDN validation must prove route hit under CUDA graph, per-gid
       block table and slot mapping selection, no cross-group/no cross-sequence
       KV leakage, and deterministic token/logit quality before any default
       enablement.

138. Spec/MTP diagnostic gate migration, 2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Continue the 0.0.3 old-only env inventory without repeating earlier
       speculative/MTP/DFlash exploration. Only migrate gates that do not alter
       model outputs in this pass; keep behavior-changing gates classified
       until they have token/quality/throughput evidence.

     Source comparison:
     - Old tree:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/vllm/v1/sample/rejection_sampler.py`
       has `VLLM_SPEC_DUMP_ALIGNMENT` and
       `VLLM_MTP_STOCHASTIC_TOKEN_MATCHING`.
     - Old tree:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5_mtp.py`
       has `VLLM_DEBUG_MTP_LOAD`, `VLLM_DEBUG_MTP_LOAD_VERBOSE`,
       `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_KEEP_QUANT`, and
       `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_SHARE_IO_WEIGHTS`.
     - Latest tree already has upstream MTP embedding/lm_head sharing in
       `vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py`; do not blindly reintroduce
       the old model-local sharing path unless we need the debug opt-out.
     - Latest tree already has DFlash skeletons:
       `vllm/v1/spec_decode/dflash.py`,
       `vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py`,
       `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_dflash.py`, and
       `vllm/v1/attention/backends/gdn_attn.py`. The old DFlash profile/dump
       helpers remain a separate larger pass because they touch multiple
       metadata and cache paths.

     Migrated now:
     - Registered default-off `VLLM_SPEC_DUMP_ALIGNMENT`,
       `VLLM_DEBUG_MTP_LOAD`, and `VLLM_DEBUG_MTP_LOAD_VERBOSE` in
       `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Restored `VLLM_SPEC_DUMP_ALIGNMENT` in
       `vllm/v1/sample/rejection_sampler.py`. It writes at most three
       `/tmp/spec_alignment_pid*.pt` payloads containing draft token ids,
       target top-k, draft-prob slices when present, index tensors, bonus
       tokens, and sampling-mode flags. It is after target-logit processing and
       before `rejection_sample`; it does not change logits, draft probs, or
       output tokens.
     - Restored Qwen3.5 MTP load diagnostics in
       `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5_mtp.py`. The envs only log
       `quant_config`, loaded count, and optional missing/loaded param names.

     Classified but not migrated in this pass:
     - `VLLM_MTP_STOCHASTIC_TOKEN_MATCHING` changes the sampling/acceptance
       path when draft probs are absent. It may be useful for MTP throughput,
       but it is a behavior-changing path and must pass greedy/non-greedy
       token, logprob, acceptance, and speed gates before default use.
     - `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_KEEP_QUANT` and
       `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_SHARE_IO_WEIGHTS` are not registered yet. Latest
       quantization already honors `modules_to_not_convert` by prefix in the
       quant methods, and latest proposer already shares MTP IO weights.
       Re-adding old opt-outs would be functional behavior, not a pure
       diagnostic; keep them pending until an AWQ/FP8 MTP load case proves the
       need.
     - Old DFlash debug/profile envs
       (`VLLM_DFLASH_PROFILE`, `VLLM_DFLASH_PROFILE_LOG_INTERVAL`,
       `VLLM_DFLASH_DEBUG_*`, `VLLM_DFLASH_DUMP_*`) remain identified but
       not fully restored. They should be migrated together with a DFlash
       route-hit/quality pass, not as unused env declarations.
