184. Old GDN and attention custom-op schemas classified, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The refreshed inventory still lists old-only torch op/schema names:
       `vllm::gdn_attention_core`, `vllm::unified_attention`, and
       `vllm::unified_mla_attention`.
     - These looked like possible SM70 migration gaps, but they overlap with
       major upstream architecture changes in latest vLLM. They needed
       explicit closure before adding bindings back.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py` and
       `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next.py` define and register the
       model-local custom op `gdn_attention_core`. Qwen3.5 calls it after
       splitting `[q, k, v, z]` and `[b, a]`, with projections outside the op.
     - Latest Qwen3.5 no longer carries a model-local GDN op. It builds
       `QwenGatedDeltaNetAttention` from
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` with
       `gqa_interleaved_layout=False`; Qwen3Next uses the same shared layer
       with its interleaved layout.
     - Latest shared layer registers `qwen_gdn_attention_core`. With
       `fast_kernel=False`, it dispatches to the same conceptual core:
       `_forward_core(mixed_qkv, b, a, core_attn_out)`. With
       `fast_kernel=True`, it supports the ROCm AITER packed `[q,k,v,z]` /
       `[b,a]` route. CUDA/Qwen3.5 uses the standard `fast_kernel=False`
       branch.
     - The compilation attention-op allowlist moved from old
       `vllm::gdn_attention_core` to latest
       `vllm::qwen_gdn_attention_core` and `vllm::gdn_attention_core_xpu`.
     - Old attention layers registered both Tensor-returning
       `unified_attention` / `unified_mla_attention` and in-place
       `_with_output` variants. Latest keeps only
       `unified_attention_with_output` and
       `unified_mla_attention_with_output` as torch custom ops, while the
       backend-local Triton `unified_attention(...)` function still exists.

     Classification:
     - `vllm::gdn_attention_core`: `closed-replaced` by
       `vllm::qwen_gdn_attention_core`. Do not re-register the old model-local
       schema; the latest shared GDN layer is the decoupled migration target.
     - `vllm::unified_attention` and `vllm::unified_mla_attention`:
       `closed-upstream-schema-replaced` by the latest `_with_output` custom
       op API. These are not FlashAttention-V100 integration gaps.
     - Old GDN tuning/quality-risk knobs are separate from this schema
       closure. Existing ledger entries still apply:
       `VLLM_QWEN3_NEXT_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING` is upstream-split diagnostic,
       `VLLM_QWEN3NEXT_ENABLE_SHARED_MOE_OVERLAP` is upstream-replaced, and
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_EMPTY_CORE_OUT` remains paused-unsafe.

     Decision:
     - Do not add compatibility bindings for these old schema names just to
       reduce the raw old-only torch-op count. The right migration path is the
       latest shared GDN layer and latest `_with_output` attention custom-op
       API.
     - Flash-V100 acceptance remains controlled by the Flash-V100 backend
       routing, exactness, model-quality, and decode-speed gates. This item
       only closes stale schema names in the inventory.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` under `vllm-0.0.5-t210` passed for the
       same script with `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`,
       `NVCC_THREADS=1`, and single-thread Python/JIT envs.
     - Refreshed inventory:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_sm70_unquantized_closure.json`.
       It reports `diff.env_vars.old_only=0`,
       `diff.torch_ops.old_only=28`, classified old-only torch ops `28`, and
       `unclassified=[]`. `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` now reports
       `status=closed-replaced`, `doc_item=188`.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, GPU run, or benchmark was triggered.

185. Old sparse/Cutlass auxiliary schemas classified as non-SM70 target,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The torch-op inventory still lists these old-only schemas:
       `_C::convert_vertical_slash_indexes`,
       `_C::convert_vertical_slash_indexes_mergehead`,
       `_C::cutlass_scaled_sparse_mm`,
       `_C::cutlass_sparse_compress`,
       `_C::cutlass_sparse_scaled_mm_supported`, and
       `_C::get_cutlass_pplx_moe_mm_data`.
     - They should not be blindly reintroduced into latest vLLM without
       checking whether they can affect the V100 AWQ/FP8 decode target.

     Source comparison:
     - Old `csrc/sparse/cutlass/sparse_scaled_mm_entry.cu` states that sparse
       CUTLASS scaled-mm needs CUDA 12.2 and SM90. The runtime support function
       returns true only for `cuda_device_capability >= 90`, and the CUDA entry
       only launches an SM90 kernel. Otherwise it throws
       `No compiled cutlass_scaled_sparse_mm for a compute capability less
       than ...`.
     - Old `csrc/quantization/w8a8/cutlass/scaled_mm_entry.cu`
       `get_cutlass_pplx_moe_mm_data` is compiled only with CUTLASS MoE
       kernels for SM90/SM100/SM120. The fallback error says required
       capability is `90, 100, or 120`.
     - Old `csrc/attention/vertical_slash_index.cu` implements MInference-style
       vertical/slash sparse attention index conversion. It is an auxiliary
       sparse-attention route, not part of the FlashAttention-V100 full
       prefill/decode backend path.
     - Latest source has no same-name Python callers or bindings for this
       group.

     Classification:
     - `_C::cutlass_scaled_sparse_mm`,
       `_C::cutlass_sparse_compress`,
       `_C::cutlass_sparse_scaled_mm_supported`, and
       `_C::get_cutlass_pplx_moe_mm_data`:
       `closed-non-sm70-target`. They are explicitly not V100/SM70
       acceleration paths.
     - `_C::convert_vertical_slash_indexes` and
       `_C::convert_vertical_slash_indexes_mergehead`:
       `closed-not-current-attention-target`. Keep out of the default
       migration unless a future sparse-attention/MInference goal is opened
       with its own exactness and speed gates.

     Decision:
     - Do not migrate these schemas into latest just to eliminate the raw
       old-only inventory names. For the current goal, adding them would
       increase CUDA build surface without helping Qwen3.6 AWQ/FP8 V100
       decode.
     - The current attention target remains: when `FLASH_ATTN_V100` is
       selected, prefill and decode should use Flash-V100 paths, not a
       sparse-attention side route.

     Checks:
     - Static source comparison only. No CUDA/C++ build, no model load, no GPU
       run, and no benchmark were triggered.

186. Remaining non-SM70/upstream-replaced torch-op schemas classified,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - After closing AWQ/FP8 monolithic MoE, GDN/attention schema names, and
       sparse/Cutlass auxiliaries, the old-only torch-op list still contains
       FlashInfer, ROCm, CPU, MXF4, and generic fused-experts names.
     - These should not be interpreted as missing V100 decode acceleration
       work without source-level classification.

     Source comparison:
     - Old FlashInfer MoE exposed Python custom ops:
       `vllm::fi_trtllm_fp8_per_tensor_moe`,
       `vllm::flashinfer_fused_moe_bf16`, and
       `vllm::flashinfer_fused_moe_blockscale_fp8`.
       Latest has moved FlashInfer MoE behind the modular expert/oracle stack
       and direct FlashInfer functional APIs, e.g. TRTLLM FP8/FP4/NVFP4/MXFP
       expert classes under `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/experts/`.
     - Old `vllm::inplace_fused_experts` and
       `vllm::outplace_fused_experts` were generic fused-MoE custom op
       facades. Latest uses `vllm::fused_experts` plus the modular
       `FusedMoEKernel`/runner/oracle architecture. The migrated SM70
       AWQ/FP8 routes hook into quant methods and the latest runner rather
       than restoring the old two-schema API.
     - Old `_C_cpu::mla_decode_kvcache` maps to latest
       `_C::mla_decode_kvcache` through `vllm._custom_ops.mla_decode_kvcache_cpu`.
       This is CPU MLA decode, not CUDA/V100 decode.
     - Old `_C_utils::init_cpu_threads_env` is CPU worker/NUMA setup.
       Latest CPU bindings expose newer CPU memory/env setup; this is not a
       CUDA SM70 acceleration path.
     - Old `_qutlass_C::matmul_ada_mxf4_bf16_tn` is superseded by latest
       generic Qutlass/MXFP4 helpers such as
       `_qutlass_C::matmul_mxf4_bf16_tn` and the vLLM wrapper
       `vllm::matmul_mxf4_bf16`. This is not AWQ/FP8 V100 TurboMind work.
     - Old ROCm AITER schemas
       `vllm::rocm_aiter_gemm_a8w8`,
       `vllm::rocm_aiter_rms_norm`, and
       `vllm::rocm_aiter_rmsnorm2d_fwd_with_add` are AMD/ROCm paths. Latest
       still has ROCm AITER code, but it is outside the CUDA V100 goal.

     Classification:
     - FlashInfer MoE old schemas: `closed-upstream-modular-replaced` for this
       migration. Do not re-add the old custom-op wrappers for V100; if a
       future FlashInfer backend is evaluated, it needs a separate supported
       GPU/quality/speed gate.
     - `vllm::inplace_fused_experts` and `vllm::outplace_fused_experts`:
       `closed-upstream-schema-replaced` by latest `vllm::fused_experts` and
       modular FusedMoE runner/oracle.
     - `_C_cpu::mla_decode_kvcache` and `_C_utils::init_cpu_threads_env`:
       `closed-cpu-only/non-v100-target`.
     - `_qutlass_C::matmul_ada_mxf4_bf16_tn`:
       `closed-upstream-qutlass-replaced/non-awq-fp8-v100-target`.
     - ROCm AITER schemas: `closed-rocm-only/non-v100-target`.

     Decision:
     - Do not spend CUDA build or model-test time restoring these exact schema
       names. They do not represent missing SM70/V100 AWQ/FP8 decode fast
       paths.
     - The remaining performance work should stay on the actual V100 routes:
       Flash-V100 full prefill/decode, safe AWQ/FP8 TurboMind paths, GDN/FLA
       schedules that pass the A/B quality policy, and any latest-runner
       integration needed to hit the old 0.0.3 speed baseline.

     Checks:
     - Static source comparison only. No CUDA/C++ build, no model load, no GPU
       run, and no benchmark were triggered.

187. Torch-op inventory now reports classified old-only schemas, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The raw old-only torch-op count is useful for discovery but too easy to
       misread after source-level closure items have been written. It should
       not keep sending future work back into already-closed schema names.
     - The previous inventory also captured `aten::full`, a PyTorch builtin
       used by an old Inductor attention-quant fusion replacement. That is not
       a vLLM extension/custom-op migration item.

     Code change:
     - Updated `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` to ignore
       `torch.ops.aten.*` references when building the custom-op/schema
       inventory.
     - Added a static `OLD_ONLY_TORCH_OP_CLASSIFICATIONS` map keyed by old
       schema name. The JSON now includes:
       `diff.old_only_torch_op_classification.classified` and
       `diff.old_only_torch_op_classification.unclassified`.
     - Existing raw `diff.torch_ops.old_only` is preserved. The new
       classification layer is advisory evidence, not a way to hide names.

     Refreshed evidence:
     - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_torch_op_classification.json`.
     - `diff.env_vars.old_only` count remains `0`.
     - `diff.torch_ops.old_only` count is now `28` after filtering the
       `aten::full` builtin.
     - All `28` old-only torch-op names have a classification entry, and
       `unclassified` is `[]`.
     - The only classification deliberately left as active source work is
       `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` with status
       `pending-source-closure`.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` under `vllm-0.0.5-t210` passed for the
       same script with `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`,
       `NVCC_THREADS=1`, and single-thread Python/JIT envs.
     - The inventory refresh completed under `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, GPU run, or benchmark was triggered.

188. Old `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` wrapper closed against latest
     `_sm70_ops` dense path, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 187 intentionally left `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` as
       `pending-source-closure` because it looked like the only remaining
       raw old-only torch-op name that could be a real V100 dense fast path.
     - This needed source-level verification before closing the torch-op
       inventory.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` registered
       `sm70_unquantized_gemm` as a Python custom op. The implementation
       reshaped input to 2D, allocated output, called
       `ops.sm70_f16_gemm_out(out, x_2d, tm_weight, k_ld, False)`, optionally
       added bias, and reshaped back.
     - Latest keeps the actual SM70 dense TurboMind kernel family:
       `_C::sm70_f16_prepare`, `_C::sm70_f16_gemm`, and
       `_C::sm70_f16_gemm_out`, exposed through `vllm/_sm70_ops.py`.
     - Latest `vllm/model_executor/layers/linear.py`
       `_maybe_sm70_dense_forward` directly calls
       `_sm70_ops.sm70_f16_gemm_out(...)` when prepared packed weights are
       available outside compile, or `_sm70_ops.sm70_f16_gemm(...)` inside
       compile/fallback contexts. Bias is applied in Python after the kernel,
       preserving the old wrapper's behavior without the extra
       `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` schema.
     - Latest `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` still carries the SM70
       dense suffix allowlist and dispatch plumbing. `linear.py` gates the
       fast path through `_sm70_f16_prepared`, `_sm70_f16_forbidden`,
       `VLLM_SM70_F16_DENSE_ALLOWLIST`, `VLLM_SM70_F16_DENSE_MAX_M`, and
       related trace/debug envs.

     Classification:
     - `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm`: `closed-replaced`.
     - The accepted latest route is not the old wrapper schema; it is the
       lower-level `_C::sm70_f16_*` / `_sm70_ops` path wired into
       `linear.py`.

     Decision:
     - Do not re-register the old `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` custom op.
       Restoring it would add another schema layer around a kernel that latest
       already exposes directly.
     - Future speed work should prove route-hit and performance for the
       existing latest SM70 dense path under CUDA graph/model decode, not add
       back the old wrapper.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` under `vllm-0.0.5-t210` passed for the
       same script with `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`,
       `NVCC_THREADS=1`, and single-thread Python/JIT envs.
     - Refreshed inventory:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_sm70_unquantized_closure.json`.
       It reports `diff.env_vars.old_only=0`,
       `diff.torch_ops.old_only=28`, classified old-only torch ops `28`, and
       `unclassified=[]`. `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm` now reports
       `status=closed-replaced`, `doc_item=188`.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, GPU run, or benchmark was triggered.

189. Shared-memory message-queue chunk count made tunable for compile bursts,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Multiple CUDA-graph route-hit attempts recorded
       `No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds` during
       first compile / weight prepare / graph setup. This happened in item 137,
       item 138, and the 35B-A3B graph route-hit attempts.
     - The warning comes from `shm_broadcast.py` when the writer cannot find a
       ring-buffer chunk whose previous message has been read by all readers.
       During long TorchInductor compile or kernel warmup, worker readers can
       stop consuming scheduler broadcasts long enough to fill the default
       ring buffer.
     - This is startup/compile plumbing, not model math. It should be
       adjustable without changing default behavior or touching decode
       kernels.

     Code change:
     - Added envs:
       `VLLM_MQ_MAX_CHUNKS` default `10`, and
       `VLLM_MQ_BROADCASTER_MAX_CHUNKS` default `6`.
     - `MessageQueue` now uses `envs.VLLM_MQ_MAX_CHUNKS` as the default
       `max_chunks`, preserving the old value when unset.
     - `parallel_state.py` now uses
       `envs.VLLM_MQ_BROADCASTER_MAX_CHUNKS` instead of hard-coded `6` for
       model-parallel message-queue broadcasters.

     How to use for SM70 compile-heavy route-hit runs:
     - Keep the existing low-memory compile envs:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`.
     - If compile bursts still fill the queue, try
       `VLLM_MQ_MAX_CHUNKS=32` and
       `VLLM_MQ_BROADCASTER_MAX_CHUNKS=16`.
       Memory cost is bounded by `max_chunk_bytes * max_chunks`, so do not
       raise these blindly on memory-constrained hosts.

     Classification:
     - `startup/compile-plumbing`, no model arithmetic change.
     - This does not count as a decode speed improvement by itself. It is a
       guardrail to reduce first-run stalls while validating the real SM70
       Flash/AWQ/FP8 routes under CUDA graph.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/envs.py
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/shm_broadcast.py
       vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` under `vllm-0.0.5-t210` passed for the
       same files.
     - Env parse smoke with
       `VLLM_MQ_MAX_CHUNKS=32 VLLM_MQ_BROADCASTER_MAX_CHUNKS=16` printed
       `32 16`.
     - Refreshed inventory:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_mq_max_chunks.json`.
       It reports `diff.env_vars.old_only=0`; the two new MQ envs are
       latest-only by design. Old-only torch-op count remains `28` with
       `unclassified=[]`.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, GPU run, or benchmark was triggered.

159. Re-runnable 0.0.3-to-latest SM70 source inventory added, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The updated active goal requires a complete inventory of old 0.0.3
       SM70/V100 routes, not only env names remembered during active work.
     - Item 147's env-gate scan was useful but too weak as a long-term guard:
       it did not record file-level keyword hits, torch op schemas, or a
       reusable command, and it could not distinguish "already ported under a
       new name" from "silently dropped."

     Source change:
     - Added `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py`.
     - The tool is static-only: it does not import vLLM, start CUDA, build
       extensions, or run a model.
     - It scans old and latest source trees under:
       `vllm`, `csrc`, `flash-attention-v100`, `lmdeploy`, `benchmarks`, and
       `docs/design`.
     - It excludes generated or heavy directories such as `.git`, `.deps`,
       `bench_results`, `build`, caches, and `dist`.
     - It records:
       `VLLM_*` env gates, source files containing SM70/V100 migration
       keywords, and torch op/schema references from C++ and Python.
     - The first implementation accidentally missed multi-line
       `ops.def(\n "...")` schemas. That was fixed before accepting the JSON;
       latest torch-op count increased from `382` to `415`, and false
       old-only entries such as `sm70_f16_gate_mul_out` disappeared.

     Command:
     - Low-memory conda run:
       `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py --out
       bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_20260602.json`.

     Evidence file:
     - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_20260602.json`.

     Inventory counts:
     - Old 0.0.3 source scan: `2246` text files, `387` env vars,
       `225` keyword files, `303` torch op/schema references.
     - Latest migration source scan: `2464` text files, `457` env vars,
       `281` keyword files, `415` torch op/schema references.
     - Diff: `54` old-only env vars, `124` latest-only env vars,
       `29` old-only torch op/schema names, and `141` latest-only op/schema
       names.

     Important old-only env groups from this wider scan:
     - DFlash / speculative / MTP diagnostics:
       `VLLM_DFLASH_*`, `VLLM_DEBUG_MTP_LOAD*`,
       `VLLM_SPEC_DEBUG_CORRUPTION`, `VLLM_SPEC_DUMP_ALIGNMENT`, and
       `VLLM_SPEC_DUMP_DRAFT_LOGITS`. These remain inventory items for the
       broader goal, but they are outside the current no-MTP fast-path lane.
     - Qwen3Next/GDN historical knobs:
       `VLLM_QWEN3NEXT_ENABLE_SHARED_MOE_OVERLAP`,
       `VLLM_QWEN3_NEXT_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING`,
       `VLLM_QWEN3_NEXT_SM70_TRACE`, and `VLLM_SM70_GDN_EMPTY_CORE_OUT`.
       Latest has newer GDN/FLA schedule and mixed-QKV gates, but these exact
       old knobs are not closed by name; each needs an explicit migrated,
       replaced, or rejected status.
     - Old FP8 MoE compact/router quality-risk family:
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_COMPACT_COMPARE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_COMPACT_STRICT_COMPARE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_EXACT_LAYOUT`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_EXACT_REDUCE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_ROUTER_TOPK_COMPARE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_ROUTER_TOPK_PERSISTENT`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_ALLOW_UNSAFE_BROADCAST_INPUT`. This family remains
       paused/unsafe unless repaired under the quality rules; it must not be
       defaulted from speed evidence alone.
     - Old AWQ/runtime warmup and debug gates:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISABLE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_M`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_MOE_TOKENS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_F16_DENSE_DEBUG`,
       `VLLM_SM70_SHARED_GATE_MAX_M`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_UNQUANT_DEBUG`. These are not all proven useful routes, but
       they now have explicit inventory visibility.
     - Old Triton attention/Q-head split:
       `VLLM_TRITON_ATTN_SM70_QHEAD_SPLIT`. Latest has separate
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_*` schedule controls; whether q-head split is
       upstream-replaced, still needed, or irrelevant to the final full-Flash
       path remains a pending source decision.

     Important old-only torch op/schema names:
     - AWQ MoE old compact/exact-layout helpers:
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_compact_prepare`,
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_exact_layout_prepare`,
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_sm70_out`, and
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_weighted_reduce_out`.
       Latest has the graph-safe dense-stage and active-expert route, but these
       exact old compact/exact-layout schemas are not fully closed by the
       inventory alone.
     - FP8 MoE old monolithic routes:
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_router_sm70_out` and
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_sm70_out`. These overlap with the paused
       compact/router-topk family and stay non-default until repaired or
       explicitly rejected.
     - Remaining source-only or non-SM70 ops worth tracking:
       `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70`, `_C::get_cutlass_pplx_moe_mm_data`,
       `vllm::gdn_attention_core`, and `vllm::sm70_unquantized_gemm`.
       Some may be upstream-replaced or intentionally dropped, but the
       inventory prevents them from disappearing silently.

     Latest-only evidence from the same scan:
     - Latest has many migration/diagnostic controls absent in 0.0.3, including
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND`, `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`, `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_*`, `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_*`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_*`, `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE`,
       `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE`, and sampler-logits dump
       diagnostics.
     - This confirms the latest tree is not merely a partial old-tree copy; it
       now has decoupled migration controls and diagnostics. The remaining work
       is to classify each old-only route as migrated/replaced/paused/rejected,
       then remove or keep gates accordingly.

     Classification:
     - This item is inventory tooling and evidence plumbing only. It changes no
       model math, kernel, backend selection, or default runtime policy.
     - The JSON is not acceptance evidence for any route; it is a map of what
       still needs source-level closure.
     - Next non-duplicative source work should pick one old-only group from
       this inventory and close it explicitly instead of repeating broad
       benchmarks. Good candidates are:
       1. AWQ MoE old compact/exact-layout schemas vs the latest active-expert
          route;
       2. FP8 monolithic router/compact schemas, likely paused unless exactness
          can be repaired;
       3. old Qwen3Next shared-MoE overlap and GDN trace gates;
       4. old `VLLM_TRITON_ATTN_SM70_QHEAD_SPLIT` vs latest full-Flash policy.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for the script.
     - The inventory command completed under `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - No CUDA/C++ compile, no model load, and no GPU benchmark were run.

160. AWQ MoE old compact/exact-layout schemas closed against latest
     active-expert route, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 159 surfaced old-only AWQ MoE schemas from 0.0.3:
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_compact_prepare`,
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_exact_layout_prepare`,
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_weighted_reduce_out`, and
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_sm70_out`.
     - These names looked like a possible migration gap. Before adding any
       binding back, the source needed a route-level comparison against the
       latest accepted AWQ MoE implementation.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 binds the compact prepare, exact-layout prepare,
       weighted-reduce, and monolithic single-token AWQ MoE schemas in
       `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp`.
     - Latest `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp` does not expose those old schemas.
       It exposes the newer split route instead:
       `_C::awq_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`,
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`, and
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`.
     - Latest `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` still contains some
       old helper function bodies, including compact/exact-layout prepare and
       weighted reduce. Their presence in C++ is not acceptance evidence,
       because the production Python facade does not call or bind them.
     - Latest `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py`
       routes the single-token decode case through:
       `awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`, upstream
       `silu_and_mul`, `awq_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`, and
       upstream `moe_unpermute`.

     Replacement mapping:
     - Old compact/exact-layout prepare idea:
       keep only the useful part, namely active-expert single-token packing,
       stable expert order, CUDA-resident offsets, `inv_permuted_idx`, and
       compact input preparation. In latest this lives inside
       `_C::awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`.
     - Old monolithic `_C::awq_moe_single_token_sm70_out`:
       replaced by the split latest route so each stage remains independently
       testable and graph-capturable.
     - Old `_C::awq_moe_single_token_weighted_reduce_out`:
       not migrated for the default route. Latest uses upstream
       `_moe_C::moe_unpermute` for the weighted combine, preserving the same
       accepted arithmetic as the dense oracle instead of introducing another
       custom reduce path.
     - Old Tensor-returning `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70`:
       latest keeps the in-place `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70_out` facade for the
       experimental grouped/batched route. This is API cleanup, not a missing
       runtime requirement for the accepted default route.

     Classification:
     - `awq_moe_single_token_compact_prepare` and
       `awq_moe_single_token_exact_layout_prepare`: `closed-replaced` by the
       latest active-expert dense-w13 preparation path.
     - `awq_moe_single_token_sm70_out`: `closed-replaced`; do not re-bind as a
       default route because the split latest path already preserves the
       speed idea with stronger per-stage evidence and better decoupling.
     - `awq_moe_single_token_weighted_reduce_out`: `closed-not-default`; keep
       upstream `moe_unpermute` in the accepted route unless a future custom
       reduce proves Type A equality and model-level quality.
     - The old grouped/batched AWQ MoE route remains experimental behind
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` and is not closed by this item.
     - Old warmup/debug env gates such as `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISABLE` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_M*` remain separate inventory items.

     Quality and performance evidence reused:
     - Item 130 real-weight strict AWQ dense-stage and single-token tests:
       `sm70_awq_moe_single_token_dense_vs_dense_strict_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260601.json`.
     - Item 130 CUDA graph replay:
       `sm70_awq_moe_single_token_dense_graph_capture_qwen36_35a3b_20260601.json`.
     - Item 130 micro timing:
       `sm70_awq_moe_single_token_dense_micro_timing_qwen36_35a3b_20260601.json`,
       where the active-expert single-token stage chain was `24.14x` faster
       than the all-expert graph-safe dense bridge for the tested op chain.
     - Item 156 later proved the AWQ route can be reached under breakable
       CUDA graph in the Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ model path.

     Decision:
     - Do not re-introduce the old 0.0.3 compact/exact-layout Python-visible
       schemas just to make the inventory diff disappear.
     - The accepted migration target is the latest split active-expert route:
       it carries forward the old single-token active-expert scheduling idea,
       avoids the old monolithic custom weighted reduce, and already has
       stricter exactness/capture evidence than the old bound schemas.

     Checks:
     - Static source comparison only. No CUDA/C++ build, no model load, and no
       GPU benchmark were run for this closure item.

161. FP8 MoE old monolithic/router schemas classified against latest safe
     dense-stage route, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 159 surfaced old-only FP8 MoE op schemas:
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_sm70_out` and
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_router_sm70_out`.
     - These are tied to the old 0.0.3 compact/router-topk/gated-SiLU/
       broadcast/direct-reduce family that produced the historical no-MTP
       speed gain but also the known strict-diff risk. They must not be
       silently carried into latest vLLM as default routes.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp` binds
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_sm70_out` with compact input, active pointer
       row copies, optional fused gated-SiLU, optional fused weighted reduce,
       optional broadcast input, optional direct W2 reduce, and optional
       indexed expert pointers.
     - Old 0.0.3 also binds `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_router_sm70_out`, which
       fuses router top-k preparation for the hard-coded `[1, 256]` router
       logits / `top_k=8` shape.
     - Old Python `fp8_sm70_moe.py` exposes many env-controlled branches for
       this family:
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_CPP`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_GATED_SILU_EPILOGUE`,
       broadcast-input/indexed-pointer gates, W2 direct reduce, weighted
       reduce, router-topk CPP/compare/persistent, and strict-compare gates.
     - Latest `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp` intentionally does not bind those old
       monolithic/router schemas. It binds only:
       `_C::fp8_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`,
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`, and
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`, plus the existing
       gated experimental grouped GEMM `_C::fp8_moe_gemm_sm70_out`.
     - Latest `vllm/_sm70_ops.py` and
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8_sm70_moe.py` expose only
       those split safe-stage facades and the explicit
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` experiment. The old compact/router env
       gates are absent from latest `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Latest `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` still contains C++
       function bodies for `fp8_moe_single_token_sm70_out` and
       `fp8_moe_single_token_router_sm70_out`. This is source residue, not a
       production route, because they are not bound or called by the latest
       Python path.

     Replacement mapping:
     - Old compact active-expert prepare and pointer-row scheduling:
       replaced by `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`, which builds
       compact input, expert offsets, `inv_permuted_idx`, and sorted expert
       IDs on CUDA before launching only active-expert dense W13 stages.
     - Old monolithic C++ single-token W13/SiLU/W2/reduce chain:
       replaced by the split latest route:
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`,
       upstream `_C::silu_and_mul`,
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`, and upstream
       `_moe_C::moe_unpermute`.
     - Old router-topk fused path:
       not migrated by default. Latest keeps upstream router/top-k semantics
       and only optimizes the post-top-k active-expert MoE stages. A future
       router fused path must prove token/top-k exactness separately before it
       can be counted.
     - Old fused gated-SiLU, broadcast-input, weighted-reduce, W2 direct-reduce,
       and indexed-pointer knobs:
       not part of the accepted migration route. They remain repair targets
       only if a future harness proves classified numeric safety and
       model-level output quality.

     Quality evidence reused:
     - Item 60 proved the old-style batched/grouped FP8 MoE path is not strict
       against the per-expert dense oracle on real Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8
       weights (`w13 max_diff=0.000244140625`, `silu_and_mul max_diff` up to
       `6.103515625e-05`, and `w2 max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`), while the
       dense-out self-check is `max_diff=0.0`.
     - Item 61 localized the grouped/batched nonzero to multi-active-expert
       grouped execution rather than top-k sorting, duplicated routes, or
       obvious pointer-row resolution.
     - Item 106 added the graph-safe dense-stage replacement and proved
       Type A equality plus CUDA graph replay for the dense-stage op
       (`all_equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`).
     - Item 132 confirmed the latest safe single-token FP8 active-expert route
       is already wired and added compile-safe route logs.

     Classification:
     - `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_sm70_out`: `closed-replaced` for the accepted
       default route. The useful scheduling idea is preserved by the split
       active-expert dense route; the old monolithic fused options remain
       `paused-unsafe`.
     - `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_router_sm70_out`: `paused-unsafe`, not
       migrated to the default route. It fuses router/top-k semantics and must
       get a separate strict token/top-k/logit quality gate before any future
       enablement.
     - `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_CPP`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_GATED_SILU_EPILOGUE`,
       broadcast/indexed-pointer gates, direct-reduce, weighted-reduce, and
       router-topk gates: `paused-unsafe` in the latest migration ledger.
     - `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`: remains the only latest exposed
       grouped/batched experiment, still `B-pending` until repaired or proven
       as acceptable Type B under the quality rules.

     Decision:
     - Do not re-bind the old FP8 monolithic/router schemas in latest vLLM just
       to recover the old 13% path. That would re-open a known quality-risk
       route and bypass the split-stage exactness evidence.
     - Future FP8 MoE speed recovery should work on:
       1. counting and benchmarking the already-wired safe single-token
          active-expert route in production graph decode; and
       2. repairing or re-proving grouped/batched FP8 MoE under the A/B
          quality classification.

     Checks:
     - Static source comparison only. No CUDA/C++ build, no model load, and no
       GPU benchmark were run for this closure item.

162. Old Triton SM70 q-head split gate classified, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 159 surfaced old-only `VLLM_TRITON_ATTN_SM70_QHEAD_SPLIT`.
     - This needed explicit status because the final attention target is full
       Flash-V100 prefill plus decode when `FLASH_ATTN_V100` is selected, while
       old 0.0.3 also carried Triton-side SM70 attention experiments.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3
       `vllm/v1/attention/ops/triton_unified_attention.py` defines
       `_get_sm70_decode_head_groups()`.
     - That helper only activates when all conditions are true:
       `max_seqlen_q == 1`, `num_kv_heads <= 2`,
       `num_queries_per_kv >= 16`, CUDA capability major is `7`, and
       `VLLM_TRITON_ATTN_SM70_QHEAD_SPLIT` is set.
     - When active, old code changes the 2D Triton grid from
       `(total_num_q_blocks, num_kv_heads)` to
       `(total_num_q_blocks, num_kv_heads, decode_head_groups)`, reduces
       `num_queries_per_kv_group = ceil(num_queries_per_kv / groups)`, and
       passes `HEAD_GROUPS=decode_head_groups` into the Triton kernel.
     - Latest `vllm/v1/attention/ops/triton_unified_attention.py` has no
       `HEAD_GROUPS`, `decode_head_groups`, or old q-head split env.
       It keeps upstream grouping and exposes separate SM70 diagnostic schedule
       controls:
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_PREFILL_TILE_SIZE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_DECODE_TILE_SIZE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_NUM_WARPS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_PREFILL_NUM_WARPS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_DECODE_NUM_WARPS`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_SAFE_DEFAULTS`.

     Relevance to target models:
     - The old q-head split is not a general V100 attention fast path. It only
       targets decode shapes with very high GQA ratio
       (`num_queries_per_kv >= 16`).
     - Current Qwen3.6/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B evidence in this ledger uses Qwen-like
       shapes such as `q_heads=6`, `kv_heads=1`, `head_dim=256`; that is
       `num_queries_per_kv=6`, so the old 0.0.3 q-head split would not have
       activated for that measured target shape.
     - The old gate is also a Triton backend optimization. It cannot satisfy
       the active Flash rule that selecting `FLASH_ATTN_V100` means full
       Flash-V100 prefill plus decode by default.

     Latest replacement / related route:
     - The latest `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_*` schedule controls are the accepted
       Triton-side diagnostic/safe branch. They have separate evidence from
       item 49:
       prefill `num_warps=4` and decode `num_warps=8` preserved
       `max_diff=0.0` in the synthetic Qwen-like schedule harness and passed
       model-level token gates, while remaining separate from the full
       Flash-V100 migration target.
     - These latest controls do not reproduce old q-head split semantics. They
       replace the need for a generic SM70 Triton schedule knob for the current
       target models, but they are not a drop-in implementation of the old
       high-GQA head-group split.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_TRITON_ATTN_SM70_QHEAD_SPLIT`: not migrated for the current
       target. It is not needed for Qwen3.6/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B shapes currently
       under optimization, and it does not count toward the final full
       Flash-V100 backend.
     - If a future SM70 target has `num_queries_per_kv >= 16` and depends on
       Triton decode, this can be reopened as a separate Triton-only feature
       request with its own Type A equality and model-level token gates.
     - Do not silently use this old Triton split to claim Flash-V100 decode or
       full-attention performance.

     Checks:
     - Static source comparison only. No CUDA/C++ build, no model load, and no
       GPU benchmark were run for this closure item.

163. Safe SM70 AWQ warmup/LUT subset migrated to latest tree, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 159 and item 160 left old AWQ warmup / compact / LUT gates open:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP*`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_ENABLE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_COMPACT_COMPARE`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH`.
     - Old short AWQ baseline logs showed `SM70 AWQ warmup finished (16 dense
       calls, 0 MoE calls, 0 compact MoE calls)`, so warmup is part of the
       old 0.0.3 production behavior and should not disappear silently.
     - Latest still has much slower init/profile/warmup than old 0.0.3 in the
       same decode口径. Warmup is not the whole startup problem, but restoring
       the safe subset is aligned with the migration goal.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 `awq_sm70_warmup.py` did three things:
       1. ran representative AWQ dense `awq_gemm_sm70_out` decode shapes;
       2. ran AWQ MoE grouped/compact warmups when matching layers existed;
       3. optionally imported/exported the TurboMind GEMM LUT via
          `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH`.
     - Latest already had `_C::sm70_gemm_import_cache`,
       `_C::sm70_gemm_export_cache`, AWQ dense facade
       `awq_gemm_sm70_out`, AWQ MoE dense-stage facades, and the accepted
       single-token active-expert facades, but it did not call an AWQ-specific
       warmup from `kernel_warmup()`.

     Source change:
     - Added `vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py`.
     - Added env registrations:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP` default `1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_M` default `8`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_MOE_TOKENS` default `8`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH`.
     - Hooked `sm70_awq_warmup(worker)` into
       `vllm/model_executor/warmup/kernel_warmup.py` after DeepGEMM warmup
       and before FlashInfer warmup.
     - The warmup no-ops unless:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP=1`, CUDA device capability is exactly `(7, 0)`,
       `_C::awq_gemm_sm70_out` is registered, and the loaded model actually
       has SM70 AWQ-prepared layers.

     Safety boundary:
     - Migrated warmup routes:
       AWQ dense `awq_gemm_sm70_out`;
       AWQ MoE graph-safe dense-stage
       `awq_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`;
       AWQ MoE single-token active-expert
       `awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out` plus
       `awq_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`;
       and optional GEMM LUT import/export.
     - Not migrated:
       old `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_ENABLE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_COMPACT_COMPARE`, and old compact/monolithic AWQ MoE
       warmup. Item 160 already replaced those with the split active-expert
       route; rewarming the old compact path would re-open an unaccepted route.
     - Not closed by this item:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISABLE`, `VLLM_SM70_F16_DENSE_DEBUG`,
       `VLLM_SM70_SHARED_GATE_MAX_M`, and `VLLM_SM70_UNQUANT_DEBUG`.
       They remain separate inventory/debug items.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_M`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_MOE_TOKENS`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH`: migrated as a safe warmup/LUT subset.
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_ENABLE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_COMPACT_COMPARE`: closed-replaced by the latest split
       active-expert route; not restored as env gates.
     - Runtime acceptance is still pending: a future model route-hit should
       confirm the `SM70 AWQ warmup finished` log in latest and measure whether
       it reduces cold/warm startup or decode first-token variance.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py
       vllm/model_executor/warmup/kernel_warmup.py vllm/envs.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for the same files with
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`, `MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, and `NVCC_THREADS=1`.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched source files.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, no model load, and no GPU benchmark were run.

191. AWQ warmup runtime evidence made less ambiguous, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 163 restored the safe AWQ warmup/LUT subset but left runtime
       acceptance pending. The next route-hit needs to prove not only that the
       final `SM70 AWQ warmup finished` line appeared, but also which decode
       sizes were warmed and whether a `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH` cache was
       actually imported or missed.
     - Without this extra log context, a future 35B-A3B or 27B-AWQ startup run
       could show a warmup line while still leaving us unsure whether the run
       covered the CUDA graph capture sizes or reused the old-style LUT cache.

     Source change:
     - `vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py` now logs the selected
       dense decode `m_values`, MoE token counts, and resolved LUT path in the
       `Warming up SM70 AWQ accepted routes ...` line.
     - If `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH` is set but the resolved file does not
       exist, the warmup now logs `No SM70 GEMM LUT cache found ...` instead
       of silently treating it like the unset-cache case.

     Classification:
     - This is observability and acceptance plumbing only. It does not change
       AWQ GEMM math, MoE routing, CUDA graph capture, or the default warmup
       policy.
     - Runtime acceptance is still pending until a model route-hit shows the
       new warmup log, route-hit logs, and startup/decode behavior under the
       same low-memory compile envelope.

     Checks:
     - `env MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 NVCC_THREADS=1
       TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1 OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1
       NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2
       uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py` passed.
     - `env MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 NVCC_THREADS=1
       TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1 OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1
       NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2
       conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py` passed.
     - `rg -n "[ \t]+$" vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py`
       found no trailing whitespace.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, or GPU benchmark was run.

192. `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE` source status closed, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 147/159 listed `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE` as a
       latest-only SM70/V100 addition worth keeping, but it did not yet say
       whether the knob had real runtime behavior or was only a registered
       placeholder.
     - Because this name is tied to AWQ GEMM LUT import/reuse, it belongs with
       the item 163/191 warmup and cache migration thread.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 `csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` records devices in
       `imported_cache_devices` when `sm70_gemm_import_cache()` imports a LUT.
       During CUDA graph capture, dense and MoE dispatch policy selection
       returns `kReuse` if the current device has imported cache records;
       otherwise it falls back to `kDefault`.
     - Latest `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` keeps the same
       imported-cache device set and capture-time reuse behavior. It also adds
       `awq_reuse_imported_cache_enabled()` and routes AWQ dense GEMM through
       `select_awq_dense_dispatch_policy()`.
     - When `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE=1` and a LUT has been imported,
       latest AWQ dense dispatch can return `kReuse` before doing a dynamic
       measure, even outside CUDA graph capture. When unset, default behavior
       remains the normal tune/measure/default policy.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE`: `latest-only-decoupling-knob`,
       implemented in C++ and safe to keep as a default-off tuning control.
     - It is not an old-tree omission, and it should not be counted as a
       missing migration item. Acceptance still requires a model route-hit with
       `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH` import evidence and startup/first-token timing
       before claiming any practical speed benefit.

     Checks:
     - Source inspection only:
       `rg -n "VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE|REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE"
       vllm docs/design/sm70_v100_migration_control.md
       /home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm`.
     - Compared old
       `1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/csrc/quantization/awq/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`
       with latest `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for:
       `vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py`,
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`, and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for the same Python files with
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, and single-thread BLAS/tokenizer
       envs.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched warmup, benchmark, and
       document files.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, or GPU benchmark was run.

193. Model-token quality artifacts now record the packed recurrent GDN switch,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The migration goal treats GDN/FLA packed recurrent decode and mixed-QKV
       gating as first-class SM70 routes. Several model-level quality checks
       compare default packed recurrent against candidate mixed-QKV or schedule
       routes, so the generated JSON must preserve whether
       `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE` was enabled.
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` already recorded this env, but
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` did not. That made future
       token/logprob/perplexity evidence weaker than the decode benchmark
       evidence for the same GDN/FLA route.

     Source change:
     - Added `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE` to
       `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` `_tracked_env()`.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change model execution,
       FLA scheduling, attention routing, logits, or token generation.
     - Existing historical JSON files remain as-is; future model-token quality
       artifacts will include this env when it is set.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`,
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`, and
       `vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py`.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for the same Python files with
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, and single-thread BLAS/tokenizer
       envs.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched benchmark, warmup, and
       document files.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, or GPU benchmark was run.

194. SM70 TurboMind tune-policy defaults are now explicit in benchmark JSON,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - AWQ/FP8/F16 TurboMind timing and quality evidence depends on whether the
       small-shape dynamic-measure selector is active. The source policy is not
       obvious from `env` alone because C++ reads `getenv()` directly and an
       unset env has different effects for different selectors.
     - Latest `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` splits the selector:
       AWQ dense calls `select_awq_dense_dispatch_policy()`, while FP8 and F16
       calls still use the generic `select_dense_dispatch_policy()`.
     - With `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` unset, generic FP8/F16 dynamic
       measure remains enabled like old 0.0.3, but latest AWQ dense remains on
       the fixed-dispatch route. Setting the env to `0` disables both generic
       and AWQ dynamic measure; setting it nonzero enables both.

     Source change:
     - Added `sm70_tune_policy` to `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` JSON payloads.
     - The field records the raw `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` value plus
       the effective `awq_dense_dynamic_measure_enabled` and
       `generic_fp8_f16_dynamic_measure_enabled` booleans.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change C++ selector behavior,
       GEMM math, model output, or timing.
     - Future same-口径 AWQ/FP8/F16 benchmark artifacts should use this field
       to disambiguate fixed-dispatch versus dynamic-measure runs instead of
       inferring policy from whether an env string happened to be present.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`,
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`, and
       `vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py`.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for the same Python files with
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, and single-thread BLAS/tokenizer
       envs.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched benchmark, warmup, and
       document files.
     - No CUDA/C++ build, model load, or GPU benchmark was run.

195. Qwen3.5 MTP keep-quant/share-IO old envs are source-closed by latest
     upstream defaults, 2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Close the two behavior-changing old MTP envs left pending in item 138
       without silently dropping them from the 0.0.3 migration inventory.
     - This checkpoint is source classification only. It does not enable MTP,
       change quantization, or change generated tokens.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3
       `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5_mtp.py` implemented
       `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_SHARE_IO_WEIGHTS`, default-on. When enabled, the old
       model-local path skipped separate MTP embed/lm_head weights and later
       shared the target IO weights.
     - The same old file implemented `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_KEEP_QUANT`,
       default-off. When unset, old code inspected
       `quantization_config.modules_to_not_convert` for `mtp` or `model.mtp`;
       if present, it shallow-copied `vllm_config` and set the MTP branch
       `quant_config` to `None` to avoid creating quantized qweight/qzeros
       parameters that do not exist in full-precision MTP checkpoints.
     - Latest `vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py` now performs
       proposer-level MTP IO sharing: for PP world size 1 it shares the target
       embedding and lm_head with MTP draft models. It also explicitly shares
       per-layer MTP `shared_head.head` when the checkpoint omits that copy.
     - Latest AWQ and FP8 quant methods already honor
       `modules_to_not_convert` / `ignored_layers` by prefix. For AWQ this
       routes skipped MTP linears/experts to unquantized methods; for FP8,
       `modules_to_not_convert` is mapped to `ignored_layers` before
       `is_layer_skipped()`.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_SHARE_IO_WEIGHTS`: `closed-upstream-replaced-default`.
       The old default behavior is covered by latest proposer-level sharing in
       the normal PP=1 case. The old opt-out is not restored now because it is
       a behavior-changing/debug knob, not a missing fast path. Revisit only if
       an MTP load/quality investigation needs a separate-weight diagnostic.
     - `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_KEEP_QUANT`: `closed-upstream-replaced-default` for
       the safe config-driven default, because latest quantization already
       respects checkpoint skip lists. The old force-quant opt-in remains
       paused: forcing quantized MTP against a checkpoint that excluded MTP can
       create missing-parameter or quality-risk paths, so it must not become a
       default migration item without a concrete AWQ/FP8 MTP reproduction.

     Checks:
     - Source inspection only; no CUDA/C++ build, model load, GPU benchmark, or
       MTP run was executed in this checkpoint.
     - Compile-memory rule for any later follow-up remains:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, and `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2` unless the user
       explicitly approves a higher-thread build.

       drop to zero even when latest runtime code did not actually register or
       use that env.

       the existing old-only torch-op classification.

       still-pending envs separate instead of hiding them behind documentation

198. Old Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 SM70 server-default profile is not restored as a
     default policy, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 196 left `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_QWEN35_MTP_DEFAULTS` as the last
       old-only runtime env requiring a source-level decision.
     - The env is not a standalone fast path. It is the kill switch for an old
       default-on OpenAI-server profile that changed multiple runtime defaults
       for SM70 Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 AWQ models with MTP layers.

     Old 0.0.3 behavior:
     - Old `vllm/engine/arg_utils.py` called
       `_maybe_apply_qwen35_sm70_mtp_server_defaults()` after
       `create_model_config()` and before generic chunked-prefill,
       prefix-cache, max-num-batched-token, and max-num-seq defaults were
       resolved.
     - The profile only ran for `OPENAI_API_SERVER`, CUDA capability major 7,
       Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 text or MoE model types, checkpoints with
       `mtp_num_hidden_layers`, and AWQ quantization.
     - When active, it could set:
       `enable_prefix_caching=True`, auto `speculative_config=mtp4` for
       non-MoE AWQ, `use_local_argmax_reduction=True` for MTP configs,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=8192`, `max_num_seqs=4` for TP4,
       custom `cudagraph_capture_sizes`, `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` for
       TP4, `mamba_cache_mode=align` for linear-attention models with prefix
       caching, and text-only multimodal profiling limits.
     - For MoE AWQ, old code intentionally did not auto-enable MTP and logged
       `speculative_config=none_for_moe`, because those checkpoints commonly
       kept the MTP branch in full precision.
     - `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_QWEN35_MTP_DEFAULTS=1` merely disabled that whole
       profile.

     Latest source comparison:
     - Latest upstream already defaults prefix caching through
       `model_config.is_prefix_caching_supported` and `CacheConfig`
       (`enable_prefix_caching=True` by default when supported).
     - Latest OpenAI-server default batching for non-H100/H200 hardware is
       `max_num_batched_tokens=2048` and `max_num_seqs=256`, while old 1Cat
       changed the same target to `8192` and `4`.
     - Latest `SpeculativeConfig` still supports
       `use_local_argmax_reduction`, but it defaults to `False` and is not
       auto-injected for Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 AWQ.
     - Latest cache default `gpu_memory_utilization` is `0.92`, not the old
       1Cat `0.88` / `0.849` server profile.
     - Latest does not contain `_maybe_apply_qwen35_sm70_mtp_server_defaults`
       or the old disable env.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_QWEN35_MTP_DEFAULTS`:
       `paused-default-policy-not-restored`.
     - Do not restore this as a default-on profile in latest vLLM. It would
       silently mix no-MTP and MTP baselines, alter compile/capture shapes,
       change memory policy, and override upstream serving defaults without
       fresh route-hit, quality, and same-criterion throughput evidence.
     - If a future production profile is needed, add it as an explicit opt-in
       SM70 profile with route logs and benchmark JSON fields rather than
       reintroducing an old default-on profile controlled only by a disable
       env. The profile must keep no-MTP, MTP, greedy, and official sampling
       口径 separate.

     Inventory update:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` now maps
       `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_QWEN35_MTP_DEFAULTS` to item 198.
     - Refreshed
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_20260602.json`
       reports old-only runtime env classification
       `classified_count=27`, `unclassified_count=0`, and an empty
       `pending` list.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - Low-memory `python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - No CUDA/C++ compile, model load, GPU benchmark, or route-hit run was
       executed for this checkpoint.
