197. Old shared gate-up gated-SiLU envs paused with source evidence,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 196 left two old-only runtime envs pending:
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_SHARED_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU` and
       `VLLM_SM70_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU`.
     - They look related to existing latest SM70 TurboMind gated-SiLU C++
       support, but the old route was model-local and not equivalent to latest
       generic shared-expert routing. It must not be marked migrated merely
       because low-level epilogue kernels still exist.

     Source comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen2_moe.py` defines
       `_maybe_sm70_gate_up_silu()`. For small 2D SM70 fp16 inputs it can
       replace `gate_up_proj(x)` plus `SiluAndMul()` with a TurboMind GEMM
       using `gated_silu=True`.
     - The same old file uses `VLLM_SM70_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU` for F16 prepared
       weights and `VLLM_SM70_FP8_SHARED_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU` for FP8
       TurboMind-prepared `gate_up_proj` weights. Both are bounded by
       `VLLM_SM70_SHARED_GATE_MAX_M`.
     - Old `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/fp8.py` prepares an
       additional interleaved FP8 `gate_up_proj` weight when
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_SHARED_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU=1`.
     - Latest still has the low-level pieces:
       `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` supports gated-SiLU
       epilogues, `vllm/_sm70_ops.py` exposes `fp8_gemm_sm70_out(...,
       gated_silu)` and `sm70_f16_gemm_out(..., gated_silu)`, and
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/linear.py` prepares F16 interleaved
       `gate_up_proj` weights.
     - Latest does not call the old model-local `_maybe_sm70_gate_up_silu`
       branch. `qwen2_moe.py`, `qwen3_moe.py`, and `qwen3_next.py` route shared
       experts through latest generic `FusedMoE` / `MoERunner` shared-expert
       handling instead.
     - Latest generic shared-gate fusion in `MoERunner` fuses router and
       shared-expert gate weights/logits. It is not the same as fusing
       shared-expert `gate_up_proj` GEMM with `SiluAndMul`, and it does not
       provide Type A equivalence evidence for the old gate-up epilogue path.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_SM70_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU`:
       `paused-quality-unproven-gated-silu-route`.
     - `VLLM_SM70_FP8_SHARED_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU`:
       `paused-quality-unproven-gated-silu-route`.
     - Do not register or default-enable these old envs in latest runtime code
       yet. Re-enabling them would require a new generic hook in shared-expert
       MLP execution plus op-level Type A exactness for F16/FP8 gated-SiLU
       epilogue, CUDA graph replay, and model-level token/logit/perplexity
       quality gates.

     Inventory update:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` now maps both envs to
       item 197 with the paused status above.
     - Refreshed
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_20260602.json`
       reports old-only runtime env classification
       `classified_count=27`, `unclassified_count=0`; the only remaining
       `pending-*` env is `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_QWEN35_MTP_DEFAULTS`.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - Low-memory `python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` passed.
     - No CUDA/C++ compile, model load, GPU benchmark, or route-hit run was
       executed for this checkpoint.

142. Generic `all_reduce_sum2` hook for latest `FusedMoE` shared-expert
     output fusion, 2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Continue items 139-140 from low-level capability to a decoupled latest
       vLLM hook without hard-patching Qwen model files.
     - Old 0.0.3 used `all_reduce_sum2(routed, shared)` in Qwen MoE blocks to
       avoid materializing `routed + shared` before TP allreduce.
     - Latest vLLM moved shared experts into generic `FusedMoE` /
       `MoERunner`, where the equivalent point is:
       `result = shared_output + fused_output`, then
       `_maybe_reduce_final_output(result, ...)`.

     Source change:
     - Added env gate `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=0` in `vllm/envs.py`.
       It remains default-off.
     - Added a generic `MoERunner` hook in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py`.
     - The hook only fires when all conditions are true:
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=1`, CUDA platform, `shared_output` is
       present, non-sequence-parallel, TP size > 1, EP size = 1, PCP size = 1,
       fused output has not already been reduced, and shared/fused
       shape+dtype match.
     - Eligible route uses
       `tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce_sum2(shared_output, fused_output)`,
       then applies the same truncation and zero-expert handling as the
       original path.
     - With `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`, eager/non-compiled route-hit logs
       `SM70 MoE shared+routed all_reduce_sum2 path enabled` on first use.
     - Ineligible or default-off route is exactly the latest upstream behavior:
       materialize `shared + fused`, then use the existing TP allreduce.

     Harness update:
     - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_all_reduce_sum2.py` with:
       `--patterns exact_int,random_small`,
       `--references custom_ar,nccl`, and
       `--required-references`.
     - `custom_ar` reference captures the production-equivalent graph:
       `local_sum = a + b; custom_all_reduce(local_sum)`.
       This is the actual semantic gate for the new `MoERunner` hook.
     - `nccl` remains a diagnostic cross-implementation reference, not the
       default acceptance gate for this custom-AR route.

     Correctness result:
     - TP=2 graph log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_tp2_graph_float_refs_20260602.log`.
       Sizes `1024,8192,65536`, dtypes `float32,float16`, patterns
       `exact_int,random_small`.
       Result: all `custom_ar` and NCCL comparisons were bitwise equal with
       `max_diff=0.0`.
     - TP=4 graph log with `custom_ar` required:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_tp4_graph_float_refs_custom_required_20260602.log`.
       All `custom_ar` comparisons were bitwise equal with `max_diff=0.0`.
       Random-float NCCL diagnostics differed as expected from reduction
       order: fp32 max `2.3841858e-07`, fp16 max `0.001953125`.
     - A prior TP=4 run that required NCCL equality failed only because NCCL
       was included as a strict reference:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_tp4_graph_float_refs_20260602.log`.
       This is classified as cross-implementation `B` noise, not a production
       route failure, because the production custom-AR reference remained
       `max_diff=0.0`.

     Classification:
     - For the latest-vLLM production custom-allreduce graph path,
       `all_reduce_sum2(shared, fused)` is Type A exact against
       `tmp=shared+fused; custom_all_reduce(tmp)` for TP=2/4 on fp32/fp16 and
       both integer and random-small inputs.
     - The generic `FusedMoE` hook is migrated but not accepted/default.
       It still needs a model-level route-hit and speed check on a shared-MoE
       target with `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=1`.
     - Expected benefit is launch/HBM traffic reduction in shared-expert MoE
       layers, not a standalone GEMM speedup. It should be counted only if the
       route log proves the hook is active under CUDA graph and tokens/logits
       pass the active numeric-quality gate.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/envs.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_all_reduce_sum2.py` passed.
     - Low-thread conda `py_compile` passed for the same files.
     - After adding the trace log, the focused ruff/py_compile checks for
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py` passed.

143. `all_reduce_sum2` MoERunner integration boundary tightened to preserve
     custom-AR reduction order, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 142 proved the low-level `all_reduce_sum2` op is exact against a
       production-equivalent one-stage custom-AR reference, but it did not yet
       prove the generic `MoERunner` hook was safe against the exact original
       `shared + routed; tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce(...)` path for all
       payload sizes.
     - The original custom allreduce selector in
       `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cuh` uses one-stage for TP=2 and for small
       fully connected TP groups, then switches to two-stage for larger
       payloads. `all_reduce_sum2` currently only has a one-stage kernel.
       Therefore, replacing a large original custom-AR two-stage reduce with
       one-stage sum2 can introduce B-class reduction-order noise.

     Source changes:
     - Added `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py`.
       It initializes the real vLLM TP group under `torchrun`, constructs a
       minimal synthetic `MoERunner`, and calls
       `MoERunner._maybe_sm70_moe_sum2_allreduce()` inside CUDA graph capture.
       The reference is the original path:
       `tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce(shared + routed)`.
     - Tightened `_can_use_sm70_moe_sum2_allreduce()` so the hook only fires
       in the same reduction-order region as the existing custom-AR route:
       TP=2 always; TP<=4 only when payload is `<512 KiB`; TP<=8 only when
       payload is `<256 KiB`; other TP sizes do not use this hook.
     - This deliberately targets decode/small-MoE shapes first. Larger
       prefill or batch payloads stay on the original path until a two-stage
       `all_reduce_sum2` variant exists or a separate B-accept quality gate is
       proven.

     Boundary found before the fix:
     - Initial TP4 runner check:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/moe_sum2_runner_tp4_graph_exactness_20260602.log`.
     - `tokens=16`, `hidden=8192`, fp32 random-small produced
       `max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07` against
       `tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce(shared+routed)`.
     - Root cause: fp32 payload is exactly `512 KiB`, so original custom AR
       uses its two-stage path while sum2 still uses one-stage. This is
       classified as B reduction-order noise, not an indexing bug, but the
       route is not allowed to replace the original path there without an
       explicit B-accept/model-quality gate.

     Post-fix validation:
     - TP=2 graph log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/moe_sum2_runner_tp2_graph_exactness_after_threshold_20260602.log`.
       For tokens `1,2,16`, hidden `8192`, fp32/fp16,
       exact-int/random-small: all cases had `route_hit=true`,
       `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`.
     - TP=4 graph log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/moe_sum2_runner_tp4_graph_exactness_after_threshold_20260602.log`.
       For fp32 tokens `1,2`, fp16 tokens `1,2,16`, and exact/random inputs:
       `route_hit=true`, `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`.
       For fp32 tokens `16`, `expected_route_hit=false` and
       `route_hit=false`; the large same-threshold shape is skipped as
       intended.
     - Logs include
       `SM70 MoE shared+routed all_reduce_sum2 path enabled`, proving the
       hook is actually reached for eligible shapes.

     Classification:
     - Eligible small/decode shapes are Type A exact against the current
       production custom-AR path.
     - Large payloads that would change one-stage vs two-stage reduction order
       are now not routed through this hook, so they remain on the original
       path and do not introduce unreviewed B noise.
     - The hook remains default-off. Next acceptance step is still a real
       shared-MoE model route-hit/speed check with
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=1`, but the lightweight runner check now
       proves the integration layer and threshold gate are safe enough for
       that next model run.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py` passed.
     - Low-thread conda `py_compile` passed for the same files.

144. `all_reduce_sum2` two-stage kernel added; item 143 threshold restriction
     removed, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 143 correctly prevented the generic MoERunner hook from replacing
       large TP4 custom allreduce payloads because `all_reduce_sum2` only had
       a one-stage kernel, while the original
       `custom_all_reduce(shared+routed)` switched to two-stage at larger
       payloads.
     - That threshold was a safe temporary guard but left prefill/larger MoE
       payloads unable to use the fused add+allreduce route. The better
       migration is to implement the missing two-stage `sum2` kernel and make
       its selector mirror the existing custom allreduce selector.

     Source changes:
     - Added `cross_device_reduce_sum2_2stage` in
       `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cuh`.
       Stage 1 computes the same local `input_a + input_b` value per rank and
       reduce-scatters it using the same rank order as
       `cross_device_reduce_2stage`; stage 2 uses the identical allgather
       pattern as the original two-stage custom allreduce.
     - `CustomAllreduce::allreduce_sum2()` now reads
       `VLLM_CUSTOM_ALLREDUCE_ALGO` and supports the same
       `1stage/oneshot` and `2stage/twoshot` override semantics as
       `allreduce()`.
     - The default `sum2` selector now mirrors `allreduce()`:
       TP=2 uses one-stage; fully connected TP<=4 uses one-stage only below
       `512 KiB`; fully connected TP<=8 uses one-stage only below `256 KiB`;
       larger payloads use the new two-stage sum2 kernel.
     - Removed the payload threshold from
       `_can_use_sm70_moe_sum2_allreduce()`; it now allows supported
       custom-AR TP sizes `(2,4,6,8)` and relies on the C++ selector to choose
       the matching reduction order.
     - Updated `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py` so TP4 large
       payloads are expected to hit again instead of being skipped.

     Build:
     - Narrow `_C` build only, with controlled memory/thread env:
       `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`, `MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.
     - Build log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_build_sm70_C_only_sum2_twostage_20260602.log`.
       It rebuilt `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cu.o` and linked `_C.abi3.so`
       successfully.
     - `cmake --install` log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_install_sm70_C_only_sum2_twostage_20260602.log`.
       It failed on an unrelated missing `spinloop.abi3.so` install entry
       after `_C` had already linked. To avoid a broad rebuild, the generated
       `/tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260602_sum2/_C.abi3.so` was installed
       directly to `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.
     - Symbol check:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_twostage_symbol_check_20260602.log`.
       Fresh conda process reported `_C_custom_ar.all_reduce_sum2=True` and
       `_C_custom_ar.all_reduce=True` from
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm/vllm/_C.abi3.so`.

     Low-level exactness:
     - TP4 large-payload graph log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_tp4_twostage_large_float_refs_20260602.log`.
     - Sizes `131072` and `262144`, dtypes `float32,float16`, random-small
       inputs, references `custom_ar,nccl`, required reference `custom_ar`.
     - Result: all `custom_ar` comparisons had `equal=true`,
       `max_diff=0.0`, `mean_diff=0.0`.
     - NCCL remained a different cross-implementation reduction reference:
       fp32 max diff `2.384185791015625e-07` / `4.76837158203125e-07`;
       fp16 max diff `0.001953125`. This remains B-class reduction-order
       noise and is not the production custom-AR acceptance oracle.

     MoERunner integration exactness:
     - TP4 runner graph log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/moe_sum2_runner_tp4_graph_exactness_twostage_20260602.log`.
     - Tokens `1,2,16`, hidden `8192`, fp32/fp16,
       exact-int/random-small.
     - All cases now had `expected_route_hit=true`, `route_hit=true`,
       `equal=true`, and `max_diff=0.0`, including the formerly skipped
       fp32 `tokens=16` / `512 KiB` case.
     - Log contains
       `SM70 MoE shared+routed all_reduce_sum2 path enabled`, proving the
       MoERunner hook actually reached the fused route.

     Classification:
     - The temporary item 143 payload guard is superseded by this two-stage
       repair. For TP sizes supported by custom allreduce, the sum2 route now
       mirrors the original custom-AR one-stage/two-stage selector and is
       Type A exact against `custom_all_reduce(shared+routed)` for the tested
       TP4 small and large CUDA-graph payloads.
     - The hook remains default-off until a real shared-MoE model route-hit,
       token/logit quality check, and same-criterion decode/prefill timing are
       recorded.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check` passed for
       `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cuh`,
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py`, and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py`.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py` passed.
     - Low-thread conda `py_compile` passed for the same Python files.

145. `all_reduce_sum2` route evidence tightened: C++ trace is the actual
     graph-hit oracle, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 142-144 proved the low-level and synthetic graph correctness, but
       the real-model acceptance requirement is stricter: under CUDA graph the
       production model must actually reach the custom `all_reduce_sum2` op.
     - The previous Python MoERunner log could be misleading in eager mode.
       `tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce_sum2(a,b)` is a semantic facade:
       outside the custom-allreduce CUDA graph path,
       `CudaCommunicator.all_reduce_sum2()` falls back to
       `all_reduce(a + b)`. Therefore a Python-side "candidate selected" log
       proves the shared+routed hook condition, but not the custom C++ op
       route.

     Source changes:
     - Added a host-side, env-gated one-shot trace in
       `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cu::all_reduce_sum2`.
       With `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`, the real custom op now prints:
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached rank=... world_size=...
       numel=... dtype=... capture=...`.
     - Changed the MoERunner Python log to:
       `SM70 MoE shared+routed all_reduce_sum2 candidate selected; actual
       custom op route is reported by C++ trace during CUDA graph capture.`
       This prevents future reports from counting eager fallback as a fast
       path hit.

     Build:
     - Narrow `_C` rebuild only, still with controlled thread/memory env:
       `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`, `MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.
     - Build log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_build_sm70_C_only_sum2_trace_20260602.log`.
       The rebuilt `/tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260602_sum2/_C.abi3.so`
       was installed directly to `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.

     Synthetic graph validation:
     - TP4 log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/moe_sum2_runner_tp4_trace_log_20260602.log`.
     - Command shape: `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`, `tokens=16`,
       `hidden=8192`, dtypes `float32,float16`, pattern `random_small`.
     - Result:
       every rank printed
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached ... capture=active` for the
       fp32 `numel=131072` graph capture, and rank 0 JSON reported
       `all_equal=true`.
     - Exactness: both fp32 and fp16 cases had `route_hit=true`,
       `expected_route_hit=true`, `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`, and
       `mean_diff=0.0`.

     Real-model CUDA graph smoke:
     - Attempted:
       `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP4, `input_len=64`, `output_len=4`,
       `max_model_len=1024`, `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=1`, CUDA graph enabled.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sum2_full_flash_graph_i64_o4_20260602.log`.
     - It selected `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`, initialized TP4
       custom allreduce, logged `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path
       enabled (256 experts)`, and bounded compile to
       `compile_ranges_endpoints=[1024]`.
     - It did not reach generation or the C++ `sum2` route trace. After the
       first compile (`torch.compile took 54.92 s`) it repeated
       `No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds` with
       GPU utilization at 0. The run was stopped and GPUs were freed.
     - Classification: this is the same latest-vLLM first compile / worker
       synchronization blocker already seen in earlier 35B-A3B graph work.
       It is not evidence that `sum2` fails the model route; it is also not
       performance evidence.

     Real-model eager diagnostic:
     - Ran the same model/Flash/sum2 gates with `--enforce-eager`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sum2_full_flash_eager_i64_o4_20260602.log`
       and `.json`.
     - It completed generation. `load_seconds=118.93`, output token hash
       `014a116aae9bca2423c6d7e9c850e7935581c0274797ae1442957bb6e76d66a5`.
       `request_metrics=None`, so no pure-decode metric can be taken from
       this eager diagnostic.
     - Route evidence: all TP workers selected `FLASH_ATTN_V100`; route
       summary showed `prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash=10` and
       `decode_scalar_paged=30`; AWQ MoE logged per-expert dense,
       CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage, and single-token active-expert paths.
     - The MoERunner Python candidate log appeared, but there was no
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached` C++ log. This is expected:
       outside CUDA graph capture, `custom_all_reduce_sum2()` cannot use
       arbitrary activation addresses and the communicator falls back to
       `all_reduce(a+b)`.
     - Classification: useful model-path diagnostic, not fast-path acceptance
       and not a throughput baseline.

     Current acceptance status:
     - Low-level and synthetic TP4 CUDA graph `sum2` route is Type A exact
       against `custom_all_reduce(shared+routed)`.
     - This item originally left the real shared-MoE model graph route-hit as
       pending. Item 146 supersedes that route diagnosis: a later normal
       Inductor CUDA-graph smoke did print the C++ `capture=active` trace, but
       still did not complete generation/timing acceptance.
     - Do not use the Python candidate log, eager output, or eager speed as
       proof that the production custom `sum2` path is active.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check` passed for `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cu` and
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py`.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py` passed.
     - Low-thread conda `py_compile` passed for the same Python files.

146. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ CUDA-graph `sum2` route-hit clarified: normal
     Inductor graph reaches the C++ op, but cold compile/warmup is still not a
     throughput acceptance result, 2026-06-02.

     Why this entry exists:
     - Item 145 recorded the first real-model attempts, but the later
       `max_num_batched_tokens=64` log is stronger than the earlier
       interpretation: the normal latest-vLLM Inductor CUDA-graph path did
       eventually reach graph capture and printed the C++ `all_reduce_sum2`
       trace on every TP rank.
     - This corrects the route diagnosis: the current blocker is not "the
       model cannot reach the custom sum2 op"; it is the very high cold
       profile/compile/warmup cost and the lack of a completed same-criterion
       generation/timing run.

     Source-level trace:
     - `GPUWorker.determine_available_memory()` always calls
       `self.model_runner.profile_run()` during startup, even when memory is
       manually specified.
     - `GPUModelRunner.profile_run()` calls
       `_dummy_run(self.max_num_tokens, is_profile=True)`, where
       `self.max_num_tokens` is initialized from
       `scheduler_config.max_num_batched_tokens`.
     - Therefore lowering `max_num_batched_tokens` from `1024` to `64`
       correctly reduces the profile dummy-run shape and compile range, but
       it does not remove the first-compile / PTXAS / graph warmup cost.

     Normal Inductor CUDA-graph route evidence:
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sum2_full_flash_graph_i64_o4_maxbt64_20260602.log`.
     - Shape and gates: `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP4, `input_len=64`,
       `output_len=4`, `max_model_len=1024`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=64`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE=1`, CUDA graph enabled.
     - The config reported `compile_ranges_endpoints=[64]`, proving the
       profile shape was narrowed.
     - Cold compile/profile cost was still large:
       `torch.compile took 55.13 s`, then repeated shared-memory broadcast
       waits while compilation/warmup continued; profile_run ended at roughly
       `533.7 s`, with `Initial profiling/warmup run took 467.13 s`.
     - During graph capture, all four ranks printed the real C++ route:
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached rank={0,1,2,3}
       world_size=4 numel=4096 dtype=float16 capture=active`.
     - The run was stopped after route evidence and graph address
       registration, before user generation completed. It has no JSON output
       and no accepted decode/prefill timing.

     Breakable CUDA-graph diagnostic:
     - Log and JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_sum2_full_flash_breakable_graph_i64_o4_maxbt64_20260602.log`
       and `.json`.
     - `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` printed:
       `disabling vLLM's torch.compile pipeline. Equivalent to -cc.mode=none`,
       so this is a diagnostic bypass of Inductor, not the default production
       compilation mode.
     - It still ran with `enforce_eager=false`, CUDA graph enabled, TP4 custom
       allreduce enabled, and full Flash-V100 gates.
     - Startup evidence:
       `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`, profile_run completed in about
       `15.3 s`, graph capture finished in `2 s`, and all four ranks printed
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached ... capture=active`.
     - Route summary at shutdown:
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 10}`. This confirms full
       Flash-V100 prefill+decode routing for this smoke; it is not a
       Triton-prefill/Flash-decode mixed path.
     - Output: token ids `[369, 1429, 310, 1791]`, token hash
       `014a116aae9bca2423c6d7e9c850e7935581c0274797ae1442957bb6e76d66a5`,
       matching the prior eager diagnostic for this 4-token prompt.
     - JSON reported `request_metrics=None` and `output_tps_mean=1.9362`.
       This value is only smoke-run wall-clock output speed, not a pure decode
       throughput baseline.

     Classification:
     - Real-model CUDA graph `all_reduce_sum2` route-hit is now proven for
       both normal Inductor graph capture and the breakable graph diagnostic.
     - `sum2` remains default-off for production until a completed
       same-criterion benchmark records route hit, pure decode speed, prefill
       speed, and model-quality checks.
     - `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` may be used as a diagnostic or as a
       candidate production mode only after it is benchmarked against normal
       Inductor/cached startup and accepted explicitly; do not silently treat
       it as the normal latest-vLLM path.
     - Future compile/build runs must keep the low-memory guard:
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`; source builds should keep
       `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`, `MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, and `NVCC_THREADS=1` unless the user
       explicitly approves a higher memory-risk compile.

     Next acceptance work:
     - Add `disable_log_stats=false` (or equivalent request-metrics capture)
       before timing so TTFT/prefill and pure decode are recorded instead of
       only wall-clock `output_tps`.
     - Run a meaningful output length for speed after the route is already
       warm/cached, and keep the model/TP/max_model_len/input-output/sampling
       criteria matched to the old-tree baseline.
     - Run token/logit/perplexity quality checks before defaulting this
       route.

147. Updated-goal source inventory checkpoint and decode metrics harness fix,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The active goal was expanded to require a complete, non-silent inventory
       of all old `0.0.3` SM70/V100 acceleration routes, not just the routes
       already under active implementation.
     - Before changing more kernels, I re-read this document and re-ran a
       source-level old-tree vs latest-tree env-gate scan so the next work does
       not repeat already paused or failed routes.

     Source inventory scan:
     - Command shape:
       `rg -o "VLLM_[A-Z0-9_]+"` over old
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/{vllm,benchmarks,csrc}`
       and latest
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm/{vllm,benchmarks,csrc}`, then
       `sort -u` and `comm`.
     - Result counts: old tree `387` unique `VLLM_*` names, latest tree `419`.
       This is only an inventory signal, not proof that every route is wired or
       accepted.
     - Old-only groups still needing explicit status as this migration
       continues:
       - DFlash/MTP/model-quality/debug gates:
         `VLLM_DFLASH_*`, `VLLM_MTP_STOCHASTIC_TOKEN_MATCHING`,
         `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_KEEP_QUANT`,
         `VLLM_QWEN35_MTP_SHARE_IO_WEIGHTS`,
         `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_QWEN35_MTP_DEFAULTS`, and related spec-debug
         dump gates.
       - Old FP8 MoE fast-path experiments:
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_CPP`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_GATED_SILU_EPILOGUE`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BROADCAST_INPUT`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BROADCAST_INPUT_IDXS`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_INDEXED_EXPERT_PTRS`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_W2_DIRECT_REDUCE`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_WEIGHTED_REDUCE_EPILOGUE`,
         router-topk compare/CPP/persistent gates, and the old compact strict
         compare gates.
       - Old shared-gate/fused-sigmoid/GDN/FLA tuning gates:
         `VLLM_SM70_FP8_SHARED_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU`,
         `VLLM_SM70_GATE_UP_GATED_SILU`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_*`,
         `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV*`, and
         `VLLM_SM70_FLA_*`.
       - Old AWQ warmup / compact / LUT gates:
         `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP*`,
         `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_ENABLE_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`,
         `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_COMPACT_COMPARE`, and
         `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH`.
       - Old worker/runtime hooks:
         `VLLM_SM70_DENSE_CUDAGRAPH_CAPTURE`,
         `VLLM_SM70_DISABLE_QWEN3NEXT_SHARED_MOE_OVERLAP`,
         `VLLM_SM70_DISABLE_UNQUANTIZED_MOE_INPLACE`,
         `VLLM_SM70_MOE_DENSE_ALLOWLIST`,
         old single-token MoE permute/unpermute gates, and
         `VLLM_TP_ALLREDUCE_TRACE`.
     - These old-only names must not be silently ignored. Each route should be
       promoted to migrated/accepted, marked upstream-replaced, kept
       `paused-unsafe`, or rejected with source evidence as the relevant module
       is reached. The already-known quality-risk FP8 compact/gated/broadcast
       family stays paused unless a future exactness repair proves otherwise.

     Current latest-only SM70/V100 additions worth keeping:
     - Latest now has new diagnostic and migration gates that did not exist in
       old `0.0.3`, including
       `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_COMPARE_*`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_*`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_TRITON_ATTN_*`. These are decoupling/diagnostic controls, not
       old-tree omissions.

     Benchmark harness fix:
     - Problem: `LLM` injects `disable_log_stats=True` by default. Therefore
       previous `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` route-hit smokes often
       emitted `request_metrics=None`, making TTFT/prefill and pure decode TPS
       unavailable even though the script already knew how to summarize those
       fields.
     - Change: added `--disable-log-stats` to
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and now passes
       `disable_log_stats=False` by default to `LLM`. Existing
       `--engine-arg disable_log_stats=true` or the new flag can still opt out.
     - Quality/performance classification: harness-only. It changes no model
       math, kernels, scheduling, or backend selection. It is required evidence
       plumbing for the goal's pure-decode vs prefill/TTFT separation rule.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`,
       `docs/design/sm70_v100_migration_control.md`,
       `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cu`, `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cuh`, and
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py`.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/runner/moe_runner.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_moe_sum2_runner.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`.
     - No CUDA compile and no heavy benchmark were run for this item.

148. Old-tree SM70 FLA recurrent schedule knobs migrated as default-off
     controls, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 147 identified old-only `VLLM_SM70_FLA_*` env gates. These should
       not be silently lost during the 0.0.3-to-latest migration, but previous
       GDN/FLA strict checks also proved that directly copying old schedules
       into the full latest op is unsafe without op/model evidence.
     - The current Flash policy was rechecked before this change: selected
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100` now means Flash prefill plus Flash decode by default.
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1` is only an explicit diagnostic
       fallback and does not count as final performance.

     Source finding:
     - Old `1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3` `fused_recurrent.py` had SM70-specific recurrent
       GDN/FLA launch scheduling knobs:
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_BV`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_TARGET_WAVES`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_BV_CANDIDATES`.
     - Latest `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py` used
       fixed launch settings in both normal recurrent forward and packed decode:
       `BV=min(next_power_of_2(V), 32)`, `num_stages=3`, `num_warps=1`.

     Change:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE=0` as the explicit opt-in gate in
       `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Registered the old `VLLM_SM70_FLA_*` env names in `vllm/envs.py` for
       migration parity and reproducible benchmark commands.
     - Added SM70-only launch selection helpers in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py`.
     - When the new gate is off and no old `VLLM_SM70_FLA_*` override is set,
       latest behavior is unchanged.
     - When the new gate is on, or when a legacy `VLLM_SM70_FLA_*` override is
       set, the packed decode and recurrent forward launch can use the SM70 BV
       selection path. `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES` are explicit overrides and must be validated
       independently before any performance claim.

     Quality/performance classification:
     - Status: `validation-needed`, not accepted.
     - This patch intentionally migrates only launch schedule selection. It does
       not copy old math, load/cast order, softmax/recurrent equations, or
       reference logic.
     - Because previous strict GDN/FLA work found full-op and sub-op nonzero
       differences, this route must pass the same numeric policy before it can
       be promoted: Type A schedule-only cases need `max_diff=0.0`; any Type B
       claim needs matched evidence, dtype-bound reasoning, and non-divergence.
     - No CUDA timing or model-level gate has been run for this new recurrent
       schedule gate yet, so it does not count toward the accepted migration
       performance target.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py
       vllm/envs.py benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py`, `vllm/envs.py`,
       and `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`.
     - Compile guard used for checks:
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`, `MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, and `NVCC_THREADS=1`.
     - No CUDA/C++ rebuild and no heavy benchmark were run for this item.

149. FLA packed recurrent decode schedule split: BV-only exact, automatic
     multi-warp route not accepted, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 148 migrated the old `VLLM_SM70_FLA_*` recurrent schedule knobs but
       left the new route as `validation-needed`. The next required question was
       whether the packed no-spec GDN decode path can change launch scheduling
       without changing tensors.

     Harness change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` with
       `run-packed-recurrent`.
     - The new subcommand builds a Qwen-like packed decode fixture:
       `mixed_qkv=[B, 2048]`, `a/b=[B, 8]`, `A_log/dt_bias=[8]`,
       `initial_state=[B+1, 8, 128, 128]`, and
       `ssm_state_indices=[1..B]`.
     - It saves inputs and outputs so default-vs-schedule runs compare the same
       tensors with `torch.equal` and `max_diff == 0`.

     Evidence files:
     - Default packed recurrent run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_packed_recurrent_default_b16.pt`.
     - Original migrated schedule before narrowing:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_packed_recurrent_sm70_sched_b16.pt`.
     - Strict compare for that original schedule:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_packed_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_b16_strict.json`.
     - Warps/stages isolation:
       `gdn_packed_recurrent_sm70_sched_w1_s3_b16.pt`,
       `gdn_packed_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_w1_s3_b16_strict.json`,
       `gdn_packed_recurrent_sm70_sched_w8_s3_b16.pt`,
       `gdn_packed_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_w8_s3_b16_strict.json`,
       `gdn_packed_recurrent_sm70_sched_w1_s1_b16.pt`, and
       `gdn_packed_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_w1_s1_b16_strict.json`.
     - Narrowed BV-only schedule after the fix:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_packed_recurrent_sm70_sched_bv_only_b16.pt`.
     - Strict compare after the fix:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_packed_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_bv_only_b16_strict.json`.

     Result:
     - Original automatic SM70 schedule was not strict-pass:
       inputs matched exactly, `out` matched exactly, but `final_state` had
       `torch_equal=false`, `max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`, and
       `num_different=9`.
     - Forcing `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES=3` made both `out` and `final_state`
       strict-pass (`max_diff=0.0`).
     - Forcing `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES=1` also strict-passed.
     - Forcing `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS=8` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES=3` reproduced the same `final_state` nonzero
       (`max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`, `num_different=9`).
     - Therefore the current root cause is the multi-warp Triton reduction
       shape for the K-dimension `tl.sum`, not input mismatch, state-index
       mismatch, or BV tiling.

     Source fix:
     - Narrowed `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE=1` to preserve latest
       `num_warps=1` and `num_stages=3` by default.
     - `VLLM_SM70_FLA_BV` / adaptive BV selection remains available.
     - `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS` and `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES` remain explicit
       experimental overrides, but they are not part of the accepted default
       route.

     Classification:
     - BV-only packed recurrent decode on the measured B=16, fp16, K/V=128
       fixture is Type A strict-pass: `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`,
       `out max_diff=0.0`, `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - The multi-warp packed recurrent route is `B-pending`: the nonzero is
       consistent with a changed reduction order, but it is not needed for a
       quality-safe default and has not passed dtype-bound/non-divergence/model
       gates.
     - Timing in this small synthetic fixture is not a performance claim:
       default averaged `0.0002338 s`, narrowed BV-only gate averaged
       `0.0002310 s`, and the earlier automatic multi-warp route averaged
       `0.0002636 s`.
     - No model-level GDN route-hit, token equality, or throughput run was done
       for this item.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py`
       passed.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py`
       passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`,
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py`, and
       `vllm/envs.py`.
     - No CUDA/C++ rebuild was run.

150. FLA non-packed recurrent forward BV-only schedule exactness, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 149 only covered `fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_packed_decode`,
       but item 148's gate also changes `fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_fwd`.
       The non-packed recurrent forward path needed its own strict Type A
       evidence before the gate can be considered fully bounded.

     Harness change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` with
       `run-recurrent`.
     - This subcommand calls `fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule` directly and
       saves the same input/output payloads used by the existing strict
       comparator.
     - The fixture is intentionally restricted to `B=1` and uses
       `inplace_final_state=False`. A first attempted `B=2` fixture failed
       during Triton compilation because the non-continuous/in-place kernel
       path dereferences `ssm_state_indices` when none is supplied. That is an
       invalid harness shape for this path, not evidence about the SM70
       schedule itself.

     Evidence files:
     - Fixed single-sequence recurrent default:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_recurrent_default_b1_t16.pt`.
     - Fixed single-sequence recurrent BV-only schedule:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_recurrent_sm70_sched_bv_only_b1_t16.pt`.
     - Strict fixed-shape compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_bv_only_b1_t16_strict.json`.
     - Single-segment `cu_seqlens=[0,16]` default:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_recurrent_default_b1_t16_cu.pt`.
     - Single-segment `cu_seqlens=[0,16]` BV-only schedule:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_recurrent_sm70_sched_bv_only_b1_t16_cu.pt`.
     - Strict `cu_seqlens` compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_recurrent_default_vs_sm70_sched_bv_only_b1_t16_cu_strict.json`.

     Result:
     - Fixed B=1/T=16 recurrent forward:
       `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`, `out max_diff=0.0`, and
       `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - Single-segment `cu_seqlens=[0,16]` recurrent forward:
       `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`, `out max_diff=0.0`, and
       `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - Timing in this tiny synthetic fixture is not a performance claim:
       fixed default averaged `0.0003130 s`, fixed BV-only averaged
       `0.0003185 s`; `cu_seqlens` default averaged `0.0003133 s`, and
       `cu_seqlens` BV-only averaged `0.0003478 s`.

     Classification:
     - Non-packed recurrent forward BV-only schedule is Type A strict-pass on
       the measured B=1/T=16 fp16 fixture, both with no `cu_seqlens` and with a
       single `cu_seqlens` segment.
     - This expands the accepted op-level coverage of
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE=1` to both packed decode and the legal
       non-packed single-sequence recurrent forward fixture.
     - It still does not promote the gate to model-level/default acceptance;
       continuous-batching model route-hit, token/logit quality, and real
       throughput remain pending.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py
       docs/design/sm70_v100_migration_control.md` passed before this doc
       append.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py`
       passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`,
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py`, and

151. Fused sigmoid gating SM70 schedule migration and strict split,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 147 identified old-only
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_SCHED/BV/WARPS/STAGES` knobs in
       0.0.3. Latest upstream still exposes
       `fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update` for the non-packed GDN
       fallback, so the env names need to be present for migration parity.
     - The old mixed-qkv fused-sigmoid fast path is not accepted here. Old
       evidence for that route was tolerance-based, and it needs a separate
       strict harness before it can become part of the main route.

     Source changes:
     - Registered `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_SCHED`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_BV`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_WARPS`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_STAGES` in `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Added SM70 schedule selection to
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py`.
       The accepted default path is intentionally conservative: with
       `SCHED=1` and no explicit override, it preserves latest upstream
       `BV=min(next_power_of_2(V), 32)`, `num_warps=4`, and
       `num_stages=3`. Explicit `BV/WARPS/STAGES` overrides are allowed only
       as experiments.
     - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` with
       `run-sigmoid-gating`, which saves fixed inputs/outputs and uses the
       existing strict compare path.

     Evidence files:
     - Default fixed B=1/T=16 fixture:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_b1_t16_meta.pt`.
     - `SCHED=1`, no overrides, fixed B=1/T=16:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_sm70_sched_no_override_b1_t16_meta.pt`.
     - Strict compare for fixed B=1/T=16:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_vs_sm70_sched_no_override_b1_t16_meta_strict.json`.
     - Explicit `BV=8` negative evidence:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_vs_sm70_sched_bv8_b1_t16_meta_strict.json`.
     - Single-segment `cu_seqlens=[0,16]` strict compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_vs_sm70_sched_no_override_b1_t16_cu_meta_strict.json`.

     Result:
     - `SCHED=1` with no overrides is Type A strict-pass on the measured
       B=1/T=16 fixture: `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`,
       `out max_diff=0.0`, and `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - The same no-override gate also strict-passes the single-segment
       `cu_seqlens=[0,16]` fixture with `out max_diff=0.0` and
       `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - Explicit `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_BV=8` keeps `out` exact but
       changes `final_state`: `max_diff=7.62939453125e-06`,
       `num_different=61`. This is not accepted for the main route.

     Classification:
     - The migrated fused-sigmoid env names are safe to keep because the
       no-override schedule is a strict no-op relative to latest upstream.
     - Explicit tiling overrides are `B-pending`/experimental, not
       default-safe. They may be compiler/reduction-order differences, but no
       matched-tiling proof or model-level gate has promoted them to
       `B-accept`.
     - No model-level GDN route-hit, token equality, or throughput run was
       done in this step.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py
       docs/design/sm70_v100_migration_control.md` passed.
     - `rg -n "[ \t]+$"` over the same files found no trailing whitespace.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py vllm/envs.py`
       passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`,
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py`,
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_recurrent.py`, and
       `vllm/envs.py`.

152. Fused sigmoid mixed-QKV low-level route migrated with strict op evidence,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 151 kept the old 0.0.3
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV` route out of the accepted path
       because old evidence was only tolerance-based. The source diff showed
       this route is not new math: it uses the same fused sigmoid recurrent
       kernel and only reads q/k/v from a packed `mixed_qkv` row by offset.
       That makes it a Type A layout-equivalent candidate, so it must either
       pass `torch.equal` or stay out of the main route.

     Source changes:
     - Restored the packed-qkv read mode in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py` by adding
       `mixed_qkv`, q/k/v offsets, qkv stride, and the `MIXED_QKV` constexpr
       to the existing kernel. The normal q/k/v path still passes
       `MIXED_QKV=False`.
     - Added
       `fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update_mixed_qkv()` as a low-level
       callable. It is not wired into `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` yet.
     - Registered the old env names
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV_COMPARE` as default-off controls in
       `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` with
       `run-sigmoid-gating-mixed-qkv` and a `--decode-segments` fixture that
       uses `cu_seqlens=[0,1,...,T]`, `ssm_state_indices=[1..T]`, and in-place
       state update to approximate the old decode/continuous-state call.

     Evidence files:
     - Single-segment unpacked baseline:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_for_mixed_b1_t16.pt`.
     - Single-segment packed mixed-QKV run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_mixed_qkv_b1_t16.pt`.
     - Single-segment strict compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_vs_mixed_qkv_b1_t16_strict.json`.
     - Decode-segment unpacked baseline:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_for_mixed_decode_segments_b1_t16.pt`.
     - Decode-segment packed mixed-QKV run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_mixed_qkv_decode_segments_b1_t16.pt`.
     - Decode-segment strict compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_sigmoid_gating_default_vs_mixed_qkv_decode_segments_b1_t16_strict.json`.

     Result:
     - Single-segment B=1/T=16 fixture:
       `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`, `out max_diff=0.0`, and
       `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - Decode-segment fixture with per-token `cu_seqlens` and
       `ssm_state_indices`: `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`,
       `out max_diff=0.0`, and `final_state max_diff=0.0`.
     - Tiny synthetic timing is not a performance claim, but it did not show
       an obvious slowdown: single-segment unpacked `0.0004658 s` vs mixed
       `0.0004046 s`; decode-segment unpacked `0.0004554 s` vs mixed
       `0.0004203 s`.

     Classification:
     - Low-level mixed-QKV fused-sigmoid is Type A strict-pass on the measured
       fixtures. This repairs the previous "old evidence only tolerance-based"
       blocker at op level.
     - It remains default-off because the model hook, CUDA graph route-hit,
       token/logit quality gate, and same-criterion decode speed evidence are
       still pending.
     - Next safe step is to wire the route into latest
       `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` behind
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV=1`, keep a compare/debug mode that
       falls back to the unpacked output, and then run a model-level route-hit
       before any default-route decision.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py vllm/envs.py`
       passed before this doc append.
     - `rg -n "[ \t]+$"` over the same files found no trailing whitespace
       before this doc append.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py vllm/envs.py`
       passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`,
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_sigmoid_gating.py`, and
       `vllm/envs.py`.
     - After this doc append, `git diff --check`, trailing-whitespace check,
       low-memory `ruff check`, and low-memory conda `py_compile` were rerun
       for the touched files and passed.
     - No CUDA/C++ rebuild and no model benchmark were run.

153. Fused sigmoid mixed-QKV wired into latest GDN decode fallback,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 152 proved the low-level packed-qkv fused-sigmoid route is Type A
       strict-pass, but it was still only a callable/harness. The next
       migration step was to make it selectable from latest model code without
       changing the default packed recurrent decode path.

     Source changes:
     - Exported `fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update_mixed_qkv` from
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/__init__.py`.
     - Added default-off instance flags in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`:
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV_COMPARE`.
     - When the mixed-QKV env is enabled and the decode-only fp16 packed input
       is eligible, latest skips the packed recurrent decode early-return and
       uses the non-packed decode fallback with
       `fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update_mixed_qkv`.
     - If the env is enabled but the route is not eligible, latest keeps the
       existing packed recurrent decode path. This avoids an experimental env
       causing a silent slowdown on bf16/non-contiguous/unsupported cases.
     - Compare mode only runs outside CUDA graph capture. It computes the
       mixed-QKV output first with `inplace_final_state=False`, then computes
       the unpacked reference path with `inplace_final_state=True`; the
       reference output/state are used as the final result. Any nonzero
       `max_diff` is logged.

     Current status:
     - This is a model hook, not a default-route acceptance. The route is
       explicit opt-in only.
     - No model route-hit, no CUDA graph route-hit, no token/logit quality
       gate, and no same-criterion throughput benchmark were run in this step.
     - Because item 152 already gave op-level strict equality, the next safe
       test is a small Qwen3Next/GDN decode route-hit with
       `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV=1`, proving the explicit env hits the
       route log instead of silently taking packed recurrent or generic
       unpacked decode.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/__init__.py` passed.
     - `rg -n "[ \t]+$"` over those files found no trailing whitespace.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/__init__.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for the same two files.
     - A low-memory import/env smoke with
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV_COMPARE=1` confirmed
       `envs.VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV == True`, compare env `True`,
       the mixed-QKV function export, and `qwen_gdn_linear_attn` import.
     - No CUDA/C++ rebuild and no model benchmark were run.

154. Mixed-QKV latest model-layer synthetic route smoke, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 153 wired mixed-QKV into `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`, but only static
       import/py_compile evidence existed. Before starting a heavy model
       route-hit, the model-layer branch needed a low-cost proof that the env
       actually selects the mixed-QKV branch and that compare mode returns the
       unpacked reference result.

     Harness change:
     - Added `run-model-mixed-qkv-route` to
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`.
     - The harness constructs a synthetic `QwenGatedDeltaNetAttention`
       instance via `__new__`, patches `get_forward_context` with a
       `GDNAttentionMetadata` decode-only fixture, and patches
       `causal_conv1d_update` to identity. This isolates the model-layer GDN
       route decision without loading a full model or compiling TP graphs.
     - It runs three paths on the same tensors:
       1. generic non-packed unpacked reference;
       2. mixed-QKV route without compare;
       3. mixed-QKV route with compare enabled.
     - The smoke records call counters for the model-level imported functions,
       plus strict output/state comparisons.

     Invalid first attempt:
     - Initial smoke incorrectly left `enable_packed_recurrent_decode=True` for
       the generic baseline. That baseline hit packed recurrent instead of the
       intended unpacked reference, so counters showed
       `generic={mixed:0, ref:0}` and state differed from mixed by
       `max_diff=1.9073486328125e-06`. This was a harness bug, not accepted
       evidence.
     - The harness was fixed so the generic baseline explicitly disables
       packed recurrent decode while the mixed route keeps it enabled. This
       makes the comparison match the intended item-153 fallback decision.

     Valid evidence:
     - File:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_model_mixed_qkv_route_smoke_b1_t16.json`.
     - Console route logs appeared:
       `SM70 fused sigmoid GDN mixed-QKV decode route enabled.`
       and
       `SM70 fused sigmoid mixed-QKV compare matched exactly.`
     - Counters:
       `generic={mixed:0, ref:1}`,
       `mixed={mixed:1, ref:0}`,
       `mixed_compare={mixed:1, ref:1}`.
     - `strict_ok=true` and `route_ok=true`.
     - `mixed_vs_generic`: `out max_diff=0.0`, `state max_diff=0.0`.
     - `mixed_compare_vs_generic`: `out max_diff=0.0`,
       `state max_diff=0.0`.

     Classification:
     - This proves the latest model-layer hook is wired and can select the
       mixed-QKV route behind the explicit env while preserving Type A equality
       in the synthetic decode fixture.
     - This is still not full model acceptance: no real model weights, no
       CUDA graph capture, no token/logit/perplexity gate, and no throughput
       benchmark were run.
     - Next gate remains a real Qwen3Next/GDN model route-hit with the route
       log present, followed by same-criterion quality and speed checks before
       any default-route discussion.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`
       passed.
     - `rg -n "[ \t]+$" benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` found no
       trailing whitespace.
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` passed.
     - Low-memory conda `py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`.
     - No CUDA/C++ rebuild and no model benchmark were run.

139. Old-tree `all_reduce_sum2` source path ported as a non-default
     communication capability, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The updated migration goal requires a complete SM70/V100 inventory and
       best-effort migration of old 0.0.3 communication optimizations, not just
       the already accepted AWQ/Flash short-decode route.
     - The old tree exposed `all_reduce_sum2(input_a, input_b)`, a fused
       local-add plus TP allreduce helper used by old Qwen3Next/shared-expert
       hooks.
     - Latest upstream has a richer allreduce dispatch chain, and latest
       `qwen3_next.py` now routes shared experts through `FusedMoE` rather than
       returning the old `(routed, shared)` pair. Therefore this port does not
       hook the model path or change default output; it only restores the
       low-level capability for later profiling and graph validation.

     Source changes:
     - `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cuh`: restored `packed_reduce_sum2`,
       `cross_device_reduce_sum2_1stage`, and
       `CustomAllreduce::allreduce_sum2`.
     - `csrc/custom_all_reduce.cu`, `csrc/ops.h`, and
       `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp`: declared and registered
       `_C_custom_ar::all_reduce_sum2`.
     - `vllm/_custom_ops.py`: added the Python wrapper.
     - `vllm/distributed/device_communicators/custom_all_reduce.py`: added
       `all_reduce_sum2()` and `custom_all_reduce_sum2()`.
     - `vllm/distributed/device_communicators/base_device_communicator.py` and
       `cuda_communicator.py`: added fallback semantics
       `all_reduce(input_a + input_b)`, with the custom path only used when
       the custom allreduce graph path can serve it.
     - `vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py` and
       `vllm/distributed/communication_op.py`: added the public TP facade and
       `torch.ops.vllm.all_reduce_sum2` custom op wrapper for compile support.

     Safety and default status:
     - No Qwen3Next/FusedMoE model hook was changed.
     - Outside the active CUDA graph custom-allreduce path, the communicator
       falls back to the exact semantic reference:
       `all_reduce(input_a + input_b)`.
     - During graph capture, the source path mirrors old 0.0.3 behavior by
       registering both input buffers and reducing `(a + b)` from each TP rank
       in one kernel. The required acceptance gate remains:
       `all_reduce_sum2(a, b) == all_reduce(a + b)` bitwise under TP=2/4 before
       any model hook or default route may use it.

     Validation performed:
     - No CUDA/C++ build was started in this step.
     - Low-thread Python checks passed:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check vllm/_custom_ops.py
       vllm/distributed/communication_op.py vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/base_device_communicator.py
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/cuda_communicator.py
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/custom_all_reduce.py`.
     - Low-thread conda py_compile passed for the same Python files in
       `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - Runtime import check passed for the Python custom-op registration:
       `hasattr(torch.ops.vllm, "all_reduce_sum2") == True`.
     - Current installed `_C_custom_ar` binary still reports
       `all_reduce_sum2 == False` while `top1_argmax == True`. This is
       expected because the new C++ source has not been rebuilt/installed yet.
       Do not treat runtime validation as complete until a narrow `_C` rebuild
       exposes the symbol.
     - `clang-format` is not installed in this environment; `git diff --check`
       over the touched C++/Python files passed.

     Next validation:
     - Use a narrow `_C` CMake rebuild with controlled concurrency, not a full
       editable build that pulls unrelated FlashAttention targets.
     - After rebuild: symbol check for `_C_custom_ar.all_reduce_sum2`, then
       TP=2/4 graph correctness against `all_reduce(a + b)`. Only after that
       should profiling decide whether latest Qwen3Next has a place to use this
       fused helper.

     Validation tool:
     - Added `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_all_reduce_sum2.py`.
     - It uses `torchrun`, creates a CPU/Gloo group for `CustomAllreduce`,
       captures `ca.all_reduce_sum2(a,b,out)` in a CUDA graph, replays it,
       and compares against NCCL `all_reduce(a + b)`.
     - Inputs are small integer-valued tensors so `torch.equal` is meaningful
       for both fp32 and fp16.

     Correctness result:
     - TP=2 log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_tp2_graph_correctness_20260602.log`.
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1`, sizes `1024,8192`, dtypes
       `float32,float16`.
       Result: `all_equal=true`; every case had `max_diff=0.0` and
       `mean_diff=0.0`.
     - TP=4 log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/all_reduce_sum2_tp4_graph_correctness_20260602.log`.
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3`, sizes `1024,8192`, dtypes
       `float32,float16`.
       Result: `all_equal=true`; every case had `max_diff=0.0` and
       `mean_diff=0.0`.

     Classification:
     - For the tested graph path, `all_reduce_sum2(a,b)` is Type A exact:
       it matches `all_reduce(a+b)` bitwise under TP=2 and TP=4 for fp32/fp16
       small deterministic tensors.
     - This validates the low-level custom-allreduce graph kernel/facade.
       It still does not make a model-level Qwen3Next hook accepted/default.
       Latest Qwen3Next uses `FusedMoE(shared_experts=...)`; a model hook
       should only be added after profiling proves the same local-add plus
       TP-reduce bottleneck exists in latest.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_all_reduce_sum2.py` passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_all_reduce_sum2.py` passed.
     - `git diff --check` over the touched C++/Python files passed.

141. GDN/FLA `chunk_o` SM70 schedule gate exactness/timing, 2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Complete item 138A's next step for the default-off
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE` route.
     - Keep this within the established same-implementation Type A policy:
       only the Triton autotune search space changes; arithmetic and inputs
       must remain bitwise identical.

     Harness update:
     - Updated `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` metadata capture to
       include:
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_BK`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_BV`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_WARPS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_STAGES`.
     - Static checks:
       `uv run --no-sync ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py`
       passed, and low-thread conda `py_compile` passed.

     Test shape:
     - Same Qwen-like GDN prefill shape used for earlier KKT/delta_h work:
       `q/k=[1,1024,4,128]`, `v=[1,1024,8,128]`,
       `g/beta=[1,1024,8]`, `initial_state=[1,8,128,128]`,
       `cu_seqlens=[0,1024]`.
     - dtype: q/k/v/initial_state `float16`, g/beta `float32`.
     - Commands used single V100 with low-thread/JIT guards:
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`, `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0`.

     Evidence files:
     - Default-off baseline:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_pipeline_latest_chunk_o_gate_default_qwen_shape.pt`
       and `.log`.
     - Gated schedule:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_pipeline_latest_chunk_o_sm70_sched_qwen_shape.pt`
       and `.log`.
     - Strict comparison:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_pipeline_latest_default_vs_chunk_o_sm70_sched_strict.json`
       and `.log`.

     Exactness result:
     - Gated run metadata shows `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE=1`; default
       run has the env unset.
     - `inputs_ok=true`, `strict_ok=true`.
     - Representative outputs:
       `out`, `final_state`, `stage_out`, `stage_g_cumsum`, `stage_A_kkt`,
       `stage_h`, and `stage_v_new` all have
       `torch_equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_different=0`.
     - Therefore the `chunk_o` schedule gate is Type A exact on this
       Qwen-like prefill case.

     Timing result:
     - Default-off full GDN op timing: average `0.0037917118 s` over
       5 repeats after 1 warmup.
     - Gated `chunk_o` schedule timing: average `0.0038537914 s` over
       5 repeats after 1 warmup.
     - This is essentially runtime-neutral/slightly slower on the measured
       case. The gate should not be counted as decode-speed recovery.

     Classification:
     - `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE` is now migrated and op-level
       strict-safe for the tested Qwen-like cu_seqlens prefill case.
     - Keep it default-off: it is useful as a compile/autotune search-space
       reduction and future tuning knob, but it has no demonstrated speed gain
       on this shape.
     - Next GDN/FLA speed work should continue to prioritize the already
       faster-but-not-default `delta_h` route and broader model determinism,
       not defaulting `chunk_o` alone.

138A. 0.0.3 -> latest SM70 migration inventory checkpoint and first GDN
     chunk-output schedule port, 2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Continue the broader goal: migrate all useful 0.0.3 SM70/V100 speed
       work into latest vLLM in decoupled, quality-gated form, without
       repeating already classified Flash/AWQ/MoE routes.
     - Respect the compile-memory rule for this phase: no CUDA/C++ rebuild was
       started. Static checks and any later Triton JIT must run with low thread
       envs such as `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`,
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

     Inventory finding:
     - The AWQ short no-MTP `1k/256` pure-decode lane is not the current
       missing piece: item 136 measured latest full-Flash graph at
       `75.313 tok/s`, which is `97.9-98.3%` of the old same-criterion
       `76.64-76.90 tok/s` baseline. Do not re-run this as if unknown.
     - Flash-V100 remains a required migration lane, but production defaulting
       is still blocked by the documented model-quality/long-context gates.
       Selecting Flash-V100 must not silently become Triton; mixed Triton
       prefill + Flash decode remains a diagnostic fallback, not the target
       final route.
     - Old 0.0.3 has additional SM70 routes not fully migrated or not fully
       classified in latest:
       `all_reduce_sum2` shared-expert add+allreduce fusion, old DFlash/MTP
       debug/spec paths, Qwen3Next shared-MoE overlap knobs, fused sigmoid/GDN
       schedule knobs, FP8/AWQ compact MoE gates, and GDN/FLA autotune
       schedules.
     - Several old routes are intentionally not default candidates yet:
       compact/gated FP8 MoE has known nonzero diff risk; DFlash/MTP is outside
       the current no-MTP migration priority; old mixed-QKV GDN decode may be
       partially superseded by latest `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` packed
       recurrent decode and must be classified before transplanting code.

     Concrete source diff selected:
     - Old `1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/chunk_o.py`
       already had a V100-specific compact autotune space:
       `BK/BV=[32,64]`, `WARPS=[4,8]`, `STAGES=[2]`, tunable through
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_*`.
     - Latest `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/chunk_o.py` still used the
       generic upstream search space for this kernel:
       `BKV_LIST`, `NUM_WARPS`, and stages `[2,3,4]`.
     - This is a good first migration item because it changes only Triton launch
       meta-parameters, not the arithmetic body of the kernel. It should be an
       A/layout-equivalent path after fixed input alignment; any stable nonzero
       diff must be treated as a bug in the validation setup or schedule
       interaction, not accepted as model noise.

     Code change:
     - Added a default-off gate in latest:
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE=1`.
     - Added tunables:
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_BK`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_BV`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_WARPS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_STAGES`.
     - The default path remains latest upstream behavior when the gate is off.
       This keeps the migration decoupled and prevents unvalidated GDN schedule
       changes from silently affecting model output.

     Files changed:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/chunk_o.py`
     - `vllm/envs.py`

     Validation performed:
     - No CUDA/C++ build was triggered.
     - `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1 OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1
       NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2
       CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/chunk_o.py vllm/envs.py` passed.
     - Same low-thread env with
       `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/chunk_o.py vllm/envs.py` passed.

     Status:
     - `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE` is migrated but not accepted/default.
       Next acceptance step is a focused GDN exactness/timing run using the
       existing `benchmark_sm70_gdn_exactness.py` harness: baseline save,
       gated replay with identical inputs, strict stage comparison, then timing
       under the same low-thread/JIT controls. Only after strict equality and
       speed evidence should this gate be considered for default-on SM70.
