       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`.
     - The run loaded AOT compile artifacts from cache:
       `Directly load the compiled graph(s) for compile range (1, 1024)`;
       `torch.compile took 11.03 s in total`.
     - Flash route summary on all ranks:
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 64, "metadata_none_zero_output": 160,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 48}`.
     - `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0` and
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`; therefore this is not the
       historical Triton-prefill + Flash-decode mixed default.
     - The `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` Triton/FLA log is the separate GDN path,
       not an attention-backend fallback.

     Metrics:
     - `load_seconds=90.09` on cached compile startup.
     - Repeats produced identical token hash:
       `ff89250a51b95d29fb52a1d803b8f43e15fa094b86437b4a438e1f55481c9c2e`.
     - `prefill_time`: `0.4582 s`, `0.4599 s`; mean `0.4591 s`.
     - `first_token_latency`: `0.4605 s`, `0.4623 s`; mean `0.4614 s`.
     - `decode_time`: `3.3862 s`, `3.3855 s`; mean `3.3859 s`.
     - Pure steady decode TPS: `75.306`, `75.320`; mean `75.313 tok/s`.
     - End-to-end output TPS including first token/prefill: mean
       `66.538 tok/s`.
     - `tpot_seconds` mean: `0.013278 s`.

     Classification:
     - Valid latest-vLLM full-Flash route and pure-decode measurement for the
       key AWQ `1k/256` no-MTP case.
     - Important reconciliation with earlier source-of-truth rows: the user's
       remembered "`80 tok/s`" was later narrowed by item 104 / the Performance
       matrix to old-tree same-criterion `76.64-76.90 tok/s` for this short
       `1k/256`口径. Using that exact old baseline, this run is
       `97.9-98.3%` of 0.0.3 and satisfies the `>=95%` pure-decode migration
       gate. Do not regress this route while fixing remaining init/prefill
       issues.
     - Remaining work for this lane is therefore not the short `1k/256` pure
       decode baseline itself; it is startup/warmup cost, prefill/TTFT, long
       context, quality gates, and the broader GDN/FLA / custom allreduce /
       MoE / FP8 migration scope.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`
       passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.

137. Full-Flash AWQ graph route-hit with bounded compile range, 2026-06-01.

     Purpose:
     - Continue item 136 without repeating the same failed route. The new
       question was whether the `(1025, 8192)` compile range came from the
       Flash-V100 backend or from latest vLLM default scheduler sizing, and
       whether a bounded route-hit can reach token generation under CUDA graph.

     Source finding:
     - `EngineArgs.get_batch_defaults()` defaults `UsageContext.LLM_CLASS` to
       `max_num_batched_tokens=8192` on non-H100/H200 GPUs.
     - `VllmConfig._set_compile_ranges()` always adds
       `scheduler_config.max_num_batched_tokens` as the upper compile range.
       Therefore item 136's `(1025, 8192)` range was a latest-vLLM scheduler
       default, not a Flash-V100 arithmetic/backend requirement.

     Harness fix:
     - Added explicit `--max-num-batched-tokens` and `--max-num-seqs` options
       to `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`.
     - These are still passed through normal `LLM(**kwargs)`; no production
       code path or benchmark reference logic was changed.

     Command shape:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ`, TP4, CUDA graph enabled
       (`enforce_eager=False`), `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `input_len=64`,
       `output_len=4`, `max_model_len=1024`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`, `max_num_seqs=1`.
     - Flash policy env:
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=1`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ROUTE_SUMMARY=1`.
     - Files:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_full_flash_graph_routehit_i64_o4_maxbt1024_20260601.log`
       and
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_full_flash_graph_routehit_i64_o4_maxbt1024_20260601.json`.

     Result:
     - The engine config used `compile_ranges_endpoints=[1024]`,
       `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1, 2]`, and
       `max_cudagraph_capture_size=2`.
     - The log only compiled `compile range (1, 1024)`; the unwanted
       `(1025, 8192)` range did not appear.
     - The run reached token generation and wrote JSON. Token IDs:
       `[369, 1429, 310, 9640]`, hash
       `eb8f2dedc874bebf0afe4f78234e10384a294cbdf065e3d57f76afbb234c3263`.
     - Load/startup was still very high: `load_seconds=620.11`.
       `torch.compile took 74.69 s`, followed by several
       `No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds`
       messages before the run recovered. This is startup/compile overhead,
       not steady-state decode performance.

     Route evidence:
     - All four TP workers selected `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
     - Rank 0 logged `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`.
     - Rank 0 logged `SM70 LM head top1 layout prepared` and later
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`.
     - CUDA graph capture happened for `mixed prefill-decode, PIECEWISE` and
       `decode, FULL`.
     - Flash-V100 route summary on every rank:
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 64, "metadata_none_zero_output": 160,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 16}`.
     - This proves CUDA-graph execution did not silently become Triton
       attention for this bounded route-hit. The separate
       `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` Triton/FLA log is a GDN path, not a
       Flash-attention fallback; it remains part of the broader migration
       scope.

     Classification:
     - This is valid route-hit evidence for full Flash-V100 prefill + scalar
       paged Flash decode under CUDA graph with implicit Triton fallback
       disabled.
     - It is not a throughput baseline. `output_tps=0.865` is dominated by a
       4-token request and cold post-load/JIT effects; do not compare it with
       the `1k/256` AWQ no-MTP decode baseline.
     - Remaining work for this lane: warm/cached startup or a proper 1k/256
       run with enough warmup to remove cold JIT spikes, then compare pure
       decode TPS against the old 0.0.3 baseline.

     Checks:
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`
       passed.
     - `conda run --live-stream -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.

200. Model-token quality harness now supports bounded compile and request
     timing evidence, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 136 and 137 showed that latest vLLM can silently widen compile
       ranges through the LLM-class scheduler default
       `max_num_batched_tokens=8192`, and item 136 needed request metrics to
       separate prefill/TTFT from pure steady decode.
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` already had
       `--max-num-batched-tokens`, `--max-num-seqs`, default-on request
       metrics, and separate `prefill_time` / `decode_time` fields.
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` did not, so future
       token/logprob/perplexity quality checks could accidentally re-open the
       same broad compile range or lose the timing evidence needed to compare
       quality and speed in one artifact.

     Source change:
     - Added `--max-num-batched-tokens`, `--max-num-seqs`, and
       `--disable-log-stats` to `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`, passed
       through normal `LLM(**kwargs)`.
     - Added per-request metrics serialization to model-token JSON:
       `first_token_latency`, `queued_time`, `prefill_time`, `decode_time`,
       `inference_time`, `steady_decode_tokens`, `steady_decode_tps`,
       `tpot_seconds`, and raw vLLM timestamps.
     - Default behavior keeps request metrics enabled for this SM70 harness;
       `--disable-log-stats` is available only when a caller explicitly wants
       to suppress them.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change production routing,
       attention selection, CUDA graph capture policy, GEMM math, logits, or
       generated tokens.
     - Future model-level quality artifacts for Flash-V100, TurboMind AWQ/FP8,
       MoE, GDN/FLA, or custom allreduce should use these flags when a bounded
       compile range is needed, instead of repeating item 136's wide default
       compile path.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Checks were run with
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` passed.
     - Low-memory `python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` passed.
     - No CUDA/C++ compile, model load, GPU benchmark, or route-hit run was
       executed for this checkpoint.

201. Flash-V100 SM70 selector policy is now covered by a lightweight
     regression test, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The migration goal requires that selecting/defaulting
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100` on V100 does not silently become Triton. Items 137 and
       138 already gave runtime route-hit evidence for bounded CUDA-graph
       AWQ runs, but there was no small source-level regression protecting the
       selector priority itself.
     - The current source default is `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=1` in
       `vllm/envs.py`, and `vllm/platforms/cuda.py` prioritizes
       `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100` only for exact SM70
       `DeviceCapability(7, 0)`.

     Source change:
     - Added
       `tests/v1/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_policy.py`.
     - The test stubs `vllm._C` and `_C_stable_libtorch` so the selector logic
       can be checked without importing the local CUDA extension. It also
       pins `sys.path` to this source tree because running with
       `--confcutdir` bypasses the repo-level pytest path setup.
     - Covered cases:
       1. unset `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100` on SM70 defaults to
          `[FLASH_ATTN_V100, TRITON_ATTN, ...]`;
       2. `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=0` restores the upstream-style
          `[FLASH_ATTN, FLASHINFER, TRITON_ATTN, ...]` priority;
       3. `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=1` does not select Flash-V100 on
          non-SM70 capability such as `(7, 5)`.

     Classification:
     - Regression protection for routing policy only. It does not validate
       Flash-V100 kernel math, CUDA graph replay, model output quality, or
       throughput. Those remain governed by the numeric/model-level gates.
     - This makes the current default-on selector state explicit and
       supersedes older historical text that described the selector as
       default-off during initial migration.

     Local test environment note:
     - A normal repo-root pytest invocation currently fails before this test
       executes because the local `vllm/_C.abi3.so` reports an undefined Torch
       ABI symbol:
       `_ZN3c104cuda29c10_cuda_check_implementationEiPKcS2_jb`.
     - Therefore the selector test was run with
       `--confcutdir=tests/v1/attention` plus internal `_C` stubs. This is a
       Python selector-policy check, not a substitute for rebuilding the CUDA
       extension before full test suites or model runs.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Checks used
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       tests/v1/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_policy.py` passed.
     - Low-memory `python -m py_compile
       tests/v1/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_policy.py` passed.
     - Low-memory isolated pytest passed:
       `uv run --no-sync pytest -q --confcutdir=tests/v1/attention
       tests/v1/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_policy.py`
       -> `3 passed in 1.83s`.
     - No CUDA/C++ compile, model load, GPU benchmark, or route-hit run was
       executed for this checkpoint.

202. Benchmark JSON now records effective Flash-V100 policy defaults,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 201 made the current selector default explicit:
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100` defaults to enabled for exact SM70. However,
       `env` fields in benchmark JSON only include variables that are present
       in the process environment. When the default-on selector is left unset,
       old artifacts could be misread as "Flash-V100 selector was not active."
     - The same ambiguity applies to the Flash fallback policy:
       unset `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON` means false,
       unset `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK` means false, and
       unset `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED` means true.

     Source change:
     - Added `sm70_attention_policy` to both:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - The new JSON field records raw env values plus effective defaults for:
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK`, and
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED`.
     - It also records the expected SM70 backend priority list and a
       `full_flash_default_policy` boolean. With all defaults unset, this
       boolean is true: Flash-V100 selector enabled, Triton prefill disabled,
       implicit Triton fallback disabled, scalar paged decode enabled.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change production routing,
       attention selection, CUDA graph capture, kernel math, logits, tokens, or
       performance.
     - Runtime acceptance still requires route logs or
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ROUTE_SUMMARY` evidence proving the backend actually
       hit Flash prefill/decode paths. The new field only prevents default env
       ambiguity in future artifacts.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Checks used
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,

       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py
       tests/v1/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_policy.py` passed.
     - Low-memory `python -m py_compile

203. Benchmark JSON now records effective SM70 MoE sum2 policy, 2026-06-02.

204. Benchmark JSON now records effective SM70 greedy/top1 sampling policy,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 125-128 migrated the old-tree pure-greedy shortcut, LM-head top1
       epilogue, and experimental custom top1 allreduce. Item 127 showed the
       accepted production CUDA-graph AWQ path back in the expected pure-decode
       speed band, but future benchmark JSON still only preserved raw env
       variables. If the default-on greedy/top1 gates were left unset, an
       artifact could again be misread as "not enabled."
     - This is especially important because several related gates have
       different default status:
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1_TC=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=0`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_ENABLE_LM_HEAD_FASTPATH=0`.

     Source change:
     - Added `sm70_sampling_policy` to both:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - The new JSON field records raw/effective values for:
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH_TRACE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1_TC`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR`,
       `VLLM_SM70_ENABLE_LM_HEAD_FASTPATH`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_ENABLE_DENSE_F16_FASTPATH`.
     - It also records `pure_greedy_top1_default_policy` and the route-hit
       oracle: accepted default greedy decode needs
       `SM70 LM head top1 layout prepared` plus
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`; the full
       `SM70 dense fp16 fast path enabled for LM head` log is expected only
       when the explicit full LM-head fast path gate is enabled. Custom top1
       allreduce remains experimental/default-off.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change GPU model runner
       gating, LM-head math, sampler behavior, CUDA graph behavior, generated
       tokens, or speed.
     - Runtime acceptance still requires route logs proving the greedy fast
       path and LM-head top1 actually hit. The JSON field only makes effective
       defaults visible in future artifacts.

       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,

205. Source inventory refreshed after sampling-policy evidence plumbing,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 204 added new benchmark JSON policy fields for greedy/top1
       sampling gates. The source inventory should be refreshed after this
       kind of harness change so future work starts from current counts instead
       of the earlier item-189 snapshot.
     - While reviewing the old-only keyword file list, the FP8 KV/cache source
       group was checked specifically because the active goal includes FP8 KV
       cache and long-context behavior.

     Static source finding:
     - Old `csrc/cache_kernels.cu` and `csrc/cache_kernels_fused.cu` are not
       missing runtime features in latest. The latest tree has the equivalent
       cache kernels under `csrc/libtorch_stable/cache_kernels.cu` and
       `csrc/libtorch_stable/cache_kernels_fused.cu`.
     - The latest stable files still expose the relevant cache operations:
       `reshape_and_cache`, `reshape_and_cache_flash`, `convert_fp8`,
       `gather_and_maybe_dequant_cache`, `cp_gather_cache`,
       `cp_gather_and_upconvert_fp8_kv_cache`, and
       `cp_gather_indexer_k_quant_cache`.
     - The latest cache path also contains newer upstream coverage such as
       NVFP4, `fp8_ds_mla`, and stable-libtorch tensor APIs. Therefore this
       file group remains classified as
       `closed-upstream-moved-libtorch-stable`, not a new FP8 KV migration
       gap.

     Refreshed evidence:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_sampling_policy.json`.
     - Counts:
       old files `2246`, latest files `2465`;
       old env vars `383`, latest env vars `500`;
       old runtime env vars `367`, latest runtime env vars `458`;
       old keyword files `225`, latest keyword files `286`;
       old torch ops `291`, latest torch ops `399`.
     - Diff classification:
       `env_vars.old_only=0`;
       `runtime_env_vars.old_only=27`,
       `old_only_runtime_env_classification.classified=27`,
       `unclassified=0`;
       `keyword_files.old_only=67`,
       `old_only_keyword_file_classification.classified=67`,
       `unclassified=0`;
       `torch_ops.old_only=28`,
       `old_only_torch_op_classification.classified=28`,
       `unclassified=0`.

     Classification:
     - Inventory/tooling checkpoint only. It changes no runtime policy, kernel,
       backend selection, model output, or speed.
     - This confirms that the inventory dimensions currently have no
       unclassified old-only env, runtime-env, keyword-file, or torch-op names.
       It does not by itself accept Flash-V100, FP8 KV, MoE, GDN/FLA, or
       custom allreduce performance; those still need their route-specific
       quality and speed gates.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Inventory and checks used

206. Benchmark JSON now records effective SM70 TurboMind dense policy,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 202-204 made attention, `sum2` MoE, and greedy/top1 sampling
       policy visible in future benchmark JSON. The core AWQ/FP8/F16
       TurboMind dense policy was still only implicit in raw env dumps.
     - This matters because the accepted default state intentionally mixes
       default-on safe routes and default-off experimental routes:
       AWQ TurboMind dense `on`, FP8 TurboMind dense `on`, FP8 dequant
       fallback `on`, AWQ warmup `on`, AWQ/FP8 batched MoE `off`, and F16
       dense fast path `off`.

     Source change:
     - Added `sm70_turbomind_policy` to both:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - The new JSON field records raw/effective values for:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_M`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_WARMUP_MAX_MOE_TOKENS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GEMM_LUT_PATH`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_REUSE_IMPORTED_CACHE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_DENSE_TUNE_MAX_M`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_TUNE_MAX_TOKENS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISABLE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`,
       `VLLM_SM70_ENABLE_DENSE_F16_FASTPATH`,
       `VLLM_SM70_F16_DENSE_ALLOWLIST`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_DENSE_ALLOWLIST`,
       `VLLM_SM70_F16_DENSE_MAX_M`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_DENSE_CUDAGRAPH_CAPTURE`.
     - It also records `accepted_dense_default_policy` and the route-hit
       oracle: accepted dense evidence needs logs such as
       `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`, and when warmup is
       relevant `SM70 AWQ warmup finished`.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change quantization,
       weight preparation, C++ dispatch, CUDA graph capture, model output, or
       speed.
     - The policy field cannot by itself prove route-hit. Future AWQ/FP8
       model runs still need runtime logs plus the existing token/logit and
       pure-decode/prefill timing evidence.
     - Batched MoE and F16 dense fast paths remain experimental/default-off.
       They cannot count toward accepted performance unless their dedicated
       numeric and model-level gates pass.

213. Model-level quality pass gate added to the SM70 verifier, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The active quality rule requires deterministic token equality plus
       bounded logits/logprob/perplexity evidence before any SM70 route can be
       default-accepted. `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` already writes this
       as `model_quality_gate`, but the general artifact verifier did not yet
       enforce it.
     - Future default-enable evidence should fail mechanically if the compare
       artifact is `B-pending`, `model-fail`, missing sampler argmax evidence,
       missing numeric bounds, or has failed/pending quality evidence.

     Source change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` with
       `--require-model-quality-pass`.
     - The new gate accepts `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` compare-mode JSON
       where SM70 policy blocks live under `left_meta` and `right_meta` rather
       than at the top level.
     - It requires:
       `model_quality_gate.label=model-pass`,
       `model_quality_gate.default_acceptance=model-level gate passed`,
       empty `failed_evidence`, empty `pending_evidence`,
       `checks.token_equal=true`,
       `checks.sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=true`, configured numeric
       bounds, and every bounded metric value within its bound.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only acceptance guard. It does not change model generation,
       logits, sampler behavior, kernels, CUDA graph behavior, tokens, or speed.
     - Passing this gate proves only that a model-token compare artifact meets
       the active model-quality policy. It still must be combined with route
       logs, full Flash/TurboMind/MoE gates as applicable, and same-criterion
       decode/prefill timing before counting as default-route acceptance.
     - Existing historical compare artifacts that are `B-pending` or
       `model-fail` should continue to fail this verifier gate and remain
       non-default.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_model_quality_verifier.json`.
     - Counts were unchanged from item 212:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;

212. AWQ/FP8 MoE safe-route policy and verifier gates added, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The accepted SM70 MoE mainline is the graph-safe dense-stage route plus
       single-token active-expert route. The old compact/router/gated family
       remains paused for quality risk, and batched MoE remains experimental.
     - Future AWQ/FP8 MoE artifacts must prove that they hit the safe route
       rather than only proving that the model loaded an SM70 MoE backend or
       that a default-off batched/compact experiment was enabled.

     Source change:
     - Added `accepted_awq_moe_default_policy` and
       `accepted_fp8_moe_default_policy` to `sm70_turbomind_policy` in both:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - Extended `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` with:
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route` and `--require-fp8-moe-safe-route`.
     - The AWQ gate requires:
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.accepted_awq_moe_default_policy=true`,
       `awq_moe_disable_effective=false`,
       `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=false`, and logs:
       `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`, and
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`.
     - The FP8 gate requires:
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.accepted_fp8_moe_default_policy=true`,
       `fp8_moe_batched_gemm_effective=false`, and logs:
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`, and
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only policy/evidence plumbing. It does not change MoE kernels,
       routing math, quantization, CUDA graph behavior, logits, tokens, or
       speed.
     - Passing these gates proves policy plus route-hit evidence for the safe
       AWQ/FP8 MoE route. It still must be combined with model-level token/
       logit/perplexity gates and same-criterion prefill/pure-decode timing.
     - A benchmark artifact that hits only `SM70 AWQ/FP8 MoE TurboMind
       per-expert dense path enabled` but lacks dense-stage or single-token
       active-expert logs is not enough to prove the production prefill+decode
       safe route.

211. AWQ/FP8 TurboMind dense route-hit gates added to the SM70 verifier,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - AWQ/FP8 TurboMind dense is one of the already-migrated mainline SM70
       speed paths. Policy JSON alone cannot prove the model actually reached
       the dense TurboMind kernels during a benchmark.
     - Future Qwen3.6-AWQ and Qwen3.6-FP8 artifacts must mechanically show the
       corresponding dense route log before they can count toward speed or
       quality acceptance.

     Source change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` with:
       `--require-turbomind-default-policy`,
       `--require-awq-turbomind-dense`, and
       `--require-fp8-turbomind-dense`.
     - `--require-turbomind-default-policy` checks
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.accepted_dense_default_policy=true`.
     - `--require-awq-turbomind-dense` checks
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.awq_turbomind_effective=true` and requires the
       runtime log `SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled`.
     - `--require-fp8-turbomind-dense` checks
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.fp8_turbomind_effective=true`,
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.fp8_dequant_fallback_effective=true`, and
       requires the runtime log
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only acceptance guard. It does not change TurboMind C++/CUDA
       kernels, quantization code, weight preparation, model output, CUDA graph
       behavior, or speed.
     - Passing these gates proves only policy plus dense route-hit evidence for
       the chosen quantization family. It still must be combined with full
       Flash/Triton route gates as appropriate, decode timing, Type A/B numeric
       evidence, model-quality checks, and same-criterion throughput.
     - A benchmark that lacks the AWQ or FP8 dense route log must not be used
       as proof that the migrated TurboMind dense path restored old-tree speed.

210. Decode timing evidence gate added to the SM70 verifier, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The active performance rule requires pure decode to be recorded
       separately from TTFT/prefill and overall output TPS. Earlier route-hit
       smokes could reach the target backend but still lacked usable
       `request_metrics`, which made prefill time and steady decode TPS
       ambiguous.
     - Future AWQ/FP8 no-MTP, MTP, greedy, and official-sampling speed
       artifacts need a mechanical way to reject JSON files where `output_tps`
       is the only speed number.

     Source change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` with
       `--require-decode-timing`.
     - The new gate expects `benchmark_sm70_decode.py`-style evidence:
       summary values and per-repeat request metrics for:
       `output_tps`, `first_token_latency`, `prefill_time`, `decode_time`,
       `steady_decode_tps`, and `tpot_seconds`.
     - If `cases` are present, every case must also contain the same summary
       and repeat-level timing evidence. This prevents a multi-case run from
       passing because only the first/top-level case had metrics.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only acceptance guard. It does not change benchmark execution,
       vLLM runtime behavior, kernels, tokens, logits, or speed.
     - Passing `--require-decode-timing` is necessary before using a decode
       JSON as same-criterion performance evidence. It still does not prove
       route-hit or quality by itself; use it together with route logs,
       policy checks, Type A/B numeric evidence, and model-quality gates.
     - Artifacts generated with request metrics disabled, missing repeat
       metrics, or only overall `output_tps` must not be counted toward the
       0.0.3 same-口径 decode baseline.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_decode_timing_verifier.json`.
     - Counts were unchanged from item 209:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `env_vars.old_only=0`;
       `runtime_env_vars.old_only=27`, all `27` classified;
       `keyword_files.old_only=67`, all `67` classified;
       `torch_ops.old_only=28`, all `28` classified.

208. Added a lightweight SM70 benchmark artifact verifier, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 202-207 made benchmark JSON record the effective/default SM70
       policy state for attention, MoE, sampling, TurboMind, GDN/FLA, and
       tuning. Those fields are necessary evidence, but they are not enough
       to prove runtime route-hit.
     - Future speed/quality artifacts need a mechanical guard so a run cannot
       be accepted while silently missing one policy block, a required JSON
       value, or a route-hit log string.

     Source change:
     - Added `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`.
     - The verifier is dependency-free: it reads benchmark JSON and optional
       logs without importing vLLM, loading models, touching CUDA, or starting
       compilation.
     - By default it requires these top-level policy objects:
       `sm70_tune_policy`, `sm70_turbomind_policy`,
       `sm70_attention_policy`, `sm70_gdn_fla_policy`, `sm70_moe_policy`,
       and `sm70_sampling_policy`.
     - It also supports:
       - `--expect-json PATH=VALUE` for exact JSON value checks, with VALUE
         parsed as JSON when possible.
       - `--expect-log TEXT` for literal runtime route-hit log checks.
       - `--log PATH` repeated for multiple logs.
       - `--out PATH` to save the verification summary JSON.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only verification tooling. It does not change any runtime
       selector, kernel, CUDA graph behavior, logits, tokens, or speed.
     - Passing this verifier is still not a complete acceptance gate. It only
       proves that the artifact preserved the required policy/log evidence.
       Final acceptance still needs the existing Type A/B numeric classification,
       token/logit/perplexity quality, production CUDA-graph route-hit, and
       same-criterion prefill/pure-decode throughput.
     - Failing the verifier should block using that artifact as route-hit or
       speed evidence until the missing policy/value/log is explained or the
       benchmark is rerun with the correct evidence collection enabled.

     Example use:
     - `python benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --json result.json
       --log server.log
       --expect-json sm70_attention_policy.selector_enabled_effective=true
       --expect-log "SM70 AWQ TurboMind dense path enabled"`

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_artifact_verifier.json`.
     - Counts:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `env_vars.old_only=0`;
       `runtime_env_vars.old_only=27`, all `27` classified;
       `keyword_files.old_only=67`, all `67` classified;
       `torch_ops.old_only=28`, all `28` classified.
     - The new verifier adds one latest-only SM70 keyword file, and no old-only
       migration gap was introduced.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Checks used
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,

209. Full Flash-V100 artifact gate added to the SM70 verifier, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The updated migration rule says `FLASH_ATTN_V100` cannot be accepted as
       a Triton-prefill plus Flash-decode mixed route. If a benchmark artifact
       is used as final Flash-V100 evidence, it must mechanically prove full
       Flash policy and reject fallback logs.
     - This protects against a repeated historical failure mode: route logs
       or benchmark JSON showing `FLASH_ATTN_V100` selection while the measured
       work silently includes explicit Triton prefill or decode fallback.

     Source change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` with:
       - `--require-full-flash-v100`;
       - `--reject-log TEXT`.
     - The full-Flash preset requires exact JSON policy values:
       `sm70_attention_policy.selector_enabled_effective=true`,
       `prefill_use_triton_effective=false`,
       `allow_triton_fallback_effective=false`,
       `decode_scalar_paged_effective=true`, and
       `full_flash_default_policy=true`.
     - It also requires runtime log text containing
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active` and `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode`.
     - It rejects fallback/mixed-route log text including:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill uses explicit Triton diagnostic fallback`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode fallback to Triton`, and
       `set VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=1`.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only acceptance guard. It does not change FlashAttention-V100
       backend code, selectors, kernels, CUDA graph behavior, tokens, logits,
       or speed.
     - Passing `--require-full-flash-v100` is necessary evidence for a future
       full Flash-V100 benchmark artifact, but still not sufficient for final
       default enablement. Numeric Type A/B classification, model-level
       quality, production CUDA-graph route-hit, and same-criterion throughput
       remain required.
     - Artifacts from explicit diagnostic fallback runs can still be recorded,
       but they must not pass this gate and must not count as final
       Flash-V100 performance.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_full_flash_verifier.json`.
     - Counts were unchanged from item 208:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `env_vars.old_only=0`;
       `runtime_env_vars.old_only=27`, all `27` classified;
       `keyword_files.old_only=67`, all `67` classified;
       `torch_ops.old_only=28`, all `28` classified.

       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

207. Benchmark JSON now records effective SM70 GDN/FLA policy,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - GDN/FLA is a first-class migration lane in the active goal: packed
       recurrent decode, fused sigmoid mixed-QKV, KKT/delta-H/chunk-O
       schedules, and Qwen3Next shared-MoE overlap all have separate safety
       status in this ledger.
     - Items 148-154 and 141 show that several low-level GDN/FLA routes are
       migrated but still default-off, or accepted only at op/mock level.
       Future model-token and decode artifacts must preserve whether those
       routes were actually enabled.

     Source change:
     - Added `sm70_gdn_fla_policy` to both:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - The tracked-env filter now also records `FLA_*`,
       `VLLM_QWEN3NEXT_*`, and `VLLM_QWEN3_NEXT_*` variables.
     - The new JSON field records raw/effective values for:
       `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_BV`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_WARPS`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_STAGES`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_TARGET_WAVES`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLA_BV_CANDIDATES`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_KKT_*`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DELTA_H_*`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_*`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING_*`,
       `VLLM_QWEN3_NEXT_FUSED_SIGMOID_GATING`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FUSED_SIGMOID_MIXED_QKV_COMPARE`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_EMPTY_CORE_OUT`,
       `VLLM_QWEN3NEXT_ENABLE_SHARED_MOE_OVERLAP`,
       `VLLM_SM70_DISABLE_QWEN3NEXT_SHARED_MOE_OVERLAP`,
       `FLA_USE_FAST_OPS`,
       `FLA_COMPILER_MODE`,
       `FLA_USE_CUDA_GRAPH`, and
       `FLA_USE_TMA`.
     - It also records `accepted_gdn_fla_default_policy`: packed recurrent
       decode is on, while SM70 schedule experiments, mixed-QKV, compare mode,
       empty-core output, and Qwen3Next overlap override/disable gates are off.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only evidence plumbing. It does not change GDN/FLA kernels,
       Qwen3Next model routing, recurrent decode math, CUDA graph behavior,
       logits, tokens, or speed.
     - The policy field cannot prove route-hit. Future mixed-QKV acceptance
       still needs runtime logs such as
       `SM70 fused sigmoid GDN mixed-QKV decode route enabled`, compare-mode
       exactness evidence when enabled, and model-level token/logit/perplexity
       quality plus throughput.
     - Default-off GDN/FLA schedule gates remain unaccepted unless their
       dedicated Type A/B numeric and model-level gates pass.

214. Compare-artifact JSON expectations now check both `left_meta` and
     `right_meta`, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Model-level ON-vs-OFF quality artifacts are compare artifacts. Their
       route and policy metadata is intentionally stored under `left_meta` and
       `right_meta`, while single-run decode artifacts store the same policy
       blocks at the top level.
     - The verifier already accepted required policies from compare metadata,
       but exact `--expect-json` checks still looked only at the top level.
       That made combined gates such as `--require-model-quality-pass` plus
       `--require-full-flash-v100`, AWQ/FP8 TurboMind dense gates, or
       AWQ/FP8 MoE safe-route gates unsuitable for compare artifacts.
     - This matters for the active goal because a final default-enable decision
       needs quality evidence and route-hit policy evidence in the same
       artifact, not separate files that can drift.

     Source change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`
       `_evaluate_json_expectation()`:
       - first checks the requested JSON path at the top level;
       - if absent, checks the same path inside both `left_meta` and
         `right_meta`;
       - compare-meta matching passes only when both sides equal the expected
         value.
     - Each JSON expectation result now records `location` as `top-level`,
       `compare-meta`, or `null`.
     - This preserves single-run behavior while making compare artifacts strict
       enough for combined quality and route gates.

     Classification:
     - Harness-only acceptance guard. It does not change any vLLM runtime
       selector, FlashAttention/Triton path, TurboMind path, MoE path, CUDA
       graph behavior, kernel math, logits, tokens, or speed.
     - The gate is intentionally stricter for compare artifacts: if one side
       says full Flash/TurboMind/safe MoE is enabled and the other side does
       not, the expectation fails. This prevents accepting an ON-vs-OFF model
       quality result where only one run actually used the intended SM70 route.
     - It still does not replace numeric Type A/B exactness classification,
       real route-hit logs, CUDA-graph production checks, same-criterion
       decode/prefill timing, or model-token/logit/perplexity quality.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_compare_meta_verifier.json`.
     - Counts:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `env_vars.old_only=0`, `env_vars.latest_only=117`,
       `env_vars.common=383`;
       `runtime_env_vars.old_only=27`, all `27` classified,
       `runtime_env_vars.latest_only=118`, `runtime_env_vars.common=340`;
       `keyword_files.old_only=67`, all `67` classified,
       `keyword_files.latest_only=129`, `keyword_files.common=158`;
       `torch_ops.old_only=28`, all `28` classified,
       `torch_ops.latest_only=136`, `torch_ops.common=263`.
     - No new old-only migration gap was introduced by the verifier update.

     Memory/compile guard:

215. Source inventory now has a mechanical "no silent old-only gap" gate,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The active goal explicitly requires that every SM70/V100 acceleration,
       kernel, backend, env gate, and quality/memory fix from 0.0.3 is either
       migrated, replaced by upstream, paused as unsafe, rejected with evidence,
       or left as a documented future item.
     - `benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` already produced old-only
       classifications, but the artifact did not expose a single pass/fail
       field, and `verify_sm70_artifact.py` could not reject an inventory with
       unclassified old-only items.
     - This created a maintenance risk: after upstream rebases or new source
       scans, an old-only env/op/file could appear in the raw diff and be
       missed unless a human inspected the JSON carefully.

     Source change:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py` now emits
       `inventory_gate`.
     - `inventory_gate.complete=true` only when:
       - broad old-only `env_vars` is empty;
       - old-only runtime env classifications have zero unclassified entries;
       - old-only keyword-file classifications have zero unclassified entries;
       - old-only custom torch-op classifications have zero unclassified
         entries.
     - The script's stdout summary now includes
       `inventory_complete`, `old_only_env_vars`,
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified`,
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified`, and
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified`.
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` now supports
       `--require-inventory-complete`. This checks `inventory_gate` directly
       and skips default benchmark policy-block requirements because source
       inventory artifacts are not runtime route artifacts.

     Classification:
     - Harness/inventory-only. It does not change runtime selectors, kernels,
       FlashAttention, TurboMind, MoE, CUDA graph behavior, logits, tokens, or
       speed.
     - Passing this gate proves only that the current static inventory has no
       unclassified old-only migration item in the tracked dimensions. It does
       not prove that a classified route is safe, fast, compiled, route-hit, or
       default-accepted.
     - Future source refreshes must pass this gate before their artifact can be
       used as "complete inventory" evidence. Any failure must be resolved by
       migrating the item or documenting an explicit status and doc item.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_inventory_gate.json`.
     - Counts:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_env_vars=500`, `old_env_vars=383`;
       `latest_runtime_env_vars=458`, `old_runtime_env_vars=367`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Checks used
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,

       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and

216. AWQ/FP8 MoE safe-route C++ wrappers now expose production graph route
     traces, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 160, 161, 212, and the updated goal make the accepted MoE route
       the split graph-safe dense-stage plus single-token active-expert path,
       not the old compact/router/gated FP8 family.
     - The Python facades already log
       `SM70 AWQ/FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled` and
       `SM70 AWQ/FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`, but
       those logs are skipped while `torch.compiler.is_compiling()`.
     - Production acceptance needs CUDA graph / Inductor route-hit evidence.
       A Python log from eager, profile, or non-compiled execution is weaker
       than a C++ wrapper trace emitted when the actual custom op is reached.

     Source change:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1` guarded one-shot C++ trace support to
       `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`.
     - The helper records current CUDA stream capture status via
       `cudaStreamIsCapturing`, input dtype, token count, and
       `experts_or_top_k`.
     - New trace sites:
       - `awq_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out` prints
         `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled C++ op reached`.
       - `awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out` prints
         `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled C++ op reached`.
       - `fp8_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out` prints
         `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled C++ op reached`.
       - `fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out` prints
         `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled C++ op reached`.
     - Each string intentionally keeps the existing verifier-required prefix,
       so `--require-awq-moe-safe-route` and
       `--require-fp8-moe-safe-route` can accept either the old Python log
       prefix or the stronger C++ trace line.

     Classification:
     - Runtime observability only. It does not alter MoE routing decisions,
       TurboMind dispatch policy, tensor shapes, pointer rows, CUDA launches,
       math, logits, tokens, or speed.
     - This strengthens future production route-hit evidence but is not itself
       model-level acceptance. The next real gate still needs a low-thread
       SM70 rebuild, model route-hit with `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`,
       model-level token/logit/perplexity quality, and same-criterion
       prefill/pure-decode timing.
     - The old unsafe FP8 compact/router/gated/broadcast/direct-reduce family
       remains paused. This trace work does not re-enable or default any of
       those routes.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_moe_cpp_trace.json`.
     - Counts:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.

     Memory/compile guard:
     - Checks used
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,

       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

     Checks:
     - `git diff --check -- csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu

     - Installed `/tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260602_sum2/_C.abi3.so` to
       `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.
     - `strings vllm/_C.abi3.so` confirms all four trace strings are present:
       AWQ dense-stage, AWQ single-token active-expert, FP8 dense-stage, and
       FP8 single-token active-expert.
     - Fresh conda import/schema check loaded
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm/vllm/_C.abi3.so` and confirmed
       `torch.ops._C` has:
       `awq_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`,
       `awq_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`,
       `fp8_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`, and
       `fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`.

     AWQ exactness classification:
     - Unpinned trace smoke is not safe evidence:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_single_token_cpp_trace_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-bug`, `path_type=awq_moe:single_token_dense`,
       `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`, `num_nonzero=3`.
     - Unpinned dense-stage trace smoke is also not safe evidence:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_trace_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-bug`, `path_type=awq_moe:dense_graphsafe`,
       `max_diff=0.000244140625`, `num_nonzero=3`.
     - With `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`, no-trace single-token
       active-expert remains exact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_single_token_notrace_tune0_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`.
     - With both `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`, single-token active-expert is exact
       and logs the actual C++ op:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_single_token_cpp_trace_tune0_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`; its log starts
       with `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled C++ op reached`.
     - With both trace and tune0, graph-safe dense-stage is exact and logs the
       actual C++ op:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_trace_tune0_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`; its log starts
       with `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled C++ op reached`.

     Conclusion:
     - The C++ trace code is runtime observability only; it does not cause the
       AWQ MoE strict diff.
     - Current accepted AWQ MoE op-level quality evidence must pin
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`. Unpinned/default exactness artifacts
       remain `A-bug` and must not be used to claim default-safe behavior.
     - This checkpoint proves installed-op route-hit plus op-level exactness for
       the tune0 AWQ MoE dense-stage and single-token active-expert routes. It
       still does not prove full-model CUDA graph route-hit, token/logit quality,
       or AWQ 35B same-criterion decode speed.
     - The next accepted production gate must run the real model with
       `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`, cudagraph enabled, and explicit route logs,
       then record pure decode and prefill timing separately.

218. MoE C++ trace now proves capture-state route-hit in a real
     Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ CUDA-graph diagnostic run, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 217 proved that the installed C++ trace strings can be reached and
       that tune0 AWQ MoE op-level exactness remains `max_diff=0.0`.
     - The first real-model trace run showed a weakness in the evidence:
       both AWQ MoE C++ routes logged only once, and profile/dummy execution
       reached them before CUDA graph capture. The log therefore showed
       `capture=none` and could not strictly prove that the C++ op was reached
       during graph capture.

     Source change:
     - Changed `maybe_log_sm70_moe_route_once()` in
       `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` from a single
       `std::atomic<bool>` to a `std::atomic<unsigned>` capture-state bitmask.
     - Each route now logs at most once per CUDA stream capture status:
       `none`, `active`, `invalidated`, or `unknown`.
     - This is route observability only. It does not alter dispatch selection,
       tensor shapes, expert pointer rows, CUDA launch arguments, GEMM math,
       logits, tokens, or speed.

     Build/install:
     - Rebuilt `_C` with the same low-memory conda envelope:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false`, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.
     - Build log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_build_sm70_C_only_moe_cpp_trace_capture_status_20260602.log`.
     - Installed the rebuilt `/tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260602_sum2/_C.abi3.so`
       to `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.
     - Fresh conda import/schema check confirmed the four C++ MoE wrapper ops
       still exist in `torch.ops._C`.

     Op-level exactness after the trace change:
     - Single-token active-expert:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_single_token_cpp_trace_capture_mask_tune0_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`.
     - Graph-safe dense-stage:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_trace_capture_mask_tune0_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_20260602.json`
       reports `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`.
     - These rechecks confirm the trace bitmask change did not alter AWQ MoE
       outputs.

     First real-model trace before the source change:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_breakable_graph_cpp_moe_trace_tune0_i64_o4_maxbt64_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`, `input_len=64`, `output_len=4`,
       `max_model_len=1024`, `max_num_batched_tokens=64`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, greedy, `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`.
     - Full Flash policy and timing verifier passed, and both C++ MoE route
       strings were present, but the C++ trace lines showed `capture=none`.
       This run is useful evidence that the C++ op is reached in the real
       model, but it is weaker than the post-change run for graph capture.
     - Output token IDs were `[369, 1429, 310, 1791]`, matching earlier
       item-156 route-hit smokes.
     - Short-shape timing: `prefill_time=0.3213157919999503 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps=59.383778095928555 tok/s`,
       `tpot_seconds=0.01683961566717092 s`. This is not a throughput baseline.

     Post-change real-model capture-state route-hit:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_breakable_graph_cpp_moe_trace_capture_mask_tune0_i64_o4_maxbt64_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Same shape and gates as the first run.
     - Verifier passed with:
       `--require-full-flash-v100`,
       `--require-turbomind-default-policy`,
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route`,
       `--require-decode-timing`, and explicit log checks for both
       `capture=active` C++ MoE routes.
     - Runtime route evidence:
       all workers selected `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`;
       breakable CUDA graph enabled;
       graph capture completed with
       `Capturing CUDA graphs (mixed prefill-decode, PIECEWISE)`,
       `Capturing CUDA graphs (decode, FULL)`, and
       `Graph capturing finished in 2 secs`;
       route summary was
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 20}` on all ranks.
     - C++ MoE capture-state evidence:
       dense-stage logged
       `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled C++ op reached tokens=16 experts_or_top_k=256 dtype=float16 capture=active`;
       single-token active-expert logged
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled C++ op reached tokens=1 experts_or_top_k=8 dtype=float16 capture=active`.
     - TP all-reduce trace showed `backend=custom` for the small
       `(64, 2048)`, `(2, 2048)`, and `(1, 2048)` decode/profile shapes, while
       larger profile shapes used `pynccl`. This is route evidence only, not a
       custom-allreduce performance conclusion.
     - Output token IDs were `[369, 1429, 310, 1791]`, token hash
       `014a116aae9bca2423c6d7e9c850e7935581c0274797ae1442957bb6e76d66a5`,
       matching item 156 and the pre-change trace run.
     - Request metrics were present:
       `prefill_time=0.312570386002335 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps=59.40915927950692 tok/s`,
       `tpot_seconds=0.016832421332461916 s`,
       `output_tps=10.951020365076719 tok/s`.
       This short `64/4` diagnostic is not the AWQ no-MTP `1k/256` or
       `16324/512` speed baseline.

     Current classification:
     - Real-model Full Flash + AWQ MoE C++ route-hit under breakable CUDA graph
       is now proven with `capture=active` evidence.
     - This still does not complete the final production gate because
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` disables the normal torch.compile
       pipeline. A normal production-mode route-hit remains pending, or the
       project must explicitly decide that breakable CUDA graph is accepted
       for this lane.
     - The next throughput gate should not reuse this short shape as a speed
       baseline. It must use the same-criterion AWQ no-MTP `1k/256` and/or
       `16324/512` pure decode口径, with route logs and request metrics.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_moe_cpp_capture_trace.json`.

       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_env_vars=500`, `old_env_vars=383`;
       `latest_runtime_env_vars=458`, `old_runtime_env_vars=367`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.

219. Normal CUDA graph production-mode route-hit attempt for Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
     AWQ still blocks after compile.

     Context:
     - This run intentionally left `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH` unset to
       exercise the latest vLLM normal production compile path, not the
       breakable diagnostic lane from item 218.
     - Command used the same short route-hit shape as item 218:
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP4, AWQ, `input_len=64`,
       `output_len=4`, `max_model_len=1024`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=64`, `max_num_seqs=1`, greedy,
       `--attention-backend FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
     - Full Flash-V100 policy was still enforced:
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=1`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ROUTE_SUMMARY=1`.
     - MQ chunk guards were tried:
       `VLLM_MQ_MAX_CHUNKS=32`,
       `VLLM_MQ_BROADCASTER_MAX_CHUNKS=16`.
     - Compilation was constrained to the small diagnostic range:
       `compilation_config={"cudagraph_capture_sizes":[1,2],
       "compile_ranges_endpoints":[64],"max_cudagraph_capture_size":2}`.

     Artifact:
     - Log only:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_normal_graph_cpp_moe_trace_tune0_i64_o4_maxbt64_mq32_20260602.log`.
     - No JSON was produced because generation never completed.

     Evidence:
     - Runtime config was normal production compile:
       `CompilationMode.VLLM_COMPILE`,
       `CUDAGraphMode.FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`,
       `compile_ranges_endpoints=[64]`,
       `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1, 2]`.
     - All four workers selected
       `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`; this was not a Triton attention
       fallback case.
     - Model loading completed and the AWQ TurboMind per-expert dense path was
       initialized:
       `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled (256 experts)`.
     - Profile started at the intended small shape:
       `profile_run start max_num_tokens=64 max_num_seqs=1`.
     - Torch compile cached only `compile range (1, 64)`;
       no large `(1025, 8192)` range was compiled.
     - `torch.compile took 60.24 s in total`, after which the engine continued
       logging:
       `No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds`
       at 09:51:39, 09:52:39, 09:53:39, and 09:54:39.
     - During the blocked state, GPUs held about `12499 MiB` each and had
       near-zero utilization. The run was terminated by process group after
       `SIGTERM` left worker/`ptxas` processes alive; `SIGKILL` freed all four
       GPUs back to `5 MiB`.

     Classification:
     - This is not speed evidence and not quality evidence.
     - It does prove that `FLASH_ATTN_V100` selection alone is insufficient for
       the 35B-A3B AWQ normal production CUDA graph path: startup/compile
       synchronization still blocks before generation and before the
       `capture=active` C++ MoE route trace can be observed.
     - Compared with item 218, the breakable CUDA graph lane remains the only
       35B-A3B AWQ real-model lane that both completes generation and proves
       Full Flash + AWQ MoE C++ routes under CUDA graph capture.
     - The MQ chunk settings `32/16` and the narrowed compile range did not
       clear this blocker. A future fix should target the normal graph
       compile/broadcast synchronization itself, or make a controlled SM70
       breakable-graph policy explicit instead of treating it as an implicit
       diagnostic workaround.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_normal_graph_moe_trace_attempt.json`.
     - Counts:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_env_vars=500`, `old_env_vars=383`;
       `latest_runtime_env_vars=458`, `old_runtime_env_vars=367`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.

     Checks:
     - `python benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --json
       bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_normal_graph_moe_trace_attempt.json
       --require-inventory-complete` passed.
     - `git diff --check -- csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py
       benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py
       docs/design/sm70_v100_migration_control.md vllm/config/vllm.py
       vllm/envs.py` passed.
     - Tail-whitespace scan found no matches for the same files.
     - Low-memory `python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_source_inventory.py
       benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` passed.
     - After terminating the blocked run, all four GPUs were back to `5 MiB`
       used and no compute processes were listed.

220. Explicit SM70 breakable CUDA graph gate added and route-hit verified,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Items 218 and 219 split the CUDA graph state clearly:
       breakable CUDA graph completes generation and proves Full Flash +
       AWQ MoE C++ `capture=active`, while the latest normal
       `VLLM_COMPILE` path still blocks after compile/broadcast sync.
     - Continuing to use the generic `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` by hand
       made the working SM70 lane look like an implicit diagnostic workaround.
       The migration goal requires SM70/V100 behavior to be decoupled,
       explicit, route-logged, and easy to audit.

     Source change:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH` to `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Added config mapping in `vllm/config/vllm.py`:
       when `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` and the current platform is
       exactly SM70/V100, config sets
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` if the generic env was not already
       set.
     - If the generic env is explicitly set to `0`, that opt-out takes
       precedence and a warning is emitted.
     - If the SM70-specific gate is requested on a non-SM70 platform, it is
       ignored with a warning.
     - Default behavior is unchanged because the new gate defaults to `0`.
       This is a controlled production-candidate lane, not a final default
       enablement.

     Route-hit verification:
     - Ran Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP4 short route-hit with only the SM70-specific
       graph gate:
       `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`.
       The command did not manually set `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH`.
     - Shape and policy:
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`,
       `--attention-backend FLASH_ATTN_V100`, AWQ, TP4,
       `input_len=64`, `output_len=4`, `max_model_len=1024`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=64`, `max_num_seqs=1`, greedy,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=1`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ROUTE_SUMMARY=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_sm70_breakable_gate_cpp_moe_trace_i64_o4_maxbt64_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.

     Evidence:
     - Config logged:
       `Auto-enabling VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1 because
       VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1 was requested on SM70/V100`.
     - The benchmark JSON records both env values after mapping:
       `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` and
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`.
     - Runtime config used `CompilationMode.NONE` plus
       `CUDAGraphMode.FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`, matching the intended breakable
       graph lane.
     - All workers selected `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
     - `Breakable CUDA graph enabled` was logged.
     - CUDA graph capture completed:
       `Capturing CUDA graphs (mixed prefill-decode, PIECEWISE)`,
       `Capturing CUDA graphs (decode, FULL)`, and
       `Graph capturing finished in 2 secs`.
     - C++ MoE capture-state evidence was present:
       dense-stage logged
       `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled C++ op reached
       tokens=16 experts_or_top_k=256 dtype=float16 capture=active`;
       single-token active-expert logged
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled C++ op
       reached tokens=1 experts_or_top_k=8 dtype=float16 capture=active`.
     - Full Flash route summary was present on all ranks:
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 10}`.
     - Generated token IDs were `[369, 1429, 310, 1791]`, token hash
       `014a116aae9bca2423c6d7e9c850e7935581c0274797ae1442957bb6e76d66a5`,
       matching item 218 for the same short shape.
     - Request metrics were recorded:
       `prefill_time=1.2614226740042795 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps=19.914479524839056 tok/s`,
       `tpot_seconds=0.05021471933287103 s`,
       `output_tps=2.719351139978333 tok/s`.
       This run had heavy trace logging and only 4 output tokens; it is not a
       speed baseline and must not be compared with the `1k/256` or
       `16324/512` pure-decode throughput gates.

     Classification:
     - The new gate is accepted as decoupled route-control plumbing:
       it changes graph policy only when explicitly requested and leaves
       default behavior unchanged.
     - The route-hit is valid evidence that the SM70-specific gate can replace
       the manual generic breakable env for the 35B-A3B-AWQ diagnostic graph
       lane.
     - This does not by itself finish the final production graph gate. Normal
       `VLLM_COMPILE` remains blocked as in item 219, and making the SM70
       breakable lane default still needs same-criterion quality and
       throughput evidence.

221. SM70 graph policy evidence added to benchmark JSON and verifier,

       first-class JSON evidence block, not only inferred from raw env fields

       speed, and quality artifacts to prove which CUDA graph lane they used.

222. SM70 graph-policy verifier behavior checked for model-quality compare
     artifacts, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - The final default-enable route needs model-level quality evidence and
       graph-route evidence in the same artifact chain.
     - `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --compare` stores source dump metadata
       in `left_meta` and `right_meta`, not at the top level. The verifier
       already supports policy lookup in compare metadata, but this needed an
       explicit check for the newly added `sm70_graph_policy`.

     Validation:
     - Negative check:
       ran `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` with
       `--require-model-quality-pass --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`
       against the older accepted 27B AWQ quality artifact
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260601/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_vs_flash_v100_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_candidate_bounds_20260601.json`.
     - Result:
       the verifier failed as expected. The old compare artifact is
       model-quality `model-pass`, but it predates the policy schema and lacks
       all `sm70_*_policy` blocks, including `sm70_graph_policy`. Therefore it
       cannot satisfy the updated graph-policy evidence requirement.
     - Positive check:
       created synthetic left/right model-token dump JSON files with identical
       token IDs, prompt logprobs, output logprobs, prompt perplexity, identical
       one-step sampler-logits dumps, and matching `sm70_graph_policy` blocks
       in both inputs.
     - Ran `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --compare ...` with
       `--require-model-quality-gate` and zero-diff acceptance bounds, then
       ran `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-model-quality-pass
       --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route` with synthetic logs containing
       the SM70 auto-enable message, `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`, and
       `Graph capturing finished in 2 secs`.
     - Result:
       the verifier passed. It reported `policy_results` location
       `compare-meta` for `sm70_graph_policy` and all other SM70 policy blocks,
       and the three graph-policy JSON expectations matched through
       `left_meta` and `right_meta`.

     Classification:
     - This is verifier/harness evidence only. No model load, no GPU benchmark,
       no CUDA build, and no CUDA kernel compile was run.
     - Existing older model-quality artifacts remain useful historical evidence
       but should not be treated as satisfying the new graph-policy gate.
     - The next real model-quality artifact for SM70 breakable graph must be
       generated after item 221 so the compare output carries
       `sm70_graph_policy` in `left_meta`/`right_meta` and can pass the combined
       quality plus graph-route verifier gates.

223. 27B-AWQ Flash-V100 breakable-graph model-quality gate rerun with graph
     policy evidence, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Earlier accepted 27B AWQ model-quality evidence was generated before

       graph lane after item 221.
     - The user also flagged that prior `enforce_eager=True` checks did not
       cover CUDA-graph decode. This rerun explicitly uses
       `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` and `enforce_eager=false`.

     Scope:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ`.
     - TP4 on four V100/SM70 GPUs.
     - Same attention-quality isolation口径 as the older accepted gate:
       `--disable-custom-all-reduce`, `--max-model-len 4096`,
       `--max-num-batched-tokens 64`, `--max-num-seqs 3`, greedy
       `max_tokens=16`, `logprobs=5`, `prompt_logprobs=1`, seed 0, three fixed
       prompts.
     - Low-memory/low-compile guards were kept for both runs:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.
     - This is not production custom-all-reduce evidence, not a long-context
       gate, and not a `1k/256` or `16324/512` steady decode speed baseline.

     Artifacts:
     - TRITON comparison dump:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_breakable_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - TRITON sampler logits:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sampler_logits_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_breakable_graph_greedy16_20260602/`.

       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_vs_flash_v100_breakable_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_candidate_bounds_20260602.json`.

       `sm70_breakable_mapping_effective=true`, with logs for the SM70

       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 128, "metadata_none_zero_output": 192,

     Model-quality result:
     - Compare passed `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --compare` with
       `--require-model-quality-gate` and then passed
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-model-quality-pass
       --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`.
     - `model_quality_gate.label=model-pass`.
     - Token sequences were exactly equal across all three prompts:

     - Other observed diffs were within the declared model-quality bounds:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.007169246673583984` (`<=0.02`),

       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.4374977996284315` (`<=0.5`).

     Timing note:

     - TRITON generated 48 tokens in `4.301105205006024 s`
       (`11.159922325111499 tok/s`).
     - Flash-V100 generated 48 tokens in `2.4166572989997803 s`
       (`19.862145956675988 tok/s`).
       Do not mix this with the old `~80 tok/s` AWQ no-MTP pure-decode

     Refreshed inventory:

       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_awq_breakable_graph_quality.json`.
