       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_env_vars=501`, `old_env_vars=383`;
       `latest_runtime_env_vars=459`, `old_runtime_env_vars=367`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.
     - After the runs, all four GPUs were idle at `5 MiB` used with no compute
       processes.

     Classification:
     - The op-level Flash attention residuals remain classified under the
       existing A/B numeric policy. This run is model-level B evidence:
       Flash-V100 vs Triton is a cross-kernel comparison, not a layout-only
       exactness check.
     - For this 27B-AWQ breakable-graph attention-quality口径, the nonzero
       logits/logprob differences are bounded, all sampler argmaxes are equal,
       greedy tokens are identical, and the artifact is accepted as
       `model-pass`.
     - This supports using the fast scalar Flash decode path for the current
       default-enable work after the remaining gates are satisfied, but it does
       not close production custom-all-reduce, long-context, broader prompt
       coverage, or same-criterion steady-throughput acceptance.

227. AWQ MoE safe-route verifier now requires explicit tune0 pinning,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 217 proved that the accepted AWQ MoE dense-stage and single-token
       active-expert op-level evidence requires
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`.
     - The same item recorded unpinned AWQ MoE exactness artifacts as
       `A-bug`, with nonzero diffs. Therefore a model artifact that hits the
       AWQ MoE dense-stage/active-expert logs is not enough by itself.
     - Current benchmark policy plumbing still reported
       `accepted_awq_moe_default_policy=true` when the tune env was unset, and
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-awq-moe-safe-route` did not require
       the tune0 pin. That could let an unpinned/A-bug AWQ MoE route be counted
       as safe production evidence.

     Source changes:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now record
       `sm70_tune_policy.awq_tune0_pinned_effective`.
     - Their `sm70_turbomind_policy.accepted_awq_moe_default_policy` now
       requires:
       - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND` effective true;
       - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` exactly `"0"`;
       - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISABLE` effective false;
       - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` effective false.
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-awq-moe-safe-route` now
       requires `sm70_tune_policy.VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES == "0"` in
       addition to the accepted AWQ MoE policy and route logs.
     - The verifier intentionally checks the raw env field rather than the new
       `awq_tune0_pinned_effective` helper so older valid artifacts that
       already recorded `"0"` remain verifiable.

     Validation:
     - Positive verifier rerun passed on the existing tune0 route/timing
       artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_sm70_breakable_gate_cpp_moe_trace_i64_o4_maxbt64_20260602.json`
       with its `.log`, using
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route --require-decode-timing`.
       The verifier matched
       `sm70_tune_policy.VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES="0"`, the AWQ MoE
       accepted policy, all three AWQ MoE route logs, and timing metrics.
     - Negative verifier smoke used a temporary copy of the same artifact with
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=null` and
       `accepted_awq_moe_default_policy=false`. It failed as expected under
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route`, despite the runtime route logs still
       being present.
     - Low-memory checks passed:
       - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
         benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
         benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py
         benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`;
       - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` on the same three files;
       - `git diff --check` for the touched benchmark/verifier/doc paths.

     Classification:
     - This is evidence-gate hardening only. It changes no model math, no CUDA
       kernel, no route selection, no token output, and no benchmark speed.
     - It prevents a known unsafe/unpinned AWQ MoE path from entering the
       accepted default route by route logs alone.
     - Future AWQ MoE speed or quality artifacts must explicitly set and record
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0` before
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route` can pass.

226. 2026-06-02 follow-up: generic TP custom allreduce op-level root cause.

     User concern:
     - The production custom-allreduce model gate in item 225 failed with large
       token/logit divergence. Before changing model code, isolate whether the
       generic custom allreduce kernel itself has an address/synchronization bug
       under CUDA graph capture.
     - Keep compile/memory pressure controlled. No source rebuild was run in
       this step; all commands still carried low-memory guards:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

     New diagnostic:
     - Added `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_custom_all_reduce.py`.
     - Scope:
       - model-free TP4 custom allreduce;
       - eager path and CUDA graph registered-buffer path;
       - `graph_multi` mode with 160 captured allreduce ops to simulate many
         graph-registered addresses;
       - input patterns `exact_int`, `rank_marker`, `model_like`;
       - dtypes `float16` and `float32`;
       - sizes `5120,10240,20480,65536,262144,524288`, covering both the
         custom kernel's 1stage and 2stage choices.
     - The script also computes a matched custom-order reference: it gathers
       all rank inputs and replays the same rank accumulation order and
       1stage/2stage partition rule as `custom_all_reduce.cuh`. This is the
       mechanical A/B classifier for custom-vs-NCCL differences.

     Artifacts:
     - Main graph/eager/multi exactness:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sm70_custom_allreduce_tp4_graph_exactness_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Forced 1stage model-like:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sm70_custom_allreduce_tp4_1stage_model_like_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Forced 2stage model-like:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sm70_custom_allreduce_tp4_2stage_model_like_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Matched-reference run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sm70_custom_allreduce_tp4_matched_reference_model_like_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.

     Results:
     - `exact_int` and `rank_marker` passed bitwise equality for TP4 eager,
       graph, and `graph_multi`: `required_exact_passed=true`.
       This rules out a direct registered-address, graph replay, or barrier
       bug for exact-valued inputs.
     - `model_like` vs NCCL/PyTorch allreduce was not bitwise equal:
       - fp16 max diff:
         `1.220703125e-4` for 5120/10240/20480 elements and
         `2.44140625e-4` for 65536+ elements.
       - fp32 max diff: `2.9802322387695312e-8`.
       - eager and graph produced the same max diff envelope.
       - `graph_multi` did not introduce a new larger error; it only multiplied
         the nonzero count because 160 allreduces were checked.
     - Forcing `VLLM_CUSTOM_ALLREDUCE_ALGO=1stage` or `2stage` did not reduce
       the fp16 model-like max diff. Algorithm selection is therefore not the
       main quality lever.
     - Matched-reference classification:
       - For every checked `model_like` case, custom output vs the matched
         custom-order reference was `equal=true` and `max_diff=0.0`.
       - The same outputs vs NCCL/PyTorch allreduce had the small diffs above.

     Classification:
     - Generic custom allreduce is not currently an A-class layout/indexing bug
       in this isolated TP4 CUDA-graph test.
     - The op-level nonzero is a B-class custom-vs-NCCL reduction-order /
       fp rounding difference:
       - input source is aligned by construction;
       - exact-valued inputs are bitwise equal;
       - matched custom-order reference makes the diff disappear;
       - fp16/fp32 magnitudes are within dtype-level reduction noise.
     - However, item 225 already proved this B-class op difference is not
       acceptable as a production default for the tested 27B-AWQ route:
       greedy tokens and sampler argmaxes changed. Default enablement must be
       decided by model-level behavior, not by op-level tolerance alone.

     Engineering decision:
     - Do not spend more time looking for a generic custom allreduce address or
       synchronization bug unless a new exact-valued counterexample appears.
     - Do not make TP custom allreduce part of the default SM70 production route
       yet. It remains optional/experimental until either:
       1. a quality-preserving variant is implemented and passes model-level
          greedy/logprob/perplexity gates; or
       2. the final production policy explicitly disables it on SM70 while
          retaining the code path for controlled experiments.
     - The safe Flash-V100 quality evidence continues to be the route with
       `--disable-custom-all-reduce` from item 223.

224. SM70 production communication policy and verifier gates added,
     2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 223 deliberately used `--disable-custom-all-reduce` to isolate the
       attention quality question. That was useful evidence, but it must not be
       mistaken for production TP communication evidence.
     - The final default route needs artifacts that mechanically prove custom
       allreduce was not disabled and that TP communication actually selected
       the custom backend. For `all_reduce_sum2`, the Python MoE candidate log
       is still insufficient; the C++ op must be reached under CUDA graph
       capture.

     Source change:
     - Added `sm70_comm_policy` to both benchmark JSON schemas:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - The policy records:
       `disable_custom_all_reduce`,
       `custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective`,
       `production_custom_allreduce_default_policy`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_ADD_ALLREDUCE`,
       `all_reduce_sum2_requested_effective`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR`,
       and `top1_custom_allreduce_effective`.
     - Added log-regex support to `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` via
       `--expect-log-regex` and `--reject-log-regex`.
     - Added `--require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route`.
       It requires `sm70_comm_policy.custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=true`,
       `production_custom_allreduce_default_policy=true`, and the runtime log
       selecting `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for group `tp:0`. It rejects PYNCCL-only
       `tp:0` logs and explicit `disable_custom_all_reduce=true` evidence.
     - Added `--require-sm70-all-reduce-sum2-route`.
       It requires `sm70_comm_policy.all_reduce_sum2_requested_effective=true`,
       `sm70_moe_policy.moe_add_allreduce_effective=true`, the MoE
       shared+routed `all_reduce_sum2` candidate log, and a same-line regex
       match for
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached[^\n]*capture=active`.
     - The verifier de-duplicates required policy names when multiple gates
       require `sm70_comm_policy`.

     Validation:
     - No model load, no GPU benchmark, no CUDA build, and no CUDA kernel
       compile was run for this item.
     - Direct helper smoke confirmed both benchmark scripts map
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=False` to
       `custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=true` and
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=True` to
       `custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=false`.
     - Synthetic verifier positive smoke passed with:
       `sm70_comm_policy.custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=true`,
       `all_reduce_sum2_requested_effective=true`,
       `sm70_moe_policy.moe_add_allreduce_effective=true`,
       a `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` `tp:0` log, the MoE candidate log, and
       `SM70 custom all_reduce_sum2 op reached ... capture=active`.
     - Negative verifier smoke against the real item-223 Flash-V100 27B-AWQ
       quality artifact failed as expected under
       `--require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route`: that artifact predates
       `sm70_comm_policy`, used `disable_custom_all_reduce=true`, and selected
       PYNCCL-only TP communication. This confirms the new gate does not allow
       the attention-isolation quality artifact to count as production
       communication evidence.
     - The existing item-223 Flash-V100 full-route verifier still passed with
       `--require-full-flash-v100 --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`, proving
       the new communication gate is opt-in and does not retroactively break
       attention-only evidence.
     - Generated and verified refreshed source inventory:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_comm_policy_gate.json`.
       Counts remained complete:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_env_vars=501`, `old_env_vars=383`;
       `latest_runtime_env_vars=459`, `old_runtime_env_vars=367`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.

     Classification:
     - This is evidence plumbing only. It changes no model math, no
       communication kernel, no attention kernel, no CUDA graph policy, and no
       runtime default.
     - Future production-route model-quality or speed artifacts should combine
       the relevant Flash/TurboMind/MoE/graph gates with
       `--require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route`; MoE add+allreduce artifacts
       that claim old 0.0.3 `all_reduce_sum2` behavior must also pass
       `--require-sm70-all-reduce-sum2-route`.
     - This item does not close production custom-allreduce quality, the
       `1k/256` or `16324/512` throughput gate, long-context behavior, or
       broader prompt coverage.

225. 27B-AWQ production custom-allreduce model-quality gate fails; do not
     default custom TP allreduce yet, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 223 proved Flash-V100 model quality only with
       `--disable-custom-all-reduce`.
     - Item 224 added mechanical route gates for production TP communication.
       This item uses those gates on a real model to test whether the same
       27B-AWQ graph quality口径 remains safe when custom allreduce is enabled.

     Scope:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ`.
     - TP4 on four V100/SM70 GPUs.
     - Same three prompts, greedy `max_tokens=16`, `logprobs=5`,
       `prompt_logprobs=1`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=64`, `max_num_seqs=3`, seed 0.
     - `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`.
     - Production communication was enabled by omission of
       `--disable-custom-all-reduce`; JSON reported
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=false` and
       `sm70_comm_policy.custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=true`.
     - Low-memory/low-compile guards were kept:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MKL_NUM_THREADS=1`, `NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1`,
       `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.

     Artifacts:
     - TRITON custom-allreduce dump:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_breakable_graph_custom_ar_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - TRITON custom-allreduce sampler logits:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sampler_logits_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_breakable_graph_custom_ar_greedy16_20260602/`.
     - Flash-V100 custom-allreduce dump:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_27b_awq_flash_v100_breakable_graph_custom_ar_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_20260602.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Flash-V100 custom-allreduce sampler logits:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sampler_logits_qwen36_27b_awq_flash_v100_breakable_graph_custom_ar_greedy16_20260602/`.
     - TRITON custom AR vs Flash custom AR compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_vs_flash_v100_breakable_graph_custom_ar_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_candidate_bounds_20260602.json`.
     - TRITON no-custom vs custom compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_triton_no_custom_ar_vs_custom_ar_breakable_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_20260602.json`.
     - Flash no-custom vs custom compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_flash_v100_no_custom_ar_vs_custom_ar_breakable_graph_greedy16_logprobs_sampler_logits_20260602.json`.

     Route evidence:
     - TRITON custom-allreduce dump passed
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route
       --require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route`.
       Logs showed `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`, `Graph capturing finished`,
       and `Using ['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL'] all-reduce backends ... group 'tp:0'`.
     - Flash-V100 custom-allreduce dump passed
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-full-flash-v100
       --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route
       --require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route`.
       Logs showed all workers using `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`, both Flash prefill routes, graph
       capture completion, and `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for `tp:0`.
     - Flash route summary on all ranks:
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 128, "metadata_none_zero_output": 192,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 16, "prefill_prefix_flash": 16}`.
       This was full Flash prefill+decode, not a Triton-prefill/Flash-decode
       mixed route.

     Model-quality result:
     - TRITON custom-allreduce vs Flash-V100 custom-allreduce compare failed
       the model-quality gate:
       `equal=false`, `model_quality_gate.label=model-fail`,
       `token_equal=false`,
       `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=false`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=4.039547622203827`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.9793437592716145`,
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=27.9794921875`.
     - First token mismatches:
       prompt 0 at output index 2 (`198` vs `216935`);
       prompts 1 and 2 at output index 14 (`271` vs `121350`).
     - This is not a small B-type numeric residual. It changes deterministic
       greedy output tokens and sampler argmaxes.

     Cross-checks isolating custom allreduce:
     - TRITON no-custom vs TRITON custom-allreduce also failed:
       `equal=false`, `model_quality_gate.label=model-fail`,
       `token_equal=false`,
       `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=false`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=2.3827431201934814`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=1.3150157928466797`,
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=17.13671875`.
       First token mismatches appeared at output index 10 for prompt 0 and
       output index 8 for prompts 1/2.
     - Flash no-custom vs Flash custom-allreduce also failed:
       `equal=false`, `model_quality_gate.label=model-fail`,
       `token_equal=false`,
       `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=false`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=4.044324159622192`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=9.319465398788452`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.705755921186551`,
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=27.9794921875`.
       First token mismatches appeared at output index 2 for prompt 0 and
       output index 8 for prompts 1/2.
     - Sampler dump localization:
       TRITON no-custom vs custom first argmax mismatch at dump index 56,
       step 15, with `max_abs_diff=3.7744140625`;
       Flash no-custom vs custom first argmax mismatch at dump index 24,
       step 7, with `max_abs_diff=27.9794921875`;
       TRITON-custom vs Flash-custom first argmax mismatch also at dump index
       24, step 7, with `max_abs_diff=27.9794921875`.

     Timing note:
     - These quality dumps generated only 48 output tokens and included
       logprob/sampler-logit dump overhead. They are not steady throughput
       baselines.
     - TRITON custom-allreduce quality dump: `48 / 2.194575964997057 s =
       21.872106851431944 tok/s`.
     - Flash custom-allreduce quality dump: `48 / 1.866229352002847 s =
       25.720311358561663 tok/s`.
       Do not compare these numbers to the old `~80 tok/s` pure decode
       `1k/256` baseline.

     Classification:
     - Route-hit succeeded, quality failed.
     - This is a production communication quality failure, not merely a
       FlashAttention route failure: the same backend changes outputs when TP
       custom allreduce is enabled.
     - Custom allreduce remains not accepted for the default production route
       on this evidence. The safe attention-quality evidence remains item 223
       with `--disable-custom-all-reduce`; production custom allreduce must be
       root-caused and fixed or explicitly default-disabled before it can be
       part of the final default path.
     - Because deterministic tokens change, this route is not `B-accept` even
       if the underlying allreduce difference is ultimately a reduction-order
       issue. Current label: `model-fail / B-pending-root-cause`.

     Refreshed inventory:
     - Generated and verified:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/source_inventory_old003_vs_latest_refresh_20260602_after_custom_ar_model_fail.json`.
     - Counts:
       `latest_files=2466`, `old_files=2246`;
       `latest_keyword_files=287`, `old_keyword_files=225`;
       `latest_env_vars=501`, `old_env_vars=383`;
       `latest_runtime_env_vars=459`, `old_runtime_env_vars=367`;
       `latest_torch_ops=399`, `old_torch_ops=291`;
       `inventory_complete=true`;
       `old_only_env_vars=0`;
       `old_only_runtime_env_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_keyword_files_unclassified=0`;
       `old_only_torch_ops_unclassified=0`.
     - After the runs, all four GPUs were idle at `5 MiB` used with no compute
       processes.

228. AWQ MoE tune0 safe route source-default hardening.

     Context:
     - Earlier AWQ MoE exactness evidence showed a real Type-A failure when
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` was unset/default at the C++ MoE
       selector level. The tune0 artifacts with
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0` passed with `max_diff=0.0`, but
       relying on the benchmark launcher to pin the env is too easy to miss in
       production.
     - Python `vllm/envs.py` already defaults the env to false, but the SM70
       C++ MoE selector read `getenv()` directly and treated an unset env as
       dynamic-measure tuning enabled. That mismatch is the root cause of the
       unsafe unpinned AWQ MoE path.

     Source change:
     - In `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`, the MoE dispatch logic
       was split into `select_moe_dispatch_policy_impl(..., tune_enabled)`.
     - The existing generic selector still calls the impl with
       `tune_small_shapes_enabled()`, preserving the old unset-as-enabled
       behavior for FP8/F16 generic MoE paths.
     - A new AWQ-specific selector `select_awq_moe_dispatch_policy(...)` calls
       the impl with `awq_tune_small_shapes_enabled()`, so AWQ MoE uses fixed
       dispatch when the env is unset or exactly `0`, and only enables dynamic
       measure when the env is explicitly nonzero.
     - Only `awq_moe_gemm_sm70_out_impl` was changed to use the new AWQ-specific
       selector. `fp8_moe_gemm_sm70_out_impl` still uses the existing
       `awq_select_moe_dispatch_policy(...)`; this avoids accidentally changing
       FP8 MoE behavior without separate FP8 evidence.

     Checks:
     - Route ownership check:
       `rg -n "select_awq_moe_dispatch_policy|awq_select_moe_dispatch_policy|select_moe_dispatch_policy_impl|op.dispatch" csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`.
       Result: AWQ MoE `op.dispatch` uses `select_awq_moe_dispatch_policy`,
       FP8 MoE `op.dispatch` still uses `awq_select_moe_dispatch_policy`.
     - `python -m py_compile benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`
       passed.
     - `git diff --check` on the touched Python/doc files passed.
     - `clang-format` was not available in the current shell
       (`/bin/bash: clang-format: command not found`), so C++ formatting has
       only been kept manually consistent with nearby code.

     Build/validation status:
     - No full C++ rebuild was started in this step. The next runtime validation
       must rebuild with low-memory guards:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, single-thread BLAS/OpenMP envs, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.
     - Until that rebuilt artifact proves unset-env AWQ MoE exactness, the
       external acceptance verifier remains conservative and still requires
       `sm70_tune_policy.VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES == "0"` for
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route`.
     - Required next evidence after rebuild: rerun AWQ MoE dense-stage and
       single-token active-expert exactness with the env unset and with
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`. Both must remain Type-A
       `max_diff=0.0`; only then can the benchmark/verifier policy accept
       unset as an equivalent safe default.

229. AWQ MoE safe-default selector rebuild and env-unset exactness closure.

     Build route:
     - First attempt used full editable install with low-memory guards:
       `MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       single-thread OpenMP/BLAS/JIT envs, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`.
     - Host memory stayed safe during that attempt; observed available memory
       stayed around `27-31 GiB`, and CUDA compiler subprocesses stayed within
       the intended `2`-way concurrency.
     - The full editable build was intentionally stopped after it moved past
       `_C/_moe_C` into bundled `vllm-flash-attn` FA2/FA3/SM80/SM90 targets.
       This was not a compile failure; it avoided wasting time on targets not
       needed for the AWQ MoE C++ selector validation. `pip` removed its
       temporary build directory, so it could not be reused.
     - Rebuilt with a persistent narrow CMake directory instead:
       `/tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260602_awq_moe_policy`.
       Configure log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_configure_sm70_C_only_awq_moe_policy_20260602.log`.
       Build log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_build_sm70_C_only_awq_moe_policy_20260602.log`.
       Install logs:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_install_sm70_C_only_awq_moe_policy_20260602.log`
       and
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_install_sm70_C_only_awq_moe_policy_prefix_source_20260602.log`.
     - Configure confirmed `CUDA target architectures: 7.0`,
       `Building SM70 TurboMind GEMM backend for archs: 7.0`, and
       `FA2_ARCHS:` / `FA3_ARCHS:` empty. The narrow build completed
       `[35/35] Linking CXX shared module _C.abi3.so`.
     - First install without `--prefix .` wrote to `install/vllm/_C.abi3.so`;
       the corrected install with `--prefix . --component _C` wrote to
       `vllm/_C.abi3.so`, which is the source-tree module imported by the
       editable environment.

     Env-unset exactness:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` was explicitly unset for both runs.
     - Dense graph-safe AWQ MoE:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_trace_unset_exactness_after_safe_default_20260602.json`.
       Result: `moe_classification.label=A-pass`, `path_type=awq_moe:dense_graphsafe`,
       `max_diff=0.0`, `max_mean_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`,
       `num_reports=6`. Stages `w13_gate_up`, `silu_and_mul`, and
       `w2_sorted_output` all had `max_diff=0.0`.
     - Single-token active-expert AWQ MoE:
       the first command incorrectly used `--m 1 2` and failed with
       `ValueError: single_token_dense AWQ MoE check requires --m 1`.
       This was a benchmark invocation error, not a kernel failure.
       Rerun with `--m 1` produced
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/awq_moe_single_token_cpp_trace_unset_exactness_after_safe_default_20260602.json`.
       Result: `moe_classification.label=A-pass`,
       `path_type=awq_moe:single_token_dense`, `max_diff=0.0`,
       `max_mean_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`, `num_reports=3`.

     Policy/verifier update:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now record
       `sm70_tune_policy.awq_moe_safe_default_selector_effective` and
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.awq_moe_safe_default_selector_effective`.
       For AWQ MoE these fields are true when
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` is unset or exactly `"0"`.
     - Their `accepted_awq_moe_default_policy` now accepts AWQ TurboMind with
       AWQ MoE enabled, batched AWQ MoE disabled, and the safe-default selector
       effective. This makes env-unset the intended safe default after the C++
       selector fix, while explicit nonzero tune remains outside the accepted
       default route.
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-awq-moe-safe-route` now
       uses a dedicated `awq_moe_safe_tune_gate`:
       existing artifacts with raw `"0"` still pass;
       raw `null` passes only if both new safe-default fields are present and
       true; old raw-null artifacts without these fields still fail.

     Checks:
     - `python -m py_compile` passed for
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`,
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`, and
       `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for the same three files.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched benchmark/verifier/doc paths.
     - Existing tune0 route artifact still passed
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-awq-moe-safe-route
       --require-decode-timing`; the new gate reported
       `reason=accepted raw tune0 pin`.
     - Synthetic old raw-null artifact without new fields failed as expected:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verifier_smoke_awq_moe_old_null_without_safe_field_20260602.json`.
     - Synthetic raw-null artifact with both new safe-default fields passed:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verifier_smoke_awq_moe_new_null_with_safe_field_20260602.json`;
       the gate reported `reason=accepted source-level unset-env safe default`.

     Current conclusion:
     - The earlier AWQ MoE unpinned/default Type-A issue is fixed at the C++
       source-selector level for the tested dense graph-safe and single-token
       active-expert paths.
     - Env-unset AWQ MoE is now accepted only for new artifacts that explicitly
       record the safe-default selector field. This preserves backward safety:
       old raw-null artifacts remain rejected.
     - No build/ninja/nvcc processes were left running after the step, and all
       four GPUs were idle at `5 MiB` used with no compute processes.

230. FP8 MoE C++ graph-safe/single-token exactness harness gap and tune-default
     fix.

     Harness gap found:
     - Earlier FP8 MoE exactness evidence was too weak for the production
       C++ graph-safe branches. In
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py`, the FP8 MoE
       `dense_graphsafe` / `single_token_dense` names did not actually call
       `_C::fp8_moe_dense_stage_sm70_out`,
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_w13_sm70_out`, or
       `_C::fp8_moe_single_token_dense_stage_sm70_out`; the old path could
       compare the Python dense oracle against itself.
     - The CLI choices for `--fp8-moe-actual` also initially rejected
       `dense_graphsafe`. The first corrected invocation failed at argparse
       before touching kernels. This is a benchmark harness bug, not a kernel
       failure.
     - The harness now explicitly supports and calls the FP8 production C++
       `dense_graphsafe` and `single_token_dense` ops. `single_token_dense`
       requires `--m 1`, matching decode.

     Initial strict evidence with old source default:
     - With both tune envs effectively unset, the corrected harness exposed
       nonzero Type-A diffs on real
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`,
       layer `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`.
     - Dense graph-safe artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/fp8_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_after_harness_20260602.json`.
       Result: `label=A-bug`, `max_diff=0.0001220703125`,
       `num_nonzero=6`, `num_reports=6`; W13 `1.22e-4`, SiLU/W2
       `1.53e-5`.
     - Single-token artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/fp8_moe_single_token_dense_cpp_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_after_harness_20260602.json`.
       Result: `label=A-bug`, `max_diff=0.0001220703125`,
       `num_nonzero=3`, `num_reports=3`.
     - Batched grouped-MoE diagnostic:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/fp8_moe_batched_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_after_harness_20260602.json`.
       Result: `label=B-pending`, `max_diff=0.000244140625`, all within
       the fp16 nominal output bound but not default-accepted.
     - Single-expert diagnostic:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/fp8_moe_dense_graphsafe_single_expert0_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_after_harness_20260602.json`.
       With all routes on expert0, `m=2` was exactly `0.0`; `m=1` only
       showed `9.54e-7` W13 noise. This weakened the indexing/leakage theory
       and pointed toward dispatch/tuning selection.

     Root cause:
     - FP8 dense and FP8 MoE were still using the generic
       `tune_small_shapes_enabled()` C++ selector. Unlike the AWQ-specific
       safe selector from item 229, this generic helper treated an unset
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` as dynamic measure enabled.
     - Therefore the old source default could let TurboMind measure/select a
       schedule that was numerically different from the fixed dense oracle.
       This was not an inherent quality loss in the FP8 MoE C++ route.
     - Proof before source change: explicitly setting
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0` made both FP8 production C++ paths
       bitwise exact:
       `fp8_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_after_harness_20260602.json`
       and
       `fp8_moe_single_token_dense_cpp_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_after_harness_20260602.json`
       both reported `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, all stages `0.0`.

     Source change:
     - Added `fp8_tune_small_shapes_enabled()` in
       `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu`.
       Default is fixed dispatch. Dynamic measure is enabled only by
       explicit `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1`; if that env is unset,
       explicit legacy `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1` can still enable
       FP8 tuning for debug/offline experiments.
     - Added `select_fp8_dense_dispatch_policy(...)` and
       `select_fp8_moe_dispatch_policy(...)`.
       `fp8_gemm_sm70_out` now uses the FP8 dense selector, and
       `fp8_moe_gemm_sm70_out_impl` now uses the FP8 MoE selector.
     - Registered `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` in `vllm/envs.py`.
     - Updated `benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` to record
       `fp8_safe_default_selector_effective`, `fp8_dynamic_measure_enabled`,
       and to require the safe selector for
       `accepted_fp8_moe_default_policy`.
     - Updated `verify_sm70_artifact.py` help text so the FP8 MoE safe-route
       requirement explicitly includes fixed-dispatch tune policy.

     Build:
     - Rebuilt only `_C` through the persistent narrow CMake directory
       `/tmp/vllm_sm70_c_only_build_20260602_awq_moe_policy`.
       Build log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_build_sm70_C_only_fp8_tune_safe_default_20260602.log`.
       Install log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/cmake_install_sm70_C_only_fp8_tune_safe_default_prefix_source_20260602.log`.
     - The command used CMake `-j 2`; only one `cc1plus` was observed for the
       changed CUDA TU and system memory stayed safe (`~31-32 GiB` available).
       No full editable build or bundled flash-attn rebuild was started.
       Future builds should still use the full low-memory env prefix
       (`MAX_JOBS=2`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       single-thread BLAS/OpenMP, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, and
       `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`) in the command itself.

     Source-default validation after rebuild:
     - Both tune envs were explicitly unset with
       `env -u VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES -u VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES`.
     - Dense graph-safe:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/fp8_moe_dense_graphsafe_cpp_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_source_default_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.json`.
       Result: `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`,
       `num_reports=6`; W13, SiLU, and W2 all `0.0`.
     - Single-token active-expert:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/fp8_moe_single_token_dense_cpp_exactness_qwen36_35a3b_source_default_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.json`.
       Result: `label=A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`,
       `num_reports=3`; W13, SiLU, and W2 all `0.0`.

     Checks and current conclusion:
     - `python -m py_compile` passed for the touched benchmark/verifier/env
       files.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for the same Python files.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched C++/Python/doc paths.
     - The FP8 MoE C++ graph-safe and single-token paths are no longer
       considered a quality-trading optimization under the source default.
       The previous nonzero diff is reclassified as a default tune-policy bug
       that has been fixed by making FP8 fixed-dispatch by default.
     - Remaining gate before final default claim: rerun model-level FP8 serving
       quality/speed artifacts on the rebuilt `_C`, with route logs and
       `accepted_fp8_moe_default_policy=true`, then verify decode speed and
       generated-token quality under the standard model-level gates.

231. FP8 35B-A3B source-default model-level route-hit after FP8 tune fix.

     Harness change:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_serving_quality.py` now records the same
       SM70 policy blocks as `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` in dump mode:
       `env`, `sm70_tune_policy`, `sm70_turbomind_policy`,
       `sm70_attention_policy`, `sm70_graph_policy`, `sm70_comm_policy`,
       `sm70_gdn_fla_policy`, `sm70_moe_policy`, and
       `sm70_sampling_policy`.
     - Added `--server-arg KEY=VALUE` to the serving quality dump. This records
       known server-side settings for policy metadata, for example
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=true`; it does not modify the HTTP request.
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`
       no longer requires the auto-enable log line. That log appears only when
       `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1` is mapped internally to
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`. If both env vars are explicitly set,
       the correct evidence is the JSON mapping fields plus
       `Breakable CUDA graph enabled` and `Graph capturing finished`.

     Model-level route-hit run:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`.
     - Shape: TP4, `max_model_len=4096`, `max_num_batched_tokens=512`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, greedy `max_tokens=8`, one short prompt, non-eager.
     - Env: both tune envs explicitly unset with
       `env -u VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES -u VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES`;
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100=1`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`, and
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`.
     - Output artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_source_default_full_flash_breakable_routehit_greedy8_20260602.json`.
     - Runtime log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_source_default_full_flash_breakable_routehit_greedy8_20260602.log`.

     Route evidence:
     - JSON:
       `sm70_tune_policy.fp8_safe_default_selector_effective=true`,
       `sm70_tune_policy.fp8_dynamic_measure_enabled=false`,
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.accepted_fp8_moe_default_policy=true`,
       `sm70_turbomind_policy.fp8_moe_batched_gemm_effective=false`,
       `sm70_attention_policy.full_flash_default_policy=true`,
       `sm70_graph_policy.sm70_breakable_mapping_effective=true`.
     - Required logs all appeared:
       `Using AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100 backend`;
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active (no prefix/chunked context)`;
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`;
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`;
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`;
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`;
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`;
       `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`;
       `Graph capturing finished`.
     - Rejected full-Flash logs were absent:
       no Triton diagnostic prefill fallback,
       no decode fallback to Triton, and no instruction to set
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=1`.

     Verifier:
     - Passed:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verify_model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_source_default_full_flash_breakable_routehit_greedy8_20260602.json`.
     - Command required all four gates together:
       `--require-full-flash-v100`,
       `--require-fp8-turbomind-dense`,
       `--require-fp8-moe-safe-route`, and
       `--require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`.
     - The earlier verifier invocation failed only because it required the
       auto-enable breakable graph log even though both breakable env vars had
       been explicitly set. This was a verifier predicate issue, not a runtime
       route miss.

     Timing and scope:
     - Load time: `124.02 s`; generation wall time: `2.27 s`.
     - Request metrics recorded:
       `first_token_latency=1.9886 s`, `prefill_time=1.7984 s`,
       `decode_time=0.2537 s`, `steady_decode_tokens=7`,
       `steady_decode_tps=27.59`, `tpot_seconds=0.03625`.
     - This is a short 8-token route-hit smoke, not the 1k/256 or 16k/512
       decode benchmark and not a speed acceptance baseline.
     - Output token IDs:
       `[271, 248068, 198, 90700, 8340, 25, 271, 16]`.

     Current conclusion:
     - The rebuilt latest tree can load and generate on real
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 TP4 while simultaneously hitting source-default
       fixed-dispatch FP8 TurboMind dense, source-default FP8 MoE safe route,
       full FlashAttention-V100 prefill+decode, and breakable CUDA graph.
     - This closes the route-hit half of the FP8 MoE post-fix model gate.
       Remaining work is still required before final default acceptance:
       compare against a stable baseline for model-level quality, then run
       same-口径 decode/prefill speed benchmarks. The `27.59 tok/s` short-smoke
       steady decode number must not be mixed with the old 1k/256 or 16k/512
       pure-decode performance baselines.

232. FP8 35B-A3B full-Flash model-quality gate is still not default-accepted,
     2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Follow up item 231 with the missing same-shape model-level quality
       compare: latest-tree full Flash-V100 + FP8 TurboMind safe route against
       latest-tree TRITON attention, both under breakable CUDA graph.
     - This was deliberately a short greedy-8 quality dump, not a speed
       baseline. It must not be compared with the old 1k/256 or 16k/512 pure
       decode throughput targets.
     - A user-level auto-restarting OpenAI API server for
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ was seen during the first run and was stopped before
       it could load onto the GPUs. The service then reported inactive; no
       second model was loaded concurrently.
     - No build was started. The runtime commands still carried the low-memory
       build/compile env prefix (`MAX_JOBS=2`,
       `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2`, `NVCC_THREADS=1`,
       `TORCHINDUCTOR_COMPILE_THREADS=1`, single-thread BLAS/OpenMP,
       `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, `CUDA_MODULE_LOADING=LAZY`) to preserve the
       current safety convention.

     TRITON baseline dump:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.log`.
     - Sampler logits:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sampler_logits_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602/`
       with `32` files (`4 ranks x 8 steps`).
     - Route logs showed `AttentionBackendEnum.TRITON_ATTN`,
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`,
