       `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`, and `Graph capturing finished`.

     Full Flash-V100 dump:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.log`.
     - Sampler logits:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sampler_logits_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602/`
       with `32` files.
     - Full-route verifier passed:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verify_model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_breakable_quality_routehit_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.json`.
     - Required route evidence was present on the Flash side:
       `Using AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100 backend`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active (no prefix/chunked context)`,
       FP8 TurboMind dense, FP8 MoE safe-route logs, breakable graph, and graph
       capture completion. Rejected full-Flash logs were absent: no explicit
       Triton diagnostic prefill fallback, no decode fallback to Triton, and no
       instruction to set `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=1`.

     Same-shape timing, quality-dump only:
     - Both sides generated the same greedy output tokens:
       `[271, 248068, 198, 90700, 8340, 25, 271, 16]`, text
       `"\n\n<think>\nThinking Process:\n\n1"`.
     - TRITON metrics: load `127.15 s`, generation `1.81 s`,
       `prefill_time=1.3271 s`, `first_token_latency=1.4997 s`,
       `decode_time=0.2823 s`, `steady_decode_tps=24.80`,
       `tpot_seconds=0.04033`.
     - Flash metrics: load `107.41 s`, generation `1.64 s`,
       `prefill_time=1.3131 s`, `first_token_latency=1.3673 s`,
       `decode_time=0.2690 s`, `steady_decode_tps=26.03`,
       `tpot_seconds=0.03842`.
     - These short-run numbers only show that this route was live and did not
       obviously regress on the tiny sample. They are not accepted performance
       evidence for the 0.0.3 speed baseline.

     Compare result:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_triton_vs_full_flash_breakable_quality_greedy8_after_fp8_tune_fix_20260602.json`.
     - Command used the current candidate model-quality bounds:
       prompt logprob `0.02`, output logprob `0.02`, output top-logprob
       `0.125`, prompt perplexity abs diff `0.5`, and sampler-logits max diff
       `0.125`.
     - `equal=true` at token-record level and
       `model_quality_gate.checks.token_equal=true`.
     - In-bound scalar metrics:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.014801979064941406`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.006277918815612793`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.006277918815612793`, and
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.2639104611574794`.
     - Sampler argmax evidence:
       `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=true`, `num_argmax_mismatch=0`,
       `left_dump_count=32`, `right_dump_count=32`,
       `same_dump_count=true`.
     - Failure:
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.3154296875`, exceeding the candidate
       bound `0.125`. Therefore the harness returned nonzero and labeled the
       gate `model-fail`; default acceptance remains `not default-accepted`.

     Offline sampler-logits localization:
     - The worst diff repeats on all four ranks at step 1.
     - Worst token id: `265`.
     - TRITON value: `1.0185546875`; Flash value: `1.333984375`;
       diff `0.3154296875`.
     - Both sides had the same argmax token id `271`, with identical top1 value
       `21.546875`; top1 margin was `3.78125` on TRITON and `3.765625` on
       Flash.
     - The worst-diff token was far from the decision boundary:
       rank about `30485` on TRITON and `24997` on Flash. This explains why
       greedy output and sampler argmax remained stable, but it does not make
       the path accepted under the current full-vocab sampler-logits bound.

     Current label and next action:
     - Full Flash-V100 + source-default FP8 TurboMind/MoE safe route has valid
       route-hit evidence on Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 TP4.
     - Its current model-level quality label is
       `model-fail / B-pending`, not `B-accept`, because the full-vocab sampler
       logits max diff exceeds the active bound even though tokens, logprobs,
       prompt perplexity, and sampler argmax all pass.
     - Do not default-accept this FP8 full-Flash route yet. The next useful
       step is not another broad benchmark sweep; it is to classify whether the
       large non-argmax full-vocab diff is expected FP8/attention cross-kernel
       noise by comparing against same-backend FP8 self-noise and/or adding a
       top-k/top-margin quality metric. If the same-backend noise is comparable
       and argmax/top-k margins stay stable across more realistic prompts, this
       can move toward a bounded `B-accept`; otherwise it must be root-caused
       in the Flash/FP8 path before default enablement.

233. Project requirement update: 35B-A3B decode baselines must align with
     0.0.3, and work must bias toward development progress.

     User correction:
     - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is part of the hard speed target, not just a route-hit
       or quality-smoke target. Both `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ` and
       `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` must be compared against 1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3 under
       the same decode criteria.
     - Accepted 35B speed evidence must separate pure decode from prefill/TTFT
       and must match model, quantization, TP size, GPU set, max model length,
       input/output length, sampling parameters, no-MTP/MTP state, attention
       backend, CUDA graph state, and runtime route logs.
     - Short `64/4`, greedy8, or route-hit artifacts are useful only for
       proving that kernels are wired; they are not 35B speed baselines.
     - The implementation pace is currently too slow. Future work should spend
       less time on broad or repeated tests and more time on development:
       restoring old 0.0.3 speed, fixing route/diff blockers, and making the
       migrated fast paths defaultable. Run only the smallest benchmark or
       diagnostic that proves route-hit, validates a fix, or changes an
       implementation decision.

     Operational consequence:
     - The migration goal now has explicit 35B-A3B AWQ and FP8 speed parity
       gates in addition to the existing 27B-AWQ gate.
     - The current lack of a formal 35B `1k/256` or `16324/512` pure-decode
       baseline is itself an open migration gap, but the next run should be
       tied directly to a development decision rather than another broad
       exploratory sweep.
     - This requirement has also been added to the workspace `AGENTS.md` so
       future turns inherit it as a project rule.

234. FP8 KV cache status clarification: not yet developed as an independent
     accepted route.

     User correction:
     - Do not confuse FP8 model quantization / FP8 MoE work with FP8 KV cache
       work. The active goal includes FP8 KV cache, and it needs its own
       route-hit, quality, and speed evidence.

     Current factual status:
     - Latest source inventory checked the old FP8 KV/cache kernel file group.
       Old `csrc/cache_kernels*.cu` functionality is present in latest under
       `csrc/libtorch_stable/cache_kernels*.cu`, including
       `reshape_and_cache`, `reshape_and_cache_flash`, `convert_fp8`,
       `gather_and_maybe_dequant_cache`,
       `cp_gather_and_upconvert_fp8_kv_cache`, and
       `cp_gather_indexer_k_quant_cache`.
     - The existing 35B-FP8 256k startup artifacts used `quantization=fp8`,
       but did not explicitly set `kv_cache_dtype=fp8` or
       `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`. They prove FP8-weight model startup and long
       max-model-len capacity, not an accepted FP8 KV-cache route.
     - The Flash prefill raw-vs-cache diagnostics checked whether Flash and
       Triton consumed aligned K/V sources. They were attention-source
       diagnostics, not FP8 KV-cache performance or quality validation.

     Classification:
     - FP8 KV cache is therefore still an open migration gap. It is not
       default-accepted and must not be counted as completed.
     - The next useful work should be development-oriented and minimal:
       wire/verify SM70 runtime support for explicit FP8 KV cache, prove the
       selected Flash-V100/TRITON path actually reads FP8 KV cache without
       hidden fallback, then run only the smallest quality/speed check needed
       to decide whether the route is viable for 35B-A3B AWQ/FP8 speed parity.

235. FP8 KV cache route-hit wiring added; still needs model speed/quality run.

     Code changes:
     - `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py` now emits one-shot
       runtime logs when Flash-V100 actually reads FP8 KV cache:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache decode path active ...` and, for
       prefix/chunked or paged-cache prefill only,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache prefill path active ...`.
     - The log is intentionally not emitted for no-prefix dense prefill,
       because that attention path consumes raw K/V tensors; FP8 cache storage
       is only proven by later decode or by paged/prefix prefill reading the
       cache.
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now expose
       `--kv-cache-dtype` and pass it to `LLM(..., kv_cache_dtype=...)`.
       Their `sm70_attention_policy` records `kv_cache_dtype`,
       `fp8_kv_cache_requested_effective`, and
       `fp8_kv_cache_full_flash_policy`.
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` now has
       `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route`. This gate requires full
       Flash-V100 policy, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8*`, Flash prefill/decode route
       logs, no mixed Triton fallback logs, and the FP8 KV decode log. FP8
       model-weight quantization alone no longer passes as FP8 KV evidence.

     Minimal verification:
     - `python -m py_compile vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn_v100.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py
       benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`
     - `python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py --help` shows
       `--kv-cache-dtype`.
     - `python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py --help` shows
       `--kv-cache-dtype`.
     - `python benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --help` shows
       `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route`.
     - Negative gate check on the previous 35B-FP8 full-Flash route-hit
       artifact failed as expected: full Flash logs were present, but
       `sm70_attention_policy.fp8_kv_cache_requested_effective`,
       `sm70_attention_policy.fp8_kv_cache_full_flash_policy`, and
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache decode path active` were missing. This
       confirms old FP8-weight artifacts no longer pass as FP8 KV-cache
       evidence.

     Remaining gap:
     - This is route-hit plumbing, not accepted performance progress yet. The
       next development step is a small 35B-A3B FP8/AWQ run with
       `--kv-cache-dtype fp8_e5m2` or `fp8`, full Flash-V100, CUDA graph on,
       and verifier flags `--require-full-flash-v100`,
       `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route`, and `--require-decode-timing`.

236. FP8 KV cache on SM70: fixed Triton fp8 write-cache failure by routing
     SM70 FP8 KV updates through the C++ cache op.

     Failure path:
     - First 35B-FP8 route-hit attempt with `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2` failed
       during model initialization:
       `fp8_e5m2 kv-cache is not supported with fp8 checkpoints`.
       Source gate: `vllm/model_executor/layers/attention/attention.py`
       refuses `fp8_e5m2` when the FP8 checkpoint provides KV quant scales.
     - Second attempt with `kv_cache_dtype=fp8` passed the checkpoint gate and
       reached model loading, but failed during KV cache initialization in
       `triton_reshape_and_cache_flash`:
       `ValueError("type fp8e4nv not supported in this architecture. The
       supported fp8 dtypes are ('fp8e4b15', 'fp8e5')")`.
     - Root cause: latest `TritonAttentionImpl.do_kv_cache_update` viewed
       quantized KV cache as `current_platform.fp8_dtype()` and then called the
       Triton reshape/cache kernel. On SM70/V100, Triton cannot compile the
       e4nv FP8 store used by CUDA `torch.float8_e4m3fn`.

     Source fix:
     - `vllm/v1/attention/backends/triton_attn.py` now detects
       CUDA compute capability `< 8` plus quantized KV cache and routes the KV
       cache write through existing `_C_cache_ops.reshape_and_cache_flash`
       instead of the Triton reshape/cache kernel.
     - The branch logs once:
       `SM70 FP8 KV cache C++ reshape_and_cache_flash path enabled
       (kv_cache_dtype=fp8).`
     - Non-SM70 or non-quantized KV cache paths keep the original Triton
       behavior. No C++ rebuild was required because the cache op already
       exists in the current `_C_cache_ops` extension.
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route`
       now requires both the C++ FP8 KV write-cache log and the Flash-V100 FP8
       KV decode log.

     Route-hit artifact after fix:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_fp8_cppcache_routehit_i64_o8_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_fp8_cppcache_routehit_i64_o8_20260602.log`.
     - Model/route:
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8, TP4, `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `quantization=fp8`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8`, full Flash policy, breakable
       CUDA graph enabled, `64/8` route-hit only.
     - Required logs present:
       `SM70 FP8 KV cache C++ reshape_and_cache_flash path enabled`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache decode path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`,
       `Graph capturing finished`,
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`, and
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`.
     - Route summary on each TP worker:
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "fp8_kv_decode": 1,
       "metadata_none_zero_output": 100, "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 10}`.
     - Timing from this tiny smoke:
       `steady_decode_tps_mean=28.519379539797423`,
       `prefill_time_mean=1.1057279630040284`,
       `tpot_seconds_mean=0.035063876428466756`.
       This is not a 35B speed baseline.

     Verification:
     - `python -m py_compile vllm/v1/attention/backends/triton_attn.py
       benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`
     - `python -m ruff check vllm/v1/attention/backends/triton_attn.py
       benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py`
     - Passed:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verify_decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_fp8_cppcache_routehit_allgates_i64_o8_20260602.json`
       with `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route`,
       `--require-decode-timing`, `--require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`,
       `--require-fp8-turbomind-dense`, and
       `--require-fp8-moe-safe-route`.

     Remaining gap:
     - FP8 KV cache is now actually wired and route-hit on 35B-FP8 full Flash,
       but it is not default-accepted. It still needs model-level quality
       comparison and a real 35B 1k/256 decode-speed run before it can count
       toward the P0 speed target.

237. 35B-FP8 latest-tree 1k/256 baseline with full Flash + FP8 KV cache.

     Purpose:
     - Fill the P0 gap for a real 35B-A3B-FP8 `1k/256` latest-tree decode
       data point after the FP8 KV cache C++ write-cache fix.
     - This is a single-run baseline, not a sweep. It is useful because it
       proves the fixed FP8 KV route survives real decode length, not only
       `64/8` smoke.

     Artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_fp8_cppcache_i1024_o256_repeat1_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_fp8_cppcache_i1024_o256_repeat1_20260602.log`.
     - Verifier:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verify_decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_fp8_cppcache_i1024_o256_repeat1_allgates_20260602.json`,
       `passed=true`.

     Benchmark criteria:
     - Model: `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`.
     - TP: 4 x V100/SM70.
     - no-MTP, greedy, single request.
     - Input/output: `1024/256`.
     - `max_model_len=4096`, `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`.
     - `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, full Flash policy:
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`,
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_USE_SCALAR_PAGED=1`.
     - `quantization=fp8`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8`.
     - Breakable CUDA graph on:
       `VLLM_SM70_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`,
       `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`.
     - Custom allreduce was disabled in this run, so this data point does not
       validate custom-AR speed recovery.

     Route evidence:
     - Verifier required and passed:
       `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route`,
       `--require-decode-timing`,
       `--require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`,
       `--require-fp8-turbomind-dense`,
       `--require-fp8-moe-safe-route`.
     - Required runtime logs present:
       `SM70 FP8 KV cache C++ reshape_and_cache_flash path enabled`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache decode path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       `Graph capturing finished`,
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`, and
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`.
     - No Triton diagnostic fallback or Flash decode fallback logs were present.
     - Log still contains non-fatal FA2 probe messages:
       `Cannot use FA version 2 is not supported due to FA2 is only supported
       on devices with compute capability >= 8`; the actual selected backend
       was `FLASH_ATTN_V100`.

     Timing:
     - `steady_decode_tps_mean=43.68053846471055`.
     - `tpot_seconds_mean=0.022893490674523136`
       (`22.893 ms/token`).
     - `prefill_time_mean=1.7869004219974158`.
     - `first_token_latency_mean=1.9275555610656738`.
     - `output_tps_mean=32.8872161818143`.

     Interpretation:
     - Latest-tree 35B-FP8 full Flash + FP8 KV cache is now runnable and
       measurable at `1k/256`.
     - This is not yet P0 acceptance: old 0.0.3 same-criterion `1k/256`
       baseline is still missing, custom allreduce remains disabled, and FP8
       KV cache still needs model-level quality comparison before default
       acceptance.

238. 35B-AWQ latest-tree 1k/256 safe-route baseline and GDN causal-conv
     prefill warmup fix.

     Purpose:
     - Fill the missing latest-tree Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ `1k/256` data point
       without treating a short smoke as a speed baseline.
     - Keep the run narrow: no MTP, greedy, TP4, four V100s, full
       Flash-V100, breakable CUDA graph, AWQ TurboMind/MoE safe route, and
       custom allreduce disabled because the current production custom-AR
       route is still model-quality unsafe.

     Artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_breakable_safe_i1024_o256_repeat1_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_breakable_safe_i1024_o256_repeat1_20260602.log`.
     - Verifier:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verify_decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_breakable_safe_i1024_o256_repeat1_20260602.json`,
       `passed=true`.

     Route evidence:
     - Verifier passed with `--require-full-flash-v100`,
       `--require-decode-timing`, `--require-sm70-breakable-graph-route`,
       `--require-turbomind-default-policy`, and
       `--require-awq-moe-safe-route`.
     - Runtime logs proved `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`, `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path
       active`, route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 10}`, `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`,
       `Graph capturing finished`, `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense
       path enabled`, `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ warmup finished`, and `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`.
     - The TP communication log was PYNCCL-only by design:
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=true`. This is the safe route, not the
       production custom-allreduce recovery route.

     Timing:
     - `steady_decode_tps_mean=50.29709065373797`.
     - `tpot_seconds_mean=0.019881865670607773`
       (`19.882 ms/token`).
     - `prefill_time_mean=2.5285390399949392`.
     - `first_token_latency_mean=2.5869975090026855`.
     - `output_tps_mean=33.43235825027452`.

     Development finding:
     - The first real request still triggered Triton JIT after warmup:
       `_zero_kv_blocks_kernel`, `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`, and
       `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel`.
     - `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel` was a code gap in the migrated GDN warmup:
       `QwenGatedDeltaNetAttention._warmup_prefill_kernels()` warmed the
       chunk/FLA core but did not run the prefill `causal_conv1d_fn` path.
       Therefore prefill/TTFT could be polluted by hot-path JIT even after
       graph capture and SM70 AWQ warmup.

     Source fix:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` now
       calls a minimal `causal_conv1d_fn` prefill warmup inside
       `_warmup_prefill_kernels()` using the layer's real conv weight, dtype,
       device, activation, dummy `cu_seqlens`, dummy `cache_indices`, and a
       64-token single-sequence shape.
     - This only moves JIT/warmup work before real inference. It does not
       change real request tensors, GDN math, attention output, logits,
       decode route, or token quality.

     Checks:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` passed.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` passed.
     - Minimal single-GPU causal-conv call with `T=64`, `D=128`, `W=4`,
       fp16, `cache_indices`, and `cu_seqlens=[0,64]` completed and returned
       finite fp16 output of shape `(128,64)`.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched GDN file and control doc.

     Remaining gap:
     - Latest-tree 35B-AWQ safe-route speed is now measured but still not P0
       accepted against old 0.0.3 because the old same-criterion 35B-AWQ
       baseline is still missing, and the safe route is only `50.30 tok/s`
       pure decode.
     - The next useful development step is not another broad sweep: either
       verify that the causal-conv JIT warning disappears on the same
       `1k/256` route, or directly work on the larger decode bottlenecks
       that can safely recover old speed, especially GDN/FLA decode
       scheduling and a quality-preserving alternative to production custom
       allreduce.

239. Closed the other two first-real-request JIT gaps from the same 35B-AWQ
     safe-route run: `_zero_kv_blocks_kernel` and
     `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`.

     Source fix:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_AUX_KERNEL_WARMUP=1` as a default-on SM70 gate with
       explicit opt-out.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py` now has
       `warmup_slot_mapping_kernel()`, which launches
       `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel` on temporary one-token tensors using the
       real block-table `block_size` and CP constants. It does not mutate the
       live block table, request rows, or slot mappings.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/utils.py` now has `KVBlockZeroer.warmup_kernel()`,
       which launches the real zero-block kernel once through the initialized
       metadata path.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` wires both helpers through
       `_warmup_sm70_aux_kernels()` and logs
       `SM70 auxiliary kernel warmup finished: [...]`.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_worker.py` calls that helper before activating the
       Triton JIT monitor, so first inference should no longer pay those two
       compile costs.

     Quality and route classification:
     - This is a warmup-only change. It does not alter attention math, MoE,
       GDN, logits, sampling, block allocation semantics, or CUDA-graph route
       selection.
     - The slot-mapping warmup uses separate temporary tensors. The zero-block
       warmup only zeroes block `0` before real serving starts, matching the
       production safety intent of zeroing newly allocated KV blocks.

     Checks:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/envs.py vllm/v1/worker/utils.py vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_worker.py`
       passed.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` on the same files passed.
     - Minimal GPU0 slot-mapping warmup call completed:
       `slot_warmup_ok torch.Size([1])`.
     - Minimal GPU0 KV-zero warmup call completed and verified the dummy
       block was zeroed: `zero_warmup_ok True`.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched files and this document.

     Remaining gap:
     - This should remove two known TTFT/prefill pollutants from the 35B-AWQ
       `1k/256` route, but it is not a decode-speed acceptance result. P0
       remains recovering same-criterion 35B-A3B AWQ/FP8 pure decode speed
       against the 0.0.3 baseline, with GDN/FLA decode scheduling, safe custom
       allreduce/top1 communication, FP8 KV cache, and safe MoE fast-path
       recovery still open.

240. GDN packed recurrent decode JIT warmup added, 2026-06-02.

     Root cause:
     - A previous 35B-A3B-AWQ real decode log showed first-inference Triton JIT
       for `fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_packed_decode_kernel`.
     - The migrated `QwenGatedDeltaNetAttention._warmup_prefill_kernels()`
       warmed chunked prefill and causal-conv prefill, but its comment assumed
       the fixed-parameter decode recurrent kernel did not need warmup. That
       assumption is wrong for the current latest-tree JIT monitor: fixed
       parameters still compile on first use.

     Source fix:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` now also
       calls `fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_packed_decode()` once during
       the existing GDN warmup, using a one-token dummy decode shape and the
       layer's real `A_log`, `dt_bias`, head dimensions, dtype, device, and
       state dtype.
     - The new decode warmup is independent from the prefill/autotune warmup.
       Failure only emits a warning and does not change the real request path.

     Quality and route classification:
     - This is warmup-only. It does not alter GDN arithmetic, attention, MoE,
       logits, sampling, state indexing, or CUDA-graph route selection.
     - It specifically targets a known 35B-A3B decode-path kernel compile
       warning, so it is closer to P0 pure-decode recovery than the generic
       auxiliary warmup from item 239.

     Checks:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` passed.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` passed.
     - Minimal GPU0 direct packed-decode call completed:
       `packed_decode_warmup_ok (1, 1, 1, 16) True`.

     Remaining gap:
     - This removes another first-token/decode JIT pollutant, but still is not
       a full 35B throughput acceptance result. The next useful check is a
       single same-route 35B-AWQ/FP8 run only to confirm that the JIT warnings
       for `_zero_kv_blocks_kernel`, `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`,
       `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel`, and
       `fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_packed_decode_kernel` disappear.
       Broader speed work remains GDN/FLA fusion/scheduling, FP8 KV cache, and
       quality-preserving MoE/communication recovery.

241. SM70 auxiliary warmup startup crash fixed, 2026-06-02.

     Route evidence:
     - A short 35B-A3B-AWQ full-Flash/CUDA-graph route check reached model
       load and graph capture with the intended paths active:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ warmup finished`, and `Breakable CUDA graph enabled`.
     - The run then failed before the first real request in
       `_warmup_sm70_aux_kernels()` because
       `logger.info_once("SM70 auxiliary kernel warmup finished: %s", warmed)`
       passed a Python `list` into the logger's once-cache path. Lists are
       unhashable, so this produced `TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'`.

     Source fix:
     - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` now logs `tuple(warmed)` instead
       of `warmed`. This keeps the log content the same while making the
       `info_once` argument hashable.

     Quality and route classification:
     - This is a startup/logging fix only. It does not alter attention,
       GDN/FLA, MoE, KV cache, logits, sampling, CUDA graph capture, or any
       arithmetic path.
     - The failed route check is not a throughput baseline. It only proves the
       previous auxiliary warmup code could crash a real 35B-AWQ startup after
       graph capture, which would block any useful speed validation.

     Checks:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` passed.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` passed.

242. Slot-mapping warmup changed from fake shape to real request shape,
     2026-06-02.

     Root cause:
     - After item 241, the same short 35B-A3B-AWQ full-Flash/CUDA-graph route
       no longer crashed, but the JIT monitor still reported
       `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel` during inference.
     - The previous warmup compiled a fake variant:
       `num_tokens=1`, `max_num_tokens=1`, and a `1x1` block table with
       stride `1`. The real request uses the engine's configured
       `max_num_batched_tokens` and the real block-table stride, so Triton
       still had to compile the production variant on the first request.

     Source fix:
     - `vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py::warmup_slot_mapping_kernel()` now uses
       the real `self.max_num_batched_tokens` and a temporary block table with
       the real `self.max_num_blocks_per_req` stride.
     - It warms both `num_tokens=1` decode shape and
       `num_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens` chunk-prefill shape.
     - This still uses temporary tensors and does not mutate live block-table
       state.

     Route evidence:
     - Retry artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_slotwarmup_jitcheck_i64_o4_20260602.json`.
     - The route completed with CUDA graph enabled and full Flash evidence:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active` and route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 10}` on all TP ranks.
     - `SM70 auxiliary kernel warmup finished:
       ('zero_kv_blocks', 'compute_slot_mapping')` appeared before JIT monitor
       activation.
     - `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel` no longer appeared in the inference JIT
       warnings. The remaining inference JIT warning is
       `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel`, which is now the next narrow warmup gap.

     Checks:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check
       vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - Minimal GPU0 direct warmup call completed:
       `slot_warmup_real_shape_ok 64 64`.

     Scope:
     - This is still not a 35B speed baseline. The retry used `64/4` only to
       prove route-hit and JIT cleanup. It is useful because the first retry
       with slot-mapping JIT showed `steady_decode_tps=7.116`, while the fixed
       retry showed `steady_decode_tps=22.667`; the number is not a baseline,
       but it proves first-request JIT was materially polluting decode timing.

243. GDN causal-conv real-state warmup added and inference-time JIT warnings
     cleared for the 35B-AWQ short route, 2026-06-02.

     Root cause:
     - After item 242, `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel` no longer JIT-compiled
       during inference, but `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel` still did.
     - The existing GDN prefill warmup used dummy tensors that did not fully
       match the production conv path:
       - real prefill passes a transposed `[dim, T]` view with stride
         `(1, dim)`;
       - default mamba conv-state layout is `SD`, so real KV cache is
         transposed to a `[cache_lines, dim, state_len]` view with stride
         `(state_len * dim, 1, dim)`;
       - `_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel` also specialized on `num_cache_lines`
         even though that value is unused in the kernel body.

     Source fix:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`
       now builds the profile-time dummy conv input with the same transposed
       stride as real prefill.
     - The dummy conv state now mirrors the active `DS`/`SD` conv-state layout.
     - A new `_warmup_sm70_causal_conv1d_real_state()` method warms one
       causal-conv prefill launch using a `conv_state[:1]` view from the real
       KV cache, preserving the real KV-cache strides. It zeroes that view in
       `finally` so the warmup does not leak state into serving.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py::_warmup_sm70_aux_kernels()` calls
       the first available GDN real-state warmup before activating the JIT
       monitor and records `gdn_causal_conv1d` in the warmup log.
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/ops/causal_conv1d.py` marks
       `num_cache_lines` as `do_not_specialize` and removes its `tl.constexpr`
       annotation because the fwd kernel does not use that value.

     Route evidence:
     - Final short-route artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_gdnaux_nojit_i64_o4_20260602.json`.
     - Runtime log showed:
       `SM70 auxiliary kernel warmup finished:
       ('zero_kv_blocks', 'compute_slot_mapping', 'gdn_causal_conv1d')`.
     - Final grep found no `Triton kernel JIT compilation during inference`
       warnings.
     - Full Flash route remained active:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active` and route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 10}` on all TP ranks.

     Checks:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/ops/causal_conv1d.py` passed.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` on the same files passed.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched files and this document.

     Scope:
     - This is a startup/JIT cleanup and route-hit improvement, not a formal
       35B speed baseline. It removes first-request compile pollution so the
       next useful P0 run can be a real `1k/256` pure-decode comparison instead
       of another smoke.

244. 35B-A3B-AWQ latest-tree clean `1k/256` no-MTP baseline established,
     2026-06-02.

     Source fix before the clean run:
     - The first formal `1k/256` artifact after item 243 still showed
       inference-time JIT for `_zero_kv_blocks_kernel`.
     - Root cause was the same specialization class as slot mapping:
       warmup called the kernel with `n_blocks=1`, while a real 1k prefill
       zeroes more newly allocated blocks. Triton specialized on the Python
       integer `n_blocks`.
     - `vllm/v1/worker/utils.py::_zero_kv_blocks_kernel` now uses
       `@triton.jit(do_not_specialize=["n_blocks"])`.
     - A `1024/4` route check confirmed no `_zero_kv_blocks_kernel` inference
       JIT after the fix:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_zerojit_i1024_o4_20260602.json`.

     Clean formal baseline:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_clean_i1024_o256_20260602.json`.
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`.
     - Quantization: AWQ, dtype fp16, TP=4, no MTP, greedy sampling,
       `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`, `max_num_seqs=1`.
     - CUDA graph enabled via breakable graph. Custom allreduce disabled, so
       TP/EP communication used PYNCCL. This isolates the known custom
       allreduce quality issue but is likely below the old-tree speed ceiling.
     - Full Flash route evidence:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`, and route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 30}` on all TP ranks.
     - SM70 route evidence:
       `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 LM head top1 layout prepared`, and
       `SM70 auxiliary kernel warmup finished:
       ('zero_kv_blocks', 'compute_slot_mapping', 'gdn_causal_conv1d')`.
     - Final grep found no `Triton kernel JIT compilation during inference`
       warnings.

     Result:
     - `steady_decode_tps_mean=51.6425 tok/s`
       (`51.6362`, `51.6488`).
     - `tpot_seconds_mean=0.0193639`.
     - `prefill_time_mean=1.3966s`.
     - `first_token_latency_mean=1.3989s`.
     - `output_tps_mean=40.3985 tok/s`.

     Decision:
     - This is the current latest-tree clean 35B-AWQ P0 baseline, not an
       accepted endpoint. It is still well below the old-tree target the user
       expects around ~80 pure decode tok/s.
     - Since JIT pollution and full-Flash route misses are no longer the
       immediate blocker for this case, the next high-value development target
       is recovering a quality-safe SM70 communication path
       (`custom_allreduce` / `all_reduce_sum2` / top1 custom AR) or proving via
       profiling that GDN/FLA or MoE scheduling dominates instead.

245. `all_reduce_sum2` local implementation isolated; production custom AR
     remains blocked by model-level quality, 2026-06-02.

     Evidence:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/sm70_all_reduce_sum2_quality_4gpu_20260602.log`.
     - Shape coverage: TP4, sizes `1024`, `5120`, `8192`, `16384`, dtypes
       `float16` and `float32`, patterns `exact_int` and `random_small`.
     - Required reference was the custom-allreduce semantic reference:
       `custom_ar(input_a + input_b)`.
     - Result: every tested case had
       `all_reduce_sum2(input_a, input_b) == custom_ar(input_a + input_b)`
       with `equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`, and `mean_diff=0.0`.
     - Against NCCL, exact integer-like patterns were bitwise equal, while
       `random_small` showed reduction-order noise:
       fp16 `max_diff=0.001953125`, fp32
       `max_diff=2.384185791015625e-07`.

     Classification:
     - `all_reduce_sum2` itself is Type A exact relative to the custom-AR
       semantics it is meant to fuse. This rules out a local sum2 pointer,
       capture, or add-order bug for the tested shapes.
     - The nonzero NCCL comparison is Type B custom-vs-NCCL reduction-order
       difference at the op level.

     Decision:
     - Do not spend the next P0 cycle trying to find a generic
       `all_reduce_sum2` address/synchronization bug; this route is locally
       exact.
     - This does not make custom allreduce production-safe. Item 225 still
       blocks default custom AR because the full model changed sampler logits
       and tokens. Any future communication speed recovery must either make the
       model-level custom-AR path quality-safe or replace it with another
       quality-safe communication/top1 strategy.

246. Canonicalized the SM70 GDN `delta_h` schedule gate, 2026-06-02.

     Reason:
     - Item 140/earlier delta_h work proved the gated schedule can be both
       strict-equal and much faster, but the default gated path still exposed a
       12-config Triton autotune search space. That keeps avoidable compile
       cost and autotune-winner variability in the candidate fast path.
     - The fastest previously measured strict-equal candidate on the Qwen-like
       V100 fixture was `BV=16`, `num_warps=8`, `num_stages=1`.

     Source changes:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/chunk_delta_h.py` now makes
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DELTA_H_SCHEDULE=1` canonical by default:
       `BV=[16]`, `warps=[8]`, `stages=[1]`.
     - Explicit env overrides still work, so the broader old search space can
       be used for diagnostics without making the accepted fast path depend on
       autotune variability.
     - `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` now logs enabled SM70 GDN/FLA schedule gates
       once per process, so later model benchmarks can prove route policy from
       logs instead of only from the shell command.

     Validation:
     - Default-off artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_delta_h_canonical_patch_default_qwen_shape.pt`.
     - Canonical gated artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_delta_h_canonical_patch_sched_qwen_shape.pt`.
     - Strict compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/gdn_delta_h_canonical_patch_default_vs_sched_strict.json`.
     - Fixture: single V100, Qwen-like GDN prefill,
       `seqlen=1024`, `cu_seqlens=[0,1024]`, random initial state,
       `input_scale=1.0`, `gate_scale=5.0`, `beta_scale=1.0`.
     - Result: `strict_ok=true`, `inputs_ok=true`; `out`, `final_state`,
       `stage_h`, `stage_v_new`, `stage_out`, and all saved stages had
       `torch_equal=true`, `max_diff=0.0`, `num_different=0`.
     - Timing on the same fixture after one warmup / three repeats:
       default average `0.0039622487 s`; canonical gated average
       `0.0013148060 s`.

     Classification:
     - This is a Type A strict-safe op-level schedule improvement for the
       measured Qwen-like shape. It is a real development step toward recovering
       the old GDN speed path, not a final model-level acceptance.
     - The gate remains explicit until a focused 35B-AWQ/FP8 model run proves
       route-hit, token/logit quality, and same-criterion pure decode speed.

247. 35B-AWQ `1k/256` model run with canonical `delta_h` schedule,
     2026-06-02.

     - Test whether the strict-safe canonical `delta_h` schedule from item 246
       moves the current P0 35B-AWQ pure-decode gap, without changing the
       benchmark口径 from item 244.

     Shape and route:
     - Same as item 244: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, AWQ,
       dtype fp16, TP=4, no MTP, greedy, `input_len=1024`,
       `output_len=256`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`, `max_num_seqs=1`, breakable CUDA graph,
       custom allreduce disabled.
     - Added only `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DELTA_H_SCHEDULE=1`.
     - Route evidence:
       `SM70 GDN/FLA schedule gates enabled: kkt=False delta_h=True
       chunk_o=False recurrent=False sigmoid_gating=False`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 30}`,
       plus accepted AWQ MoE dense-stage / single-token active-expert logs.
     - No `Triton kernel JIT compilation during inference` warning appeared.

     Result versus item 244 clean baseline:
     - Pure decode TPS: `51.6425 -> 51.6681 tok/s`, effectively unchanged.
     - TPOT: `0.0193639 -> 0.0193543 s`, effectively unchanged.
     - Prefill time: `1.3966 -> 1.3189 s`, about `5.5%` faster.
     - TTFT: `1.3989 -> 1.3211 s`, about `5.6%` faster.
     - Output TPS: `40.3985 -> 40.9170 tok/s`, improved mostly from lower
       prefill/TTFT, not from decode.

     Decision:
     - Canonical `delta_h` is useful and strict-safe for the measured prefill
       op/model route, but it is not the current 35B-AWQ pure-decode bottleneck.
     - Do not spend the next P0 cycle trying to squeeze 35B decode from
       `delta_h`. Continue pure-decode recovery in GDN recurrent decode,
       MoE/LM-head/sampling, or quality-safe communication/top1 paths.

248. 35B-AWQ `1k/256` model run with BV-only recurrent schedule,
     2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Test the already op-level strict-safe `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE`
       decode gate from items 149-150 under the same 35B-AWQ `1k/256`口径.

     Artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_recurrent_sched_i1024_o256_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_recurrent_sched_i1024_o256_20260602.log`.

     Shape and route:
     - Same baseline口径 as item 244: 35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, no MTP, greedy,
       `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`, full Flash-V100, breakable CUDA graph,
       custom allreduce disabled.
     - Added only `VLLM_SM70_FLA_RECURRENT_SCHEDULE=1`.
     - Route evidence:
       `SM70 GDN/FLA schedule gates enabled: kkt=False delta_h=False
       chunk_o=False recurrent=True sigmoid_gating=False`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 30}`,
       and accepted AWQ MoE dense-stage / single-token active-expert logs.

     Result versus item 244 clean baseline:
     - Pure decode TPS: `51.6425 -> 52.3364 tok/s`, about `+1.34%`.
     - TPOT: `0.0193639 -> 0.0191072 s`.
     - Prefill time: `1.3966 -> 1.3973 s`, unchanged.
     - TTFT: `1.3989 -> 1.3995 s`, unchanged.
     - The run paid a large cold load/profile cost (`load_seconds=240.4`) and
       logged two shared-memory broadcast waits during startup, but those are
       outside the measured pure decode window.

     Decision:
     - BV-only recurrent schedule is a small but real decode-side improvement
       on the measured 35B-AWQ path. It is not enough to close the P0 gap by
       itself.
     - Before defaulting it, run a focused model-quality gate or in-process
       compare because the current evidence is speed/route plus earlier
       op-level strictness, not full model-quality acceptance.
     - Larger decode recovery still has to come from MoE scheduling/GEMM,
       LM-head/sampler, or a quality-safe communication/top1 replacement.

249. SM70 AWQ/FP8 MoE single-token weighted-reduce default subpath,
     2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Continue the MoE decode recovery without re-entering the paused
       compact/gated/direct-W2 quality-risk route.
     - Latest already had the CUDA implementation of
       `awq_moe_single_token_weighted_reduce_out`, but the op was not
       registered in `_C` and the AWQ/FP8 safe MoE single-token path still
       called generic `_moe_C.moe_unpermute`.
     - This item migrates only the low-risk final weighted reduce from old
       0.0.3: W13, `silu_and_mul`, W2, expert order, and top-k weights are
       unchanged. The replacement removes the generic unpermute call for
       single-token AWQ/FP8 safe MoE decode when the op is available.

     Source change:
     - Registered `_C::awq_moe_single_token_weighted_reduce_out` in
       `csrc/torch_bindings.cpp` and declared it in `csrc/ops.h`.
     - Added the Python facade in `vllm/_sm70_ops.py`.
     - `vllm/envs.py` now keeps
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_UNPERMUTE_FASTPATH` default-on while
       leaving the combined `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_FASTPATH` and
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_PERMUTE_FASTPATH` default-off.
     - `awq_sm70_moe.py` and `fp8_sm70_moe.py` now use the weighted-reduce op
       in the `num_tokens == 1` active-expert dense path and log:
       `SM70 AWQ/FP8 MoE single-token weighted-reduce path enabled`.
       If the rebuilt `_C` op is missing, they fall back to generic
       `moe_unpermute` instead of failing at import time.
     - `benchmark_sm70_decode.py`, `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`, and
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py` now record/require the unpermute fast-path
       effective state and route log for AWQ/FP8 MoE safe-route acceptance.

     Quality classification:
     - This is Type A for the packed single-token reduce case: it only changes
       how the same sorted W2 rows are reduced back to one token.
     - Added
       `test_sm70_single_token_weighted_reduce_matches_moe_unpermute()` with
       strict `torch.equal` coverage for `topk=2/8`, hidden `256/2048/7168`,
       fp16 output, and a padded-row-stride case.
     - The padded-row-stride case intentionally compares the strided view
       against `moe_unpermute` on the same logical matrix after
       `.contiguous()`. During the first run, comparing generic
       `moe_unpermute` directly on the non-contiguous view produced
       `max_diff=7.96875`, showing the generic reference assumes packed rows.
       The weighted-reduce op uses `sorted_output.stride(0)` and therefore
       preserves logical row semantics for padded AWQ layouts.
     - Existing
       `test_sm70_single_token_moe_fastpath_matches_generic()` was rerun to
       confirm the earlier C++ permute/unpermute Type-A regression still
       passes.

     Build and checks:
     - Low-memory `_C` target rebuild:
       `MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 NVCC_THREADS=1
       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0 cmake --build
       /tmp/tmp0blpbo1u.build-temp -j 2 --target _C` completed and linked
       `_C.abi3.so`.
     - Synced the rebuilt `_C.abi3.so` into `vllm/_C.abi3.so` for the active
       editable tree; conda runtime check shows
       `weighted_reduce_op=True` and
       `env_unpermute=True`.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for the touched Python files.
     - Conda `python -m py_compile` passed for the touched Python files.
     - `git diff --check` passed for touched C++/Python/test files.
     - Conda pytest passed:
       `tests/kernels/moe/test_moe_permute_unpermute.py::test_sm70_single_token_weighted_reduce_matches_moe_unpermute`
       and
       `tests/kernels/moe/test_moe_permute_unpermute.py::test_sm70_single_token_moe_fastpath_matches_generic`.

     Status and next gate:
     - This is a real source-level migration/defaulting step for the
       AWQ/FP8 safe MoE single-token decode path, not a benchmark-only change.
     - It still needs a focused 35B-AWQ/FP8 `1k/256` route-hit/timing run to
       quantify decode gain and ensure the new weighted-reduce log appears
       under CUDA graph. Do not rerun broad quality sweeps before that route
       and speed evidence.

250. 35B-AWQ `1k/256` run with weighted-reduce default subpath,
     2026-06-02.

     Artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_weighted_reduce_i1024_o256_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_full_flash_weighted_reduce_i1024_o256_20260602.log`.

     Shape:
     - Same as item 244 clean baseline: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`,
       TP=4, no MTP, greedy, `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`,
       `max_model_len=4096`, `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, full Flash-V100, breakable CUDA graph,
       custom allreduce disabled.
     - Explicitly set
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_UNPERMUTE_FASTPATH=1`.

     Route evidence:
     - Verifier passed:
       `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-full-flash-v100
       --require-awq-moe-safe-route --require-awq-turbomind-dense`.
     - Logs include:
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE single-token weighted-reduce path enabled`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active`,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`,
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`,
       and route summary
       `{"decode_scalar_paged": 40, "metadata_none_zero_output": 100,
       "prefill_no_prefix_dense_flash": 30}`.
     - JSON records
       `sm70_moe_policy.single_token_unpermute_fastpath_effective=true`.

     Result versus item 244 clean baseline:
     - Pure decode TPS: `51.6425 -> 51.7909 tok/s`, about `+0.29%`.
     - TPOT: `0.0193639 -> 0.0193084 s`.
     - Prefill time: `1.3966 -> 1.3974 s`, unchanged.
     - Output TPS: `40.3985 -> 40.4852 tok/s`, about `+0.21%`.

     Decision:
     - This is a safe/defaultable source-level migration with real route hit,
       but it only removes a small final-reduce overhead. It does not explain
       the 35B-AWQ gap to the old 0.0.3 `~80 tok/s` no-MTP target.
     - Keep this subpath enabled, but do not spend more P0 time tuning generic
       unpermute. The next decode-speed work should move to higher-cost MoE
       pieces: W13/W2 launch fusion that preserves Type-A math, FP8/AWQ
       dense-stage scheduling, router/topk/sampler small-op fusion, or
       communication/top1 once quality is repaired.
     - This run is route/timing evidence only. It is not a new model-quality
       acceptance gate; the benchmark repeats still show non-identical token
       hashes, and the older clean baseline also showed repeat-to-repeat hash
       variance under the same greedy harness.

251. Default-off MoE single-token indexed dense-stage launch-reduction
     candidate, 2026-06-02.

     Source change:
     - Added AWQ/FP8 C++ ops that call TurboMind MoE GEMM once with
       `b_group_indices` instead of looping over `top_k` single-expert GEMM
       launches:
       `awq_moe_single_token_indexed_dense_w13_sm70_out`,
       `awq_moe_single_token_indexed_dense_stage_sm70_out`,
       `fp8_moe_single_token_indexed_dense_w13_sm70_out`, and
       `fp8_moe_single_token_indexed_dense_stage_sm70_out`.
     - Added Python facade wrappers in `vllm/_sm70_ops.py`.
     - Added default-off env gate
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_INDEXED_STAGE_FASTPATH=0`.
     - AWQ/FP8 single-token MoE apply paths use the indexed W13/W2 ops only
       when that env is explicitly enabled and the C++ ops are present.
     - Benchmark JSON policy now records
       `single_token_indexed_stage_fastpath_effective`.

     Why this was attempted:
     - The previous safe weighted-reduce default path only gave about `+0.29%`
       on 35B-AWQ `1k/256`.
     - Current safe single-token MoE still launches W13 once per active route
       and W2 once per active route. For `top_k=8`, that is 16 TurboMind GEMM
       launches per MoE layer per decode token, before SiLU/reduce overhead.
       Reducing that launch count is a higher-yield candidate for the 35B
       decode gap.

     Exactness evidence:
     - AWQ indexed candidate artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/exactness_awq_moe_single_token_indexed_35a3b_20260602.json`.
       Classification: `B-pending`.
       Stage max diffs:
       W13 gate-up `2.44140625e-4`, SiLU `6.103515625e-5`,
       W2 sorted output `1.52587890625e-5`.
     - AWQ control artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/exactness_awq_moe_single_token_dense_control_35a3b_20260602.json`.
       Existing per-route single-token dense path remains `A-pass` with
       `max_diff=0.0` for W13, SiLU, and W2.
     - FP8 indexed candidate artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/exactness_fp8_moe_single_token_indexed_35a3b_20260602.json`.
       Classification: `B-pending`.
       Stage max diffs:
       W13 gate-up `2.44140625e-4`, SiLU `6.103515625e-5`,
       W2 sorted output `1.52587890625e-5`.

     Decision:
     - The indexed path is not an indexing/prepare bug: the per-route
       single-token dense control is bitwise exact on the same real 35B-AWQ
       layer, while AWQ and FP8 indexed grouped dispatch show the same small
       fp16-level diffs.
     - Keep indexed-stage default-off. It cannot enter the default safe path
       until it passes a model-level quality gate and a real decode timing gate.
     - This source migration is still useful: it creates a concrete, env-gated
       launch-reduction candidate for the old compact/direct-reduce family
       without accepting the old quality-risk route.
     - Next P0 development should either:
       1. make this grouped/indexed stage bitwise match the per-route policy by
          forcing the same TurboMind per-route dispatch inside fewer framework
          launches, or
       2. prove the `B-pending` diffs are model-quality safe and large enough in
          speed to justify a guarded default, or
       3. move to another high-yield decode gap: router/topk fusion,
          sampler/logits/top1, custom allreduce repair, or FP8 KV cache.

     Checks:
     - Low-memory `_C` target rebuild succeeded and `vllm/_C.abi3.so` was
       refreshed.
     - Runtime op registration check shows all four indexed ops are present.
     - `uv run --no-sync ruff check` passed for touched Python files.
     - Conda `python -m py_compile` passed for touched Python files.
     - `git diff --check` passed.

252. Latest-tree SM70 source-build unblock plus FP8 KV e5m2 cache-op smoke,
     2026-06-02.

     Source changes:
     - Added `fp8_e5m2` support to
       `csrc/libtorch_stable/cache_kernels.cu::convert_fp8`, covering
       float/half/bfloat16 to uint8 and uint8 back to float/half/bfloat16.
       This is a real FP8 KV cache prerequisite; it is not merely FP8 weight
       quantization.
     - Added `tests/kernels/attention/test_cache.py::test_fp8_e5m2_conversion_smoke`
       as the minimal regression guard.
     - Extended the KV cache test factory in `vllm/utils/torch_utils.py` so
       `fp8_e4m3` and `fp8_e5m2` initialize through the matching
       `ops.convert_fp8(..., kv_dtype=...)` path instead of only supporting the
       legacy `fp8` alias.
     - Added
       `tests/kernels/attention/test_cache.py::test_reshape_and_cache_flash_fp8_e5m2_smoke`
       to prove `reshape_and_cache_flash` writes `fp8_e5m2` KV cache correctly
       for both NHD and HND cache layouts.
     - Added SM70 architecture gating around CUDA external projects in
       `CMakeLists.txt`: DeepGEMM, FlashMLA, QuTLASS, and upstream
       `vllm-flash-attn` are skipped when `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`.
       This prevents wasting build time fetching/compiling sm80/sm90/sm100
       projects that cannot run on V100. This does not skip the custom
       `flash-attention-v100/` package.
     - Mirrored that architecture gate in `setup.py` so setuptools no longer
       asks ninja for skipped targets such as `_vllm_fa2_C`, `_vllm_fa3_C`,
       `_vllm_fa4_cutedsl_C`, `_deep_gemm_C`, and FlashMLA targets on SM70.

     Build environment decision:
     - Do not use the Torch 2.9 SM70 env for the latest-tree stable extension.
       It fails on Torch stable API symbols such as
       `torch::headeronly::IntHeaderOnlyArrayRef` and `TORCH_BOX`.
     - Use `vllm-0.0.5-t210`: Torch `2.10.0+cu128`, CUDA 12.8, with SM70 in
       `torch.cuda.get_arch_list()`.
     - Build command shape used the low-memory policy:
       `MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 NVCC_THREADS=1
       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0 VLLM_TARGET_DEVICE=cuda
       python setup.py build_ext --inplace
       --build-temp build/temp.linux-x86_64-cpython-312-t210-sm70`.

     Result:
     - The SM70 latest-tree CUDA extension build completed successfully for
       `_C`, `_C_stable_libtorch`, `_moe_C`, `cumem_allocator`, `spinloop`, and
       `triton_kernels`.
     - Optional Rust `vllm-rs` did not build because system `protoc` is missing;
       setup treated this as an optional failure. This is not a blocker for the
       CUDA SM70 kernels validated here.
     - Import check passed:
       `import vllm._C; import vllm._C_stable_libtorch; import vllm._moe_C;
       import flash_attn_v100`.
       `flash_attn_v100` resolves to the latest-tree custom package under
       `flash-attention-v100/`.
     - Direct CUDA FP8 e5m2 roundtrip check passed:
       `torch_equal=True`, `max_diff=0.0`, encoded dtype `torch.uint8`.
     - Pytest checks passed:
       `pytest -q tests/kernels/attention/test_cache.py::test_fp8_e5m2_conversion_smoke
       tests/kernels/attention/test_cache.py::test_reshape_and_cache_flash_fp8_e5m2_smoke`
       returned `3 passed`, covering conversion plus Flash cache write for NHD
       and HND.

     Decision:
     - Latest-tree SM70 development should continue from the Torch 2.10+cu128
       conda environment, not Torch 2.9.
     - Keep the sm80+ external-project gates. They are build hygiene for V100
       and do not remove any intended custom SM70 Flash-V100/TurboMind route.
     - FP8 KV cache work can now move past the basic e5m2 conversion-op gap and
       into real route-hit, Flash-V100 cache-source correctness, model-quality,
       and decode-speed validation.

253. Flash-V100 direct `fp8_e5m2` paged-KV read smoke, 2026-06-02.

     Source change:
     - Added
       `tests/kernels/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_varlen_layout.py::test_sm70_flash_v100_fp8_e5m2_paged_kv_read_exact`.
     - The test constructs a single-token paged KV cache stored as `uint8`
       `fp8_e5m2`, then calls the custom Flash-V100 kernels directly:
       `flash_attn_decode_paged` and `flash_attn_prefill_paged`.
     - The expected output is the same cache value dequantized through
       `ops.convert_fp8(..., kv_dtype="fp8_e5m2")`. With `seq_len=1`, attention
       should read exactly that V row, so any mismatch is a cache-source,
       dtype, block-table, scale, or kernel-read bug rather than a model-level
       quality question.

     Result:
     - Initial test shape used `head_dim=16` and failed with
       `Unsupported head_dim for paged decode: 16`; this was a test-construction
       issue, not a kernel correctness failure. The test now uses supported
       `head_dim=64`.
     - Minimal verification passed:
       `pytest -q tests/kernels/attention/test_cache.py::test_fp8_e5m2_conversion_smoke
       tests/kernels/attention/test_cache.py::test_reshape_and_cache_flash_fp8_e5m2_smoke
       tests/kernels/attention/test_sm70_flash_v100_varlen_layout.py::test_sm70_flash_v100_fp8_e5m2_paged_kv_read_exact`
       returned `4 passed`.
     - `python -m py_compile` passed for touched Python files.
     - `git diff --check` passed.

     Decision:
     - The FP8 KV path is no longer only a write-cache smoke: custom Flash-V100
       decode and paged-prefill kernels can directly read `fp8_e5m2` cache in a
       Type-A single-token setup.
     - Remaining FP8 KV work is now model/runtime level: prove real
       `--kv-cache-dtype fp8_e5m2` route-hit under CUDA graph, confirm no mixed
       Triton fallback, classify any model-level nonzero diff, and measure
       35B-FP8 `1k/256` pure decode against the old-tree baseline.

254. Flash-V100/FP8 KV route-hit verifier tightened, 2026-06-02.

     Problem:
     - The previous `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route` gate was too weak for
       production evidence. It required full-Flash policy JSON plus an FP8 KV
       decode log, but did not prove the FP8 cache write route and did not
       reject dense/reference/debug decode bridges.
     - This could let a mixed or debug artifact look like a valid Flash-V100
       FP8 KV speed/quality artifact.
     - Important correction: normal no-prefix prefill should use Flash over raw
       K/V and then write FP8 KV cache; it should not be forced into the
       no-prefix paged-cache diagnostic path just to emit an FP8 prefill-read
       log.

     Source change:
     - `benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` now requires the scalar paged
       decode log for `--require-full-flash-v100` by matching
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`, not the broader
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode` substring.
     - `--require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route` now requires:
       full-Flash policy JSON, `fp8_kv_cache_requested_effective=True`,
       `fp8_kv_cache_full_flash_policy=True`, the C++ FP8 cache write log,
       the FP8 KV Flash decode log, and `route=scalar_paged`.
     - The same gate now rejects no-prefix paged-cache diagnostics,
       decode-as-paged-prefill, dense-cache, and dense-reference debug bridge
       logs/routes.
     - `benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` metadata
       notes were updated to state the same evidence rule: policy JSON alone
       is not route-hit; FP8 KV route-hit needs Flash-V100 prefill, FP8 cache
       write, and FP8 KV scalar-paged decode logs.

     Verification:
     - `python -m py_compile benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py` passed in
       `vllm-0.0.5-t210`; the same syntax check passed for the two benchmark
       metadata scripts.
     - Minimal verifier fixtures passed:
       a full-Flash FP8 KV scalar-paged positive artifact returned rc=0;
       a missing-FP8-cache-write artifact returned rc=1; a dense-reference debug
       bridge artifact returned rc=1; a no-prefix paged-cache diagnostic prefill
       artifact returned rc=1.

     Decision:
     - Future 35B AWQ/FP8 speed evidence must pass the strengthened verifier
       when claiming Flash-V100 FP8 KV route-hit. A benchmark that only proves
       Triton prefill plus Flash decode, or a debug bridge route, is not an
       accepted production baseline.

255. 35B-FP8 explicit `fp8_e5m2` KV cache checkpoint gate, 2026-06-02.

     Problem:
     - Latest vLLM still had a hard initialization error:
       `fp8_e5m2 kv-cache is not supported with fp8 checkpoints`.
     - That blocks the target route for local
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` when we want explicit
       `--kv-cache-dtype fp8_e5m2` plus Flash-V100 decode.
     - The local 35B-FP8 and 27B-FP8 safetensors indexes do not contain
       `k_scale`, `v_scale`, `q_scale`, `prob_scale`, or `kv_scale` keys.
       Their `quantization_config` is FP8 e4m3 weight quantization, not a
       calibrated e5m2 KV-scale checkpoint.

     Source change:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/attention/attention.py` now allows
       `fp8_e5m2` KV cache with FP8 checkpoints only on the SM70 Flash-V100
       path and only when the KV scale loader uses `BaseKVCacheMethod`'s normal
       processing. Other platforms and specialized KV-scale loaders still keep
       the protective error.
     - The allow path marks the layer with
       `_force_unit_fp8_e5m2_kv_scales=True`.
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/kv_cache.py` consumes that mark
       after weight loading and forces `_k_scale`, `_v_scale`, `_q_scale`, and
       `_prob_scale` to `1.0`, then deletes the checkpoint-loadable scale
       parameters. This prevents e4m3 checkpoint scales from being reused for
       e5m2 KV cache.

     Verification:
     - `python -m py_compile` passed for touched attention/quantization files
       and `tests/quantization/test_fp8.py`.
     - Added and passed two CPU-level tests:
       `test_fp8_e5m2_checkpoint_override_forces_unit_scales` and
       `test_fp8_e5m2_checkpoint_override_init_gate`.

     Decision:
     - This removes a real 35B-FP8 route blocker without making e5m2 globally
       accepted for every FP8 checkpoint format. The next useful FP8 KV step is
       a real short 35B-FP8 CUDA-graph route-hit run with the strengthened
       verifier, then a same-criterion pure-decode timing run.

256. 35B-FP8 Flash-V100 CUDA-graph route candidate: no-compile full-decode graph, 2026-06-02.

     Problem:
     - The first non-eager 35B-FP8 `fp8_e5m2` route-hit attempt used latest
       vLLM's default `VLLM_COMPILE + FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` CUDA graph mode. It
       selected Flash-V100 and loaded the model, but stalled after
       `torch.compile took 65.21 s` with repeated shared-memory broadcast waits
       before `_dummy_run model forward exit`.
     - A second attempt forced `mode=NONE + FULL_DECODE_ONLY` manually. That
       avoided Inductor, but with `max_num_batched_tokens=512` profile dummy
       forward still spent minutes in Triton `ptxas` JIT with GPU utilization at
       0. This is startup/profile JIT pressure, not a Flash decode correctness
       failure.

     Source change:
     - Added SM70 model-layer tracing in `qwen3_next.py`, gated by
       `VLLM_SM70_PROFILE_TRACE`, to identify profile/capture hotspots without
       changing computation.
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_DECODE_GRAPH_NO_COMPILE=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_DECODE_GRAPH_CAPTURE_SIZE=<n>`.
     - When that env is set on exact SM70 with Flash-V100 enabled and
       `enforce_eager=false`, `VllmConfig` now sets
       `compilation_config.mode=NONE`,
       `cudagraph_mode=FULL_DECODE_ONLY`, and default capture sizes
       `1..n`.
     - Both SM70 benchmark scripts now record this graph policy in their JSON
       metadata.

     Verification:
     - `python -m py_compile` passed for `vllm/envs.py`,
       `vllm/config/vllm.py`, `qwen3_next.py`, and the two benchmark scripts.
     - Manual no-compile/full-decode route-hit, still using explicit engine
       args, passed on `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`,
       TP4, `input_len=16`, `output_len=2`, `max_model_len=1024`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=64`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`.
       Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_e5m2_layertrace_i16_o2_20260602.json`.
     - Env-only no-compile/full-decode route-hit passed with no
       `compilation_config` engine arg. Log showed:
       `Using SM70 Flash-V100 no-compile decode CUDA graph policy:
       mode=NONE, cudagraph_mode=FULL_DECODE_ONLY, capture_size=1`.
       Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_e5m2_env_no_compile_graph_i16_o2_20260602.json`.
     - Strengthened verifier passed for the env-only artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/verify_decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_e5m2_env_no_compile_graph_i16_o2_20260602.json`.
       Required logs were present: Flash-V100 prefill, Flash-V100 scalar-paged
       decode, C++ FP8 KV cache write, FP8 KV decode, and `route=scalar_paged`.
       Rejected Triton/debug bridge logs were absent.

     Decision:
     - This is a valid 35B-FP8 short route-hit for full Flash-V100 + FP8 KV
       decode under CUDA graph, and it proves the new env can replace manual
       engine-arg config.
     - It is not a speed baseline: `i16/o2`, profile trace on, and short output.
       The recorded steady decode was about `41.9 tok/s`.
     - Next development target is to recover accepted 35B speed using this
       production-candidate graph policy, then scale to same-criterion old-tree
       baselines (`1k/256` and the user's final 35B AWQ/FP8 criteria). The
       default latest `VLLM_COMPILE + FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` path should not be
       retried for 35B-FP8 route-hit unless there is a concrete compile/JIT
       fix to test.

257. Top1-only custom allreduce decoupled from hidden-state custom allreduce,
     2026-06-02.

     Problem:
     - The previous custom allreduce model-quality failure was caused by
       enabling production TP custom allreduce globally. That changed
       hidden-state reductions and produced token/logit mismatches, so it
       cannot be used as the default route.
     - The old-tree `top1_argmax` route is a narrower greedy-LM-head
       communication optimization: it only reduces `(local_max_value,
       token_id)` pairs. It should not require changing hidden-state
       allreduce semantics.
     - In the latest tree before this change, `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=1`
       still required the custom allreduce communicator to exist, which in
       practice coupled top1 to `disable_custom_all_reduce=false`.

     Source change:
     - `vllm/distributed/device_communicators/cuda_communicator.py` now tracks
       `use_top1_custom_ar` separately from `use_custom_allreduce`.
     - When `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=1` and
       `--disable-custom-all-reduce` remains true, the communicator initializes
       the CustomAllreduce IPC/signaling resources only for top1 argmax.
       Normal hidden-state `all_reduce()` and `all_reduce_sum2()` still require
       `use_custom_allreduce=true` before dispatching to CUSTOM.
     - Runtime log added:
       `SM70 custom top1 argmax resources enabled ... while hidden-state
       custom all-reduce dispatch remains disabled`.
     - `benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now
       record `hidden_state_custom_allreduce_effective` and
       `top1_only_custom_allreduce_effective` so top1-only evidence is not
       confused with accepted production custom allreduce.

     Verification:
     - Syntax check passed:
       `python -m py_compile
       vllm/distributed/device_communicators/cuda_communicator.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - Minimal 35B-FP8 CUDA-graph route-hit passed:
       `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`, TP4, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`, `input_len=16`, `output_len=4`,
       no-compile full-decode graph, `--disable-custom-all-reduce`,
       `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=1`.
       Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_top1only_custom_ar_i16_o4_20260602.json`.
     - Observed runtime evidence:
       `Using ['PYNCCL'] all-reduce backends ... group 'tp:0'`;
       `TP all-reduce trace backend=pynccl ... custom_enabled=False` for
       profile, prefill, and decode shapes;
       `SM70 custom top1 argmax resources enabled ... hidden-state custom
       all-reduce dispatch remains disabled`;
       `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`;
       `SM70 custom top1 argmax path enabled`;
       Flash-V100 prefill/decode and FP8 KV cache route logs still appeared.

     Result:
     - The top1 custom AR route is now decoupled from the unsafe production
       custom allreduce route.
     - Short-run steady decode was `47.39 tok/s`, but `i16/o4` is not a speed
       baseline. This item proves route separation, not old-tree performance
       recovery.
     - Same-criterion single-run speed check:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_top1only_custom_ar_i1024_o256_r1_20260602.json`
       and matching `.log`.
       Compared with the prior no-top1 full-Flash/no-compile graph artifact
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_full_flash_fp8kv_e5m2_env_no_compile_graph_i1024_o256_r2_20260602.json`,
       steady decode moved from `47.85597339952235 tok/s` to
       `48.19404611229799 tok/s`, about `+0.71%`.
       Prefill stayed effectively unchanged (`0.9852 s` vs `0.9849 s`).
     - The 1k/256 log preserved the intended separation:
       `Using ['PYNCCL'] all-reduce backends ... group 'tp:0'`;
       `SM70 custom top1 argmax resources enabled ... hidden-state custom
       all-reduce dispatch remains disabled`;
       `SM70 custom top1 argmax path enabled`;
       full Flash/FP8-KV route logs were present.

     Decision:
     - Keep `VLLM_SM70_TOP1_CUSTOM_AR=0` by default until it passes a focused
       token/logit quality check and a same-criterion speed check.
     - Since the measured gain is only about `+0.71%`, top1-only custom AR is
       not the main P0 speed-recovery path. Keep the decoupled code because it
       is architecturally correct and may still matter for greedy-only serving,
       but move the next development cycle back to MoE launch/reduce recovery
       and quality-safe hidden-state communication replacement.
     - Do not re-enable global custom allreduce until its model-level quality
       failure is root-caused.

258. FP8 MoE indexed dense-stage speed recovery is large but still
     quality-gated, 2026-06-02.

     Purpose:
     - Determine whether the default-off
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_INDEXED_STAGE_FASTPATH=1` route is worth
       spending quality/debug effort on.
     - This is the launch-reduction candidate from item 251. It replaces the
       per-route W13/W2 TurboMind GEMM loop with a grouped/indexed dispatch
       using `b_group_indices`, so it is not Type-A exact yet.

     Speed artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_indexed_stage_i1024_o256_r1_20260602.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_indexed_stage_i1024_o256_r1_20260602.log`.
     - Criteria: `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`, TP4, no-MTP, greedy,
       `input/output=1024/256`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       full `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`,
       no-compile full-decode CUDA graph, `--disable-custom-all-reduce`.
     - Route logs present:
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token indexed dense-stage path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token weighted-reduce path enabled`,
       Flash-V100 prefill/decode, FP8 KV cache decode, and PYNCCL-only TP
       allreduce.

     Speed result:
     - Prior safe baseline:
       `47.85597339952235 tok/s` steady decode.
     - Indexed-stage:
       `79.55409613397676 tok/s` steady decode,
       `tpot_seconds_mean=0.012570062996076327`.
     - Gain: about `+66.24%`, enough to reach the user's expected old-tree
       `~80 tok/s` band for the tested 35B-FP8 greedy 1k/256 criterion.
     - Prefill stayed effectively unchanged (`0.9852 s` baseline vs
       `0.9848 s` indexed).

     Quality artifact:
     - Base dump:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_base_safe_i32_20260602.json`.
     - Indexed dump:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_indexed_stage_i32_20260602.json`.
     - Compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/compare_model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_base_vs_indexed_stage_i32_20260602.json`.
     - Sampler logits dirs:
       `sampler_logits_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_base_safe_i32_20260602/` and
       `sampler_logits_qwen36_35a3b_fp8_indexed_stage_i32_20260602/`.

     Quality result:
     - Greedy 32-token output IDs matched exactly: `equal=true`,
       `token_equal=true`.
     - Prompt logprobs and prompt perplexity matched exactly:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.0`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.0`.
     - Generated-token logprob diff was small:
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.005525046959519386`.
     - Sampler argmax was stable across all dumps:
       `sampler_logits_all_argmax_equal=true`, `num_argmax_mismatch=0`.
