     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_legacy_fused_sigmoid_gdn_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Same shape as the latest 27B-FP8 baseline: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4,
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`,
       CUDA graph on, maxlen `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`, input/output
       `128/512`, greedy no-MTP.
     - Only deliberate route change:
       `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE=0`.

     Route and startup evidence:
     - Logs still hit the intended production route: Flash-V100 prefill,
       Flash-V100 scalar paged decode, FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path, fused
       LM-head top1 path, production custom allreduce, and
       `VLLM_COMPILE + FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` graph capture.
     - JSON records `packed_recurrent_decode_effective=false` and
       `qwen3_next_legacy_fused_sigmoid_effective=true`.
     - `torch.compile took 63.04s`, `Initial profiling/warmup run took 1.50s`,
       graph capture took `71s`, and engine init profile/create-kv/warmup took
       `81.57s`. The P0 `473s` startup issue did not regress.

     Timing:
     - Latest packed-recurrent baseline:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.4725 tok/s`, `prefill_time=0.1375s`.
     - Latest legacy fused-sigmoid rollback:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.3470 tok/s`, `prefill_time=0.1419s`,
       `output_tps=53.6310`.
     - This is effectively neutral/slightly slower and remains far below the
       old 0.0.3 `70.9624 tok/s` same-criterion API baseline.

     Quality/status:
     - The output token hash changed from the packed-recurrent latest baseline
       (`913f6943...`) to the legacy fused-sigmoid run (`629edaf3...`), so this
       rollback is not a safe-default quality improvement candidate.
     - Classification: `rejected-for-speed` and `B-pending` for quality. Do not
       spend more time on packed-recurrent rollback as the 27B-FP8 speed gap.
       The next 27B-FP8 suspects are latest engine scheduling / compile graph
       partitioning, per-token launch count, GDN wrapper overhead, or another
       old-tree SM70 decode scheduling difference.

     Artifact-recording fix:
     - During this run the logs correctly showed mixed/piecewise graph capture,
       and the env did not set
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_DECODE_ONLY_CAPTURE`, but the benchmark JSON
       reported `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_decode_only_capture_effective=true`.
     - Root cause: `benchmark_sm70_decode.py` and
       `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` still used the old default `True` for
       the reporting helper, while `vllm/envs.py` had already changed the real
       runtime default to `False`.
     - Fixed the reporting helper default to `False` in both benchmark scripts.
       Static check passed:
       `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.

282. 2026-06-03 Qwen3.5 GDN `mixed_qkv.contiguous()` old-tree parity
     candidate rejected and reverted.

     Source difference investigated:
     - Old 0.0.3 `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet.forward()` made `mixed_qkv`
       contiguous after splitting the `in_proj_qkvz` output. Latest
       `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet.forward_cuda()` split the same tensor but did not
       compact `mixed_qkv`.
     - This looked like a plausible layout-only old-tree parity gap because a
       split along the last dimension can preserve the parent row stride.

     Code candidate:
     - Temporarily added `mixed_qkv = mixed_qkv.contiguous()` in
       `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py` before calling
       `torch.ops.vllm.qwen_gdn_attention_core(...)`.
     - Static check passed before the run:
       `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py`.

     Artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdn_mixed_qkv_contiguous_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdn_mixed_qkv_contiguous_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Same shape: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, Flash-V100, CUDA graph on,
       maxlen `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`, input/output `128/512`,
       greedy no-MTP.

     Result:
     - Decode speed did not improve:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.432 tok/s` vs latest baseline
       `54.473 tok/s` and old 0.0.3 target `70.962 tok/s`.
     - Startup became unacceptable with a fresh Inductor cache:
       `load_seconds=595.19s`, engine init profile/create-kv/warmup
       `543.08s`, while `torch.compile` itself was only `63.92s` and
       graph capture `69s`. The long window was Triton/Inductor kernel
       compilation before KV cache sizing, observed through changing `ptxas`
       child processes and cache files under
       `/tmp/torchinductor_ymzx_gdn_contig_20260603`.
     - Output token hash changed to `629edaf3...`, while the accepted latest
       packed-recurrent baseline was `913f6943...`. Because the candidate did
       not produce speed and changed output, there is no reason to pursue a
       quality rescue for this line.

     Decision:
     - Classification: `rejected` / `rejected-for-speed` / not safe-default.
     - The temporary `mixed_qkv.contiguous()` source change was reverted.
     - Static check after revert passed:
       `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`.
     - Do not repeat this as a 27B-FP8 recovery path. The 27B-FP8 gap remains
       in another old/latest scheduling or kernel boundary.

283. 2026-06-03 Restored old-tree `eliminate_noops=True` compile-graph
     parity, but it does not recover the 27B-FP8 speed gap.

     Source difference:
     - Old 0.0.3 Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 engine logs used
       `VLLM_COMPILE + FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` with `pass_config` including
       `eliminate_noops=True`.
     - Latest 27B-FP8 artifacts were already using the same compile/cudagraph
       lane, but the effective serialized `pass_config` did not show no-op
       elimination. `benchmark_combo_kernel=False` had already been tested and
       excluded, so this was the remaining old compile-preset difference worth
       a single targeted timing run.

     Code change:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_ELIMINATE_NOOPS` in `vllm/envs.py`.
     - In the SM70 Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile graph branch of
       `vllm/config/vllm.py`, set
       `self.compilation_config.pass_config.eliminate_noops = True` when that
       env is enabled.
     - After the token-hash audit below, the env default was changed to `0`.
       It is now an explicit diagnostic/old-parity switch only:
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_ELIMINATE_NOOPS=1`.

     Static/config evidence:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/envs.py vllm/config/vllm.py` passed.
     - A no-model config check printed:
       `Using eliminate_noops=True for SM70 Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile graph
       parity`, `mode=VLLM_COMPILE`,
       `cudagraph_mode=FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`, and
       `benchmark_combo_kernel=False`.

     Timing artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_eliminate_noops_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, maxlen `16384`, max batched tokens
       `8192`, input/output `128/512`, greedy no-MTP, Flash-V100,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`, CUDA graph on,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`.
     - Runtime route evidence in the run output/JSON policy still showed:
       Flash-V100 prefill, Flash-V100 scalar paged decode, FP8 TurboMind W8A16
       dense path, fused LM-head top1, production custom allreduce, and
       0.0.3 compile graph policy.

     Startup/P0 evidence from this run:
     - `torch.compile took 62.86s in total`.
     - `Initial profiling/warmup run took 1.45s`.
     - `Graph capturing finished in 68 secs`.
     - Engine init profile/create-kv/warmup took `78.43s`
       (`compilation: 63.40s`).
     - This confirms the P0 `473s` profile/warmup issue did not regress under
       the 27B-FP8 production compile graph lane.

     Speed result:
     - Latest baseline:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.4725 tok/s` in
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_0dot3_compile_graph_tm_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - New `eliminate_noops=True` run:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.5060 tok/s`, `output_tps=53.8123`,
       `prefill_time=0.1370s`.
     - Old 0.0.3 target remains `70.9624 tok/s` in
       `qwen36_fp8_sm70_tp4_128_tm.json`.
     - The delta is only noise-level (`+0.06%` vs latest), so no-op
       elimination is not the missing 27B-FP8 54 -> 70 tok/s fast path.

     Quality/status:
     - A later token-hash audit showed this run changed greedy output hash from
       the earlier fixed-dispatch baseline `913f6943...` to `629edaf3...`
       while providing only noise-level speed change.
     - Therefore this is not safe-default. Classification:
       `rejected-for-speed` and `B-pending`/quality-pending for output changes.
     - The env default was changed to `0`, and
       `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/envs.py vllm/config/vllm.py` passed.
     - Do not repeat this as the 27B-FP8 speed-gap hypothesis. Keep it only as
       an explicit old-tree parity diagnostic; continue root-cause on the
       remaining old/latest runtime scheduling or kernel-boundary difference.

284. 2026-06-03 Tested old-tree `gdn_attention_core` custom-op boundary and
     mutation contract; no 27B-FP8 speed recovery, candidate reverted.

     Hypothesis:
     - Old 0.0.3 Qwen3.5 GDN used `torch.ops.vllm.gdn_attention_core(...)`
       and split on `vllm::gdn_attention_core`.
     - Latest Qwen3.5 GDN uses `torch.ops.vllm.qwen_gdn_attention_core(...)`
       and split on `vllm::qwen_gdn_attention_core`.
     - Latest `qwen_gdn_attention_core` declares
       `mutates_args=["a_or_z_out", "core_attn_out"]` because the ROCm fast
       kernel can write `z`, while the CUDA/SM70 standard path only writes
       `core_attn_out`. This looked like a plausible Inductor aliasing /
       graph-boundary slowdown relative to old 0.0.3.

     Candidate implementation:
     - Temporarily registered a legacy `vllm::gdn_attention_core` alias in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` with
       the old mutation contract: only `core_attn_out` mutated.
     - Temporarily changed `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet.forward_cuda()` to call that
       alias.
     - Temporarily added `vllm::gdn_attention_core` back into
       `CompilationConfig._attention_ops`.
     - Static checks passed, and an import check confirmed both old/new GDN ops
       existed and both were present in the split list.

     Timing artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_legacy_gdn_core_alias_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_legacy_gdn_core_alias_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Same 27B-FP8 shape: TP4, maxlen `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`,
       input/output `128/512`, greedy no-MTP, Flash-V100, CUDA graph on,
       0.0.3 compile graph lane.

     Result:
     - Runtime config showed the split list including
       `vllm::gdn_attention_core`, so the candidate was actually tested.
     - P0 startup stayed healthy: `torch.compile took 62.50s`,
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 1.31s`, graph capture `68s`, engine
       init profile/create-kv/warmup `78.41s`.
     - Decode speed did not recover:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.4434 tok/s`, `output_tps=53.7367`,
       `prefill_time=0.1399s`.
     - This is slightly below the latest baseline `54.4725` and far below the
       old 0.0.3 target `70.9624`.
     - Output token hash stayed `629edaf3...`, so the change did not obviously
       alter generated tokens in this greedy smoke, but it also did not provide
       speed.

     Decision:
     - Classification: `rejected-for-speed`.
     - The temporary source changes were reverted.
     - Static check after revert passed:
       `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py vllm/config/compilation.py`.
     - Do not repeat old-op alias / mutation-contract restoration as the
       27B-FP8 54 -> 70 recovery path.

285. 2026-06-03 27B-FP8 speed gap root-cause: FP8 small-shape dynamic tuning
     recovers old 0.0.3 speed, but is not quality-accepted yet.

     Source/behavior finding:
     - Old 0.0.3 FP8 dense W8A16 path used the generic SM70 small-shape tuning
       selector. In old source, unset `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES` meant
       dynamic measure enabled.
     - Latest tree split FP8 tuning into
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES`; source default is fixed dispatch
       because earlier FP8 MoE exactness work found dynamic tune could produce
       nonzero Type-A diffs.
     - The 27B-FP8 speed gap was therefore not GDN wrapper, LM-head, combo
       kernel, no-op elimination, or benchmark accounting. It was the latest
       safe fixed-dispatch FP8 dense selector leaving old dynamic tuning speed
       on the table.

     Speed artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_tune_small_shapes_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_tune_small_shapes_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, maxlen `16384`, max batched tokens
       `8192`, input/output `128/512`, greedy no-MTP, Flash-V100,
       CUDA graph on, 0.0.3 compile graph lane.
     - Env difference from the latest safe baseline:
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1`.

     Route/startup:
     - Runtime policy recorded
       `sm70_tune_policy.fp8_dynamic_measure_enabled=true` and
       `fp8_safe_default_selector_effective=false`.
     - Flash-V100 prefill/decode, FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense, fused LM-head
       top1, production custom allreduce, and CUDA graph capture all hit.
     - P0 stayed healthy: `torch.compile took 62.94s`,
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 1.88s`, graph capture `69s`, engine
       init profile/create-kv/warmup `79.37s`.

     Speed result:
     - Latest fixed-dispatch baseline:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.4725`, `output_tps=53.7797`.
     - Dynamic FP8 small-shape tuning:
       `steady_decode_tps=71.8713`, `output_tps=70.6516`,
       `prefill_time=0.1346s`.
     - Old 0.0.3 target:
       `decode_tps=70.9624`, `output_tps=69.6344` in
       `qwen36_fp8_sm70_tp4_128_tm.json`.
     - This recovers and slightly exceeds the old 0.0.3 27B-FP8 decode
       baseline under the same token-length/TP/Flash/CUDA-graph shape.

     Quality evidence:
     - Greedy token hash differs from the earlier latest fixed-dispatch
       baseline `913f6943...`; the tune run produced `629edaf3...`.
     - A first op-level FP8 dense exactness check on real 27B weights
       (`model.language_model.layers.0.linear_attn.out_proj`, `m=1,2,8`) was
       run with and without dynamic tune:
       `sm70_fp8_dense_tune1_exactness_qwen36_27b_layer0_linear_attn_out_proj_20260603.json`
       and
       `sm70_fp8_dense_fixed_exactness_qwen36_27b_layer0_linear_attn_out_proj_20260603.json`.
     - Both compare against a torch matmul oracle and report nonzero max diff
       up to `0.00390625`; this oracle does not yet isolate fixed-vs-dynamic
       schedule differences. It proves the route is not an A-class bitwise
       pass, but more targeted fixed-vs-dynamic diagnostics are still needed
       before a B-class accept/reject decision.

     Decision:
     - Classification: `experimental-unsafe` / `B-pending`.
     - This is the concrete switch that recovers 27B-FP8 old-tree speed, but it
       cannot enter the default route yet because greedy output changed and the
       fixed-vs-dynamic numeric source has not been classified.
     - Next required work if we want this speed safely: build a direct
       fixed-dispatch vs dynamic-dispatch FP8 dense diagnostic with identical
       inputs and real 27B layers, then do model-level greedy/logprob/perplexity
       checks. If the diff is only bounded B-class fp16 reduction noise and
       model quality passes, consider a model-specific default; otherwise keep
       fixed dispatch as safe default and treat the old 0.0.3 speed as
       quality-trading.

286. 2026-06-03 Follow-up on item 285: removing `eliminate_noops` does not
     change the 27B-FP8 small-shape speed recovery, and direct FP8 dense
     fixed-vs-dynamic checks do not find an op-level dense GEMM diff.

     Speed artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_tune_small_shapes_no_eliminate_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_tune_small_shapes_no_eliminate_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, maxlen `16384`, max batched tokens
       `8192`, input/output `128/512`, greedy no-MTP, Flash-V100,
       CUDA graph on, production 0.0.3 compile graph lane.
     - Env: `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_ELIMINATE_NOOPS=0`.
     - P0 startup stayed healthy: `torch.compile took 63.18s`,
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 2.03s`, mixed graph capture `65s`,
       decode graph capture `1.94s`.
     - Decode speed:
       `steady_decode_tps=71.9069`, `output_tps=70.6184`,
       `prefill_time=0.1417s`. This still exceeds the old 0.0.3
       `qwen36_fp8_sm70_tp4_128_tm.json` baseline
       (`decode_tps=70.9624`, `output_tps=69.6344`), so `eliminate_noops` is
       not required for the 27B-FP8 speed recovery.

     Direct fixed-vs-dynamic dense evidence:
     - New diagnostic script:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_fp8_fixed_vs_dynamic.py`.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_fp8_dense_fixed_vs_dynamic_qwen36_27b_all_candidate_layers_20260603.json`.
     - It loads real Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 safetensors, compares fixed dispatch
       (`VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=0`) against dynamic warm/reuse dispatch
       (`=1`) with identical random inputs, and does not use torch matmul as the
       oracle.
     - Coverage/result: `53` real language-model FP8 dense layers,
       `159` cases for `m=1,2,8`; `failures=0`,
       `max_fixed_vs_dynamic_diff=0.0`, `max_fixed_repeat_diff=0.0`.
       This removes the earlier item-285 concern that the dense FP8
       small-shape selector itself necessarily changes numeric output.

     Remaining quality gap:
     - The full-model greedy token hash still differs from latest fixed
       dispatch:
       fixed `913f69431ce3048fa83d388935550afaf883884dd4d27761aea1e59ec479cf1d`
       vs dynamic
       `629edaf3fb22c59b5237e0b041a351403e36717f62f19037edc48d38b17b2be1`.
       The first generated token already differs, under the same prompt hash
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`
       and greedy sampling (`temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=-1`).
     - Classification stays `B-pending` / not safe-default. The speed root
       cause is confirmed, and dense op-level Type-A evidence is clean, but the
       model-level token divergence must be classified before defaulting this
       switch. Next useful check is not another 27B speed run; it is either
       old-tree same-prompt token capture or a latest-tree logits/logprob
       comparison at the first decode step to identify whether the token flip
       is old-tree parity, graph-state, sampler/top1, or another non-dense
       path.

287. 2026-06-03 Tested the cross-route tune-cache contamination hypothesis for
     27B-FP8; it was not the token-divergence root cause.

     Source finding and fix:
     - `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` previously used one shared
       `dense_tuned_shapes` key containing only `(device, m, n, k, group_size)`.
       It did not distinguish generic F16 dense, AWQ dense, and FP8 dense.
       `moe_tuned_shapes` had the same issue for generic/AWQ/FP8 MoE.
     - Added `TuneKeyKind` to both dense and MoE tune keys, and passed separate
       kinds through `select_dense_dispatch_policy_impl()` and
       `select_moe_dispatch_policy_impl()`. This is a low-risk scheduler-cache
       hygiene fix: it does not change GEMM arithmetic or kernel code, only
       prevents one route from marking another route's shape as already tuned.

     Build/environment note:
     - Rebuilt the extension with `MAX_JOBS=1`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=1`,
       `NVCC_THREADS=1`, `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0`.
     - The first editable install was accidentally run without `--no-deps`,
       which upgraded Torch to `2.11.0+cu130`; that wheel does not support
       SM70 and made `_C` unusable. The environment was restored to
       `torch==2.10.0+cu128`, `torchvision==0.25.0+cu128`,
       `torchaudio==2.10.0+cu128`.
     - Removing the accidentally installed cu13 NCCL exposed a Torch import
       mismatch, so the final working state uses `nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.28.9`.
       Verified:
       `torch 2.10.0+cu128`, CUDA `12.8`, device capability `(7, 0)`,
       `vllm._C` imports, and `NCCLLibrary().ncclGetVersion()==2.28.9`.
       Do not repeat a dependency-resolving editable install here; use
       `pip install -e . --no-build-isolation --no-deps` for future rebuilds.

     Validation after the tune-key fix:
     - Op-level artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_fp8_dense_fixed_vs_dynamic_qwen36_27b_kind_key_fix_20260603.json`.
     - Coverage/result is unchanged and clean: `53` real 27B-FP8 dense layers,
       `159` `m=1,2,8` cases, `failures=0`,
       `max_fixed_vs_dynamic_diff=0.0`, `max_fixed_repeat_diff=0.0`.
     - Full 27B-FP8 targeted speed artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_tune_small_shapes_kind_key_fix2_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_tune_small_shapes_kind_key_fix2_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Route hit: NCCL `2.28.9`, production custom allreduce
       `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']`, `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`,
       Flash-V100 decode path active, Flash-V100 prefill not routed to Triton,
       0.0.3 compile graph policy active.
     - Startup with warm compile cache:
       `torch.compile took 10.91s`,
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 0.56s`, mixed graph capture about
       `12s`, decode graph capture about `0.14s`. This is good P0 evidence but
       should be read as warm-cache timing, not a cold-start replacement for the
       earlier `2.03s` profile/warmup evidence.
     - Speed remains recovered:
       `steady_decode_tps=71.8632`, `output_tps=70.6075`,
       `prefill_time=0.1385s`.

     Decision:
     - The tune-key kind separation is worth keeping as a scheduler-cache
       correctness fix, but it is not the 27B-FP8 token-hash fix.
     - Token hash remains
       `629edaf3fb22c59b5237e0b041a351403e36717f62f19037edc48d38b17b2be1`
       and still differs from the fixed-dispatch latest baseline
       `913f69431ce3048fa83d388935550afaf883884dd4d27761aea1e59ec479cf1d`.
     - Classification for `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1` remains
       `B-pending` / not safe-default. The next useful check is old-tree
       same-prompt token capture or first-step logits/logprob comparison; do
       not re-run GDN, LM-head, `eliminate_noops`, or tune-key contamination as
       the 27B-FP8 speed/quality hypothesis.

       old generic dense selector can recover both speed and `913f...` output.

290. 2026-06-03 Added a default-off TurboMind GEMM launch-spec trace and used
     it to narrow the 27B-FP8 gap.

     Source change:

       It is default-off and does not change dispatch, tuning, or output.
     - Optional knobs:
       `TM_GEMM_TRACE_LIMIT=<N>` limits emitted records, and
       `TM_GEMM_TRACE_FILTER=<substring>` filters on `GemmDesc`.
     - Each trace record prints:
       `desc`, dispatch `policy`, whether the call measured, final kernel
       name, `splits`, `swizzle`, CTA tile, MMA tile, stages, and shared
       memory.

     Build/validation:

       `MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 NVCC_THREADS=1
       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0 ninja -C
       build/temp.linux-x86_64-cpython-312-t210-sm70 -j 2 _C`, then copied
       `_C.abi3.so` into `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.

       vLLM latest-tree package, Torch `2.10.0+cu128`, CUDA `12.8`,
       device capability `(7, 0)`, and `_C::fp8_gemm_sm70_out_meta`
       available.
     - Minimal CUDA op trace, no model load:
       `TM_GEMM_TRACE=1 TM_GEMM_TRACE_LIMIT=4` on a `1x128x128` FP8 GEMM
       printed final kernel
       `sm70_f16_e4m3k128_f16_tnt_ibb_8x128x64_2_1x1_s884_1x4x1_mgroup_c8x128_a1x1x64_01`,
       `splits=1`, `swizzle=0`, `cta=8x128x64`.

     Real-weight focused diagnostic:
     - Ran a single-layer, single-`m` diagnostic with real
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` weights, not a vLLM engine
       benchmark:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_fp8_fixed_vs_dynamic.py --m 1
       --max-layers 1`.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_fp8_dense_trace_qwen36_27b_layer0_m1_20260603.json`.
     - Trace for `model.language_model.layers.0.linear_attn.in_proj_qkv`,
       shape `m=1,n=10240,k=5120`:
       fixed/default, dynamic measure, dynamic reuse, and fixed-after all
       selected the same kernel
       `sm70_f16_e4m3k128_f16_tnt_ibb_8x128x64_2_1x1_s884_1x4x1_mgroup_c8x128_a1x1x64_01`
       with `splits=8`, `swizzle=0`.

       `fixed_repeat_max_diff=0.0`.

     Existing exactness evidence rechecked:
     - Prior full real-weight diagnostic
       `sm70_fp8_dense_fixed_vs_dynamic_qwen36_27b_kind_key_fix_20260603.json`
       already covered `53` FP8 dense layer shapes and `159` cases
       (`m=1/2/8`) with `failures=0` and
       `max_fixed_vs_dynamic_diff=0.0`.

     New exclusion:
     - Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 safetensors index has no `experts`,
       `mlp.experts`, `shared_expert`, or `gate_up_proj` keys. This 27B-FP8
       gap is not an MoE route problem.
     - Because FP8 dense op-level fixed/dynamic comparisons are bitwise exact
       for the covered decode shapes, the `629e...` token-hash divergence from
       the dynamic model run should not be attributed to dense GEMM numeric

     Decision / next useful work:
     - Keep `TM_GEMM_TRACE` as a diagnosis-only tool, not a performance route.

       model route after the selector-source changes, then, if the `54 tok/s`
       gap persists, trace the real model path enough to identify whether the

       op-level exactness as the primary hypothesis.

       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`, FP8 tune envs unset.

       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`.
     - Output hash is now
       `629edaf3fb22c59b5237e0b041a351403e36717f62f19037edc48d38b17b2be1`,
       with first token `151373`, not the old-tree/latest-safe
       `913f69431ce3048fa83d388935550afaf883884dd4d27761aea1e59ec479cf1d`
       path whose first token was `264`.
     - Performance is still the slow fixed path:
       `steady_decode_tps=54.4475`, `output_tps=49.3460`,
       `prefill_time=0.9138s`, `decode_time=9.3852s`.
     - Startup remains healthy after the P0 fix:
       `torch.compile took 64.27s`,
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 1.40s`,
       graph capture finished in `69s`.

     First-token exclusions:
     - Packed recurrent decode is not the first-token root cause:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_no_packed_recurrent_firsttok_i128_o1_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       used `VLLM_ENABLE_FLA_PACKED_RECURRENT_DECODE=0` and still emitted
       token `151373`
       (`token_hash=8f2aa1aeb3c2e2a0753950f56df2502b5cb0c844698726edc32b119b1730c904`).
     - The actual request hidden-state allreduce is not falling back to
       PYNCCL:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_fixed_firsttok_allreduce_trace_i128_o1_r1_max16384_20260603.log`
       shows profile shape `(8192,5120)` using PYNCCL, but the real
       `(128,5120)` prefill request uses `backend=custom` on all ranks and
       still emits token `151373`.
     - Fused LM-head top1 / greedy-token fastpath is not the root cause:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_no_lmhead_top1_firsttok_i128_o1_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       set `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0` and
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH=0`; token remained `151373`.
     - FP8 fixed-vs-dynamic selector drift at prefill shape is not the root
       cause:
       `sm70_fp8_dense_fixed_vs_dynamic_qwen36_27b_m128_current_20260603.json`
       covered `53` real FP8 dense layer shapes at `m=128`; `failures=0`,
       `max_fixed_vs_dynamic_diff=0.0`, and `max_fixed_repeat_diff=0.0`.
     - TurboMind FP8 dense itself is not sufficient to explain the drift:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_no_turbomind_firsttok_i128_o1_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       set `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND=0` while keeping the SM70 FP8 dequant
       fallback valid. The model loaded through dequantized fp16 linear
       (`Model loading took 13.78 GiB`; log:
       `SM70 FP8 fallback enabled: FP8 block weights are dequantized to
       torch.float16`) and still emitted token `151373`.

     Environment/source-state note:
     - The earlier latest-safe fixed artifact
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_0dot3_compile_graph_tm_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       used the same prompt and same latest-tree Python version/Torch
       `2.10.0+cu128`, but logged NCCL `2.27.5` and output hash `913f...`.
       The current repaired environment logs NCCL `2.28.9`. The allreduce
       trace above makes actual request-path PYNCCL fallback unlikely, so this
       should be treated as a source/runtime-state difference to localize, not
       as proof that NCCL alone changed the token.

     Decision / next useful work:
     - Classification: current latest fixed 27B-FP8 route is
       `experimental-unsafe` / not recovered. Do not count `54 tok/s` as a
       safe baseline because the first token is already wrong.
     - The speed gap remains: old-tree same-prompt path is `70.6649 tok/s`
       with hash `913f...`; current safe/default fixed path is no longer
       quality-safe and is still about `54.45 tok/s`.
     - Next smallest useful diagnostic should compare prefill logits or
       layer-level outputs around the old `264` vs current `151373` split, or
       run a one-token Flash-V100-prefill-vs-Triton-prefill diagnostic. Do not
       repeat packed recurrent, LM-head top1, request allreduce, FP8
       fixed/dynamic selector, or `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TURBOMIND=0` as primary
       hypotheses.

292. 2026-06-03 Ran the one-token Triton-prefill diagnostic and ruled out
     Flash-V100 prefill as the primary 27B-FP8 first-token drift cause.

     Artifact:
     - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_triton_prefill_diag_firsttok_i128_o1_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.

     Route:
     - Same model/prompt and same first-token diagnostic shape as item 291:
       Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, max length `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`,
       input/output `128/1`, greedy, compile graph on, Flash-V100 backend
       selected, TurboMind FP8 dense enabled, custom allreduce enabled.
     - Set `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1` and kept
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_ALLOW_TRITON_FALLBACK=0`, which triggers the explicit
       diagnostic branch rather than a silent fallback.
     - Runtime route-hit log:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill uses explicit Triton diagnostic fallback
       because VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_USE_TRITON=1; this mixed route is not a
       final performance path.`
     - Triton route also logged
       `SM70 Triton attention schedule override active: prefill_tile=32
       decode_tile=16 ... safe_defaults=True`.

     Result:
     - Prompt hash stayed
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`.
     - First token still `151373`
       (`token_hash=8f2aa1aeb3c2e2a0753950f56df2502b5cb0c844698726edc32b119b1730c904`).
     - `prefill_time=3.3309s` because this path JITs
       `kernel_unified_attention` during the measured request; this timing is
       diagnostic-only and must not be used as a performance baseline.
     - Startup/profile health remained normal:
       `torch.compile took 63.89s`,
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 1.44s`,
       graph capture finished in `69s`.

     Decision:
     - Classification: `diagnostic-only` / rejected as a recovery route.
     - Since both Flash-V100 prefill and Triton prefill produce the same wrong
       first token in the current source/runtime state, the first-token drift
       should not be pursued as a Flash-V100 attention-prefill bug without new
       contradictory evidence.
     - Remaining highest-value work is a model-level prefill logits or
       layer-output compare around old token `264` vs current `151373`,
       especially in GDN/FLA/model-wrapper state. If comparing to old-tree
       requires instrumentation, keep it limited to one-token logits/layer
       dumps and avoid another 512-token speed run until the quality root is
       localized.

     - Prompt hash stayed
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`.
     - First token was `264`
       (`token_hash=bba58959c32abe688d9cb5222b97de973002a67c412d6a8c8d2a79ac692f32b7`),
       matching the old 512-token same-prompt baseline first token.
     - Runtime route logs included Flash-V100 prefill, Flash-V100 decode,
       TurboMind FP8 W8A16, dense fp16 LM-head fast path, custom allreduce,
       and CUDA graph capture.
     - The one-token latency was diagnostic-only: `prefill_time=4.5893s`,
       because this run paid first-request runtime overhead and should not be
       used as a throughput baseline.

     Decision:
     - The latest current-tree first-token drift (`151373`) is not explained
       by the model checkpoint, tokenizer/prompt, or the original old-tree
       route. Old source plus old runtime still produces the safe token `264`.
     - Do not spend more GPU time trying to run the 0.0.3 source inside the
       current Torch `2.10` env; its native extension is ABI-incompatible.
     - Next useful work is a one-token prefill logits/layer-output compare
       between old `264` and latest `151373`, starting around the
       GDN/FLA/model-wrapper path. Keep this as correctness localization
       before any more 512-token decode-speed runs.

294. 2026-06-03 Ran full-vocab first-token logprob dumps for old tree and

     Artifacts:
     - Latest:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_full_logprobs_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`

       and `.log`.

     Harness change:
     - Extended `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` with
       `--input-len` and `--prompt-base` so the model-token/logprob harness

       `benchmark_sm70_decode.py`.
     - Both runs used `--input-len 128`, `--max-tokens 1`, `--logprobs -1`,
       and `--engine-arg max-logprobs=-1`, producing full `248320`-entry
       first-step logprob maps.

     Common setup:
     - Same model/prompt shape as items 291 and 293: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4,
       max length `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`, max seqs `1`, greedy,
       Flash-V100 backend, compile graph on, TurboMind FP8 dense enabled, and
       custom allreduce enabled.
     - Prompt hash matched the decode baseline in both runs:
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`.

     Result:
     - Old tree (`1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3`, Torch `2.9.1+cu128`, NCCL `2.27.5`)
       emitted token `264`.
       - token `264`: logprob `-0.00046171018038876355`, rank `1`.
       - token `151373`: logprob `-35.79733657836914`, rank `248319`.
       - Old top-10 token IDs:
         `[264, 271, 198, 248046, 220, 69892, 64, 449, 4558, 1076]`.
     - Latest tree (`0.21.1rc1.dev438+g4ff865c38.d20260602`, Torch
       `2.10.0+cu128`, NCCL `2.28.9`) emitted token `151373`.
       - token `151373`: logprob `-1.5649828910827637`, rank `1`.
       - token `264`: logprob `-21.377483367919922`, rank `248258`.
       - Latest top-10 token IDs:
         `[151373, 151353, 150910, 155011, 174427, 158949, 158964, 163859,
         201058, 243942]`.
     - Full-map comparison:
       - `top20_overlap=0`.
       - `mean_abs_diff=10.774796679002883` across all `248320` common token
         IDs.
       - Max logprob diff was token `151373`: `34.23235368728638`.
     - Latest raw sampler logits agree with the serialized logprobs:
       sampler pre-process logits had token `151373` rank `1` with logit
       `16.703125`, while token `264` had rank `248258` with logit
       `-3.109375`.

     Decision:
     - The 27B-FP8 latest drift is not a sampler tie, top-k formatting issue,
       or token serialization artifact. The full first-step model
       distribution is different before sampling.
     - Since both attention-prefill routes from item 292 produced the same
       wrong token and latest raw sampler logits already rank the wrong token
       first, the next useful localization should dump or compare hidden states
       before the logits processor, then walk backward through the
       GDN/FLA/model-wrapper path.
     - Do not run more decode-speed baselines for 27B-FP8 until the first-token
       distribution is restored; speed data on the current latest route remains
       `experimental-unsafe`.

295. 2026-06-03 Added a default-off sample-hidden/logits dump at the Python
     sampling boundary and confirmed the 27B-FP8 drift is already present in
     model hidden state before the logits processor / LM head.

     Code change:
