       - Latest:
         `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       - Old tree:
         `../1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
     - Hook envs:
       - `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_SAMPLE_TENSORS_DIR`
       - `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_SAMPLE_TENSORS_ENABLE_FILE`
       - `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_SAMPLE_TENSORS_MAX_STEPS`
     - The dump is gated by an enable file that
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now touches only after
       `LLM(...)` returns, so profile/compile/CUDA-graph capture are not
       instrumented.

     Artifacts:
     - Latest:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sample_tensors_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sample_tensors_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Old tree:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_oldtree_qwen36_27b_fp8_sample_tensors_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_oldtree_qwen36_27b_fp8_sample_tensors_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.

     Route:
     - Same prompt/model/shape as items 294 and 293:
       Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, input/output `128/1`, max length `16384`,
       max batched tokens `8192`, max seqs `1`, greedy, Flash-V100 backend,
       compile graph on, TurboMind FP8 dense enabled, and custom allreduce
       enabled.
     - Used `--logprobs 1` only to force the normal logits path without
       writing another full-vocab JSON. The sidecar `.pt` files contain full
       `sample_hidden_states` and full pre-sampling `logits`.
     - Prompt hash stayed
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`
       in both runs.

     Result:
     - Old tree emitted token `264`; latest emitted token `151373`, matching
       the earlier one-token and full-logprob diagnostics.
     - Sidecars were produced on all four TP worker processes in both runs.
       Latest TP sidecars were identical for the sampled hidden/logits tensor.
     - Comparing one old sidecar against one latest sidecar:
       - hidden shape: `(1, 5120)` in both, dtype `torch.float16`.
       - logits shape: `(1, 248320)` in both, dtype `torch.float16`.
       - old hidden norm/mean/std:
         `111.3993 / 0.0030469 / 1.5570`.
       - latest hidden norm/mean/std:
         `81.3889 / -0.0092030 / 1.1375`.
       - hidden max/mean/RMS abs diff:
         `78.5625 / 1.3406 / 2.0628`.
       - hidden cosine similarity: `-0.1517615`.
     - Logits sidecar comparison:
       - old top-10 token IDs:
         `[264, 271, 198, 248046, 220, 69892, 64, 449, 4558, 1076]`.
       - latest top-10 token IDs:
         `[151373, 151353, 155011, 150910, 174427, 158949, 158964, 163859,
         201058, 243942]`.
       - old token `264`: logit `23.65625`, rank `1`.
       - old token `151373`: logit `-12.1328125`, rank `248319`.
       - latest token `151373`: logit `16.703125`, rank `1`.
       - latest token `264`: logit `-3.103515625`, rank `248257`.
       - logits max/mean/RMS abs diff:
         `28.8359 / 5.5126 / 6.1983`.

     Decision:
     - Classification: diagnostic-only / correctness localization.
     - This rules out a pure sampler, output serialization, top-k, or
       LM-head/logits-processor-only root cause for the 27B-FP8 latest drift:
       the sampled hidden state entering `compute_logits` is already wrong.
     - The next useful localization should move upstream into the model
       forward path, with the highest-priority candidates still
       GDN/FLA/model-wrapper state and Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 wrapper differences.
       Do not spend more time toggling sampler or LM-head top1 paths for this
       specific first-token drift unless new contradictory evidence appears.

296. 2026-06-03 Fixed the fused GDN post-conv prefill layout bug, restored
     the 27B-FP8 first token in eager/no-compile graph routes, and localized
     the remaining compile-graph drift to the compiled layer-0 downstream
     path before layer-1 GDN input.

     Code changes:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/fla/ops/fused_gdn_prefill_post_conv.py`
       now passes and uses `conv_output.stride(1)` instead of assuming the
       feature stride is `1`; q/k/v offsets multiply feature offsets by this
       stride.
     - The fused post-conv Triton masks were split into `qk_mask_2d` and
       `v_mask_2d` so `K != V` feature sizes do not trigger a Triton type
       conflict.
     - Added diagnostic-only env
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_LEGACY_PREFILL_PREP` in `vllm/envs.py`; when enabled,
       the latest Qwen GDN path uses the old rearrange+gating prefill prep for
       A/B comparison only.
     - Added default-off GDN core tensor dumps gated by
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_GDN_CORE_DIR`,
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_GDN_CORE_ENABLE_FILE`, and
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_GDN_CORE_MAX_DUMPS`. The benchmark harness touches the
       enable file only after `LLM(...)` returns, avoiding compile/profile
       capture instrumentation.
     - A temporary experiment returning `core_attn_out` from
       `qwen_gdn_attention_core` did not change the bad compile token and was
       removed; the custom op is back to its in-place mutation contract.

     Synthetic kernel check:
     - A CUDA parity probe for `fused_post_conv_prep` passed for both
       contiguous and non-contiguous `conv_output` layouts:
       - contiguous stride `(7168, 1)`.
       - non-contiguous stride `(1, 128)`.
       - fp16-level max diffs:
         `q=0.0001220703125`, `k=6.103515625e-05`, `v=0`,
         `g=1.1444091796875e-05`, `beta=1.1920928955078125e-07`.

     First-token artifacts:
     - Legacy latest prefill-prep diagnostic, eager + GDN schedule pins:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_legacy_gdn_prefill_eager_pinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
       - Output token: `264`.
       - Logprob: `-0.00047362555051222444`.
       - Diagnostic prefill time: `33.63487431599788s`, because this route
         intentionally uses the slower legacy prep path/autotune shape.
     - Fused post-conv stride fix, eager + GDN schedule pins:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_fused_post_conv_stridefix_eager_pinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
       - Output token: `264`.
       - Logprob: `-0.00046969347749836743`.
       - Prefill time: `1.2377348399968469s`.
     - Production-shaped compile graph after the stride fix, default GDN
       policy:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_fused_post_conv_stridefix_compilegraph_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
       - Output token: `151373`.
       - Logprob: `-1.5637547969818115`.
       - Compile graph remained effective, so this is still unsafe.
     - Compile graph after the stride fix with GDN schedule pins:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_stridefix_compilegraph_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
       - Output token: `151373`.
       - Logprob: `-1.5637011528015137`.
     - No-compile decode-graph route after the stride fix with the same GDN
       pins:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_stridefix_nocompilegraph_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
       - Output token: `264`.
       - Logprob: `-0.00047159992391243577`.
       - Decode CUDA graph capture succeeded with
         `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_DECODE_GRAPH_NO_COMPILE=1`.

     Compile-mode negative toggles:
     - Returning `core_attn_out` from the custom op did not fix compile graph:
       `model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnreturn_compilegraph_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603`
       still emitted `151373`.
     - `pass_config.ir_enable_torch_wrap=false` did not fix compile graph:
       `model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnreturn_compilegraph_noirwrap_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603`
       still emitted `151373`.
     - Disabling `fuse_norm_quant` and `fuse_act_quant` did not fix compile
       graph:
       `model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnreturn_compilegraph_nofuse_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603`
       still emitted `151373`.
     - Forcing `custom_ops=["all"]` failed during Dynamo tracing in
       `torch.accelerator.device_index` under the CUDA RMSNormGated path; this
       is not quality evidence.

     GDN core dump localization:
     - No-compile dump run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdncore_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and sidecar directory
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/gdn_core_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
       It emitted token `264`.
     - Compile-graph dump run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdncore_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and sidecar directory
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/gdn_core_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
       It emitted token `151373`.
     - Each dump directory contained 32 files: labels `input_qkv`, `input_a`,
       `input_b`, and `core_out` for layers 0 and 1 across four TP workers.
     - Comparing compile vs no-compile by sorted worker rank showed layer-0
       GDN inputs and core output match within fp16 noise. Example rank 0:
       - layer-0 `input_qkv`: max diff `0.015625`, mean diff
         `1.485e-06`, RMS diff `7.80e-05`, cosine approximately `1.0`.
       - layer-0 `core_out`: max diff `0.0009765625`, mean diff
         `1.74e-07`, RMS diff `1.12e-05`, cosine approximately `1.0`.
     - Before layer-1 GDN, compile and no-compile inputs diverge heavily.
       Example rank 0:
       - layer-1 `input_qkv`: max diff `34.406982421875`, mean diff
         `0.7637323`, RMS diff `1.1669067`, cosine `0.0485993`,
         norms `184.18` vs `651.096`.
       - layer-1 `input_a`: max diff `10.921875`, mean diff `2.15356`,
         RMS diff `2.99104`, cosine `-0.1020`.
       - layer-1 `input_b`: max diff `13.830078125`, mean diff `2.14435`,
         RMS diff `3.02561`, cosine `0.4393`.

     Decision:
     - The fused post-conv stride bug was real and fixed, but it only explains
       the eager/no-compile correctness path. It is not the remaining
       compile-graph root cause.
     - The remaining 27B-FP8 compile-mode correctness blocker is downstream of
       layer-0 GDN core output and upstream of layer-1 GDN input, likely in the
       compiled layer-0 output projection/residual/post-attention norm/MLP
       path or residual update.
     - GDN FLA schedules, fused post-conv prep, `qwen_gdn_attention_core`
       mutation visibility, Flash-V100 attention, sampler, and LM-head-only
       paths are no longer the leading suspects for this specific drift.
     - Treat latest 27B-FP8 compile graph as unsafe until token `264` is
       restored. The no-compile decode-graph first-token result is correctness
       evidence only; it is not yet a 512-token decode-speed baseline.

297. 2026-06-03 Ran the 27B-FP8 no-compile decode-graph route for
     input/output `128/512`; it is not an accepted fallback because only the
     warmup request matched the old output hash, while the measured repeat
     diverged late in decode and was slower than the old baseline.

     Artifact:
     - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_nocompilegraph_gdnpinned_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.

     Shape and route:
     - Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, input `128`, output `512`,
       max length `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`, max seqs `1`,
       greedy, `ignore_eos=true`, Flash-V100, TurboMind FP8 dense, custom
       allreduce on.
     - Explicit graph env:
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_DECODE_GRAPH_NO_COMPILE=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_DECODE_GRAPH_CAPTURE_SIZE=1`.
     - Explicit GDN schedule pins:
       KKT `BK=32, warps=2, stages=2`; delta-h `BV=16, warps=8,
       stages=1`; chunk-o `BK=32, BV=32, warps=4, stages=2`.
     - Route logs showed:
       - `mode=NONE`, `cudagraph_mode=FULL_DECODE_ONLY`, capture size `1`.
       - Flash-V100 prefill and scalar-paged decode paths.
       - SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path.
       - SM70 fused LM-head top1 path.
       - Custom allreduce selected for TP.
       - Decode CUDA graph capture completed.

     Result:
     - Prompt hash matched the accepted old prompt:
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`.
     - Warmup output hash matched old 0.0.3:
       `913f69431ce3048fa83d388935550afaf883884dd4d27761aea1e59ec479cf1d`.
       The warmup output exactly matched
       `decode_oldtree_src_qwen36_27b_fp8_sameprompt_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - The measured repeat output hash was different:
       `498ab129e282037d500e2fa190596be519a7b350f279b8cf70d9f54d5c478f5b`.
       It kept the same first token `264` and matched the old output until
       token index `420`, where old emitted `411` and the measured repeat
       emitted `424`.
     - Measured repeat timing:
       - `prefill_time=0.18484659399837255s`.
       - `decode_time=10.822141135999118s`.
       - `steady_decode_tps=47.21801292169376`.
       - `output_tps=46.507189423557385`.
     - Warmup timing was not accepted as speed evidence because it included
       first-request overhead:
       `prefill_time=0.9389506010047626s`,
       `steady_decode_tps=46.832043434171`.

     Comparison:
     - Old 0.0.3 same-shape source/runtime baseline:
       `decode_oldtree_src_qwen36_27b_fp8_sameprompt_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       produced the same old hash
       `913f69431ce3048fa83d388935550afaf883884dd4d27761aea1e59ec479cf1d`
       with `steady_decode_tps=70.66486036733525`.
     - The no-compile measured route is therefore both not repeat-stable and
       about `33%` below the old decode-speed baseline for this shape.

     Decision:
     - Do not accept the no-compile decode-graph route as a temporary safe
       27B-FP8 fallback yet. First-token and first-request correctness are
       insufficient because the second same-prompt greedy request diverges
       late in decode.
     - The late divergence is a different symptom from the compile-graph
       first-token failure. It suggests a repeated-request/state-reset or
       decode-state issue in the no-compile graph route, not the layer-0
       compile-mode prefill drift localized in item 296.
     - Any future fallback benchmark must require repeated same-prompt output
       hash stability in addition to pure decode TPS.

298. 2026-06-03 Recorded the 35B-AWQ eager-profile startup policy directly in
     future decode benchmark JSON.

     Code change:
     - `benchmark_sm70_decode.py` now records
       `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_eager_profile_effective` and
       `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_benchmark_combo_kernel_effective` under
       `sm70_graph_policy`.
     - This does not change runtime behavior. It makes future 0.0.3 compile
       graph artifacts self-describing: accepted 35B-AWQ startup evidence no
       longer has to infer default-on eager profile and default-off combo
       benchmarking solely from source or log lines.

     Validation:
     - `python -m py_compile benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.
     - The existing fixed 35B-AWQ artifact still passes:
       `python benchmarks/verify_sm70_artifact.py --json
       bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_0dot3_compile_graph_eagerprofile_trace_i1024_o256_20260603.json
       --log
       bench_results/sm70_migration_20260602/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_0dot3_compile_graph_eagerprofile_trace_i1024_o256_20260603.log
       --require-sm70-flash-v100-0dot3-compile-graph
       --require-full-flash-v100 --require-awq-moe-0dot3-baseline-route
       --require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route --require-decode-timing`.

     Decision:
     - No new long 35B-AWQ rerun was needed. Item 269 already proves the
       actual startup fix: the profile dummy run uses `skip_compiled=True`,
       engine init dropped from `546.11s` to `63.61s`, and pure decode stayed
       at `117.83/117.56 tok/s`.

299. 2026-06-03 Added compiled-path layer/GDN-projection tensor probes and
     localized the 27B-FP8 compile-graph first-token drift to the layer-0 GDN
     `z` slice before RMSNormGated.

     Code changes:
     - Added a diagnostic-only side-effect custom op in
       `qwen3_next.py`, gated by `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_QWEN_LAYER_DIR`, to dump
       layer input, input-norm output, attention output,
       post-attention-norm output/residual, and MLP output for selected layer
       IDs. `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now touches
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_QWEN_LAYER_ENABLE_FILE` only after `LLM(...)`
       returns.
     - Added a diagnostic-only side-effect custom op in
       `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`, gated by
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_GDN_PROJ_DIR`, to dump GDN output-projection
       `proj_core_in`, `proj_z`, `proj_norm_out`, and `proj_out`.
       The model-token harness similarly gates saves with
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_GDN_PROJ_ENABLE_FILE`.
     - Added default-off diagnostic env `VLLM_SM70_GDN_Z_CONTIGUOUS` and
       recorded it in both decode/model-token GDN policy ledgers. This gate
       materializes the GDN output-projection `z` view before the custom GDN
       core boundary to test a view/lifetime hypothesis.

     Static validation:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/envs.py
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_next.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py` passed.
     - Import/schema validation in the same env passed for both custom-op
       helpers.

     Layer-boundary artifacts:
     - Compile graph:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_qwenlayer_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `qwen_layer_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - No-compile decode graph:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_qwenlayer_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `qwen_layer_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Both used Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, Flash-V100, input/output `128/1`,
       `max_model_len=16384`, `max_num_batched_tokens=8192`, max seqs `1`,
       greedy, GDN schedule pins, FP8 dense dequant fallback, and FP8 MoE
       dequant fallback. Compile emitted token `151373`; no-compile emitted
       token `264`.
     - Each sidecar directory contained 52 tensors: layers 0 and 1 across
       four TP workers, with one dump per label.
     - Rank-0 compile-vs-no-compile layer diffs:
       - layer-0 `layer_input_hidden`: max/mean/RMS `0/0/0`, cosine
         approximately `1.0`.
       - layer-0 `input_norm_out`: max `0.000976562`, mean `2.88e-08`, RMS
         `4.68e-06`, cosine approximately `1.0`.
       - layer-0 `attn_out`: max `3353.69`, mean `2.326`, RMS `15.021`,
         cosine `-0.3869`.
       - layer-0 `post_attn_norm_out`, `post_attn_residual`, `mlp_out`, and
         all layer-1 boundaries were already divergent.
     - Interpretation: the failure is after layer-0 input norm and before
       layer-0 attention output. Combined with item 296's matching GDN core
       output, this points inside the GDN output-projection block.

     GDN projection artifacts:
     - Compile graph:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnproj_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `gdn_proj_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - No-compile decode graph:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnproj_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `gdn_proj_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Compile emitted token `151373` with logprob `-1.55377`;
       no-compile emitted token `264` with logprob `-0.0004689`.
     - Each sidecar directory contained 32 tensors: `proj_core_in`,
       `proj_z`, `proj_norm_out`, and `proj_out` for layers 0 and 1 across
       four TP workers.
     - Rank-0 layer-0 projection diffs:
       - `proj_core_in`: max `0.000976562`, mean `1.74e-07`, RMS
         `1.12e-05`, cosine approximately `1.0`.
       - `proj_z`: max `61.3672`, mean `2.7101`, RMS `4.0296`, cosine
         `0.1019`.
       - `proj_norm_out`: max `495.252`, mean `0.2276`, RMS `7.7058`,
         cosine `-0.0056`.
       - `proj_out`: max `3353.69`, mean `2.326`, RMS `15.021`, cosine
         `-0.3869`.
     - Interpretation: the GDN recurrence/core path remains matched, but the
       compile path's `z` tensor entering RMSNormGated is already wrong.
       The divergence is not caused by RMSNormGated or `out_proj`; those are
       downstream consumers of the bad `z`.

     z-materialization diagnostic:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdn_zcontig_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Same compile-graph first-token shape, with
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_Z_CONTIGUOUS=1`.
     - Result: still emitted token `151373` with logprob `-1.56395`.
     - Classification: `rejected` as a fix. Materializing the `z` view after
       split does not restore the old token, so the bug is earlier than a
       post-split view/lifetime issue across the GDN core boundary.

     Decision:
     - Classification: diagnostic-only / correctness localization.
     - The current 27B-FP8 compile-graph route remains
       `experimental-unsafe`. Do not run or accept 512-token speed baselines
       on it until token `264` is restored.
     - The next useful diagnostic is to instrument `in_proj_qkvz` itself or
       force only that layer/projection through an eager/no-compile or fp16
       dequant path. The target is now the layer-0 `in_proj_qkvz` tail `z`
       slice under compile, not Flash-V100 attention, GDN core recurrence,
       RMSNormGated, `out_proj`, sampler, LM head, or request allreduce.

300. 2026-06-03 Fixed the 27B-FP8 compile-graph first-token drift by
     materializing the Qwen3.5 GDN qkvz tail with an indexed read under the
     SM70 0.0.3 compile graph route.

     Additional localization:
     - The first pre-core GDN projection probe was added to the base GDN
       path and missed the actual Qwen3.5 override. It is superseded by the
       corrected Qwen3.5 override probes below.
     - Corrected compile artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnproj_precore2_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `gdn_proj_precore2_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Corrected no-compile artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnproj_precore2_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `gdn_proj_precore2_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_nocompile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Both used Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, input/output `128/1`, Flash-V100,
       max length `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`, max seqs `1`, greedy,
       GDN schedule pins, FP8 dense dequant fallback, and FP8 MoE fallback.
       Compile emitted token `151373`; no-compile emitted token `264`.
     - Each corrected sidecar directory contained 64 tensors:
       `in_proj_qkvz`, `in_proj_ba`, `split_mixed_qkv`, `split_z`,
       `proj_core_in`, `proj_z`, `proj_norm_out`, and `proj_out` for layers
       0 and 1 across four TP workers.
     - Compile-vs-no-compile layer-0 summary:
       `in_proj_qkvz` and `split_mixed_qkv` were near-identical
       (`max_abs <= 0.015625`, mean about `1.4e-06`), while compile
       `split_z` had `3456` NaN mismatches across ranks and finite
       `max_abs=65440.703`. `proj_z` then diverged with `max_abs=61.367188`
       and mean about `2.59`.
     - Internal consistency check: no-compile `split_z` exactly matched
       `in_proj_qkvz[..., 2560:]` for all ranks/layers. Compile `split_z`
       did not, and compile `proj_z` did not match the expected reshaped
       qkvz tail either. This localized the correctness bug to a
       nonzero-offset qkvz tail read in the compiled Qwen3.5 GDN path.

     Rejected slice-only fix:
     - Code tried replacing `split()` with explicit last-dimension slices in
       `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet.forward_cuda`.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnproj_slicefix_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `gdn_proj_slicefix_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Result: still emitted token `151373` with logprob `-1.63152`.
       `proj_z` still disagreed with the expected reshaped qkvz tail
       (`max_abs=61.367188` on rank-0/layer-0). This rejected the hypothesis
       that only `torch.split` was broken; a plain nonzero-offset view was
       also unsafe in this compiled model path.

     Accepted code change:
     - Added `_sm70_compile_graph_last_dim_select()` in `qwen3_5.py`.
     - When `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`, the helper uses
       `index_select(-1, torch.arange(start, start + size, device=...))` to
       materialize nonzero-offset qkvz/ba tails. When the compile graph env
       is off, it preserves the old cheap view path.
     - `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet.forward_cuda` now uses the helper for `z` and
       the nonzero-offset `a` tail in the split-projection layout, and for
       `z`, `b`, and `a` in the fused qkvzba layout. The `mixed_qkv` leading
       slice remains a cheap view because the leading slice was already
       proven matched.

     Validation:
     - `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py` passed after the fix.
     - Diagnostic compile artifact with sidecars:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_gdnproj_indexfix_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`,
       `.log`, and sidecar directory
       `gdn_proj_indexfix_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603/`.
     - Route evidence: `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`,
       `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_compile_graph_effective=true`, log line
       `mode=VLLM_COMPILE, cudagraph_mode=FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`,
       `torch.compile took 64.61 s`, graph capture finished in `73 s`, and
       Flash-V100 prefill/decode route logs appeared on all TP workers.
     - Diagnostic result: emitted token `264` with cumulative logprob
       `-0.0004543225804809481`.
     - Tensor result: compile `proj_z` exactly matched the expected reshaped
       qkvz tail for all four ranks and layers 0/1. Compile-vs-no-compile
       diffs then dropped to FP16-level noise: layer-0 `proj_out`
       `max_abs=0.00024414062`, layer-1 `proj_out max_abs=0.00390625`, with
       no NaN mismatches.
     - Normal no-dump compile artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_compile_gdnpinned_firsttok_i128_o1_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
     - No-dump result: emitted token `264` with cumulative logprob
       `-0.00045408427831716835`; `generate_seconds=0.3973039029951906`,
       `load_seconds=64.06037917899812`, compile route effective, graph
       capture completed, and Flash-V100 prefill/decode route logs appeared.

     Decision:
     - Classification: 27B-FP8 compile-graph first-token P0 is fixed for the
       `128/1` correctness gate.
     - This is not yet a 27B-FP8 speed recovery artifact. Next accepted
       evidence must run the matching `128/512` decode benchmark with the
       same model, TP4, max length, max batched tokens, graph policy, GDN
       schedule pins, FP8 fallback lane, repeated-output stability, and pure
       decode timing. Watch the indexed qkvz-tail materialization for decode
       TPS impact before calling the route recovered.

301. 2026-06-03 Re-ran the tuned 27B-FP8 `128/512` compile-graph decode lane
     after the qkvz-tail index fix. The measured repeat now matches old-tree
     output and speed, but the first external warmup request still diverges
     late, so the route is not safe-default yet.

     Full `128/512` artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_tune_small_shapes_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_tune_small_shapes_compile_graph_i128_o512_r1_max16384_20260603.log`.
     - Shape/env: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, input/output `128/512`,
       max length `16384`, max batched tokens `8192`, max seqs `1`,
       greedy with `ignore_eos=true`, Flash-V100, 0.0.3 compile graph,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_TUNE_SMALL_SHAPES=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_ELIMINATE_NOOPS=0`.
     - Route evidence: log showed
       `mode=VLLM_COMPILE, cudagraph_mode=FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`,
       production TP allreduce `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']`,
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`, Flash-V100 decode and
       prefill route logs, `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_compile_graph_effective=true`,
       and `fp8_dynamic_measure_enabled=true`.
     - Startup/profile:
       `torch.compile took 76.53 s`, `Initial profiling/warmup run took 2.04 s`,
       graph capture finished in `85 s`, and harness `load_seconds=134.4651`.
       This is still healthy relative to the original 35B-AWQ P0 pathology;
       it is not a 473 s profile/warmup regression.

     Output and speed result:
     - Prompt hash matched old-tree:
       `f1a2ecb6cb2cd3dc44e1afec1f9c03787fba573206e6860c197a980da48690cc`.
     - Warmup output hash:
       `498ab129e282037d500e2fa190596be519a7b350f279b8cf70d9f54d5c478f5b`.
       It matched old-tree through token index `419`, then diverged at index
       `420`: latest warmup emitted `424`, old-tree emitted `411`.
       This is the same late-divergence hash seen in the earlier no-compile
       repeated-request failure from item 297, but now it appears on the
       first external request instead of the measured repeat.
     - Measured repeat output hash:
       `913f69431ce3048fa83d388935550afaf883884dd4d27761aea1e59ec479cf1d`,
       exactly matching old-tree same-prompt output for all `512` tokens.
     - Measured repeat timing:
       `prefill_time=0.1414589319974766 s`,
       `decode_time=7.158010653001838 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps=71.38854980408601`,
       `output_tps=70.11755990479243`.
     - Old-tree same-prompt measured repeat:
       `prefill_time=0.14426936399831902 s`,
       `decode_time=7.231316914003401 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps=70.66486036733525`,
       `output_tps=69.39587014968157`.
     - Interpretation: after item 300, the measured 27B-FP8 tuned repeat both
       matches old output and slightly exceeds old pure decode speed. The
       remaining blocker is first external request stability, not first-token
       correctness or steady measured decode speed.

     Rejected Mamba/GDN page-zeroing hypothesis:
     - Source finding: `KVBlockZeroer` currently documents and implements
       zeroing only for `FullAttentionSpec`; Mamba/GDN state pages are
       skipped. Since the first-request mismatch is late in decode and the
       engine reuses blocks across warmup/repeat requests, stale recurrent
       state in newly allocated Mamba pages was a plausible hypothesis.
     - Experimental code change: extended `KVBlockZeroer` to include MambaSpec
       pages using the first state tensor's page start, and generalized zeroer
       metadata to support multiple page sizes. This was reverted after the
       validation below failed; no unaccepted Mamba-zeroing code remains from
       this attempt.
     - Minimal reproducer artifact after that temporary patch:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_zero_mamba_tune_compile_graph_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
     - The `430` output length was chosen because the known first mismatch is
       at index `420`, avoiding another full `512` speed run until the
       hypothesis had evidence.
     - Result: rejected. Warmup still diverged at index `420`
       (`424` vs old `411`), while the repeat matched the old-tree first
       `430` tokens. Timing remained in the recovered range
       (`steady_decode_tps=71.41650372789333` on the measured repeat), but
       correctness did not improve.

     Decision:
     - Classification for the tuned 27B-FP8 compile graph route remains
       `B-pending` / not `safe-default`.
     - The measured-repeat speed and old-output parity are now recovered, but
       first external request stability is still missing. Do not mark the
       27B-FP8 route fully recovered until warmup/repeat same-prompt hashes
       are both stable and old-matching.
     - Do not repeat the Mamba/GDN page-zeroing experiment as a recovery path.
       The next useful diagnostic is request-sequence state tracing around the
       first and second external requests: block table rows, Mamba/GDN
       `non_spec_state_indices_tensor`, `seq_lens`, and GDN state/cache
       values near the token-420 divergence.

302. 2026-06-03 Added and used an opt-in GDN state-table trace for the
     remaining 27B-FP8 first-request divergence. This rules out block-table
     construction and first physical block IDs as the immediate cause.

     Diagnostic hook:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_GDN_STATE_TABLE_DIR` plus optional
       `START_SEQ`, `END_SEQ`, and `MAX_DUMPS` gates in `gdn_attn.py`.
       The hook is default-off and dumps only requested GDN metadata fields:
       `seq_lens`, `query_start_loc`, `block_table_tensor`,
       `non_spec_state_indices_tensor`, and related spec/non-spec metadata.
     - Registered those env names in `envs.py` so future trace runs do not
       log unknown-variable warnings.

     First trace artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_gdn_state_trace_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - State-table sidecars:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/gdn_state_trace_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_compile_i128_o430_20260603/`.
     - Shape/env: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, input/output `128/430`, warmup 1,
       repeat 1, Flash-V100, 0.0.3 compile graph, FP8 dense/MoE fallback,
       tuned small shapes, trace range `seq_lens=545..552`.
     - Result reproduced item 301: warmup hash
       `f15917caccf6b5ac696c846460ad068ac64506ff0e96a3373b72c8d63d0837f4`
       diverged at token index `420` (`424` vs old/repeat `411`), while the
       measured repeat hash
       `f0ec3a89b698e759e48c65b3da5248a43a33ee9d4c271d358b421937be6996aa`
       matched the old-tree first `430` tokens.
     - State-table observation: the first request used GDN state/block IDs
       `[1, 2, 3]` for the three GDN groups at `seq_lens=545..552`; the
       repeat used `[5, 6, 7]` for the same logical window. Query starts and
       seq lens metadata were otherwise normal.

     Rejected initial-block reservation hypothesis:
     - Temporary experimental code reserved physical KV blocks `[1, 2, 3, 4]`
       before serving so the first external request would use the same GDN
       state/block IDs `[5, 6, 7]` that had matched old output in the repeat.
       This experimental allocator change was reverted after validation.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_reserve4_compile_firstreq_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
     - State-table sidecars:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/gdn_state_trace_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_reserve4_compile_i128_o430_20260603/`.
     - Route evidence: log showed SM70 Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile graph policy
       (`mode=VLLM_COMPILE`, `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`), Flash-V100 prefill/decode,
       FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path, graph capture finished in `13 s`, and
       `Initial profiling/warmup run took 0.58 s`.
     - Result: rejected. The single first request still produced the
       divergent hash
       `f15917caccf6b5ac696c846460ad068ac64506ff0e96a3373b72c8d63d0837f4`
       with token index `420 = 424`, even though the trace confirmed it used
       GDN state/block IDs `[5, 6, 7]`.
     - Metadata compare: earlier good repeat dumps and reserve-first bad dumps
       were `torch.equal` for `seq_lens`, `query_start_loc_cpu`,
       `non_spec_query_start_loc`, `non_spec_state_indices_tensor`, and
       `block_table_tensor` for common `seq_lens=545..547` and state IDs
       `[5, 6, 7]`.

     Decision:
     - Classification: diagnostic evidence plus `rejected` initial-block
       reservation. The tuned 27B-FP8 compile graph route remains
       `B-pending`.
     - Do not spend more time on block-table row sync, first physical block
       IDs, or initial-block reservation for this specific token-420 issue
       without new contradictory evidence.
     - Next useful trace target is inside the computation using identical
       metadata: compare first-bad vs repeat-good GDN conv/SSM state values
       and/or sampler logits around `seq_lens=545..552` / output token index
       `420`.

303. 2026-06-03 Added exact-step sampling diagnostics for the remaining
     Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 compile-graph token-420 divergence. These runs show the
     issue is not explained by block metadata, first physical block IDs, or the
     greedy top1 sampler alone.

     Diagnostic hooks:
     - Extended the existing default-off sample tensor dump with
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_SAMPLE_TENSORS_STEPS`, so dumps can target exact
       sampling steps instead of flooding every decode step.
     - Added default-off `VLLM_SM70_SYNC_SAMPLE_TENSORS_STEPS` plus
       `VLLM_SM70_SYNC_SAMPLE_TENSORS_MODE={stream,d2h,device}`. This is a
       diagnostic-only perturbation hook around `sample_hidden_states` before
       sampling; it is not a proposed recovery path.
     - Registered the new env names in `envs.py` so benchmark artifacts record
       them without unknown-env warnings.

     Exact-step tensor dump artifact:
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sample_tensors_steps_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
     - Sidecars:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sample_tensors_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_indexfix_compile_i128_o430_steps420_421_850_851_20260603/`.
     - Shape/env: Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, TP4, input/output `128/430`, warmup 1,
       repeat 1, Flash-V100, 0.0.3 compile graph, FP8 dense/MoE fallback,
       tuned small shapes, sample steps `420,421,850,851`.
     - Result: both warmup and repeat matched the old/repeat-good hash
       `f0ec3a89b698e759e48c65b3da5248a43a33ee9d4c271d358b421937be6996aa`
       with token window `[635, 440, 411, 30, 1690]` at indices `418..422`.
       This is diagnostic evidence only because the D2H dump and `torch.save`
       perturb timing.
     - Hidden-state compare from dumped sidecars: per-rank first-request step
       `420` vs repeat step `850` was not bitwise equal but had small
       differences (`max_abs=0.0078125`, `mean_abs~=0.0008915`);
       step `421` vs `851` also differed (`max_abs=0.015625`,
       `mean_abs~=0.0009316`). The dumped run was nevertheless token-good,
       so the dump does not prove hidden-state exact equality.

     Sync-only and D2H no-save diagnostics:
     - Stream sync at step `421`:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sync_sample_stream_step421_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
       Warmup flipped to the good hash, while the unsynced repeat produced the
       known bad hash
       `f15917caccf6b5ac696c846460ad068ac64506ff0e96a3373b72c8d63d0837f4`.
     - Stream sync at steps `421,851`:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sync_sample_stream_steps421_851_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
       Both warmup and repeat produced the bad hash. Plain current-stream sync
       is therefore not a reliable fix.
     - D2H no-save at steps `420,421,850,851`:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sync_sample_d2h_steps420_421_850_851_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
       Warmup was good, repeat was bad. A D2H copy without file I/O does not
       reproduce the earlier both-good tensor dump.
     - Device-wide sync at steps `420,421,850,851`:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_sync_sample_device_steps420_421_850_851_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`.
       Both warmup and repeat were bad. This argues against a simple missing
       current-device synchronization as the root cause.

     Greedy fastpath boundary check:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_fp8_disable_greedy_fastpath_compile_i128_o430_r1_max16384_20260603.json`
       and `.log`.
     - Env delta: `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH=0`, with the same
       Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile graph and FP8 fallback route.
     - Result: warmup still produced the bad hash at token index `420`
       (`424` instead of old/repeat `411`), and repeat produced the good hash.
       Disabling the top1 greedy-token fastpath is not sufficient; the fault
       boundary is earlier than the greedy sampler.

     Decision:
     - Classification remains `B-pending`. The 27B-FP8 route has recovered
       measured repeat speed, but first external request stability is still not
       accepted.
     - Do not promote tensor dump, stream sync, D2H sync, device sync, or
       greedy-fastpath disable as a recovery route. They are diagnostics and
       perturb timing or speed.
     - Next useful target: compare first-bad vs repeat-good model internals
       before sampling, preferably at layer/GDN outputs around sequence lengths
       `544..546`, or isolate compile-graph replay state for the first
       post-capture external request. The sampler is no longer the primary
       suspect.

304. 2026-06-03 Reclassified the 35B-AWQ batched MoE quality question without
     using old 0.0.3 output as the quality oracle.

     Policy correction:
     - Old 0.0.3 remains a performance/route reference only. It is not a
       quality oracle because previous migration work found old-tree fast
       routes with unresolved numeric/model-quality risk.
     - The quality oracle for `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` is same-weight,
       same-input semantic equivalence against the per-expert dense TurboMind
       bridge, not token parity with old 0.0.3.

     Harness change:
     - `benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` now records
       `num_different`, fp16 ULP-diff statistics, `sign_mismatch_count`, and
       `max_abs_diff_on_sign_mismatch` in each `_stats()` report. This is a
       diagnostic-only JSON expansion and does not change any runtime path.
     - `python -m py_compile benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py`
       passed in conda env `vllm-0.0.5-t210`.

     Rechecked strict-safe bridge:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_vs_dense_recheck_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260603.json`.
     - Shape: real Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ layer
       `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`, `num_experts=256`,
       `top_k=8`, `m=1,2`, `group_size=128`, SM70 GPU.
     - Result: `dense_graphsafe` remains `A-pass`; W13, SiLU, and W2 all
       reported `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`.

     Batched AWQ MoE classification:
     - Single-expert artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_batched_vs_dense_single_expert0_ulp_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260603.json`.
     - Round-robin artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_batched_vs_dense_roundrobin_m4_ulp_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260603.json`.
     - Single-expert `m=1,2` still produced nonzero diffs even though only
       expert 0 was active. This weakens an offset/grouping-index bug
       hypothesis and points to TurboMind batched/grouped dispatch versus
       per-expert dispatch.
     - Round-robin `m=4` W13 showed `max_diff=0.000244140625`,
       `max_nonzero_ulp_diff=4`, `mean_nonzero_ulp_diff~=1.09`, and
       `sign_mismatch_count=0`. SiLU showed `max_nonzero_ulp_diff=2`.
       W2 had `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`; the one large ULP outlier was a
       near-zero sign flip with `max_abs_diff_on_sign_mismatch=5.96e-08`.
     - Interpretation: current evidence fits bounded fp16 schedule/dispatch
       differences, not a gross indexing, zero/scales, or row-order bug.
       However, the source of the grouped TurboMind difference is not yet
       mechanically proven well enough for `B-accept`.

     Fix direction:
     - Strict-safe/default-quality route: keep or switch to the
       `dense_graphsafe`/per-expert bridge. It is `A-pass` but belongs to the
       old `~50-52 tok/s` band and does not recover 35B-AWQ throughput.
     - Throughput route: `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` remains
       `B-pending`, not quality-cleared. To promote it, either:
       1. change the batched/grouped TurboMind path to match the per-expert
          compute order exactly; or
       2. complete a real `B-accept` package: prove the bounded fp16 dispatch
          source, show no monotonic growth across relevant shapes/layers, and
          pass model-level greedy/logprob/perplexity checks against the
          strict-safe oracle.
     - The fastest 35B-AWQ decode artifacts also hit
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT`; that path still needs
       a separate full-output exactness/model-quality gate. It must not be
       accepted solely because old 0.0.3 enabled it.

305. 2026-06-03 Implemented the AWQ MoE speed-route quality recovery tooling.

     Runtime diagnostics:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISPATCH_POLICY={default,reuse,measure}` as a
       diagnostic-only override for AWQ MoE TurboMind dispatch selection.
       Unset keeps the existing selector unchanged. This lets the batched path
       be compared under fixed dispatch policy before deciding whether the
       residual is a selector artifact or a stable fp16 compute-order delta.

     Exactness harness:
     - `benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` now supports
       `--awq-moe-layers`, `--fp8-moe-layers`, and `--moe-expert-patterns`
       for one-shot layer/pattern sweeps.
     - The MoE classifier now records RMS, max nonzero ULP by stage,
       sign-mismatch counts, and explicit fp16 acceptance bounds. It reports
       `B-accept` only when the op-level bounds pass and
       `--moe-model-quality-gate-passed` is explicitly set; otherwise bounded
       nonzero diffs remain `B-pending`.
     - Added AWQ `--awq-moe-actual legacy_single_token_compact` to check the
       0.0.3-style compact single-token full route. This compares the fused
       W13+SiLU intermediate, W2 sorted output, and final weighted output
       against the per-expert dense oracle, so the fastest legacy route no
       longer inherits quality status from old-tree output.

     Policy:
     - Keep `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` as the throughput recovery target.
       Do not drop it for being quality-pending; either make it exact, or
       promote it only with bounded fp16 evidence plus model-level quality
       evidence against the strict-safe oracle.

     Legacy compact correction:
     - The first full-output smoke for
       `--awq-moe-actual legacy_single_token_compact` with the old non-
       interleaved W13 ptr rows failed hard: fused W13+SiLU `max_diff` was
       `0.253173828125`, with thousands of sign mismatches. This is a real
       layout error, not an fp16 schedule difference.
     - Root cause: TurboMind `kGatedSilu` consumes adjacent `(gate, up)` column
       pairs, while the normal W13 prepare stores `[gate..., up...]`. The
       legacy compact full op was using fused gated-SiLU with the normal
       non-interleaved W13 source ptr rows.
     - Runtime fix: build a separate interleaved W13 TurboMind weight/ptr set
       for the legacy compact route and pass those rows to
       `awq_moe_single_token_sm70_out`; normal dense/batched paths keep the
       existing non-interleaved W13 ptrs.
     - Re-smoke with interleaved W13 dropped legacy full-output error to fp16
       scale: fused W13+SiLU `max_diff=0.0001220703125`, no sign mismatches;
       W2/final weighted output `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`, no sign
       mismatches. It remains `B-pending` until broader layer/pattern and
       model-level quality gates pass.

306. 2026-06-03 Continued the AWQ MoE speed-route quality recovery on the
     correct throughput path, `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`, with
     `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.

     Build note:
     - The source contains the diagnostic dispatch-policy override from item
       305, but the active runtime `_C.abi3.so` cannot yet be rebuilt with it.
       Full `setup.py build_ext --inplace` failed in `_C_stable_libtorch`
       against the active Torch 2.10 headers (`IntHeaderOnlyArrayRef` /
       `TORCH_BOX` errors). A targeted `_C` link produced an `.so` containing
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_DISPATCH_POLICY`, but importing it in the active
       environment failed with undefined
       `c10::cuda::c10_cuda_check_implementation`. The known-good 60 MiB
       `_C.abi3.so` was restored. Therefore no dispatch-policy override result
       is counted as quality evidence in this entry.

     Harness correction:
     - The MoE stats now record `max_abs_diff_at_max_nonzero_ulp`.
       Classification no longer treats a raw high ULP count near zero as a
       blocking large error when its absolute difference is within the
       near-zero sign-mismatch bound. This matters for W2 outputs where fp16
       values can cross `+/-0` with tiny absolute error while raw ULP appears
       enormous.
     - Validation: `python -m py_compile
       benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` and `uv run
       --no-sync ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py`
       passed.

     Strict-safe oracle recheck:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_dense_graphsafe_vs_dense_recheck_after_legacy_doc_qwen36_35a3b_tune0_20260603.json`.
     - Result: `dense_graphsafe` remains `A-pass`; W13, SiLU, and W2 all
       reported `max_diff=0.0`.

     Batched path op-level evidence:
     - Tune0 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_batched_vs_dense_layers1_20_39_patterns_m1_2_4_8_nearzero_ulp_gate_tune0_20260603.json`.
     - Tune1 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_batched_vs_dense_layers1_20_39_patterns_m1_2_4_8_nearzero_ulp_gate_tune1_20260603.json`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ real layers
       `model.language_model.layers.{1,20,39}.mlp.experts`, patterns
       `single:0`, `single:17`, `single:255`, `round_robin`, and
       `m=1,2,4,8`.
     - Tune0: `B-pending`, `op_b_accept_candidate=true`,
       `max_diff=0.00048828125`, `max_mean_diff=7.070484571158886e-07`,
       `num_above_fp16_bound=0`, `num_bad_sign_mismatch_reports=0`, and 8
       raw ULP outlier reports, all near-zero.
     - Tune1: `B-pending`, `op_b_accept_candidate=true`,
       `max_diff=0.00048828125`, `max_mean_diff=6.846676114946604e-07`,
       `num_above_fp16_bound=0`, `num_bad_sign_mismatch_reports=0`, and 9
       raw ULP outlier reports, all near-zero.
     - Stage maxima stayed bounded: W13 `max_diff=0.00048828125`,
       `max_nonzero_ulp_diff=18`, no sign mismatches; SiLU
       `max_diff<=0.00048828125`, `max_nonzero_ulp_diff<=3`, no sign
       mismatches; W2 sorted output `max_diff=0.00006103515625`, with only
       near-zero sign flips (`max_abs_diff_on_sign_mismatch=
       0.0000012516975402832031`).
     - Interpretation: this is not behaving like a row-offset, expert-index,
       scale, or W13 layout bug. Current op-level evidence fits bounded fp16
       schedule/dispatch differences between grouped/batched TurboMind and
       per-expert dense compute.

     Full-model quality gate:
     - Strict-safe artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_moe_quality_greedy8_20260603.json`.
     - Batched artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_moe_quality_greedy8_20260603.json`.
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_batched_moe_quality_greedy8_20260603.json`.
     - Route evidence: strict-safe showed `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=false`
       and batched showed `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=true`; both kept
       `awq_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_effective=false`.
     - Result: tokens were equal and sampler logits argmax was equal for all
       32 dumps, but the existing numeric model gate still failed:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.04888105392456055`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.013558745384216309`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.5468740463256836`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.22821824788408662`, and
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.7421875`.

     Self-noise control:
     - Strict-safe rerun artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_moe_quality_greedy8_self2_20260603.json`.
     - Strict-safe self-compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_selfnoise_quality_greedy8_20260603.json`.
     - Result: two strict-safe runs also kept token equality and sampler
       argmax equality but failed the same strict numeric gate:
       `prompt_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.06936883926391602`,
       `output_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.0019331350922584534`,
       `output_top_logprob_max_abs_diff=0.09375`,
       `prompt_perplexity_max_abs_diff=0.772628677249088`, and
       `sampler_logits_max_abs_diff=0.359375`.

     Decision:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` remains the throughput recovery target
       and should not be dropped. Op-level evidence now supports treating the
       residual as bounded fp16 dispatch difference, not a structural kernel
       bug, for the tested layers/patterns.
     - Classification remains `B-pending`, not `B-accept`, because the formal
       model-level numeric gate has not been made decisive. The current gate
       fails even for strict-safe self-noise, so next work should either
       replace it with a calibrated same-route-noise-aware gate or run a
       broader semantic/token-quality acceptance package before rerunning the
       speed benchmark for promotion.

307. 2026-06-03 Ran the requested 2-prompt, 1024-token greedy long-output
     check for `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM`.

     Shape:
     - Model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, dtype half, quantization AWQ,
       Flash-V100 attention, breakable CUDA graph, `max_model_len=1536`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=128`, `max_num_seqs=2`.
     - Sampling: two prompts in one `llm.generate` call, `temperature=0`,
       `max_tokens=1024`, `ignore_eos=true`, no logprobs.
     - Both runs kept `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.

     Artifacts:
     - Strict-safe:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_moe_quality_greedy1024_2prompts_full_logits_20260603.json`.
     - Batched:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_moe_quality_greedy1024_2prompts_tokenonly_20260603.json`.
     - Compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_batched_moe_quality_greedy1024_2prompts_tokenonly_20260603.json`.

     Route evidence:
     - Strict-safe log hit `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path
       enabled`, `CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`, and
       `single-token active-expert dense path enabled`; policy recorded
       `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=false` and
       `awq_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_effective=false`.
     - Batched log hit `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`; policy
       recorded `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=true` and
       `awq_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_effective=false`.
     - The initial attempt set sampler-logit dump envs, but no dump files were
       produced because this token-only greedy run uses the LM-head top1 path
       and does not request full logits. The long-output decision below is
       based on token IDs only.

     Result:
     - `equal=false`; the model-quality gate failed with
       `deterministic token IDs differ`.
     - Prompt 0: `left_len=1024`, `right_len=1024`, common prefix `176`;
       first mismatch at output token index `176`, strict-safe token `3428`,
       batched token `513`; same-position mismatch count `828/1024`.
     - Prompt 1: `left_len=1024`, `right_len=1024`, common prefix `215`;
       first mismatch at output token index `215`, strict-safe token `97427`,
       batched token `97622`; same-position mismatch count `798/1024`.
     - Timing for this diagnostic shape: strict-safe generated 2048 tokens in
       `293.9370672879886 s`; batched generated 2048 tokens in
       `114.94364283399773 s`.

     Decision:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` cannot be default-enabled yet. The
       earlier op-level evidence still looks like bounded fp16 dispatch
       difference, but the full model accumulates enough difference to flip
       greedy tokens by roughly 176-215 output tokens in this 2-prompt test.
     - Next useful diagnostic is targeted, not broad: capture logits/top-token
       margin around the first divergence windows, e.g. output steps
       `160..220`, or force full logits only for a short window. Full 1024-step
       sampler-logit dumping is unnecessary for the current failure.

308. 2026-06-03 Added and exercised an AWQ MoE layer filter for targeted
     `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM` quality localization.

     Code change:
     - Added `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_LAYER_ALLOWLIST` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_LAYER_DENYLIST`.
     - Default behavior is unchanged. With `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       listed deny layers use the existing per-expert dense strict-safe path;
       other layers keep the batched/grouped TurboMind GEMM path.

     Short-window test shape:
     - Same Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ / TP4 / Flash-V100 / breakable decode graph
       setup as item 307, `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.
     - Generated only 230 greedy output tokens for the same two prompts and
       compared them offline against the existing strict-safe 1024-token
       artifact prefix.

     Artifacts and results:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_LAYER_DENYLIST=20-39`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_moe_deny20_39_greedy230_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       Prompt 0 still first diverged at index `176` with strict token `3428`
       vs candidate `513`; prompt 1 diverged earlier at index `83`.
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_LAYER_DENYLIST=0-19`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_moe_deny0_19_greedy230_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       Prompt 0 again first diverged at index `176` with strict token `3428`
       vs candidate `513`; prompt 1 diverged earlier at index `15`.
     - A candidate that kept all layers batched but changed single-token decode
       final reduction to the strict-safe weighted-reduce op was also tested:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_moe_weighted_reduce_greedy230_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       It preserved batched-level speed for this 230-token shape
       (`26.955037848005304 s` for 460 output tokens), but did not fix prompt 0
       and made prompt 1 diverge at index `15`. The code change was reverted.

     Decision:
     - The long-output drift is not fixed by exacting only the first or last
       half of MoE layers, and it is not merely the generic unpermute vs
       single-token weighted-reduce final reduction order.
     - The next focused work should inspect the W13/W2 grouped GEMM compute
       order against the per-expert dense order on actual runtime decode
       states around the index-176/index-215 divergence windows. Do not repeat
       the 1024-token proof; use short-window or op-level compare runs tied to
       W13/W2 root-cause hypotheses.

309. 2026-06-03 Repaired the AWQ MoE batched W13 quality failure without
     dropping the W13 speed path.

     Code change:
     - Added `Operation::dispatch_num_override` in the TurboMind GEMM wrapper.
       When set, `Gemm::Run` builds a dispatch-only context with the override
       `num`, uses it for `Measure`/`Dispatch`, then launches with the original
       grouped descriptors. This changes launch-spec selection only; it does
       not replace the grouped/batched GEMM with the dense W13 path.
     - Added `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70_per_expert_dispatch_out`, exposed it
       through `vllm/_sm70_ops.py`, and wired only the AWQ MoE batched W13
       stage to it. W2 stays on the existing batched grouped GEMM path.
     - Removed the earlier dense-W13 fallback knob so the accepted candidate is
       not a hidden bypass of W13 grouped/batched compute.
     - Updated benchmark metadata so `accepted_awq_moe_default_policy` no
       longer means `batched=false`; the accepted AWQ MoE policy is AWQ MoE
       enabled, safe selector active, and legacy compact disabled. The batched
       lane is recorded separately by `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective`.
     - Added a verifier route gate,
       `--require-awq-moe-batched-safe-route`, requiring batched AWQ MoE,
       legacy compact off, safe tune evidence, and the W13 per-expert-dispatch
       log.

     Build/static validation:
     - `python -m py_compile` passed for the touched Python files.
     - `ruff check` passed for the touched Python files.
     - `git diff --check` passed for the touched files.
     - Incremental `_C` build passed with
       `ninja -C build/temp.linux-x86_64-cpython-312-t210-sm70 -j4 _C`, the
       rebuilt `_C.abi3.so` was copied into `vllm/_C.abi3.so`, and Python saw
       `torch.ops._C.awq_moe_gemm_sm70_per_expert_dispatch_out`.

     Quality evidence:
     - Strict-safe 1024-token oracle:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_tune0_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
     - Fixed batched W13 1024-token candidate:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_w13_per_expert_dispatch_tune0_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, greedy, two prompts, 1024 output
       tokens, `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`, tune0.
     - Result: both prompts matched strict-safe exactly for all 1024 output
       tokens (`mismatch_count=0`, no first mismatch). This closes the item
       307 drift at prompt0 index `176` and prompt1 index `215` for the tested
       gate.
     - The fixed path differs from the old bad batched 100-token artifact at
       prompt1 index `15`: fixed/strict token `111853`, old batched token
       `95839`. This confirms the repair changed the old drifting route rather
       than reusing its output.
     - Short 100-token candidate artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_w13_per_expert_dispatch_tune0_greedy100_2prompts_20260603.json`;
       it also matched the strict-safe 100-token oracle exactly.

     Speed evidence for this diagnostic shape:
     - Strict-safe generated 2048 output tokens in `293.54494363100093 s`
       (`6.98 tok/s`).
     - Fixed batched W13 generated 2048 output tokens in
       `117.48874287700164 s` (`17.44 tok/s`).
     - This is not the final production `1k/256` speed口径, but it proves the
       quality fix did not demote W13 back to the slow per-expert dense route.

     Decision:
     - `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` with legacy compact off is accepted
       for this 2-prompt 1024-token greedy quality gate against the current
       strict-safe oracle.
     - The old 0.0.3 tree remains only a performance/route reference, not a
       quality oracle.
     - The 0.0.3 legacy single-token compact lane remains separate. Item 305
       recorded its original W13 layout quality bug and interleaved-W13 repair
       to fp16-scale residual; it still needs its own model-level acceptance
       before it can be counted with the batched W13 route.

310. 2026-06-03 Rechecked the complete 35B-AWQ speed lane after the batched
     W13 dispatch repair.

     Rule update:
     - The lesson from the W13 repair is now project memory
       `sm70/speed_quality_recovery_rule`: when a speed route fails quality,
       do not drop or bypass the accelerated subpath as the fix. Keep the
       speed path, repair the failing kernel/dispatch/order issue directly,
       and only use slow fallbacks as diagnostics.

     Shape:
     - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, V100 GPUs 0-3, greedy, no MTP,
       `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=1024`, `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=1`.

     Artifact:

       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_w13_fix_0dot3_compile_graph_i1024_o256_r1_20260603.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_w13_fix_0dot3_compile_graph_i1024_o256_r1_20260603.log`.

     Route evidence:

     - `Using ['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL'] all-reduce backends ... for group 'tp:0'`.

     Speed:

       `output_tps=122.29597771030193`, `prefill_time=0.1795305730047403 s`,

     Verifier:
     - Passed
       `verify_sm70_artifact.py --require-sm70-flash-v100-0dot3-compile-graph
       --require-full-flash-v100 --require-awq-moe-0dot3-baseline-route
       --require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route --require-decode-timing`.

       should not be confused with item 309's legacy-off batched W13 quality
       gate. The legacy compact lane remains separately quality-tracked.

311. 2026-06-03 Re-ran AWQ batched MoE op-level max-diff after the W13
     dispatch repair.

     Harness correction:
     - `benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py --awq-moe-actual batched` now
       matches current runtime semantics: W13 uses
       `_C::awq_moe_gemm_sm70_per_expert_dispatch_out`, while W2 remains on
       the existing grouped/batched AWQ MoE GEMM op. Before this correction
       the harness would have measured the old unfixed W13 dispatch path.

     Artifact:
     - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_batched_w13_dispatchfix_vs_dense_layers1_20_39_patterns_m1_2_4_8_tune0_20260603.json`.

     Shape:
     - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ real expert weights.
     - Layers: `model.language_model.layers.{1,20,39}.mlp.experts`.
     - Patterns: `single:0`, `single:17`, `single:255`, `round_robin`.
     - `m=1,2,4,8`, `top_k=8`, `num_experts=256`, tune0.

     Result:
     - Strict `max_diff == 0` failed; the command exited nonzero as expected
       for the strict harness.
     - Overall classification remains `B-pending` / `op_b_accept_candidate`.
       No report exceeded the fp16 bound `0.002`; there were no bad
       sign-mismatch reports.
     - Overall maxima:
       - W13 gate/up: `max_diff=0.00048828125`,
         `max_nonzero_ulp_diff=18`, no sign mismatches.
       - SiLU: `max_diff=0.00048828125`, `max_nonzero_ulp_diff=3`, no sign
         mismatches.
       - W2 sorted output: `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`; raw large ULP reports
         were near-zero cases, with `max_abs_diff_on_sign_mismatch=
         1.2516975402832031e-06`.
     - Decode-shape detail:
       - For `m=1`, W13 and SiLU were bitwise exact across the tested
         layers/patterns: `max_diff=0.0`, `num_nonzero=0`.
       - For `m=1`, W2 sorted output still had tiny nonzero fp16 differences,
         with max `1.52587890625e-05`.
       - For `m=2`, W13 and SiLU also stayed bitwise exact in this sweep; W2
         max was `3.0517578125e-05`.
       - For `m=4/8`, W13 and SiLU still showed bounded fp16 differences up
         to `0.00048828125`.

     Interpretation:
     - The W13 dispatch repair did not make the whole batched MoE route
       bitwise exact.
     - It did make the single-token decode W13/SILU stage exact for the tested
       layers/patterns, which explains why the long greedy decode token gate
       can pass even though the broader op-level batched path still has
       bounded fp16 differences.
     - Keep the distinction explicit: item 309 is a model-level long-output
       token pass for `BATCHED_GEMM=1` with legacy compact off; item 311 shows
       the corresponding op-level route is still not Type-A `max_diff=0` for
       all tested shapes.

312. 2026-06-03 Repaired the AWQ MoE legacy single-token compact fastpath
     quality issue without disabling the fastpath.

     Scope:
     - Target route:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=1`, used together with
       the 0.0.3 AWQ MoE throughput lane.
     - This is separate from item 309. Item 309 accepted the batched W13
       quality gate with legacy compact off; this entry validates the legacy
       compact path itself.

     Root cause:
     - Item 305 had already fixed the hard layout bug by giving legacy compact
       an interleaved W13 ptr set. That removed the `0.253173828125` fused
       W13+SiLU error and left fp16-scale residuals.
     - The remaining residual was still kernel/order related, not an
       unavoidable model-level quality loss:
       - TurboMind fused gated-SiLU rounded the gate/up activation in a
         different place than the vLLM `silu_and_mul` oracle.
       - The compact W13 grouped GEMM used a different dispatch selection than
         the per-expert compute order used by the strict oracle.

     Code change:
     - `csrc/sm70_turbomind/lmdeploy/src/turbomind/kernels/gemm/epilogue.h`
       now rounds gated-SiLU operands through the output dtype before applying
       SiLU and multiplication, matching the fp16 semantics of vLLM
       `silu_and_mul`.
     - `csrc/sm70_turbomind/ops/awq_sm70_gemm.cu` now routes legacy compact
       W13/W2 and the compact-W13 diagnostic op through
       `awq_moe_gemm_sm70_per_expert_dispatch_out`. This changes dispatch
       selection to match per-expert compute order while keeping the compact
       grouped launch path; it is not a slow-path bypass.
     - Incremental `_C` rebuild passed with
       `ninja -C build/temp.linux-x86_64-cpython-312-t210-sm70 -j4 _C`, and
       the rebuilt `_C.abi3.so` was copied into `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.

     Op-level evidence:
     - After the epilogue-only change, the full legacy compact op was reduced
       but still not exact: fused W13+SiLU `max_diff=6.103515625e-05`, W2
       `max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`, final weighted output
       `max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`.
     - The `single_token_compact_w13` diagnostic then showed W13 gate/up
       still at `max_diff=0.000244140625`, which localized the remaining
       issue to compact W13 dispatch/order rather than the final weighted
       reduction.
     - After the per-expert-dispatch repair, layer-1 `round_robin` passed
       exactly:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_single_token_compact_w13_perdispatch_roundingfix_layer1_roundrobin_m1_tune0_20260603.json`
       and
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_perdispatch_roundingfix_layer1_roundrobin_m1_tune0_20260603.json`
       both reported `A-pass`, `max_diff=0.0`.
     - Expanded sweep artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/sm70_awq_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_perdispatch_roundingfix_layers1_20_39_patterns_m1_tune0_20260603.json`.
       Shape: layers `1,20,39`; patterns `single:0`, `single:17`,
       `single:255`, `round_robin`; `m=1`, tune0. Result: `A-pass`,
       global `max_diff=0.0`, `num_reports=36`; fused W13+SiLU, W2 sorted
       output, and final weighted output were all bitwise exact.

     Model-level long-output evidence:
     - Strict-safe oracle:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_tune0_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
     - Candidate with both speed routes enabled:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_batched_legacy_compact_perdispatch_roundingfix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
     - Compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_batched_legacy_compact_perdispatch_roundingfix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
     - Shape: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, dtype half, AWQ,
       Flash-V100 attention, `max_model_len=1536`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=128`, `max_num_seqs=2`, greedy
       `temperature=0`, `top_p=1`, `top_k=-1`, `ignore_eos=true`,
       two prompts, 1024 output tokens each.
     - Route evidence: candidate recorded
       `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=true` and
       `awq_moe_legacy_single_token_compact_effective=true`, with runtime
       logs for `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE batched W13 using per-expert dispatch selection`, and
       `SM70 AWQ MoE 0.0.3 legacy single-token compact path enabled`.
     - Result: compare reported `equal=true`; both prompts had
       `prompt_equal=true`, output length `1024`, and `first_mismatch=null`.
       The model gate records `token_equal=true` and no failed evidence.

     Boundary:
     - This repairs legacy compact quality for the tested single-token decode
       fastpath and the requested 2-prompt, 1024-token greedy gate against the
       current strict-safe oracle.
     - The diagnostic token benchmark generated 2048 output tokens in
       `117.74176527600503 s`; this is a quality artifact, not the production
       `1k/256` speed baseline. The production speed lane remains item 310
       (`steady_decode_tps=133.4302050583151 tok/s`) and should be rechecked
       only when a speed-go/no-go decision is needed.

313. 2026-06-03 Reclassified the 35B-AWQ compile/full-graph quality issue:
     the target-route failure is primarily a FULL CUDA graph quality problem.

     Scope:
     - Model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP4, dtype half, Flash-V100 attention,
       `max_model_len=1536`, `max_num_batched_tokens=128`, `max_num_seqs=2`.
     - Prompts: the two deterministic 1024-output greedy prompts used by the
       current AWQ long-output gate.
     - Strict-safe oracle:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_tune0_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.

     Evidence:
     - `VLLM_COMPILE + FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` / 0.0.3 compile graph route:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_compile_graph_gdn_state_schema4_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`;
       compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_compile_graph_gdn_state_schema4_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       Result: not exact; prompt0 first mismatch at output index `103`
       (`19652` vs `318`), prompt1 first mismatch at output index `15`
       (`111853` vs `95839`), throughput `28.977 tok/s`.
     - No-compile full decode CUDA graph route
       (`mode=NONE`, `cudagraph_mode=FULL_DECODE_ONLY`):
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_no_compile_decode_graph_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`;
       compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_no_compile_decode_graph_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       Result: prompt0 still mismatches at output index `103`
       (`19652` vs `318`), while prompt1 is exact for all 1024 output tokens;
       throughput `6.713 tok/s`.
     - Swapped-prompt no-compile full decode CUDA graph route:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_no_compile_decode_graph_swapped_greedy128_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       Shape matches the no-compile full graph route, but prompt order is
       reversed and output length is 128 to cover the known prompt0 index-103
       failure window. Result: request0, now the Chinese prompt, diverged
       against the existing strict-safe prompt oracle at output index `83`
       (`98608` vs `96335`); request1, now the English prompt, matched the
       existing strict-safe oracle for all 128 checked tokens. Comparing the
       two no-compile full-graph runs by prompt text shows the same slot/order
       effect: Chinese prompt request1 -> request0 first differs at index `83`;
       English prompt request0 -> request1 first differs at index `103`.
     - Compile-only synthetic control (`mode=VLLM_COMPILE`,
       `cudagraph_mode=NONE`):
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_vllm_compile_no_cudagraph_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`;
       compare:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_vllm_compile_no_cudagraph_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`.
       Result: both prompts diverge at output index `0`
       (prompt0 `271` vs `148304`, prompt1 `271` vs `107551`);
       throughput `7.121 tok/s`.

     Interpretation:
     - Do not use the compile-only synthetic control as the quality route for
       this target. Its first-token failure is a different configuration and
       does not explain the `VLLM_COMPILE + FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` target route,
       whose first generated tokens match the strict-safe oracle.
     - The no-compile full decode CUDA graph route reproduces the same prompt0
       index-103 failure as the target route. This makes ordinary FULL CUDA
       graph replay/input-state handling the primary suspect for the current
       target-route quality issue.
     - Prompt-level asymmetry is not a pass signal. The swapped-prompt probe
       makes the request slot/order hypothesis stronger than a prompt-content
       or top-1-margin-only hypothesis: the request0/first scheduled stream is
       the one that drifts under ordinary FULL CUDA graph replay.
     - The strict-safe oracle used `VLLM_USE_BREAKABLE_CUDAGRAPH=1`; breakable
       CUDA graph remains the current correctness oracle for this test, not the
       same as the ordinary full decode CUDA graph route.

     Code note:
     - `qwen_gdn_attention_core` now falls back to the real bound
