       `self.kv_cache` when a compiled trace supplies the profile-time empty
       state placeholders. This fixes a compile-only startup crash and does not
       count as a quality fix for either compile-only or full-graph drift.

314. 2026-06-04 Localized the TurboQuant Flash-V100 attention quality question
     against Triton with CUDA graph disabled.

     Scope:
     - Model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2, dtype half, AWQ,
       `kv_cache_dtype=turboquant_4bit_nc`, `max_model_len=20000`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`, `--enforce-eager`.
     - Quality investigation kept CUDA graph off because ordinary/full graph
       quality is tracked separately. This entry is about Flash-V100 dense
       prefill and Flash-V100 TQ packed-cache decode vs the Triton TQ route.

     Decode tensor evidence:
     - Flash-V100 TQ decode tensor dumps:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/tq_decode_compare_dumps_after_splitfix/decode_compare_pid612448_idx30_max0.001953125.pt`
       and
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/tq_decode_compare_dumps_after_splitfix/decode_compare_pid612448_idx40_max0.001953125.pt`.
     - Offline replay reproduced the saved outputs exactly. The residual
       Flash-TQ decode diff is in stage1, not stage2: Flash stage1 plus Triton
       stage2 equals Flash full output, and both still differ from Triton full
       by at most `0.001953125`.
     - Stage1 intermediate diffs are small (`tmp_out max_diff=1.52587890625e-05`,
       split LSE `max_diff=3.814697265625e-06`) and only a few final fp16
       output elements cross a one-ULP boundary. This is not a CUDA graph
       replay artifact.

     Prefill tensor evidence:
     - Added a default-off TurboQuant compare dump hook controlled by
       `VLLM_SM70_TURBOQUANT_COMPARE_DUMP_DIR`, sharing the existing compare
       threshold/limit envs. It records real Flash/Triton prefill inputs and
       outputs for offline analysis.
     - Real-model prefill compare run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_flashprefill_vs_triton_dump_i16324_o1_20260604.json`;
       JSONL:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/turboquant_flashv100_vs_triton_prefill_dump_i16324_o1_20260604.jsonl`.
       It produced 20 prefill compare records. Both ranks reached
       `max_diff=0.0078125`.
     - Saved real tensor dumps:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/tq_prefill_compare_dumps_flashv100_vs_triton/prefill_compare_pid615807_idx2_max0.0078125.pt`
       and
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/tq_prefill_compare_dumps_flashv100_vs_triton/prefill_compare_pid615808_idx4_max0.0078125.pt`.
     - Offline single-query replay using the same real Q/K/V reproduced the
       Flash full-prefill output exactly. This rules out batch ordering, request
       ordering, CUDA graph, and full-causal tiling state as the source.
     - Flash QK scores are not the blocker: at representative max-diff rows,
       Flash QK scores vs fp32 QK had only `~8e-06` max score error, and using
       Flash scores with fp32 softmax plus fp32 P@V lands on the Triton/fp32
       output. The remaining difference is localized to the Flash-V100
       `P@V` WMMA path and final fp16 boundary.

     Model-token evidence:
     - Triton reference run with both TurboQuant Flash gates disabled:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_triton_prefill_decode_i16324_o1024_20260604.json`.
     - Existing post-TQ-decode-fix Flash candidate:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_turboquant4bit_i16324_o1024_tp2_flashv100_vs_triton_compare_after_tqdecode_fix_20260604.json`.
     - Prompt hash matched:
       `9868e6cfcc326f2455cfbe49591453f4164980231083baead26b623c40b52177`.
       The 1024 generated token ids matched exactly; both token hashes were
       `a8133cbbdb2b075703a6cb734a1489008a2b6db8495c981ea619a65a79afaf44`,
       with `first_mismatch=null`.
     - The older eager warm1/r2 Flash-TQ artifact
       `decode_qwen36_35b_awq_turboquant4bit_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_flashv100_tqdecode_enforce_eager_20260604.json`
       should not be used as current post-fix failure evidence. Its repeat1
       sequence matches the current Triton reference prefix exactly; warmup and
       repeat0 contain the extra token at index 123 and reflect an older or
       mixed pre-fix state.

     Interpretation:
     - The current post-fix Flash-prefill plus Flash-TQ-decode route is not
       bitwise-equal to Triton at attention tensor level, but the requested
       long single-prompt 1024 greedy token gate against the all-Triton TQ
       reference passes under eager/no-graph.
     - Do not disable or discard the Flash/TQ speed path based only on the
       tensor `max_diff` above. Treat the attention diffs as bounded
       Flash-vs-Triton fp16/MMa-order differences unless a current-code
       model-token/logit gate fails.
     - Remaining work before calling it fully quality-cleared: run a
       current-code self-repeat/two-prompt 1024 gate if default promotion needs
       repeat evidence, and only then spend kernel time rewriting Flash-V100
       P@V to match Triton bit-for-bit. A naive fp32 scalar P@V fix would be a
       speed tradeoff and is not an acceptable final repair.

315. 2026-06-04 Official-sampling 2048-token gate for TurboQuant Flash-V100
     attention failed against the all-Triton TQ reference.

     Scope:
     - Same model and engine口径 as item 314:
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2, dtype half, AWQ,
       `kv_cache_dtype=turboquant_4bit_nc`, `max_model_len=20000`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`, `--enforce-eager`.
     - Sampling used the local model `generation_config.json` values:
       `do_sample=true`, `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`;
       benchmark command used `--sampling-seed 0` and `--ignore-eos`.

     2048-token result:
     - All-Triton TQ reference:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_triton_official_sampling_i16324_o2048_seed0_20260604.json`.
       Prompt hash
       `9868e6cfcc326f2455cfbe49591453f4164980231083baead26b623c40b52177`,
       output length `2048`, output hash
       `b93cbb09c462a41b5fcf39670911188146177b6933bede3ffead618f21183f9e`,
       generate seconds `242.047`.
     - Flash-prefill plus Flash-TQ-decode candidate:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_flashprefill_flashdecode_official_sampling_i16324_o2048_seed0_20260604.json`.
       The runtime logs hit both `TURBOQUANT prefill using Flash-V100 dense
       raw-QKV path` and `TURBOQUANT decode using Flash-V100 packed-cache
       path`. Output length `2048`, output hash
       `e15c701cb9c1d1e696bf04a38e95564d8276bc8309dac08865a024a4e15c43ee`,
       generate seconds `232.283`.
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/compare_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_triton_vs_flash_official_sampling_i16324_o2048_seed0_20260604.json`.
       Result: `equal=false`, prompt equal, first mismatch at output index `16`:
       Triton token `1061`, Flash token `248046`.

     First-mismatch localization:
     - Short logprob reruns with identical sampling params and `max_tokens=20`,
       `logprobs=20` reproduced the same prefix and mismatch:
       `model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_triton_official_sampling_i16324_o20_logprobs20_seed0_20260604.json`
       and
       `model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_flashprefill_flashdecode_official_sampling_i16324_o20_logprobs20_seed0_20260604.json`.
     - The first 16 generated tokens match exactly. At output index `16`, both
       distributions still rank token `1919` first and token `1061` second, but
       the candidate raises EOS token `248046` from rank 6 to rank 5. Recorded
       logprobs:
       - token `1061`: Triton `-1.2003114`, Flash `-1.5574681`,
         abs diff `0.3571566`;
       - token `248046`: Triton `-4.7940617`, Flash `-4.0496554`,
         abs diff `0.7444062`;
       - common top-20 max abs logprob diff at this step:
         `0.9240937`.

     Interpretation:
     - Official sampling makes the Flash/Triton attention residual user-visible:
       same seed, same prefix, and same sampler settings diverge at output index
       `16`. This is not a long-tail accumulation-only failure.
     - The item-314 1024-token greedy pass remains valid, but it is not enough
       to default-accept the Flash-prefill plus Flash-TQ-decode route for model
       quality. The route is now `model-fail` for the official-sampling
       2048-token gate until the underlying Flash/TQ distribution shift is
       repaired or bounded by an explicit, accepted non-greedy quality criterion.
     - Next repair target is still the Flash-V100 attention arithmetic path,
       starting from the prefill `P@V` WMMA/final-fp16 boundary and Flash-TQ
       decode stage1 arithmetic identified in item 314. Do not solve this by
       turning off Flash prefill/decode; the speed path must be made safe.

316. 2026-06-04 Flash-V100 prefill scalar-PV repair experiment: root cause
     narrowed further, but the official-sampling 2048 gate still fails.

     Scope:
     - Same Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP2 TurboQuant 4-bit NC cache口径 as items
       314 and 315, always on physical GPUs 2/3 via
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`, `--enforce-eager`, and CUDA graph disabled.
     - Added an experimental C++ env gate
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_SCALAR_PV=1` in
       `flash-attention-v100/kernel/fused_mha_forward.cu`. The default path is
       unchanged; the env-only path keeps Flash QK and online softmax but
       replaces the D=256 `half P @ half V` WMMA update with scalar fp32
       accumulation from the same half probability tile. This is a diagnostic
       repair, not an accepted final performance implementation.

     Tensor evidence:
     - On saved real prefill dumps from item 314, single-row replay with
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_SCALAR_PV=1` moved representative old
       max-diff rows from Flash-vs-Triton `0.0078125` down to `0` to
       `3.0517578125e-05`.
     - Full-layer replay on
       `prefill_compare_pid615807_idx2_max0.0078125.pt`:
       scalar-PV vs Triton `max=0.00390625`, `mean=4.53e-07`,
       `gt_1e-3=303`; old/current WMMA path vs Triton had
       `max=0.0078125`, `mean=1.22e-05`, `gt_1e-3=6843`.
     - Full-layer replay on
       `prefill_compare_pid615808_idx4_max0.0078125.pt`:
       scalar-PV vs Triton `max=0.0078125`, `mean=9.51e-07`,
       `gt_1e-3=480`; current WMMA path vs Triton had
       `max=0.0078125`, `mean=1.39e-05`, `gt_1e-3=4426`.
     - Interpretation: scalar-PV proves the large model-visible residual is in
       Flash-V100 prefill `P@V` arithmetic, not QK, mask, request ordering,
       CUDA graph, or wrapper layout. However it does not bit-match Triton:
       some D=256 head/column positions still land one fp16 ULP away. Current
       WMMA and scalar-PV each match different subsets of those positions, so
       the final repair must match Triton's `tl.dot(p.to(fp16), v)` tensor-core
       semantics rather than blindly choosing scalar or current WMMA.

     Model-token evidence:
     - `max_tokens=20`, official sampling with `logprobs=20`, Flash prefill
       scalar-PV plus Triton decode:
       `model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_flashprefill_scalarpv_tritondecode_official_sampling_i16324_o20_logprobs20_seed0_20260604.json`.
       It matched the 20-token all-Triton reference token hash
       `ca377a6b16304e9c5d966a3caab8749a597e2fcce35549ec6175c0db14f2f645`.
       This short/logprob run is not sufficient for acceptance.
     - `max_tokens=2048`, Flash prefill scalar-PV plus Flash-TQ decode:
       `model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_flashprefill_scalarpv_flashdecode_official_sampling_i16324_o2048_seed0_20260604.json`.
       It hit both Flash prefill and Flash-TQ decode routes. Output hash
       `e0586f2994502ce0328bdd00130e2c937ebe277d486155409dd1feadb8770ae4`,
       first mismatch vs all-Triton at output index `118`: Triton token
       `2570` (`" User"`), candidate token `38622` (`" Goal"`).
     - `max_tokens=2048`, Flash prefill scalar-PV plus Triton decode:
       `model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_tq_flashprefill_scalarpv_tritondecode_official_sampling_i16324_o2048_seed0_20260604.json`.
       It hit Flash prefill and Triton `_tq_decode_stage1/_fwd_kernel_stage2`
       decode. Output hash
       `2ce2cbdf0ab40344f46aba131a123978ed49bb89b4cfcf7d65c3ece168f773c5`,
       first mismatch vs all-Triton at output index `16`: Triton token
       `1061` (`" This"`), candidate token `248046` (`"<|im_end|>"`).
     - The scalar-PV plus Flash-TQ-decode run delaying divergence to index
       `118` is not a pass; Flash-TQ decode is only partially compensating for
       the remaining prefill residual on the early prefix.

     Current status:
     - `VLLM_FLASH_V100_PREFILL_SCALAR_PV=1` is useful evidence and a partial
       repair, but it is not acceptable as the final route: it is slower on the
       16k prefill and still fails the 2048 official-sampling token gate.
     - The root cause to fix is now specifically the D=256 Flash-V100 prefill
       `P@V` update needing Triton-compatible fp16 tensor-core dot semantics
       and final fp16 rounding. Keep Flash prefill/decode enabled as the target
       speed path; do not mark this acceleration accepted until the 2048
       official-sampling gate is token-identical or a stricter accepted
       non-greedy quality criterion is explicitly defined.

317. 2026-06-04 TurboQuant stable-quality baseline and acceleration taxonomy
     correction for this work slice.

     Temporary stable-quality baseline:
     - For runs whose goal is stable TurboQuant output quality, keep the
       local Flash-V100 TurboQuant attention gates disabled:
       `VLLM_SM70_TURBOQUANT_FLASH_V100_PREFILL=0` and
       `VLLM_SM70_TURBOQUANT_FLASH_V100_DECODE=0`.
     - With both gates disabled, the run still uses `TurboQuantAttentionBackend`,
       but follows the official-style TurboQuant route instead of the local
       Flash-V100 dense-prefill / Flash-V100 packed-cache decode route.
     - This is a temporary quality baseline for this slice, not a final decision
       to abandon the Flash-V100/TurboQuant attention acceleration.

     Correction:
     - Do not classify the 90 to 120 tok/s AWQ acceleration point as the
       Flash-V100/TurboQuant attention path. That speed point belongs to the
       compile / FULL graph line.
     - The Flash-V100/TurboQuant attention work in items 314 to 316 is a
       separate attention-backend experiment and should not be used as the
       explanation for the 90 to 120 tok/s compile/FULL-graph acceleration.
     - Future progress reports must keep these two tracks separate:
       TurboQuant attention quality/stability on one side, and compile /
       FULL graph speed recovery and quality on the other.

318. 2026-06-04 Native Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 MTP smoke on latest vLLM.

     Scope:
     - This is the first-run check for the next adaptation point, MTP. It uses
       the official latest-vLLM implementation, not a local reimplementation.
     - Relevant upstream model classes and config path are present in this tree:
       `Qwen3_5MTP`, `Qwen3_5MoeMTP`, and
       `speculative_config={"method":"mtp","num_speculative_tokens":1}`.
     - All runs used physical GPU 2/3 only where TP2 was needed, with
       `VLLM_USE_V1=1`, `--enforce-eager`, `language_model_only=true`, and
       `skip_mm_profiling=true`. CUDA graph / compile were deliberately off
       for this smoke so this result stays separate from the compile/FULL graph
       quality track.

     Harness note:
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` now serializes benchmark
       payloads through a JSON-safe helper before writing. vLLM mutates the
       input `speculative_config` by attaching config objects such as
       `ModelConfig`; without the helper, MTP runs complete generation but fail
       while dumping JSON.

     Route evidence:
     - Qwen3.5-9B-AWQ TP1:
       `model_tokens_qwen35_9b_awq_mtp_smoke_textonly_i64_o16_20260604.json`.
       The run selected target `Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration`, draft
       `Qwen3_5MTP`, shared target embeddings / lm_head with the draft model,
       generated 16 tokens, and wrote the result.
     - Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ TP2:
       `model_tokens_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp_smoke_textonly_i64_o16_20260604.json`.
       The run selected target `Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration`, draft
       `Qwen3_5MTP`, used TP custom all-reduce, shared target embeddings /
       lm_head, hit Flash-V100 attention and SM70 LM-head top1 logs, generated
       16 tokens, and wrote the result.
     - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP2:
       `model_tokens_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp_smoke_textonly_i64_o16_20260604.json`.
       The run selected target `Qwen3_5MoeForConditionalGeneration`, draft
       `Qwen3_5MoeMTP`, used TP custom all-reduce plus EP PYNCCL, hit
       `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`, per-expert W13 dispatch,
       legacy single-token compact, Flash-V100 attention, and SM70 LM-head
       top1 logs, generated 16 tokens, and wrote the result.

     Current status:
     - Native official MTP can run on the tested AWQ dense and MoE model paths
       in this latest-vLLM tree. The earlier stdin-based smoke failure was a
       multiprocessing spawn artifact, not an MTP model failure.
     - This is not an accepted MTP speed or quality result. The observed first
       generate still JIT-compiles MTP / verifier kernels such as
       `eagle_prepare_next_token_padded_kernel`,
       `eagle_prepare_inputs_padded_kernel`, `rejection_greedy_sample_kernel`,
       mRoPE, GDN update, and MoE kernels. The next MTP work item should
       measure warmed 35B-A3B AWQ/FP8 decode speed, draft acceptance, and
       long-output token quality with the same CUDA graph policy used by the
       target speed lane.

319. 2026-06-04 SM70 Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile/FULL-graph AWQ quality lane:
     safe default moved to in-memory AOT compile plus local-logits greedy top1.

     Scope:
     - Target acceleration point is the AWQ 90 -> 120 tok/s class compile /
       FULL CUDA graph lane, not the TurboQuant attention lane from items 314
       to 317.
     - Validation used latest vLLM, Qwen3-8B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 0/1,
       dtype half, AWQ, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, two concurrent prompts, greedy
       `max_tokens=1024`, `ignore_eos=true`, and comparison against the
       existing eager strict oracle.

     Root-cause split:
     - Fused SM70 LM-head top1 epilogue is unsafe under the compile/FULL graph
       lane. Both full-logits diagnostics and the local-logits top1 fallback
       passed the two-prompt 1024-token gate; the fused top1 epilogue
       reproduced the original request0 drift. Therefore the compile-graph
       lane defaults `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0` while keeping
       `VLLM_SM70_GREEDY_TOKEN_FASTPATH=1`; greedy decode still avoids the
       full sampler path by using normal local LM-head logits plus local max /
       pair all-gather.
     - AOT artifact reload is unsafe for this lane. The first cold AOT compile
       run that saved/used a freshly built graph passed, but repeating with the
       cached AOT artifact loaded reproduced request0 drift at output index
       `196` (`13` vs `476`). Plain non-AOT torch.compile also reproduced the
       same drift. In-memory AOT compile with compile cache disabled passed.
       Do not call this a final root-cause closure for artifact reload; keep
       artifact/regular-compile investigation active, but do not make those
       paths the default quality lane.

     Code policy now in the compile-graph lane:
     - `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1` still selects
       `CompilationMode.VLLM_COMPILE` with
       `CUDAGraphMode.FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`; this is not an eager fallback.
     - The lane auto-sets, unless explicitly overridden:
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0`,
       `VLLM_USE_AOT_COMPILE=1`, and
       `VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1`.
     - Explicit `VLLM_USE_AOT_COMPILE=0` or
       `VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=0` is retained as a diagnostic override and
       logs a warning because those settings reproduced token drift.
     - The lane still forces `combo_kernels=True` and
       `benchmark_combo_kernel=True`; the unbenchmarked combo-kernel choice
       reproduced token drift.
     - The failed `cudagraph_copy_inputs` shape-bucket experiment is not part
       of this policy and was removed.

     Final default validation after the policy change:
     - Candidate artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen3_8b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_default_final_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
       The command did not explicitly set `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1`,
       `VLLM_USE_AOT_COMPILE`, or `VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE`; logs show the
       lane auto-set them to `0`, `1`, and `1` respectively.
       It also logged `vLLM's torch.compile cache is disabled`, compiled in
       memory, captured FULL decode CUDA graphs, and hit Flash-V100 prefill /
       decode.
     - Decode output throughput: `258.82 tok/s` for 2048 total generated
       tokens. This matches the previous good compile/FULL graph speed class
       for this 8B TP2 diagnostic and does not sacrifice decode speed.
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen3_8b_awq_tp2_enforce_eager_vs_compile_graph_default_final_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
       Result: `equal=true`; both prompts have `left_len=1024`,
       `right_len=1024`, `prompt_equal=true`, and `first_mismatch=null`.
     - The JSON now records
       `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_in_memory_aot_compile_effective=true`,
       `disable_compile_cache_effective=true`,
       `use_aot_compile_effective=true`, and
       `compile_graph_local_logits_top1_policy=true`, so future artifacts can
       distinguish the safe default from unsafe cached-AOT or non-AOT runs.

     Current status:
     - For the Qwen3-8B-AWQ two-prompt 1024-token greedy gate, the compile /
       FULL graph lane is token-safe under the in-memory AOT + local-logits
       top1 policy.
     - This does not stop the AOT artifact reload direction. Cached artifact
       reload and non-AOT compile remain valuable root-cause tracks, but they
       are not accepted/default quality routes until they pass the same
       long-output token gate without sacrificing the compile/FULL graph speed
       path.
     - Next required production validation is to rerun the same safe policy on
       the larger target models, especially Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ and the FP8
       lane, using the same GPU/TP/output-length criteria as their accepted
       speed baselines.

320. 2026-06-04 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ compile/FULL graph quality remains open;
     AOT/cache policy is valuable but not sufficient for hybrid GDN/Mamba.

     Scope:
     - Validation used latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs
       0/1, dtype half, AWQ, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, two concurrent prompts,
       greedy `max_tokens=1024`, `ignore_eos=true`, and comparison against the
       clean current-tree strict-safe TP2 oracle.
     - The candidate used the current compile/FULL graph safe default from
       item 319: `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`, in-memory AOT,
       compile cache disabled, and local-logits greedy top1 rather than the
       fused SM70 LM-head top1 epilogue.

     Evidence:
     - Candidate artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_default_final_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
       Runtime logs still hit the speed path: SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched
       path, per-expert W13 dispatch selection, legacy single-token compact,
       Flash-V100 prefill/decode, in-memory AOT compile, and FULL decode graph
       capture.
     - Candidate decode throughput was `135.05 tok/s` for 2048 generated
       tokens, so this is the intended 90 -> 120+ tok/s class acceleration and
       not a slow fallback.
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_vs_compile_graph_default_final_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
       Result: `equal=false`; request 0 first mismatched at output index `176`
       (`513` vs `3428`) and request 1 first mismatched at output index `15`
       (`111853` vs `95839`).
     - Existing offline comparisons against earlier TP2 artifacts show that
       compile/FULL graph failures also occurred when
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=0` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`, so the remaining
       failure is not explained by the accepted AWQ MoE batched/legacy speed
       paths.

     Current root-cause split:
     - The item 319 policy is still useful: it made the non-GDN Qwen3-8B-AWQ
       two-prompt 1024-token compile/FULL graph gate token-identical without
       giving up FULL graph speed.
     - The 35B-A3B remaining failure is a separate hybrid path issue. Existing
       layer0 GDN decode dumps around steps 80-90 show zero diff for
       `input_qkv/input_a/input_b` and only fp16-scale `proj_out` drift
       (`0.0001220703125` max), while full-layer propagation/debug-buffer
       comparisons can reach multi-unit hidden-state differences after request
       rows diverge.
     - Do not mark the compile/FULL graph acceleration defaultable for 35B
       until the hybrid GDN/Mamba state/replay path passes the same long-output
       token gate. Also do not repair this by disabling FULL graph, disabling
       compile, disabling GDN packed recurrent decode, or dropping AWQ MoE
       speed paths; those are diagnostics only unless a later patch restores
       equivalent speed.

321. 2026-06-04 Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ compile/FULL graph also drifts; root cause is
     not isolated to 35B-A3B MoE.

     Scope:
     - Validation used latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs
       0/1, dtype half, AWQ, `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, two concurrent prompts,
       greedy `max_tokens=1024`, `ignore_eos=true`.
     - Runtime architecture resolved to `Qwen3_5ForConditionalGeneration`.
       AWQ warmup reported `4 dense layer shapes, 0 MoE layer shapes`, so this
       is a non-MoE Qwen3.5/GDN model-path check.
     - The strict oracle used `--enforce-eager` and `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0`
       to avoid sampler fused-top1 drift. The candidate used the item 319
       compile/FULL graph safe policy: in-memory AOT compile, compile cache
       disabled, local-logits greedy top1, Flash-V100 prefill/decode, and FULL
       decode CUDA graph capture.

     Evidence:
     - Strict oracle artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_enforce_eager_lmheadtop1off_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
     - Compile/FULL graph artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_safe_default_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
       Candidate output throughput was `79.52 tok/s` for 2048 generated
       tokens; logs show `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_compile_graph_effective=true`,
       `sm70_flash_v100_0dot3_in_memory_aot_compile_effective=true`,
       `disable_compile_cache_effective=true`, `use_aot_compile_effective=true`,
       and `compile_graph_local_logits_top1_policy=true`.
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_enforce_eager_lmheadtop1off_vs_compile_graph_safe_default_greedy1024_2prompts_20260604.json`.
       Result: `equal=false`. Request 0 passed all 1024 output tokens.
       Request 1 first mismatched at output index `894` (`11910` vs `12193`).

     Current root-cause split:
     - This result rules out a 35B-A3B-MoE-only explanation. The 35B failure
       can still have MoE-specific amplification, but the underlying
       compile/FULL graph drift also exists in the non-MoE Qwen3.5/GDN 27B
       model.
     - The next root-cause pass should prioritize the shared
     Qwen3.5/GDN/Mamba + compile/FULL graph path and compare the 27B late
     request-1 divergence against the 35B early divergence. Do not spend the
     next pass only inside 35B MoE internals unless the shared 27B path has
     been ruled out.

322. 2026-06-05 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ compile/FULL graph drift is already
     reproduced; next work must use existing artifacts and patch only concrete
     replay/metadata defects.

     Scope:
     - Latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 0/1, dtype half,
       AWQ, two concurrent prompts, greedy `max_tokens=1024`, `ignore_eos=true`.
       Both candidate artifacts stayed in the 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph speed
       class, so the difference below is not a slow-path/fast-path mismatch.
     - The strict oracle remains
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.

     Evidence:
     - Best compile/FULL graph artifact so far:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdn_live_tokens_fix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Throughput was `139.52 tok/s`. Against the strict oracle, request 0
       first mismatched at output index `179` (`11` vs `321`) and request 1
       first mismatched at output index `158` (`96123` vs `96539`).
     - Current `for_cudagraph_capture=True` / capture-state padding validation:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdncapture_statepad_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Throughput was `140.08 tok/s`. Against the same strict oracle, request 0
       first mismatched at output index `176` (`513` vs `3428`) and request 1
       regressed to output index `15` (`111853` vs `95839`).
     - Offline dump comparison, without another model run, showed the
       `gdn_live_tokens_fix` candidate had exact layer0 GDN live tensors for the
       shape-2 two-request decode at steps 15-18: `state_indices_shape2`,
       `input_qkv/input_a/input_b`, conv/recurrent/proj outputs, and state
       slices all had zero diff against the strict GDN reference. Later huge
       differences at steps 156+ are downstream of already-diverged tokens and
       are not root-cause evidence by themselves.
     - A follow-up compile/FULL run tried making non-spec GDN align metadata use
       the runner-maintained current state block ids instead of re-deriving the
       current block from `seq_lens/block_table`. Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdn_current_state_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       It stayed in the compile/FULL lane (`119.34 tok/s` with first-inference
       JIT warnings, `old_0dot3_compile_graph_policy=true`,
       `awq_moe_batched_gemm_effective=true`, compact MoE active), but token
       compare was unchanged from the current failure: request 0 first
       mismatched at output index `176` (`513` vs `3428`) and request 1 at
       output index `15` (`111853` vs `95839`). The temporary current-state
       source change was reverted; this rules out the non-spec align state
       block derivation as the req1 index-15 root cause.
     - A code attempt removed the `for_cudagraph_capture=True` capture-state
       padding branch from `GDNAttentionMetadataBuilder.build()` and stopped
       passing that flag from `build_for_cudagraph_capture()`. Validation
       artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_restore_livefix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`;
       compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_vs_compile_graph_restore_livefix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       It stayed in the speed lane (`2048` generated tokens in `14.50 s`,
       old 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph effective, AWQ MoE batched and legacy
       compact effective), but token compare was unchanged from the current
       failure: request 0 first mismatched at output index `176` (`513` vs
       `3428`) and request 1 at output index `15` (`111853` vs `95839`). The
       temporary source change was reverted; this rules out that capture-state
       padding branch as the current req1 index-15 root cause.

     Current root-cause split:
     - The early request-1 index-15 failure is not inevitable fp16/Mamba
       accumulation; at least one compile/FULL graph state proved early layer0
       GDN exactness.
     - The actual default path is the old
       `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`, not the v2 runner. Runtime still
       builds fresh `attn_metadata` and passes it through `set_forward_context`,
       but `CUDAGraphWrapper` replay does not re-execute Python/model code.
       Kernels therefore use the tensor addresses captured on the first FULL
       graph call. Correctness still depends on capture-time GDN metadata
       tensors pointing at builder persistent buffers and runtime metadata build
       updating those same buffers before replay. Root-cause work should inspect
       and patch that captured metadata / persistent buffer contract,
       especially fixed Python fields such as `num_decodes` combined with live
       tensors such as `non_spec_query_start_loc` and
       `non_spec_state_indices_tensor`.
     - Do not spend more time re-running the same 1024-token repro until a
     concrete code patch changes this contract. Also do not repair the gate by
     disabling compile, FULL graph, packed GDN recurrent decode, Flash-V100,
     or accepted AWQ MoE speed paths.

323. 2026-06-05 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ compile/FULL graph: GDN native norm is the
     current best state; output-projection custom-op and GemmaRMSNorm changes
     are rejected.

     Scope:
     - Latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 0/1, dtype half,
       AWQ, Flash-V100, AWQ MoE batched GEMM, legacy compact MoE, packed GDN
       recurrent decode, and old 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy all active.
       The strict oracle is still
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.

     Accepted local source state:
     - The GDN output projection now calls `self.norm(core_attn_out, z)`
       directly. The earlier opaque `sm70_qwen_gdn_rmsnorm_gated` wrapper is
       kept registered for diagnostics/compatibility but is not used on this
       route.
     - Validation artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdn_norm_native_live_decode_fix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Throughput was `141.216 tok/s` (`2048` output tokens in `14.5026 s`).
       Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_vs_compile_graph_gdn_norm_native_live_decode_fix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Result is still `equal=false`: request 0 first mismatches at output
       index `179` (`11` vs `321`), request 1 at output index `158`
       (`96123` vs `96539`). This is the current best token-quality state and
       matches the earlier `gdn_live_tokens_fix` behavior while preserving the
       speed lane.

     Rejected experiments:
     - Removing the compile/FULL-graph GemmaRMSNorm special branch regressed
       back to the bad default pattern. Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gemma_norm_native_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Throughput was `148.544 tok/s`, but token compare failed at request 0
       index `176` (`513` vs `3428`) and request 1 index `15`
       (`111853` vs `95839`). The patch was reverted; do not retry this as the
       next root-cause direction.
     - Wrapping only `RMSNormGated + out_proj` in a `qwen_gdn_output_projection`
       custom op did not recover quality and also cost speed. The first attempt
       failed Dynamo compile because a platform capability query was inside the
       compiled region; after removing that query and registering the op as a
       split op, the full validation artifact was:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdn_outputproj_custom2_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_vs_compile_graph_gdn_outputproj_custom2_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Throughput was `113.441 tok/s`, request 0 regressed to first mismatch
       index `163` (`321` vs `13`), and request 1 regressed to index `15`
       (`111853` vs `95839`). The `qwen_gdn_output_projection` source patch was
       reverted and `py_compile` passed afterward.

     Additional fixed facts:
     - Existing primitive CUDA graph fixtures show the GDN conv-update and
       packed-recurrent graph replays are exact (`max_diff=0`) in isolation:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/gdn_conv_update_cudagraph_slot0fix_b2_s1024_20260605.json`
       and
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/gdn_packed_recurrent_cudagraph_slot0fix_b2_s1024_20260605.json`.
     - A 200-token diagnostic dump of the current best route matched the
       1024-token best artifact for the first 200 tokens exactly, so the short
       diagnostic did not introduce a new path. It confirmed the same strict
       mismatches: request 0 at index `179`, request 1 at index `158`.

     Next root-cause rule:
     - Do not run another long drift reproduction unless a concrete patch
       changes the execution path. Use existing strict/candidate dumps to
       isolate the first residual layer-level diff before changing code again.

324. 2026-06-05 Rejected the whole-GDN custom-op boundary as a 35B-AWQ
     compile/FULL graph quality repair.

     Scope:
     - Latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 0/1, dtype half,
       Flash-V100, AWQ MoE batched GEMM, legacy compact MoE, packed GDN
       recurrent decode, local-logits greedy top1, in-memory AOT compile,
       compile cache disabled, and old 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy active.
     - This run set `VLLM_SM70_QWEN_GDN_FULL_FORWARD=1`, which routes each GDN
       layer through the existing `qwen_gdn_full_forward` custom op. This does
       not disable compile or FULL CUDA graph; it changes the compile boundary
       so GDN projection/core/output-projection kernels are captured as an
       eager-order custom-op body.
     - Strict oracle:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.

     Evidence:
     - Candidate artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdn_fullforward_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Logs still showed `mode=VLLM_COMPILE`,
       `cudagraph_mode=FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`, FULL decode graph capture sizes
       `1,2`, Flash-V100 prefill/decode route hits, CUSTOM TP all-reduce,
       SM70 AWQ MoE batched route, per-expert W13/W2 dispatch, and legacy
       single-token compact.
     - Candidate generated `2048` output tokens in `18.699174086999847 s`
       (`~109.53 tok/s`). This is slower than the current best
       `gdn_norm_native_live_decode_fix` state (`~141.22 tok/s`), while still
       remaining a graph route rather than the slow strict-safe oracle.
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_vs_compile_graph_gdn_fullforward_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Result: `equal=false`, `model_quality_gate.label=model-fail`. Request 0
       first mismatched at output index `163` (`321` vs `13`), and request 1
       first mismatched at output index `15` (`111853` vs `95839`).

     Interpretation:
     - Whole-GDN custom-op wrapping is not a repair. It regresses to the same
       early request-1 failure pattern as the rejected output-projection
       custom-op experiment and costs decode throughput.
     - Do not retry `VLLM_SM70_QWEN_GDN_FULL_FORWARD=1` as the next 35B-AWQ
     quality fix. The remaining useful direction is narrower: use existing
     projection/core/layer dumps to isolate the first fp16-level residual
     inside Qwen3.5/GDN projection or AWQ dense projection arithmetic, then
     patch that operation directly without disabling compile, FULL graph,
     packed GDN decode, Flash-V100, or accepted AWQ MoE speed paths.

325. 2026-06-05 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ compile/FULL graph follow-up:
     current worktree RMSNorm and GDN core-boundary probes are not accepted
     repairs.

     Scope:
     - Latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 0/1, dtype half,
       Flash-V100, AWQ MoE batched GEMM, legacy compact MoE, packed GDN
       recurrent decode, local-logits greedy top1, in-memory AOT compile,
       compile cache disabled, and old 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy active.
     - No old-tree quality oracle was used. Comparisons below are against the
       current strict-safe oracle:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.

     Current-code RMSNorm finding:
     - The current worktree contains the `sm70_gemma_rms_norm_eager` branch in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/layernorm.py`.
     - A one-token layer0/GDN dump showed this branch clears the first valid
       prefill seed for prompt0/shape17: `layer_input_hidden`,
       `input_norm_out`, `gdn_hidden_states`, `in_proj_qkvz`, `input_qkv`,
       `in_proj_ba`, `input_a`, and `input_b` were all `max_diff=0` against
       the strict dumps for the valid shape17 records.
     - Full 1024-token validation still failed:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_current_code_rmsfix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       It generated `2048` output tokens in `14.8508s` (`~137.9 tok/s`) with
       the speed paths active, but compared against strict-safe it first
       mismatched at request 0 index `176` (`513` vs `3428`) and request 1
       index `103` (`97150` vs `97322`).
     - Decision: the current RMSNorm branch is not sufficient for long-output
       acceptance. Do not rerun it as proof of failure; use it only as the
       current source baseline unless a later patch changes the path.

     Rejected GDN core-boundary probe:
     - A diagnostic patch changed the default Qwen3.5/GDN call sites from
       `qwen_gdn_attention_core_standard(...)` back to
       `qwen_gdn_attention_core(..., fast_kernel=False)` and narrowed the
       mutation contract for the read-only qkv input.
     - Validation artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_gdn_core_boundary_fix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       The route still hit compile/FULL graph, Flash-V100, AWQ batched W13/W2,
       and legacy compact; it generated `2048` output tokens in `17.1606s`.
     - Token result: not accepted. Request 0 improved back to the current-best
       first mismatch at index `179` (`11` vs `321`), but request 1 regressed
       to index `90` (`99945` vs `1710`) instead of the current-best index
       `158`. The diagnostic patch was reverted.
     - Decision: do not pursue a wholesale default switch from
       `qwen_gdn_attention_core_standard` to `qwen_gdn_attention_core`; it
       trades one request's drift for another and costs throughput.

     Compile split boundary check:
     - Removing `vllm::qwen_gdn_attention_core_standard` and
       `vllm::qwen_gdn_full_forward` from `CompilationConfig._attention_ops`
       was tested as a possible way to restore the earlier graph partition.
     - Result: rejected before generation. AOT compilation failed during mixed
       PIECEWISE graph capture with
       `aot_autograd() does not yet handle input mutations on views with
       different dtypes`.
     - The split list was restored. `qwen_gdn_attention_core_standard` is a
       required compile boundary for the current default GDN core path, not a
       quality repair knob.

     Next rule:
     - The remaining useful work is not another long token repro. Use existing
       artifacts plus targeted layer/core/logit dumps to find the first
       residual diff after the valid layer0 prefill tensors are exact. Do not
       disable compile, FULL graph, Flash-V100, packed GDN decode, AWQ batched
       W13/W2, or legacy compact as a "fix"; only change a path when it
       directly addresses a localized arithmetic/replay defect.

326. 2026-06-05 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ compile/FULL graph: shared-expert SiLU is
     one real fixed root, but the long-output quality gate remains open.

     Scope:
     - Latest vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 0/1, dtype half,
       Flash-V100, AWQ MoE batched W13/W2, legacy compact, local-logits greedy
       top1, in-memory AOT compile, compile cache disabled, and old 0.0.3
       compile/FULL graph policy active.
     - Strict oracle remains the current-tree strict-safe GDN reference:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.

     Fixed local source state:
     - `SiluAndMul` now accepts `enforce_enable`; this lets a caller force the
       existing `_C.silu_and_mul` custom op instead of allowing torch.compile
       to lower native `F.silu(x) * y`.
     - `Qwen2MoeMLP` applies that force only for `.shared_expert` on SM70.
       This is deliberately narrow. A previous global force of all
       `SiluAndMul` uses caused large layer0 drift and is not accepted.
     - The fix keeps the compile/FULL graph speed path; it does not disable
       compile, FULL graph, Flash-V100, AWQ batched MoE, or legacy compact.

     Evidence:
     - Existing MoE/internal dumps localized the first old nonzero at
       layer0 shared expert output: router logits, top-k, and routed AWQ MoE
       W13/W2 were exact for the shape-2 decode record, while
       `moe_shared_raw` first differed by `7.62939453125e-06`.
     - Manual eager reconstruction with real weights matched strict output,
       while compiling the same shared-expert MLP introduced SiLU-gate drift.
       Splitting subops showed the first nonzero was
       `F.silu(gate) * up`; the existing C++ `_C.silu_and_mul` path was exact
       in eager and under compile for the tested tensors.
     - Long-output candidate after the targeted shared-expert SiLU custom-op:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_compile_graph_shared_silu_custom_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       It generated `2048` output tokens in `14.4249s` and stayed in the
       compile/FULL graph lane (`mode=VLLM_COMPILE`, `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`,
       Flash-V100, AWQ MoE batched, legacy compact).
     - Compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_strict_safe_gdnref_vs_compile_graph_shared_silu_custom_greedy1024_2prompts_20260605.json`.
       Result: still `equal=false`, but improved from the old
       request0/request1 first mismatches `176/15` to `676/139`.
     - Old-compile vs new-compile compare showed the new run matches strict at
       the old first-mismatch sites: request0 index `176` changed from `3428`
       back to strict token `513`, and request1 index `15` changed from
       `95839` back to strict token `111853`.

     Rejected follow-up:
     - A Python custom op for `attn_output * sigmoid(gate)` was exact as a
       single operation but regressed the full model to request0/request1 first
       mismatches `180/15` and reduced throughput. That patch was reverted.
       Do not retry that broad attention-gate boundary without a narrower
       graph-partition reason.

     Additional fixed facts:
     - Non-compile AWQ batched+legacy compact already has a 1024-token
       strict-safe pass:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260603/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35a3b_awq_strict_safe_vs_batched_legacy_compact_perdispatch_roundingfix_greedy1024_2prompts_20260603.json`
       reports `equal=true` with both prompts token-identical. Therefore the
       remaining compile/FULL graph failure is not explained by AWQ
       batched/legacy compact by itself.
     - Qwen3-8B-AWQ item 319 already passed the compile/FULL graph 1024-token
       gate under the in-memory AOT + local-logits policy. Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ
       item 321 still drifted without MoE. The shared unresolved direction is
       the Qwen3.5/GDN/Mamba compile/FULL graph path, with 35B MoE adding the
       now-fixed shared-expert SiLU amplification.

     Current rule:
     - Do not rerun the same 1024-token repro without a concrete source change.
       Next work must use existing dumps or a new targeted dump to isolate the
       first residual diff after the shared-expert SiLU fix, then patch that
       exact arithmetic/replay defect while preserving the speed lane.

327. 2026-06-08 FlashQLA SM70 down-port: official-repo public forward path is
     kernel-level runnable, with exact small-shape evidence and a Qwen-like
     kernel speed datapoint.

     Scope:
     - External official checkout:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA`.
     - Source checkout of TileLang `v0.1.8`:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/tilelang`.
     - Runtime environment for validation:
       `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210/bin/python`, CUDA device
       exposed as `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2` only.

     Implemented:
     - Added an experimental official-tree SM70/SM75 GDN forward backend under
       `flash_qla/ops/gated_delta_rule/chunk/sm70/`.
     - The public `chunk_gated_delta_rule()` API dispatches to this backend on
       SM70/SM75 for contiguous, non-varlen forward inference tensors.
     - The low-level `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd()` API deliberately raises on
       SM70/SM75 for now because official low-level semantics include
       `g=cumsum(g)`, KKT `A`, and optional `h`; returning placeholder `A/h`
       would be a false compatibility claim.
     - The SM70 CUDA extension supports `q/k/v` in fp16, bf16, or fp32 and
       `g/beta` independently in fp16, bf16, or fp32, with fp32 accumulation.
       Current supported FlashQLA/Qwen target shape is `K=V=128`, matching the
       official Hopper forward assertion.
     - Added `benchmark/bench_sm70_gdn_forward.py` for repeatable SM70 kernel
       speed and max-diff checks.

     Evidence:
     - Small correctness smoke, `T=5,Hq=2,Hv=4,K=V=128`:
       - fp16 qkv + fp32 g/beta: `out_maxdiff=0.0`,
         `state_maxdiff=1.1175870895385742e-08`.
       - bf16 qkv + fp32 g/beta: `out_maxdiff=0.0`,
         `state_maxdiff=1.30385160446167e-08`.
       - fp32 qkv + fp32 g/beta: `out_maxdiff=3.4924596548080444e-10`,
         `state_maxdiff=9.313225746154785e-09`.
     - Scripted correctness smoke,
       `benchmark/bench_sm70_gdn_forward.py --tokens 16 --q-heads 4
       --v-heads 8 --dtype fp16 --warmup 3 --repeats 10`:
       `sm70_ms=0.06862160007585771`, `out_maxdiff=4.76837158203125e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=1.862645149230957e-08`.
     - Qwen-like kernel speed,
       `benchmark/bench_sm70_gdn_forward.py --tokens 512 --q-heads 16
       --v-heads 32 --dtype fp16 --warmup 10 --repeats 50 --skip-reference`:
       `sm70_ms=0.8825584800797515` for shape
       `(B=1,T=512,Hq=16,Hv=32,K=V=128)`.

     Not accepted yet:
     - This is not yet wired into latest vLLM's `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`.
     - Varlen `cu_seqlens` and low-level `A/h` outputs are not implemented.
     - Model-level token quality and full 16k prefill throughput are not yet
       claimed.
     - Do not compare this directly to the previous fork's float32-only
       legacy backend; that fork had state/output drift and a mismatched state
       layout. The current line is the official FlashQLA API with an explicit
       SM70 public-forward backend.

328. 2026-06-08 FlashQLA SM70 speed recovery after sidecar review: official
     `[K,V]` semantics now reaches fork-class kernel speed without inheriting
     the fork state-layout bug.

     Scope:
     - Official FlashQLA checkout: `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA`.
     - Runtime: `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210/bin/python`.
     - GPUs used for this item: physical GPUs 2/3 only. GPUs 0/1 were not
       killed or used for these kernel tests.
     - Qwen-like kernel shape unless otherwise stated:
       `B=1,T=512,Hq=16,Hv=32,K=V=128`.

     Fixed after review:
     - Added C++ `OptionalCUDAGuard` around the SM70 extension launch so a
       tensor on a non-current CUDA device still launches on `q.device`.
     - SM70 public forward now selects backend by `q.device` instead of an
       import-time/default-device compute capability.
     - Added same-device checks in Python and C++.
     - `output_final_state=False` now skips final-state allocation and kernel
       writeback instead of allocating/writing then dropping the tensor.
     - Added `FLASH_QLA_SM70_COLUMN_GROUPS_PER_BLOCK` for CTA-count tuning;
       default is now `2`. This keeps official `[B,Hv,K,V]` state semantics and
       only changes how many column groups a CTA owns.

     Correctness evidence against the official `tests/ref_gdr.py` reference:
     - `T=16,Hq=4,Hv=8,fp16`: `out_maxdiff=8.868519216775894e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=2.2351741790771484e-08`.
     - `T=65,Hq=4,Hv=8,fp16`: `out_maxdiff=9.48319211602211e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=3.166496753692627e-08`.
     - `T=129,Hq=4,Hv=8,fp16`: `out_maxdiff=9.48319211602211e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=3.3527612686157227e-08`.
     - `T=65,Hq=4,Hv=8,fp32`: `out_maxdiff=1.7462298274040222e-09`,
       `state_maxdiff=2.9802322387695312e-08`.

     Speed evidence, Qwen-like shape:
     - Complete vLLM FLA default baseline, no SM70 schedule envs:
       `avg_s=0.0042991418013116345` (`4.299 ms`), from
       `/tmp/sm70_fla_bench/fla_t512_default.pt`.
     - Complete vLLM FLA with SM70 schedule envs
       (`VLLM_SM70_GDN_KKT_SCHEDULE=1`, `VLLM_SM70_GDN_KKT_BK=64`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_KKT_WARPS=4`, `VLLM_SM70_GDN_KKT_STAGES=2`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DELTA_H_SCHEDULE=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_CHUNK_O_SCHEDULE=1`):
       `avg_s=0.0013931947003584355` (`1.393 ms`), from
       `/tmp/sm70_fla_bench/fla_t512_sm70sched.pt`.
     - Official FlashQLA SM70 fp16 public-forward kernel, `output_final_state=True`,
       default `FLASH_QLA_SM70_COLUMN_GROUPS_PER_BLOCK=2`:
       hot repeat best `0.7239321899414063 ms`; later cross-GPU repeats ranged
       about `0.7609088134765625-0.7966464233398437 ms` depending on clock/cold
       state. Use the hot repeat value for kernel-to-kernel throughput after
       warmup and the range for conservative reporting.
     - Official FlashQLA SM70 fp16 with `output_final_state=False`:
       `0.7949107360839843 ms` in one cross-GPU repeat. This optimization is
       real but small at `T=512`; the main win is the CTA-count change.
     - Official FlashQLA SM70 fp32 public-forward kernel:
       hot repeat best `0.6789734649658203 ms`; later cross-GPU repeat was
       `0.7497830200195312 ms`. This is the fair dtype comparison point for
       the previous fork, because the fork legacy backend is float32-only.

     Comparison summary:
     - Versus complete vLLM FLA default: official SM70 fp16 hot kernel
       `0.724 ms` is about `5.94x` faster; conservative `0.761 ms` is about
       `5.65x` faster.
     - Versus complete vLLM FLA with SM70 schedule envs: official SM70 fp16 hot
       kernel `0.724 ms` is about `1.92x` faster; conservative `0.761 ms` is
       about `1.83x` faster.
     - Versus the previous fork's historical V100 float32-only legacy speed
       (`~0.700 ms`): official SM70 fp32 hot kernel `0.679 ms` is slightly
       faster while keeping the official state contract. Current-environment
       attempts to re-run the fork legacy API in `vllm-0.0.5-t210` hung in the
       first extension call even after clearing the torch extension cache, so
       the fork number remains historical/non-accepted evidence, not a fresh
       same-env pass.

     Fork classification:
     - The previous fork is not a quality route. Its legacy kernel uses a
       transposed state layout equivalent to `[B,Hv,V,K]` for coalesced state
       access, while official FlashQLA/ref uses `[B,Hv,K,V]`. The fork's own
       test reference multiplies with `state.transpose(0, 1)`, which explains
       why it can pass its local recurrence smoke while drifting against the
       official API contract.
     - Do not directly port the fork kernel. Reuse only the idea of increasing
       CTA count / improving state-memory access under the official `[K,V]`
       semantics.

     Not accepted yet:
     - This is still kernel-level proof. It is not wired into latest vLLM's
       `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`, and no model-level long-output quality or
       16k prefill throughput acceptance is claimed by this item.
     - Varlen `cu_seqlens`, low-level `A/h` outputs, and backward remain out of
       scope for the SM70 public-forward prototype.

329. 2026-06-08 FlashQLA SM70 real Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 shape coverage and default
     CGB auto-tuning.

     Scope:
     - Official FlashQLA checkout:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA`.
     - Runtime: `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210/bin/python`.
     - GPU used: physical GPU 2 only. GPUs 0/1 were not used or killed.
     - This item is still kernel/prefill-facing QLA evidence, not model-level
       end-to-end acceptance.

     Real GDN architecture matrix from local model configs:
     - `Qwen3.5-9B-AWQ`: global `Hq/Hv=16/32`, `K=V=128`, state dtype
       `float32`; TP2 local `8/16`, TP4 local `4/8`.
     - `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ` and `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`: global
       `Hq/Hv=16/32`, `K=V=128`, state dtype `float32`; TP2 local `8/16`,
       TP4 local `4/8`.
     - `Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ`, `Qwen3.6-27B-FP8`, and `Qwen3.5-27B-FP8`: global
       `Hq/Hv=16/48`, `K=V=128`, state dtype `float32`; TP2 local `8/24`,
       TP4 local `4/12`.
     - `Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-AWQ`: global `Hq/Hv=16/64`, `K=V=128`; TP2 local
       `8/32`, TP4 local `4/16`.
     - The earlier single fixture `Hq/Hv=16/32` does not cover the main 27B
       and 35B TP2/TP4 local shapes.

     Implemented:
     - Replaced the fixed default
       `FLASH_QLA_SM70_COLUMN_GROUPS_PER_BLOCK=2` behavior with an auto
       heuristic in `flash_qla/ops/gated_delta_rule/chunk/sm70/csrc/gdn_forward.cu`.
       The env still fully overrides the heuristic for benchmark sweeps.
     - The auto heuristic is based on V100 fp16 production data. V100/SM70 has
       no native bf16, so bf16 is kept as an API/correctness diagnostic only
       and must not be used as production speed evidence.
     - Follow-up guard: latest-vLLM `flashqla_sm70` model integration now only
       activates for `torch.float16` model activations on SM70/SM75, and the
       runtime wrapper falls back to Triton/FLA if actual q/k/v tensors are not
       fp16. The standalone FlashQLA benchmark still permits `--dtype bf16`
       for API diagnostics, but prints `native_bf16_supported False` and a
       diagnostic-only note on V100-class devices.
     - The heuristic never auto-selects `CGB=8` because it was slower across
       the real Qwen shape sweep.
     - Updated `benchmark/bench_sm70_gdn_forward.py` to print
       `column_groups_per_block auto` when no env override is set, plus the
       resolved CGB value for auditability.
     - Added a separate SM70-only vLLM-layout varlen entry point,
       `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_sm70_vlk_varlen`, which accepts flattened
       prefill tensors `[1,T,Hq,K]` / `[1,T,Hv,V]`, `cu_seqlens`, and state
       `[N,Hv,V,K]`. This does not change the public FlashQLA API contract,
       which remains `[B,Hv,K,V]`; it avoids a per-layer transpose when wiring
       into vLLM's GDN cache layout.
     - Added explicit opt-in vLLM backend plumbing:
       `--gdn-prefill-backend flashqla_sm70` / additional config
       `gdn_prefill_backend=flashqla_sm70`. It is not selected by `auto`;
       it only activates on SM70/SM75, `torch.float16` model dtype,
       `linear_key_head_dim=128`, and an importable external FlashQLA
       package/path.
     - Added `cu_seqlens` safety checks for the vLLM-layout entry point:
       start must be `0`, end must equal flattened token count, and entries
       must be non-decreasing. The C++ extension also checks start/end for
       direct extension calls.

     V100 fp16 CGB sweep, `output_final_state=True`:
     - `T=512`: `8/16` best `CGB=2` (`0.714 ms`), `4/8` best `CGB=1`
       (`0.474 ms`), `8/24` best `CGB=2` (`0.692 ms`), `4/12` best `CGB=1`
       (`0.481 ms`), `8/32` best `CGB=2` (`0.744 ms`), `4/16` best `CGB=2`
       (`0.732 ms`).
     - `T=2048`: `8/16` best `CGB=2` (`2.777 ms`), `4/8` had a small
       `CGB=1/2/4` spread and best `CGB=4` (`2.735 ms`), `8/24` best `CGB=2`
       (`2.732 ms`), `4/12` best `CGB=2` (`2.678 ms`), `8/32` best `CGB=1`
       (`2.948 ms`), `4/16` best `CGB=1` (`2.930 ms`).
     - `T=16324`: `8/16` best `CGB=2` (`22.177 ms`), `4/8` best `CGB=4`
       (`21.951 ms`), `8/24` best `CGB=2` (`21.681 ms`), `4/12` best
       `CGB=2` (`21.127 ms`), `8/32` best `CGB=1` (`23.335 ms`), `4/16` best
       `CGB=1` (`23.253 ms`).

     Auto heuristic after the sweep:
     - `Hv=24/48`: `CGB=2`.
     - `Hv=32`: `16/32` short prefill uses `CGB=4` and long prefill uses
       `CGB=2`; `8/32` short prefill uses `CGB=2` and long prefill uses
       `CGB=1`.
     - `Hv=16`: `8/16` uses `CGB=2`; `4/16` uses `CGB=2` for short prefill
       and `CGB=1` for long prefill.
     - `Hv=12`: short prefill uses `CGB=1`, long prefill uses `CGB=2`.
     - `Hv=8`: short prefill uses `CGB=1`, long prefill uses `CGB=4`.
     - `Hv>=64`: `CGB=1`.

     Correctness / non-regression:
     - Official ref gate after the auto change, `T=65,Hq/Hv=4/8,fp16`:
       `out_maxdiff=9.48319211602211e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=3.166496753692627e-08`.
     - Official ref gate after the auto change, `T=65,Hq/Hv=4/12,fp16`:
       `out_maxdiff=9.508803486824036e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=3.3527612686157227e-08`.
     - Explicit `CGB=1/2/4` on `T=65,Hq/Hv=4/8,fp16` produced identical
       official-ref maxdiffs (`out=9.48319211602211e-07`,
       `state=3.166496753692627e-08`), confirming CGB only changes CTA
       ownership and does not change recurrence math for a fixed output
       element.
     - Public API gate after adding the vLLM-layout binding,
       `T=16,Hq/Hv=4/8,fp16`: `out_maxdiff=8.868519216775894e-07`,
       `state_maxdiff=2.2351741790771484e-08`, with
       `resolved_column_groups_per_block=1`.
     - vLLM-layout varlen gate, two flattened sequences with lengths `[5,9]`,
       `Hq/Hv=4/8,fp16`: compared `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_sm70_vlk_varlen`
       state `[N,Hv,V,K]` against split public `[B,Hv,K,V]` calls and got
       exact parity, `out_maxdiff=0.0`, `state_maxdiff=0.0`.
     - vLLM-layout varlen gate, three flattened sequences with lengths
       `[7,3,11]`, `Hq/Hv=8/16,fp16`: exact parity against split public calls,
       `out_maxdiff=0.0`, `state_maxdiff=0.0`.
     - vLLM wrapper gate with `PYTHONPATH` including both latest vLLM and the
       external FlashQLA checkout: `flashqla_sm70_chunk_gated_delta_rule`
       matched the direct FlashQLA vLLM-layout call exactly on two flattened
       sequences (`out_maxdiff=0.0`, `state_maxdiff=0.0`).
     - Backend resolver smoke:
       without FlashQLA import path, `flashqla_sm70` falls back to `triton`;
       with FlashQLA import path, explicit `flashqla_sm70` activates on SM70
       and SM75, falls back on SM80, and `auto` remains `triton`.
     - Bad `cu_seqlens` checks were exercised: `[1,5,12]` rejects start,
       `[0,7,11]` rejects end, and `[0,8,7,12]` rejects non-monotonic offsets.

     Auto default speed sanity:
     - `T=512,fp16`: auto matched the explicit expected CGB within measurement
       noise on `35B_TP2 8/16`, `35B_TP4 4/8`, `27B_TP2 8/24`,
       `27B_TP4 4/12`, `122B_TP2 8/32`, and `122B_TP4 4/16`.
     - `T=16324,fp16`: auto matched explicit expected CGB within measurement
       noise on the same TP2/TP4 shape set. An initial cold-order `4/8`
       reading was slower, but an interleaved repeat gave `explicit4=21.890 ms`,
       `auto=21.812/21.801 ms`, `explicit2=21.941 ms`,
       `explicit1=21.887 ms`.
     - vLLM-layout varlen speed sanity, single flattened sequence
       `T=512,Hq/Hv=8/16,fp16` (35B TP2 local shape): `0.7352149327596028 ms`,
       same order as the public-layout `8/16` kernel sweep (`~0.714 ms`).

     Still open:
     - vLLM has an explicit opt-in backend wired to the new layout entry point,
       but it still needs a real model route-hit and model-level performance
       run with `PYTHONPATH=/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA` or an installed
       FlashQLA package. It is intentionally not an `auto` default yet.
     - Real model validation still needs 16k prefill throughput and long-output
       token quality after the kernel is wired into `qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`.
     - Further speedups remain possible: state-memory coalescing under the
       official state contract, shared q/k/g/beta loads, and dtype-specialized
       fp16 paths. Do not port the fork's transposed-state kernel as-is.

330. 2026-06-09 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ FlashQLA-SM70 model-level full-accel
     speed run.

     Scope:
     - Only one model was tested per the active request:
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`.
     - GPUs: `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`; GPUs 0/1 were not used.
     - Runtime: latest vLLM tree with
       `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210`.
     - Shape: `input_len=16324`, `output_len=256`, `warmup=1`,
       `repeat=2`. The warmup request is not counted.
     - Engine route: TP2, `dtype=half`, `quantization=awq`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `gdn_prefill_backend=flashqla_sm70`,
       `max_model_len=20000`, `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`,
       `enforce_eager=false`, `disable_custom_all_reduce=false`.
     - Env speed gates:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `PYTHONPATH=/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm:/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA`.

     Startup blocker fixed before the accepted run:
     - The first same-command graph run failed during `profile_run` with
       `vllm::qwen_gdn_attention_core_standard() is missing value for argument
       'non_spec_query_start_loc'`.
     - Root cause: the custom-op schema had been extended with
       `non_spec_query_start_loc` and `non_spec_state_indices_tensor`, while
       call sites still passed `layer_name` as a keyword argument. For this
       direct-registered Torch op, the safe call form is positional.
     - Fixed both call sites in `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_5.py` and
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py` to pass
       `layer_name` positionally. `py_compile` passed.

     Route-hit evidence from the successful run:
     - `Using SM70 Flash-V100 no-compile decode CUDA graph policy:
       mode=NONE, cudagraph_mode=FULL_DECODE_ONLY, capture_size=1`.
     - `Using FlashQLA-SM70 GDN prefill kernel
       (requested=flashqla_sm70, head_k_dim=128,
       model_dtype=torch.float16)`.
     - `Using AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN_V100 backend`.
     - `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled (256 experts)`.
     - `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active (scalar paged KV kernel,
       CUDA-graph safe, classified Type-B for long q=1 decode)`.
     - `SM70 fused LM head top1 path enabled`.
     - `Using ['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL'] all-reduce backends` for TP.
     - CUDA graph capture completed: `Graph capturing finished in 1 secs`.

     Result artifact:
     - `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_gdn_flashqla_i16324_o256_tp2_fullaccel_graph_w1_r2_20260609_fix1.json`.

     Speed result:
     - `prefill_time_mean`: `2.796139721500367 s`.
     - `first_token_latency_mean`: `2.8010361194610596 s`.
     - `decode_time_mean`: `3.485936782501085 s`.
     - `steady_decode_tps_mean`: `73.15106860268904 tok/s`.
     - `output_tps_mean`: `40.718230602573485 tok/s`.

     Interpretation:
     - Compared with the historical same-shape full-accel FLA/Flash-V100 run
       (`bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_flashv100_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_fullaccel_20260604.json`),
       FlashQLA-SM70 GDN prefill improves prefill from about `4.438 s` to
       about `2.796 s` (`~37%` lower prefill time).
     - Steady decode is still lower than the historical `~84.6 tok/s`
       evidence (`73.15 tok/s` here). FlashQLA is a prefill route, so the
       decode delta should not be accepted as a FlashQLA win/loss without a
       same-day FLA control or a decode-route audit.
     - This item is speed/route evidence only. Long-output token-quality and
       model-level logit/logprob gates for the FlashQLA route remain pending.

331. 2026-06-09 FlashQLA-SM70 fused GDN decode integrated and measured.

     Implementation:
     - Added a fused SM70 decode kernel in the local FlashQLA checkout for the
       vLLM mixed-QKV/global-state path:
       `flash_qla/ops/gated_delta_rule/chunk/sm70/csrc/gdn_forward.cu` and
       `flash_qla/ops/gated_delta_rule/chunk/sm70/fused_fwd.py`.
     - vLLM integration is opt-in with `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DECODE_FLASHQLA=1` in
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py`.
     - The real vLLM GDN state cache is page-aligned with an `as_strided`
       padded slot stride. The first model run hit the new route but failed
       CUDA graph capture because the wrapper required `state.is_contiguous()`.
       The fix keeps the global state cache in place and passes
       `state.stride(0)` into the kernel; only the inner `[Hv,V,K]` page layout
       is required to be contiguous.
     - Real model `dt_bias` can be fp16/bf16/fp32. The wrapper now accepts
       these dtypes while keeping `A_log` fp32.
     - Non-contiguous output views are handled by a temporary contiguous kernel
       output plus `copy_` back into the caller buffer. The state cache is not
       copied, so recurrent state updates remain in-place.

     Kernel-level validation:
     - Padded-state smoke passed with exact equality:
       `state1_contiguous=false`, stride `(262161, 16384, 128, 1)`,
       `out_maxdiff=0.0`, `state_maxdiff=0.0`.
     - B=8 Qwen3.6-35B TP2 local decode shape, FLA packed decode baseline:
       `0.00015765045496664244 s`.
     - Fused FlashQLA decode after stride/dtype fix:
       `0.00008227896007156232 s`, about `1.92x` faster at the operator
       level.
     - Same-input compare artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/op_gdn_decode_fla_vs_flashqla_fused_stride_dtfix_b8_compare_20260609.json`.
       Inputs matched exactly; output max diff was `9.5367431640625e-07`,
       final-state max diff was `7.450580596923828e-09`.

     35B-AWQ end-to-end route-hit and speed:
     - Command shape: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP2,
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`, `input_len=16324`, `output_len=256`,
       `warmup=1`, `repeat=2`, `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `gdn_prefill_backend=flashqla_sm70`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DECODE_FLASHQLA=1`.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_gdn_flashqla_decodeqla_i16324_o256_tp2_fullaccel_graph_w1_r2_20260609_stride_dtfix.json`.
     - Route logs showed FlashQLA-SM70 GDN prefill, SM70 FlashQLA GDN decode,
       Flash-V100 decode, AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path, custom TP all-reduce,
       LM-head top1, and FULL decode CUDA graph capture.
     - Result: `prefill_time_mean=2.7975443124996673 s`,
       `decode_time_mean=3.4661837454987108 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps_mean=73.56794227205978 tok/s`.

     Comparison against item 330 same-day FLA decode control:
     - Item 330 without QLA decode:
       `prefill_time_mean=2.796139721500367 s`,
       `decode_time_mean=3.485936782501085 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps_mean=73.15106860268904 tok/s`.
     - QLA decode changes steady decode by about `+0.57%`
       (`73.15 -> 73.57 tok/s`) while leaving prefill essentially unchanged.
       The operator speedup is real, but the 35B long-output end-to-end decode
       bottleneck is mostly elsewhere in this route.

     Status:
     - FlashQLA fused decode is no longer blocked by vLLM padded state cache
       layout and can run under the FULL decode graph route.
     - It is still opt-in. Do not mark it as an auto/default route until
       long-output token/logit quality is compared against the accepted FLA
       route and the small end-to-end decode gain is judged worth the extra
       external FlashQLA dependency.

332. 2026-06-09 FlashQLA-SM70 prefill chunk-size sweep and baseline-decode
     clarification.

     Scope:
     - Model and benchmark shape stayed fixed:
       `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP2,
       `input_len=16324`, `output_len=256`, `warmup=1`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `dtype=half`,
       `quantization=awq`, `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`.
     - GPUs: `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3`; GPUs 0/1 were not used.
     - Accepted SM70 speed gates stayed on:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`.

     Prefill speed evidence:
     - Historical FLA/Flash-V100 baseline, `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260604/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_flashv100_i16324_o256_tp2_warm1_r2_fullaccel_20260604.json`;
       prefill `4.437749651508057 s`, `3678.44 tok/s`.
     - FlashQLA prefill, `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_gdn_flashqla_i16324_o256_tp2_fullaccel_graph_w1_r2_20260609_fix1.json`;
       prefill `2.796139721500367 s`, `5838.05 tok/s`.
     - FlashQLA prefill plus FlashQLA fused GDN decode,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_gdn_flashqla_decodeqla_i16324_o256_tp2_fullaccel_graph_w1_r2_20260609_stride_dtfix.json`;
       prefill `2.7975443124996673 s`, `5835.12 tok/s`.
     - FlashQLA prefill, `max_num_batched_tokens=8192`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_gdn_flashqla_prefill_mbt8192_i16324_o256_tp2_fullaccel_graph_w1_r1_20260609.json`;
       prefill `2.976156421995256 s`, `5484.93 tok/s`.
     - A `max_num_batched_tokens=20000` run was started but intentionally
       stopped before completion at user request; it is not counted.

     Interpretation:
     - On the fixed 16k/256 35B-AWQ benchmark, FlashQLA-SM70 prefill improves
       prefill throughput from `3678.44 tok/s` to about `5838 tok/s`
       (`+58.7%`, or about `37.0%` lower prefill time).
     - `8192` chunking is slower than the single 16k chunk for this prompt
       (`5484.93 tok/s` vs `5838.05 tok/s`). The current measured best point is
       therefore `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`; do not re-sweep smaller chunks
       unless a new scheduler/kernel change lands.
     - Current latest-tree no-QLA same-shape control with
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384` and `--ignore-eos` produced
       `prefill_time_mean=4.454028982996533 s` and
       `steady_decode_tps_mean=71.650981047698` in
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260608/decode_qwen36_35b_awq_flashv100_noqla_current_i16324_o256_tp2_fullaccel_graph_w1_r2_ignoreeos_20260609.json`.
       This confirms the current `~71-73 tok/s` decode baseline has already
       regressed independently of QLA; do not attribute that decode drop to
       FlashQLA prefill/decode.

333. 2026-06-10 FlashQLA-SM70 prefill and decode default-on route check on
     current latest tree.

     Code/defaulting:

       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA` was installed into
       `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210` as an editable
       `flash_qla` package, so vLLM no longer needs a manual
       `PYTHONPATH=/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA` for this route.

       platform is SM70/SM75, `linear_key_head_dim=128`, model dtype is
       `torch.float16`, and the FlashQLA SM70 prefill symbol is importable.
       Otherwise it falls back to Triton/FLA.

       including `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DECODE_FLASHQLA=1`, only when the env var is
       not explicitly set. The standalone env default remains off so explicit
       `VLLM_SM70_GDN_DECODE_FLASHQLA=0` still disables FlashQLA decode.

     Run:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`.
     - GPUs: `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1`.
     - Shape/config: TP2, `input_len=16324`, `output_len=256`, `warmup=1`,

     - Log:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_i16324_o256_flashqla_default_prefill_decode_w1_r2_20260610.log`.

       `Auto-setting VLLM_SM70_GDN_DECODE_FLASHQLA=1`.
     - Prefill:
       `Using FlashQLA-SM70 GDN prefill kernel
       (requested=auto, head_k_dim=128, model_dtype=torch.float16)`.

       `SM70 FlashQLA GDN decode route enabled`, plus route-debug

       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active`, `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched
       path enabled`, `SM70 AWQ MoE legacy single-token monolithic compact

     Speed result:

     - `decode_time_mean=2.642443976001232 s`.
     - `steady_decode_tps_mean=96.5015744830734 tok/s`.
     - `output_tps_mean=47.50220316342984 tok/s`.

       (`prefill_time_mean=4.454028982996533 s`,
       `steady_decode_tps_mean=71.650981047698`), this route restores the QLA
       prefill win and also benefits from the later decode-speed fixes in the
       current tree.

     Quality status:
     - This run is accepted as route-hit and speed evidence only.
     - The measured repeat token hashes differ. Historical same-shape
       no-QLA, FlashQLA-prefill-only, and FlashQLA-prefill+decode artifacts
       also show repeat hash divergence, so this is not proven to be a new
       FlashQLA decode-only regression. It still means model-level long-output
       quality is not closed by this item.
     - Do not claim FlashQLA prefill/decode model quality has passed until a
       dedicated token/logprob or strict reference comparison gate passes.

334. 2026-06-12 FlashQLA-SM70 prefill direct-output and gate-semantics audit.

     Code changes:
     - External FlashQLA checkout
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/FlashQLA` now lets
       `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_sm70_vlk_varlen` accept an optional
       caller-owned `output` tensor. The C++ binding validates shape/device/
       dtype and writes directly into that tensor instead of allocating a new
       output.
     - Latest-vLLM `flashqla_sm70_chunk_gated_delta_rule` passes
       `core_attn_out[:T].unsqueeze(0)` as that output when layout matches,
       avoiding the old wrapper `out_flat.copy_` fallback.
     - Fixed the FlashQLA prefill gate contract: the model fused post-conv prep
       emits `exp(g)` for FlashQLA, so the wrapper now passes
       `gate_is_exp=True` into the FlashQLA kernel. Legacy prep and FLA keep
