       log-g semantics.
     - Added temporary prefill profile points for `post_conv_prep`,
       `state_gather`, `core_call`, `state_writeback`, `projection_norm`,
       `projection_out_proj`, and wrapper input/kernel/copy stages.

     Low-level validation:
     - Direct-output smoke passed with exact equality for same inputs:
       returned output pointer reused the caller buffer, `out_maxdiff=0.0`,
       `state_maxdiff=0.0`.
     - 4k model profile artifact:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_i4096_o1_flashqla_directout_profile_w0_r1_20260612.json`.
       It showed q/k/v/g/beta entering QLA were already contiguous. For
       4096-token prefill, `state_gather` median was about `0.243 ms/layer`,
       `state_writeback` median about `0.154 ms/layer`, `core_call` median
       about `6.328 ms/layer`, and projection total median about
       `4.176 ms/layer`. This localizes the remaining gap away from simple
       input contiguity and state gather/writeback.
     - Operator benchmark artifacts:
       - `op_gdn_prefill_compare_t4096_h8_16_directout_gatefix_20260612.json`:
         FLA `4.519 ms`; FlashQLA exp-gate direct-output `5.986 ms`
         (`0.755x` vs FLA).
       - `op_gdn_prefill_compare_t16384_h8_16_directout_gatefix_sched_20260612.json`:
         FLA `36.772 ms`; FlashQLA exp-gate direct-output `23.969 ms`
         (`1.534x` vs FLA).
       - `op_gdn_prefill_compare_t65536_h8_16_directout_gatefix_sched_20260612.json`:
         FLA `64.669 ms`; FlashQLA exp-gate direct-output `95.868 ms`
         (`0.675x` vs FLA).
       Direct-output reused the caller buffer in all direct-output cases, and
       the direct-output diffs matched the non-direct FlashQLA diffs. The old
       model-level copy was not the dominant cost.

     35B-AWQ end-to-end benchmark setup:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP2,
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1`, `dtype=half`, `quantization=awq`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `max_model_len=66000`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, `warmup=1`, `repeat=1`,
       `output_len=1`, `ignore_eos=true`.
     - Case file:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/qla_prefill_cases_4k_16k_64k_o1_20260612.json`.
     - 64k whole-prefill with `max_num_batched_tokens=65536` is not a usable
       V100 TP2 baseline in the current tree: at `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`
       engine init had only `0.51 GiB` KV cache for `66000` max length; at
       `0.95` init passed but AWQ MoE allocated a `2.00 GiB` `sorted_output`
       temporary and OOMed. The accepted measurement below uses 16k chunked
       prefill, which avoids the 64k one-shot MoE buffer.

     End-to-end QLA default artifact:
     - `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_prefill_4k16k64k_o1_qla_gatefix_chunk16k_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`.
     - Route logs: FlashQLA-SM70 GDN prefill, Flash-V100 attention,
       AWQ TurboMind batched MoE, custom TP all-reduce, compile graph.
     - Prefill results:
       - 4k: `0.661580 s`, `6191.2 tok/s`, first token `[16401]`.
       - 16k: `2.822225 s`, `5805.3 tok/s`, first token `[369]`.
       - 64k chunk16k: `25.498524 s`, `2570.2 tok/s`, first token `[16401]`.

     End-to-end FLA control artifact:
     - `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_prefill_4k16k64k_o1_fla_chunk16k_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`.
     - Same command and routes except `additional_config={"gdn_prefill_backend":
       "triton"}`.
     - Prefill results:
       - 4k: `0.508821 s`, `8050.0 tok/s`, first token `[16401]`.
       - 16k: `2.604350 s`, `6291.0 tok/s`, first token `[369]`.
       - 64k chunk16k: `24.528420 s`, `2671.8 tok/s`, first token `[16401]`.

     Interpretation:
     - In the current latest tree and this 4k/16k/64k o1 benchmark, QLA does
       not translate to an end-to-end prefill win. It is slower than FLA by
       about `30.0%` at 4k, `8.4%` at 16k, and `4.0%` at 64k chunk16k, despite
       matching the first greedy token in all three cases.
     - This supersedes the earlier item 333 conclusion for generic default
       prefill acceptance. The older 16k/256 route still records a historical
       speed win, but current same-day o1 controls show the default QLA route
       is not an accepted end-to-end throughput improvement.
     - Next work should focus on the FlashQLA prefill kernel/launch structure
       and its interaction with 16k chunked model prefill. Removing wrapper
       output copy and state gather/writeback alone is insufficient.

335. 2026-06-12 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ MTP4 official-sampling speed check and
     0.0.3 comparison.

     Scope:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`.
     - GPUs: physical 2/3 only.
     - Shape/config: TP2, `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`, `warmup=1`,
       `repeat=2`, `max_model_len=4096`, `max_num_batched_tokens=2048`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, `dtype=half/float16`, `quantization=awq`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `temperature=1.0`,
       `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, `ignore_eos=true`.
     - Accepted SM70 speed routes were kept enabled where available:
       Flash-V100 attention, AWQ TurboMind, AWQ MoE batched GEMM, legacy
       single-token compact MoE, FlashQLA/GDN decode schedules, and custom TP
       all-reduce.

     Latest-tree compile/FULL-graph MTP4 startup status:
     - First MTP4 attempt with `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`
       failed before decode because the latest SM70 compile-graph policy
       defaulted to `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1,2]`. Spec decode with
       `num_speculative_tokens=4` requires a graph query multiple of `5`.
     - Retrying with `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[5]` and
       `max_cudagraph_capture_size=5` still did not reach decode. One run
       died after very long graph capture; another died around draft readiness
       with worker `BrokenPipeError` after the engine core exited first.
     - This is an adapter gap, not an MTP model-selection failure. The logs
       selected target `Qwen3_5MoeForConditionalGeneration` and draft
       `Qwen3_5MoeMTP`.

     Latest-tree no-compile decode-graph evidence:
     - MTP4 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_official_i1024_o256_w1_r2_tp2_nocompile_mbt2048_20260612.json`.
     - No-MTP baseline artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_nomtp_official_i1024_o256_w1_r2_tp2_nocompile_mbt2048_20260612.json`.
     - Latest no-MTP: `steady_decode_tps_mean=86.10955102624565`,
       `output_tps_mean=81.63169389384396`.
     - Latest MTP4: `steady_decode_tps_mean=64.4837696527818`,
       `output_tps_mean=60.97403261485785`.
     - Result: current latest MTP4 no-compile path is a slowdown
       (`0.749x` steady decode vs no-MTP), not a speedup.
     - Console metrics during the latest MTP4 run showed MTP was active:
       mean acceptance length `3.68`, average draft acceptance rate `67.1%`,
       per-position acceptance `0.839/0.710/0.600/0.535`, accepted
       `416/620` drafted tokens. These metrics were observed from runtime logs;
       the offline JSON harness does not yet persist them.
     - First warmup was materially slower (`48.7759504518283` steady tok/s)
       and emitted request-time JIT warnings for MTP/spec kernels including
       `eagle_prepare_next_token_padded_kernel`,
       `eagle_step_slot_mapping_metadata_kernel`,
       `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`, `expand_kernel`, and
       `eagle_prepare_inputs_padded_kernel`. MTP warmup coverage is incomplete.

     Old 0.0.3 same-machine comparison:
     - Artifact:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/old003_qwen36_35b_awq_mtp4_official_i1024_o256_tp2_mbt2048_20260612/20260612-175046/summary.json`.
     - Old baseline median decode throughput: `79.07756745394818 tok/s`.
     - Old MTP4 median decode throughput: `95.11607176509995 tok/s`.
     - Old MTP4 speedup: `1.203x` over old baseline.
     - Old MTP4 median output throughput: `88.68874334047618 tok/s`.
     - Old MTP4 median acceptance rate: `57.16905901116427%`.
     - Old MTP4 median acceptance length: `3.2867623604465708`.
     - Mean per-position acceptance:
       `0.8130781499202552/0.6419457735247209/0.4625996810207337/0.36913875598086127`.

     Observed old-vs-latest slow points:
     - Old 0.0.3 server defaults explicitly added MTP graph support:
       `speculative_config.use_local_argmax_reduction=True`,
       `mtp_verifier_cudagraph_shapes=5x1..1`, and
       `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1,2,4,5,8,9]`. Latest SM70 compile policy
       did not automatically include the required MTP4 size `5`.
     - Old MTP4 logs hit `Using local argmax reduction for draft token
       generation (communication: O(2*tp_size) vs O(vocab_size))`. The latest
       MTP4 run did not log this route, making draft-token communication a
       primary suspect.
     - Old 0.0.3 used the tuned MoE config file
       `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/configs/E=256,N=256,device_name=Tesla_PG503-216.json`.
       Latest logs reported that this file was not found and used a default
       unquantized MoE config, affecting MTP draft/unquantized MoE pieces.
     - Old 0.0.3 hit `SM70 dense fp16 fast path enabled for LM head`; latest
       official-sampling runs fell back to the PyTorch native top-p/top-k
       sampler on SM70 and did not show the same old LM-head route.
     - Both old and latest disable quantization for the MTP branch based on
       `modules_to_not_convert`, so unquantized draft itself is not a
       latest-only regression.

     Follow-up root cause isolation and default fix:
     - The historical latest no-compile run above already showed normal MTP
       activity, so the later compile-graph slowdown should not be classified
       as a generic MTP acceptance problem.
     - Compile/FULL graph with the MTP drafter auto-selecting the SM70
       Flash-V100 attention backend under PIECEWISE graph was isolated as the
       bad path:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_acceptance_probe_i1024_o128_w1_r1_tp2_20260612.json`
       had `steady_decode_tps=32.727940033338434`,
       `acceptance_length=1.1043478260869566`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.02608695652173913`.
       Raising `max_num_batched_tokens` to `2048` did not fix it:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_acceptance_probe_compilegraph_mbt2048_i1024_o128_w1_r1_tp2_20260612.json`
       had `steady_decode_tps=30.483420237306532`,
       `acceptance_length=1.016`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.004`.
     - The same latest tree with no compile graph but decode CUDA graph active
       kept acceptance healthy:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_acceptance_probe_nocompile_i1024_o128_w1_r1_tp2_20260612.json`
       had `steady_decode_tps=89.52269692619682`,
       `acceptance_length=4.482758620689655`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.8706896551724138`.
     - Keeping the target model on the SM70 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph route
       while setting only `speculative_config.enforce_eager=true` for the MTP
       drafter restored acceptance during isolation, so the target verifier
       graph is not the root cause. This was an intermediate diagnostic only,
       not the accepted speed solution because it sacrifices the drafter graph:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_acceptance_probe_compilegraph_drafter_eager_i1024_o96_w1_r1_tp2_20260612.json`
       had `steady_decode_tps=123.60038694920048`,
       `acceptance_length=5.0`, and `overall_acceptance_rate=1.0`.
     - The graph-safe fix is to keep the target on Flash-V100 compile/FULL
       graph and keep the drafter on graph as well, but force only the MTP
       drafter attention backend to `TRITON_ATTN`. In current vLLM, the MTP
       drafter graph shape is PIECEWISE rather than FULL; this is still the
       graph path and not eager execution.
     - Explicit graph-safe diagnostic run:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_compilegraph_drafter_triton_graph_i1024_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260612.json`.
       The JSON/logs record `speculative_config.enforce_eager=false`,
       `speculative_config.attention_backend=TRITON_ATTN`, target
       Flash-V100 attention, target compile/FULL graph, and drafter PIECEWISE
       graph. Result: `steady_decode_tps_mean=150.38305314777907`,
       `output_tps_mean=135.69874947580558`,
       `prefill_time_mean=0.17087784500108683`,
       `acceptance_length=4.849056603773585`,
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.9622641509433962`, and per-position
       acceptance `0.9622641509433962/0.9622641509433962/0.9622641509433962/0.9622641509433962`.
     - `EngineArgs.create_speculative_config` now defaults
       `speculative_config.enforce_eager=false` for every speculative config
       when the user did not already set it. This is intentionally not tied to
       Qwen or AWQ; it makes the drafter graph intent explicit for all models
       and still lets model-specific compatibility code override it later.
     - `EngineArgs._maybe_apply_qwen35_sm70_mtp_defaults` separately defaults
       only the Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 AWQ MTP-specific knobs such as
       `speculative_config.attention_backend=TRITON_ATTN`, verifier graph shape
       `5`, local argmax reduction, and related SM70 sizing. Target
       compile/FULL graph, drafter graph, Flash-V100 target attention, AWQ
       TurboMind batched MoE, and the other SM70 speed routes remain enabled.
     - Default-on validation without explicitly passing either
       `attention_backend` or `enforce_eager`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_compilegraph_default_triton_drafter_graph_i1024_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260612.json`.
       The JSON records
       `engine_kwargs.speculative_config.attention_backend=TRITON_ATTN`, target
       Flash-V100 attention, graph enabled, and the SM70 AWQ/graph policy
       enabled. That run predated the generic explicit default of
       `engine_kwargs.speculative_config.enforce_eager=false`; effective
       drafter graph execution was still enabled because target
       `enforce_eager=false`.
       Result: `steady_decode_tps_mean=127.13122093260638`,
       `output_tps_mean=116.94243433116186`,
       `prefill_time_mean=0.16131331399810733`,
       `acceptance_length=4.063492063492063`,
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.7658730158730159`, and per-position
       acceptance `0.8095238095238095/0.7936507936507936/0.746031746031746/0.7142857142857143`.
       This run uses the official sampling settings, so acceptance and speed
       vary by output; it is still far from the collapsed `0.4%` to `2.6%`
       bad-path acceptance and keeps the drafter graph enabled.
     - 2026-06-13 regression check while isolating context length and prompt
       content found a separate local debugging mistake: the current tree had
       temporarily wrapped the MTP drafter model in a model-level CUDA graph.
       That is not the historical graph-safe line. Exact-history parameters
       (`max_model_len=4096`, `max_num_batched_tokens=2048`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.82`, target Flash-V100, drafter
       `TRITON_ATTN`, `enforce_eager=false`) collapsed with the wrapper:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_targetflash_draftertriton_exacthist_i1024_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`
       had `steady_decode_tps_mean=53.860243627372775`,
       `acceptance_length=1.6887417218543046`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.17218543046357615`.
     - The fix is to leave MTP drafter execution on the proposer dispatcher's
       PIECEWISE graph path and not wrap the drafter module with an outer
       `CUDAGraphWrapper`. External PIECEWISE wrapping also hit nested CUDA
       graph capture errors. After removing the outer drafter wrapper, the same
       exact-history test recovered:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_targetflash_draftertriton_dispatchpiece_exacthist_i1024_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`
       had `steady_decode_tps_mean=122.246842192985`,
       `output_tps_mean=112.79908727567353`,
       `prefill_time_mean=0.16159685700040427`,
       `acceptance_length=3.9846153846153847`,
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.7461538461538462`, and per-position
       acceptance
       `0.8461538461538461/0.7384615384615385/0.7076923076923077/0.6923076923076923`.
       This matches the earlier normal default validation within official
       sampling variance.
     - Context/content follow-up, one model load, GPUs 2/3, TP2, official
       sampling, target Flash-V100, drafter `TRITON_ATTN`,
       `max_model_len=8192`, `max_num_batched_tokens=4096`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260612/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_targetflash_draftertriton_dispatchpiece_context_cases_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`.
       `ctx1024_default_repeat` had `steady_decode_tps_mean=77.53859362328934`,
       `acceptance_length=2.4056603773584904`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.35141509433962265`.
       `ctx4096_default_repeat` had `steady_decode_tps_mean=102.23711358931435`,
       `acceptance_length=3.647887323943662`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.6619718309859155`.
       `ctx4096_natural_report` had `steady_decode_tps_mean=85.59774014715865`,
       `acceptance_length=2.9651162790697674`, and
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.49127906976744184`.
     - Conclusion for that check: the severe `0.17` acceptance collapse was not
       caused by 4k context length or repeated/filler prompt text; it was caused
       by the wrong drafter graph wrapping. Longer context and prompt content do
       modulate MTP acceptance under official sampling, and the 4k natural
       prompt is lower than the repeated 4k prompt in this single run, but both
       remain far above the collapsed bad path. Do not compare 4k/context
       experiments against the 1024 exact-history baseline without matching the
       engine/capture shape.

     Current conclusion:
     - Official vLLM MTP can run on the latest model stack, and the latest
       SM70 35B-AWQ MTP4 graph path is recovered without falling back to a
       drafter eager workaround when the target keeps the compile/FULL graph
       route and the MTP drafter uses Triton attention under its graph path.
       This is not yet a completed MTP speed target.
     - Same-shape latest no-MTP compile/FULL-graph baselines are
       `101.05235045946547` to `102.57947893561675 tok/s` for the official
       `1k/256` TP2 shape. The restored default MTP4 line is only
       `122.246842192985` to `127.13122093260638 tok/s` in the repeatable
       default-style runs, or roughly `1.19x` to `1.26x`. A high-acceptance
       diagnostic reached `150.38305314777907 tok/s`
       (`acceptance_length=4.849056603773585`,
       `overall_acceptance_rate=0.9622641509433962`), which is about `1.49x`
       against the `101 tok/s` baseline, but that is not yet the stable default
       result.
     - Old 0.0.3 MTP was also not a 1.5x-2x reference: the same-machine old
       summary recorded no-MTP `79.07756745394818 tok/s` and MTP4
       `95.11607176509995 tok/s`, about `1.20x`, with median acceptance
       length `3.2867623604465708`. Therefore old 0.0.3 proves MTP is wired,
       but it is not the desired final MTP performance ceiling.
     - 2026-06-13 old 0.0.3 recheck at the requested `4k/256` shape used the
       same server benchmark script and old defaults on physical GPUs 2/3:
       `/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/1Cat-vLLM-0.0.3/vllm/bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/old003_qwen36_35b_awq_mtp4_official_i4096_o256_tp2_mbt4608_20260613/20260613-091253/summary.json`.
       Shape/config: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2, `input_tokens=4096`,
       `output_tokens=256`, `max_model_len=4608`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=4608`, `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`,
       `top_k=20`, `warmup=1`, `repeat=2`,
       `cudagraph_mode=full_and_piecewise`, Flash-V100, AWQ batched MoE,
       old LM-head fast path, and Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 SM70 defaults enabled.
       Logs confirmed `speculative_config.use_local_argmax_reduction=True`,
       `mtp_verifier_cudagraph_shapes=5x1..1`,
       `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1, 2, 4, 5, 8, 9]`, MTP weight sharing,
       local argmax reduction, Flash-V100 target/draft routes, AWQ MoE batched
       GEMM, and the 0.0.3 tuned MoE config file.
     - Result for that synthetic-token 4k prompt: baseline median decode
       `79.41608349165963 tok/s`, median output `75.21124800607603 tok/s`,
       median TTFT `192.59806650006794 ms`; MTP4 median decode
       `78.50472721167873 tok/s`, median output `70.70080966621119 tok/s`,
       median TTFT `371.91340799836325 ms`,
       median acceptance rate `42.74059274059274%`, median acceptance length
       `2.7096237096237097`, and mean per-position acceptance
       `0.6959706959706959/0.491008991008991/0.2957042957042957/0.22693972693972694`.
       The decode speedup is `0.9885x` and output speedup is `0.9400x`.
       This run does not support the claim that old 0.0.3 generally gives
       1.5x-2x MTP speedup at the 4k synthetic-token shape. It also shows prompt
       content matters: this benchmark's generated 4k prompt is token-noise-like,
       not a natural 4k document.
     - 2026-06-13 latest-tree code-generation prompt check, requested to
       avoid judging MTP by token-noise-like prompts only. Case file:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/latest_code_prompt_4096_256_cases.json`.
       Shape/config: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on physical GPUs 2/3,
       `input_len=4096`, `output_len=256`, `max_model_len=4608`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=4608`, `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`,
       `top_k=20`, `ignore_eos=true`, `warmup=1`, `repeat=2`, target
       Flash-V100 attention, SM70 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy,
       AWQ MoE batched GEMM and legacy single-token compact enabled,
       `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0`.
       First-pass baseline artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_nomtp_code_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`.
       First-pass MTP4 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`.
       These first-pass artifacts are not valid production baselines because
       they set `--disable-custom-all-reduce`, which disabled the normal TP
       hidden-state custom all-reduce route. They are retained only as the
       control that explains the misleading `91 tok/s` baseline.
       The MTP4 run explicitly used
       `speculative_config={"method":"mtp","num_speculative_tokens":4,"attention_backend":"TRITON_ATTN","enforce_eager":false,"use_local_argmax_reduction":true}`;
       logs confirmed Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 SM70 MTP defaults,
       `mtp_verifier_cudagraph_shapes=5x1..1`,
       `cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1, 2, 4, 5, 8, 9]`, target
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100`, drafter `TRITON_ATTN`, MTP weight sharing, and
       local argmax reduction.
     - Invalid first-pass code prompt result with production custom all-reduce
       disabled: no-MTP steady decode
       `91.31286810190329 tok/s`, output `79.36185359836745 tok/s`,
       prefill `0.43024177299957955 s`. MTP4 steady decode
       `113.47793377759442 tok/s`, output `91.98923285610292 tok/s`,
       prefill `0.5206175089988392 s`. Speedup is `1.2427375914964731x`
       on steady decode and `1.1591114456781693x` on full output throughput.
       MTP4 acceptance length mean is `4.2602796052631575`, overall
       acceptance rate mean is `0.8150699013157895`, and mean per-position
       acceptance is
       `0.901452850877193/0.8214089912280702/0.7891995614035088/0.7482182017543859`.
       This confirms natural code-generation context can improve acceptance,
       but these speedup ratios are inflated by the wrong no-MTP baseline.
     - Corrected code prompt check with production custom all-reduce enabled:
       baseline artifact
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_nomtp_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`;
       MTP4 artifact
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`.
       Both artifacts record `disable_custom_all_reduce=false`; runtime logs
       selected `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for `tp:0`, and the JSON communication
       policy records `hidden_state_custom_allreduce_effective=true`.
       Correct no-MTP steady decode is `106.19741147906592 tok/s`, output
       `90.37314617490458 tok/s`, prefill `0.42853600200032815 s`.
       Correct MTP4 steady decode is `108.32871551803674 tok/s`, output
       `88.24300278772367 tok/s`, prefill `0.5211578174967144 s`.
       The corrected MTP speedup is only `1.0200692654301744x` on steady
       decode and `0.9764294651969035x` on full output throughput.
       Corrected MTP4 acceptance length mean is `3.8017127799736494`, overall
       acceptance rate mean is `0.7004281949934124`, and mean per-position
       acceptance is
       `0.8662714097496707/0.733201581027668/0.6525032938076416/0.5497364953886693`.
     - Corrected cost interpretation: the code prompt line is not acceptance
       collapsed, but per-speculation-step cost is too high. With custom
       all-reduce enabled, the two measured MTP repeats used `66` and `69`
       draft iterations and spent `34.989061484917656 ms` and
       `34.788073318824004 ms` per speculation step. Against the corrected
       no-MTP baseline (`9.416425386263975 ms/token`), a `1.5x` speedup would
       require roughly `6.28 ms/token`, or about `23.9 ms` per step at the
       observed `3.80` accepted-token length. The current `34.9 ms/step` is
       therefore the bottleneck. The likely cost centers are the `k+1` target
       verifier pass, four sequential MTP drafter forwards, drafter
       `TRITON_ATTN`, rejection/sampling metadata kernels, and remaining MTP
       kernel warmup/JIT coverage.
     - The remaining MTP performance target is to make official-sampling MTP4
       reliably reach the `1.5x-2x` speedup band against the current
       no-MTP baseline, not merely to recover non-collapsed acceptance. The
       next profiling/fix target is per-speculation-iteration overhead:
       target verifier graph time for `k+1` tokens, sequential drafter forward
       cost, drafter attention/backend cost, MTP sampling/logit cost, and TP
       communication.
     - 2026-06-13 MTP cost-profile pass added env-gated diagnostics
       (`VLLM_SM70_MTP_PROFILE=1`, `VLLM_SM70_MTP_PROFILE_INTERVAL=8`) to split
       runner target/verifier/rejection/draft time and proposer first/loop
       drafter time. Diagnostic artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.log`.
       This run used the same production custom-all-reduce, target
       Flash-V100, drafter `TRITON_ATTN`, MTP4, 4k/256 code prompt setup.
       Because profiling inserts CUDA events and synchronization, the
       throughput numbers in this artifact are diagnostic-only.
     - The measured repeat had acceptance length `3.923076923076923`, overall
       acceptance rate `0.7307692307692307`, steady decode
       `100.7635023252486 tok/s`, output `83.77186711356273 tok/s`, prefill
       `0.5218231879989617 s`, and `65` draft iterations. The no-MTP custom-AR
       baseline remains `106.19741147906592 tok/s` (`9.416425373015088
       ms/token`), so this repeat is `9.92422828627134 ms/token`, still slower
       than baseline.
     - Stable-profile estimate from the cumulative-log delta between
       `calls=88` (warmup boundary) and `calls=152`:
       runner target forward/verifier `24.991 ms/step`, target logits
       `0.604 ms/step`, rejection sampler `1.812 ms/step`, draft/proposer
       total `12.367 ms/step`, and bookkeeping `0.104 ms/step`. Proposer
       breakdown for the same window: total GPU `12.131 ms/step`, first MTP
       forward `5.000 ms`, subsequent loop forwards
       `1.540/1.541/1.538 ms`, draft sampling
       `0.601/0.603/0.603/0.601 ms`, and loop metadata CPU
       `0.854 ms`.
     - Interpretation: the current MTP4 line is not primarily acceptance
       collapsed. It is step-cost bound. At the observed acceptance length,
       a `1.5x` speedup over the corrected no-MTP baseline would require about
       `24.627574052501 ms/step`; the measured repeat is about
       `38.93351096921833 ms/step`, so roughly `14.305936916717332 ms/step`
       must be removed. The target verifier alone is about `25 ms/step`, and
       the four serial MTP draft forwards plus draft sampling/metadata add
       another about `12 ms/step`. Therefore simply improving acceptance will
       not recover the desired MTP speed; the next fix must reduce verifier
       and/or drafter step cost, not just keep toggling MTP quality knobs.
     - The profile also recorded first-inference Triton JIT during warmup for
       `eagle_prepare_next_token_padded_kernel`, `kernel_unified_attention`,
       `eagle_step_slot_mapping_metadata_kernel`, `reduce_segments`,
       `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`, `expand_kernel`, and
       `eagle_prepare_inputs_padded_kernel`. MTP warmup coverage still needs a
       follow-up pass so benchmark warmups and serving cold requests do not pay
       these JIT spikes.
     - Separate earlier isolation note: the drafter Flash-V100 attention +
       PIECEWISE graph attempt caused collapsed acceptance. That explains why
       the current safe MTP line keeps the drafter on `TRITON_ATTN`, but it is
       not the explanation for the restored Triton-drafter line's weak speedup.
       The restored line is step-cost bound as profiled above.
     - Target-model V100 Flash Attention is already active in the restored MTP
       runs; the current compromise is only on the MTP drafter attention backend.
       The next speed target is therefore drafter Flash-V100 attention under
       graph with stable acceptance, not re-enabling target Flash-V100.
     - Future work can revisit Flash-V100 drafter graph only after its
       metadata/replay issue is fixed and it beats the Triton drafter graph
       without lowering acceptance. The safe SM70 MTP default is graph-enabled
       drafter `TRITON_ATTN`, not `enforce_eager=true`.
     - Output-quality drift remains explicitly out of scope for this item per
       the current debugging split; this item is speed/acceptance evidence only.

336. 2026-06-12 FlashQLA-SM70 4k prefill GDN share and target audit.

     Scope:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`.
     - GPUs: physical 0/1 only, TP2.
     - Shape/config: `input_len=4096`, `output_len=1`, `max_model_len=8192`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=4096`, `max_num_seqs=1`, `dtype=half`,
       `quantization=awq`, `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, compile/FULL
       graph defaults enabled.

     Artifacts:
     - QLA end-to-end:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_prefill_4k16k64k_o1_qla_gatefix_chunk16k_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`.
     - FLA end-to-end:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_prefill_4k16k64k_o1_fla_chunk16k_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`.
     - QLA profile:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_i4096_o1_flashqla_directout_profile_w0_r1_20260612.log`.
     - FLA profile:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_i4096_o1_fla_profile_w0_r1_20260612.log`.

     End-to-end trend:
     - 4k: QLA `0.661580 s` / `6191.2 tok/s`, FLA `0.508821 s` /
       `8050.0 tok/s`, QLA is `30.0%` slower by time.
     - 16k: QLA `2.822225 s` / `5805.3 tok/s`, FLA `2.604350 s` /
       `6291.0 tok/s`, QLA is `8.4%` slower by time.
     - 64k chunk16k: QLA `25.498524 s` / `2570.2 tok/s`, FLA
       `24.528420 s` / `2671.8 tok/s`, QLA is `4.0%` slower by time.
     - The shrinking 4k -> 64k gap supports treating 4k as the primary
       FlashQLA recovery metric. At long context the full-attention/chunked
       model path dominates enough that GDN improvements are diluted.

     4k GDN profile, robust median-per-layer estimate across the 30 GDN
     layers:
     - QLA core_call: `6.404 ms/layer`, about `192.1 ms` total.
     - FLA core_call: `4.758 ms/layer`, about `142.7 ms` total.
     - QLA post_conv_prep: `0.410 ms/layer`, about `12.3 ms` total.
     - FLA post_conv_prep: `0.366 ms/layer`, about `11.0 ms` total.
     - QLA state gather/writeback: about `13.3 ms` total.
     - FLA state gather/writeback: about `11.8 ms` total.
     - QLA projection_total: `4.637 ms/layer`, about `139.1 ms` total.
     - FLA projection_total: `4.553 ms/layer`, about `136.6 ms` total.

     Interpretation:
     - The QLA GDN module estimate at 4k is roughly
       `12.3 + 13.3 + 192.1 + 139.1 = 356.8 ms`, or about `54%` of the
       `0.661580 s` QLA end-to-end prefill.
     - The QLA core alone is about `29%` of QLA end-to-end prefill.
     - Gather/writeback is only about `2%`; optimizing state movement alone
       cannot recover the 4k end-to-end gap.
     - Current 4k FlashQLA core is slower than FLA by about `49 ms` across
       the 30 GDN layers. The total 4k end-to-end deficit is about `153 ms`,
       so core parity would recover only about one third of the observed gap.
     - To match the FLA 4k baseline of `8050 tok/s`, QLA prefill must shrink
       from `0.661580 s` to about `0.508821 s`, saving about `153 ms`.
       Achieving that through core_call only would require an unrealistic
       roughly 5x core reduction. The practical path is to first make the 4k
       FlashQLA core at least match FLA, then continue with whole-GDN/module
       overhead and launch/fusion work.

     Next work:
     - Focus first on the 4k FlashQLA-SM70 prefill core kernel occupancy,
       chunk/group launch structure, and tile policy. The current 4k kernel
       does not yet show the operator-level advantage needed for end-to-end
       recovery.
     - Keep 16k/64k as secondary confirmation points only after 4k improves;
       do not use 64k chunked prefill as the primary acceptance metric for
       this optimization.

     Follow-up implementation evidence:
     - The SM70 varlen kernel now specializes `gate_is_exp` as a compile-time
       template parameter for the model FlashQLA path. This removes the
       runtime `gate_is_exp ? gate_raw : exp(gate_raw)` branch from the token
       loop when fused post-conv prep already emits `exp(g)`.
     - 4k operator artifact:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_gdn_prefill_compare_t4096_h8_16_cgb2_sched_gate_template_20260612.json`.
       With `FLASH_QLA_SM70_COLUMN_GROUPS_PER_BLOCK=2`, FLA is `4.160 ms`,
       FLA core-out is `4.160 ms`, and FlashQLA exp-gate direct-out is
       `5.223 ms`. The previous same-shape FlashQLA exp-gate direct-out was
       about `5.99 ms`, so this recovers roughly `13%` of the FlashQLA core
       time without changing the max diff (`9.1552734375e-05` vs FLA).
     - 4k CGB rescan artifacts:
       `op_gdn_prefill_compare_t4096_h8_16_cgb{1,2,4,8}_sched_gate_template_20260612.json`.
       Absolute FlashQLA exp-gate direct-out times were approximately
       `5.270/5.223/5.281/5.713 ms`; the existing 35B TP2 default `CGB=2`
       remains the best 4k tile policy among these options.
     - 16k operator artifact:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_gdn_prefill_compare_t16384_h8_16_cgb2_sched_gate_template_20260612.json`.
       FlashQLA exp-gate direct-out improved from the older `23.97 ms` record
       to `20.98 ms`, but the warmed FLA control in this run measured
       `16.23 ms`. Treat the older 16k operator-level FlashQLA speedup record
       as polluted by the FLA side of that run unless revalidated.
     - The public SM70 no-cu fallback and the vLLM varlen fallback were compared
       in
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_gdn_prefill_compare_t4096_public_vs_varlen_sm70_20260612.json`.
       Public no-cu was about `5.80 ms`, vLLM varlen was about `5.24 ms`, and
       FLA was about `4.32 ms`; public and varlen outputs matched exactly.
       This rules out vLLM varlen layout as the reason FlashQLA is slower than
       FLA at 4k.
     - Root-cause correction: the upstream FlashQLA README's `2-3x` forward
       speedup is for the SM90/Hopper TileLang fused chunked GDN path with
       automatic intra-card context parallelism. The upstream public API rejects
       low-level SM70 A/h outputs and its SM70 high-level path does not support
       `cu_seqlens`; the local SM70 path used by vLLM is an experimental
       recurrent fallback, not the upstream Hopper fused chunked algorithm.
       Therefore the current V100 result should not be interpreted as "QLA
       speedup failed to show up end-to-end"; the correct statement is that the
       real QLA chunked operator advantage has not yet been ported to SM70.
     - The public SM70 fallback was also specialized on `gate_is_exp` at compile
       time, matching the varlen specialization. A short launch smoke after the
       patch succeeded for both public and varlen paths.

337. FlashQLA original TileLang chunked path SM70 down-port checkpoint
     (2026-06-12).

     Direction correction:
     - The target is not to keep optimizing a separate handwritten SM70
       recurrent fallback. The target is to preserve the upstream FlashQLA
       chunked/TileLang algorithm and down-port its acceleration path to SM70
       where the hardware permits.
     - Hopper-only schedule features cannot be copied literally to V100:
       `alloc_barrier`, warp-specialized producer/consumer staging,
       warpgroup register allocation, and SM90/TMA-style assumptions must be
       replaced with SM70-compatible scheduling while keeping the math and data
       flow equivalent.

     Implemented checkpoint:
     - Patched TileLang's local bf16 fallback helper so original
       `chunk_local_cumsum` compiles on SM70.
     - Added a compatibility alias for `T.gemm_v1` on the installed TileLang
       version.
     - Added an SM70 schedule branch in upstream FlashQLA
       `hopper/kkt_solve.py`. The branch removes Hopper barriers and replaces
       unsupported FP32 MMA for the two 32x32 solve GEMMs with scalar
       accumulation, while preserving the original KKT data flow. Small-shape
       reference check: max diff about `3.8e-06` vs a torch triangular inverse
       reference, i.e. FP16 output quantization level.
     - Added an SM70 schedule branch in upstream FlashQLA
       `hopper/fused_fwd.py`. The first compile probe exposed an SM70 GEMM
       limitation: TileLang's SM70 MMA lowering does not accept
       `transpose_A=True`; the schedule now materializes `K^T` in shared memory
       before the state update GEMM.
     - The first apparent fused-forward quality failure was not the chunked
       algorithm. It was a state layout mismatch: upstream FlashQLA public
       forward uses `[K,V]` state, while vLLM/FLA use `[V,K]`. The accepted
       SM70 schedule adapts initial/final state load/store in-kernel via
       `state_layout_vlk=True`, avoiding an external
       `transpose().contiguous()` copy.
     - Added `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_sm70_tilelang(...)`, which explicitly
       runs the original TileLang components on SM70 with `auto_cp=False`.
       vLLM can opt into this path with
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL=1` or
       `FLASH_QLA_SM70_USE_ORIGINAL_TILELANG=1`; when enabled, vLLM keeps raw
       `g` from `fused_post_conv_prep` instead of the fallback-only
       `exp(g)` shortcut.

     Operator evidence artifact:
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_gdn_prefill_original_tilelang_sm70_4k16k_20260612.json`.

     4k single-layer/operator result, `B=1`, `T=4096`, `Hg=8`, `H=16`,
     `K=V=128`, fp16 q/k/v, fp32 state, vLLM `[V,K]` state layout:
     - FLA: `4.815 ms`.
     - Old SM70 recurrent fallback: `5.778 ms`.
     - Original FlashQLA TileLang SM70 helper: `1.041 ms`.
     - Original helper vs FLA: output max diff `0.0001220703125`, state max
       diff `0.0007962584495544434`.

     16k confirmation:
     - FLA: `17.860 ms`.
     - Old SM70 recurrent fallback: `21.344 ms`.
     - Original FlashQLA TileLang SM70 helper: `3.428 ms`.
     - Original helper vs FLA: output max diff `0.00018310546875`, state max
       diff `0.0008080005645751953`.

     vLLM wrapper check:
     - With `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL=1`, the wrapper-level 4k
       comparison against FLA produced output max diff `0.0001220703125` and
       state max diff `0.0009212493896484375`.

     Current limitations:
     - `auto_cp` is still disabled for SM70 original TileLang path. The SM90
       `cp_context` path still needs a separate SM70-compatible down-port.
     - Direct write into vLLM `core_attn_out` has not yet been added to the
       original TileLang fused forward. The wrapper may still perform an output
       copy when a preallocated core buffer is provided.
     - This is operator-level evidence. Full 35B/27B end-to-end prefill must be
       rerun after deciding whether to make this opt-in path default.

338. 35B-AWQ TP2 end-to-end prefill validation for original FlashQLA SM70 path
     (2026-06-12).

     Artifact:
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_prefill_4k16k64k_o1_qla_original_sm70_chunk16k_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`
     with log
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_prefill_4k16k64k_o1_qla_original_sm70_chunk16k_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.log`.

     Matched baseline parameters:
     - Model `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`, TP2 on
       `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1`.
     - `dtype=half`, `quantization=awq`, `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`.
     - `max_model_len=66000`, `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`.
     - Same `qla_prefill_cases_4k_16k_64k_o1_20260612.json`,
       `warmup=1`, `repeat=1`, `ignore_eos=True`.
     - Opt-in env recorded in the artifact:
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL=1`.

     End-to-end prefill result:
     - 4k: old SM70 FlashQLA fallback `0.661580 s` (`6191 tok/s`), FLA
       `0.508821 s` (`8050 tok/s`), original FlashQLA SM70 `0.427578 s`
       (`9580 tok/s`). The original path is `1.55x` old FlashQLA and `1.19x`
       FLA.
     - 16k: old SM70 FlashQLA fallback `2.822225 s` (`5805 tok/s`), FLA
       `2.604350 s` (`6291 tok/s`), original FlashQLA SM70 `2.290919 s`
       (`7152 tok/s`). The original path is `1.23x` old FlashQLA and `1.14x`
       FLA.
     - 64k: old SM70 FlashQLA fallback `25.498524 s` (`2570 tok/s`), FLA
       `24.528420 s` (`2672 tok/s`), original FlashQLA SM70 `23.303645 s`
       (`2812 tok/s`). The original path is `1.09x` old FlashQLA and `1.05x`
       FLA.
     - The one-token outputs matched the old FlashQLA and FLA artifacts for
       all three cases: 4k token `16401`, 16k token `369`, 64k token `16401`.
       This is not a long-output quality gate, but it rules out a first-token
       regression in this speed run.

     4k expected-vs-observed accounting using the prior GDN profile:
     - Prior old-FlashQLA 4k profile estimated GDN core_call at about
       `192.1 ms` total across 30 GDN layers, and old 4k end-to-end was
       `661.6 ms`.
     - Operator-level old fallback to original TileLang ratio was
       `1.041 / 5.778 = 0.180`.
     - If only the GDN core_call changed, expected new GDN core time was
       `34.6 ms`, expected saving was `157.5 ms`, and expected end-to-end time
       was about `504.1 ms`.
     - Observed end-to-end time was `427.6 ms`, saving `234.0 ms` vs old
       FlashQLA. That is about `149%` of the conservative core-only expected
       saving.
     - Interpretation: the original TileLang SM70 path's operator-level gain is
       visible end-to-end. The extra saving above the core-only estimate likely
       comes from old fallback wrapper/scheduling overhead that was not fully
       included in the `core_call` bucket, or from single-run/profile noise.
       A synchronized GDN-profile rerun can refine attribution, but it is not
       required to establish that the 4k end-to-end gain was recovered.

     Current decision:
     - Keep pushing the upstream/original FlashQLA SM70 path rather than the
       local recurrent fallback. The 4k target is now directionally achieved:
       the path moved from slower-than-FLA (`+30%` latency) to faster-than-FLA
       (`-16%` latency, `+19%` tok/s).
     - Remaining speed work should target default enablement safety, direct
       write into `core_attn_out`, and an SM70-compatible replacement for the
       currently disabled `auto_cp` path, rather than spending more time on the
       old fallback.

339. Original FlashQLA SM70 operator integration follow-up: direct output and
     external metadata (2026-06-12).

     Scope:
     - No end-to-end model run was performed for this checkpoint. The changes
       are operator/wrapper-level integration cleanup only; their measured
       4k gains are too small to justify another 35B load by themselves.

     Implemented:
     - `fused_gdr_fwd(...)` now accepts an optional `output` tensor and writes
       the original TileLang fused output directly into that buffer when
       provided. vLLM's original FlashQLA SM70 path now passes its preallocated
       `core_attn_out` candidate into the helper.
     - `fused_gdr_fwd(...)` now accepts optional `chunk_offsets`; the original
       SM70 helper forwards vLLM metadata instead of always recomputing offsets
       internally.
     - `chunk_local_cumsum(...)` and `kkt_solve(...)` now accept optional
       `chunk_indices`; the original SM70 helper forwards the vLLM-precomputed
       indices to both existing TileLang sub-kernels.
     - The GDN metadata builder now prepares FLA-style `chunk_indices` and
       `chunk_offsets` for `flashqla_sm70` only when
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL=1` or
       `FLASH_QLA_SM70_USE_ORIGINAL_TILELANG=1`, preserving the old SM70
       fallback behavior.

     Operator artifacts:
     - Direct output only:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_gdn_prefill_original_tilelang_sm70_direct_output_4k16k_20260612.json`.
     - vLLM wrapper direct `core_attn_out`:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_vllm_flashqla_original_direct_coreout_4k16k_20260612.json`.
     - External `chunk_offsets`:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_flashqla_original_directout_external_offsets_4k16k_20260612.json`.
     - External `chunk_indices` + `chunk_offsets` + direct output:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_flashqla_original_directout_external_indices_offsets_4k16k_20260612.json`.
     - vLLM wrapper with external metadata + direct `core_attn_out`:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_vllm_flashqla_original_external_metadata_coreout_4k16k_20260612.json`.

     Quality:
     - Direct output vs internally allocated output: 4k/16k output max diff
       `0.0`, state max diff `0.0`; returned output pointer matches the
       supplied buffer.
     - External `chunk_offsets` vs internal offsets: 4k/16k output max diff
       `0.0`, state max diff `0.0`.
     - External `chunk_indices` + `chunk_offsets` vs internal metadata:
       4k/16k output max diff `0.0`, state max diff `0.0`.
     - vLLM wrapper external metadata + direct `core_attn_out` vs old wrapper
       internal metadata: 4k/16k output max diff `0.0`, state max diff `0.0`.

     Timing observations:
     - 4k is noise-level for these metadata/output cleanup changes:
       external metadata + direct output measured about `1.025 ms`, essentially
       identical to internal prepare.
     - 16k shows a small operator-level win:
       internal prepare `3.646 ms`, external `chunk_indices` + `chunk_offsets`
       `3.446 ms`, external metadata + direct output `3.425 ms`.
     - vLLM wrapper-level 16k also improved in the same direction:
       internal metadata `3.784 ms`, external metadata + direct `core_attn_out`
       `3.578 ms`.

     Remaining operator work:
     - The next meaningful operator-level target is indexed state-cache
       read/write: let the original TileLang fused forward accept
       `state_indices` and `has_initial_state` so vLLM can avoid
       `ssm_state[indices].contiguous()` gather and the Python-side final-state
       writeback.
     - `auto_cp` is still not down-ported to SM70.

340. Original FlashQLA SM70 indexed state-cache and auto_cp checkpoint
     (2026-06-12).

     Scope:
     - Operator-level work only. No model end-to-end run was performed for
       this checkpoint.

     Indexed state-cache implementation:
     - `chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_sm70_tilelang(...)` now accepts
       `state_indices`, `has_initial_state`, and `inplace_final_state`.
     - The SM70 original TileLang fused forward can read initial state directly
       from a vLLM state cache and write final state back into the indexed
       cache slot. This removes the wrapper-side
       `ssm_state[indices].contiguous()` gather and final-state scatter for
       original FlashQLA prefill.
     - vLLM's `flashqla_sm70` original prefill path now uses the indexed
       state-cache mode when `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL=1` and the
       non-spec state index tensor is 1D. Other paths keep the previous
       gather/writeback behavior.
     - The SM70 original fused forward now pins `block_DV=32`. A 2-sequence
       smoke exposed that the old selector could choose `block_DV=64`, which
       asks V100 for `106496` bytes of dynamic shared memory and fails before
       launch.

     Indexed state-cache validation:
     - 2-sequence smoke, with one sequence using existing state and one using
       `has_initial_state=False`: output max diff `0.0`, selected state-cache
       slot max diff `0.0`, untouched slot max diff `0.0` versus the old
       gather/writeback path.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_flashqla_original_indexed_state_4k16k_20260612.json`.
     - 4k direct-output wrapper timing: gather/writeback `1.110 ms`, indexed
       state-cache `1.097 ms`, speedup `1.012x`; output/state max diff `0.0`.
     - 16k direct-output wrapper timing: gather/writeback `4.114 ms`, indexed
       state-cache `3.740 ms`, speedup `1.100x`; output/state max diff `0.0`.
     - Interpretation: indexed state-cache is correct and worth keeping, but
       it is not the main 4k bottleneck by itself. Its measurable win appears
       at longer prefill shapes and by removing copy/allocation noise.

     SM70 auto_cp findings:
     - The upstream `cp_context` is not just hidden behind an SM90 guard. Its
       `fused_gdr_h`/`prepare_h` helper uses Hopper-only TileLang constructs
       (`Barrier`, `tl_shuffle_elect`, `fence_barrier_init`,
       `warpgroup_reg_alloc/dealloc`). Direct SM70 compilation fails even after
       fixing SM70 GEMM lowering issues such as `transpose_A=True`.
     - A conservative SM70 auto_cp minimum path was added for operator testing:
       it reuses upstream `_calc_cp_seqs` and `get_warmup_chunks`; if any
       segment/head requires correction fallback, it disables CP. Only when
       fallback is fully false does it build CP initial states with the SM70
       original fused forward and then run the main CP fused forward.
     - The SM70 minimum auto_cp path explicitly rejects indexed state-cache
       mode for now because CP splitting changes the request/state mapping.

     SM70 auto_cp validation:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/op_flashqla_original_sm70_autocp_min_4k16k_20260612.json`.
     - Strong-decay input, 4k: no-CP `1.110 ms`, minimum auto_cp `1.941 ms`,
       speedup `0.572x`; output/state max diff `0.0`.
     - Strong-decay input, 16k: no-CP `4.170 ms`, minimum auto_cp `6.206 ms`,
       speedup `0.672x`; output/state max diff `0.0`.
     - Decision: do not default-enable this minimum auto_cp path. It proves the
       CP replay plumbing can be made quality-safe on SM70, but without a true
       SM70 replacement for upstream `prepare_h`/correction it performs an
       extra large fused pass and loses time.

     Next QLA operator target:
     - Build a real SM70-compatible `prepare_h`/correction helper on the
       upstream QLA algorithm, replacing Hopper barriers/warpgroup register
       control with SM70-safe synchronization and GEMM layouts. That is the
       missing piece before auto_cp can become a speed feature rather than a
       functional smoke path.

341. Original FlashQLA SM70 default baseline and o256 model speed checkpoint
     (2026-06-12).

     Default change:
     - `flashqla_sm70` now uses the original TileLang prefill path by default
       on SM70. `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL` and the legacy
       `FLASH_QLA_SM70_USE_ORIGINAL_TILELANG` remain as override knobs; unset
       means enabled, while `0/false/no/off` disables it.
     - The GDN metadata builder uses the same default so vLLM prepares
       `chunk_indices`/`chunk_offsets` for the original TileLang path without
       requiring the user to export the old opt-in env.
     - The indexed state-cache read/write path from item 340 is included in
       the default original TileLang path when the state index tensor is 1D.
     - The minimum SM70 `auto_cp` path remains default-off because item 340
       measured it as slower despite exact output/state equality.

     Validation before model benchmarks:
     - `python -m py_compile
       vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/gdn/qwen_gdn_linear_attn.py
       vllm/v1/attention/backends/gdn_attn.py` passed under
       `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210/bin/python`.

     Benchmark setup:
     - GPUs: physical `0,1`, TP2.
     - Script:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`.
     - Case file:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/qla_prefill_decode_cases_4k_16k_64k_o256_20260612.json`.
     - Cases: `input_len=4096/16384/65536`, `output_len=256`.
     - Common args: `dtype=half`, `quantization=awq`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `max_model_len=66000`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, `warmup=1`, `repeat=1`,
       greedy sampling with `ignore_eos=True`.
     - The command did not export `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL`; both
       artifacts record that this env is absent. The SM70 Flash-V100 baseline
       envs such as compile graph, GDN decode FlashQLA, and schedule gates were
       auto-set by config after selecting the Flash-V100 baseline.

     27B-AWQ artifact:
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_original_default_indexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`
     with log
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_original_default_indexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.log`.

     27B-AWQ results:
     - 4k: prefill `2.295529 s` / `1784.3 tok/s`; steady decode
       `5.250600 s` for 255 tokens / `48.6 tok/s`.
     - 16k: prefill `11.667532 s` / `1404.2 tok/s`; steady decode
       `5.749169 s` for 255 tokens / `44.4 tok/s`.
     - 64k chunk16k: prefill `82.364193 s` / `795.7 tok/s`; steady decode
       `8.214687 s` for 255 tokens / `31.0 tok/s`.

     35B-A3B-AWQ artifact:
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_original_default_indexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`
     with log
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_original_default_indexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.log`.

     35B-A3B-AWQ results:
     - 4k: prefill `0.480928 s` / `8516.9 tok/s`; steady decode
       `2.508509 s` for 255 tokens / `101.7 tok/s`.
     - 16k: prefill `2.375182 s` / `6898.0 tok/s`; steady decode
       `2.614911 s` for 255 tokens / `97.5 tok/s`.
     - 64k chunk16k: prefill `24.090609 s` / `2720.4 tok/s`; steady decode
       `3.500164 s` for 255 tokens / `72.9 tok/s`.

     Interpretation:
     - The optimized original FlashQLA SM70 path is now usable as the default
       prefill baseline without user-supplied QLA env flags.
     - 35B-A3B-AWQ keeps the expected sparse-MoE decode advantage over dense
       27B-AWQ at all three contexts. The long-context decode drop is visible
       for both models and is most pronounced at 64k because the same 255
       decode tokens run with a much larger KV/state footprint.
     - These are speed artifacts only. They do not replace the separate
       long-output quality gate for compile graph / decode-token equality.

343. Flash-V100 long-context bottleneck assessment checkpoint (2026-06-12).

     Question:
     - Whether the current FlashAttention-V100 path has already captured the
       main optimization wins, and where to attack the remaining long-context
       prefill/decode degradation.

     Current default route status:
     - The normal SM70 default already selects `FLASH_ATTN_V100` without an
       explicit `attention_backend` argument. The plain-default probe in item
       342 logged:
       `Using FLASH_ATTN_V100 attention backend out of potential backends`.
     - Prefill is on Flash-V100, not Triton fallback:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active (no prefix/chunked context)` for
       the first chunk and
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 prefill path active (prefix/chunked via direct paged
       prefill kernel)` for chunked/prefix prefill.
     - Decode is on scalar paged Flash-V100:
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 decode path active (scalar paged KV kernel,
       CUDA-graph safe, classified Type-B for long q=1 decode)`.
     - Therefore the observed long-context slowdown is not caused by silently
       missing the Flash-V100 route.

     Model structure:
     - 35B-A3B-AWQ text config has `40` layers: `10` full-attention layers and
       `30` linear-attention/GDN layers, with `16` query heads, `2` KV heads,
       and `head_dim=256`.
     - 27B-AWQ text config has `64` layers: `16` full-attention layers and
       `48` linear-attention/GDN layers, with `24` query heads, `4` KV heads,
       and `head_dim=256`.
     - The 3:1 linear-attention/full-attention pattern means short-context
       performance is not dominated by FlashAttention, but long-context
       prefill/decode eventually is because full attention scales as
       `O(L^2)` in prefill and `O(L)` per decode token.

     Existing profile evidence:
     - 4k prefill QLA/FLA nsys:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/nsys_prefill_qla_vs_fla_category_delta_20260611.json`.
       FlashAttention forward was about `65.8 ms` for the 35B attention
       layers in the captured 4k prefill, while the whole prefill trace was
       about `541.6 ms` for the QLA run. At 4k, attention is only around
       `12%` of the prefill GPU work.
     - 4k decode nsys:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/nsys_kernel_category_summary_latest_vs_003_i4096_o128_20260610.json`.
       The captured repeat included prefill plus `127` steady decode tokens.
       Attention decode was `141.36 ms`, about `3.4%` of total GPU kernel
       time and about `1.11 ms/token`. Dense/AWQ GEMM was about `52.1%`,
       MoE routing/activation `13.0%`, GDN/FLA `8.7%`, and communication
       `5.7%`.
     - Corrected default 35B speed from item 342:
       4k prefill `9656.7 tok/s`, 16k prefill `7159.8 tok/s`, 64k prefill
       `2803.9 tok/s`; decode `101.5`, `97.5`, and `72.9 tok/s`
       respectively. Decode TPOT grew from about `9.85 ms` at 4k to
       `13.72 ms` at 64k.

     Interpretation:
     - At 4k, further FlashAttention work cannot be the largest end-to-end
       speed lever because attention is a small share of total time.
     - At 64k, prefill degradation is expected to be dominated by the
       full-attention `O(L^2)` term. A rough extrapolation from the 4k
       FlashAttention timing puts the 64k full-attention portion into the
       large-majority range of the observed `23.37 s` prefill time. Exact
       attribution still needs a 64k nsys/ncu capture.
     - At 64k decode, the extra `~3.9 ms/token` vs 4k is consistent with
       context-length-dependent KV reads in the paged Flash decode path.
       This is the long-context decode lever; the fixed per-token overheads
       were already shown in earlier context-scale probes to be mostly
       context-independent.
     - The current scalar paged decode kernel is already the fast production
       family. Prior quality work classified it as Type-B bounded arithmetic
       difference versus strict dense/WMMA references. Partition size changes
       were not a quality fix, and the strict partitioned-WMMA route was
       `~2.8x-4.0x` slower than scalar decode before adding the final reduce.

     Practical next optimization directions:
     - First run a 64k-focused nsys/ncu capture for the corrected default
       route, with `output_len=1` for prefill attribution and a short decode
       window for per-token attribution. This should answer whether 64k is
       attention-memory-bound, launch/metadata-bound, or still mixed with
       GDN/MoE/communication.
     - Decode direction 1: sweep
       `VLLM_FLASH_V100_DECODE_PARTITION_SIZE=256/512/1024` at 64k in one
       resident model process, recording TPOT and token equality. The current
       default picks `512` past the `20480` threshold; no final 64k model-level
       speed sweep is recorded for `1024`.
     - Decode direction 2: test FP8 KV / TurboQuant KV Flash-V100 decode for
       64k. This attacks the real long-context decode cost, KV bandwidth, but
       must pass a separate long-output quality gate before defaulting.
     - Prefill direction 1: profile direct paged prefill for chunked/prefix
       chunks. If block-table/paged indirection is visible, build a
       single-request contiguous-KV prefill path or larger-chunk policy so
       long single prompts do not pay unnecessary paged-cache overhead.
     - Prefill direction 2: sweep `max_num_batched_tokens` / chunk size for
       16k/32k/64k single-request prefill. This changes how much work goes
       through dense no-prefix Flash vs direct paged-prefix Flash and may
       recover constant factors without changing model semantics.
     - Algorithmic directions such as sliding-window/sparse attention/prefix
       compression can reduce asymptotic cost, but they are model-behavior
       changes unless the model explicitly supports them. They should not be
       treated as a safe default speed fix.

     Current answer:
     - Flash-V100 has captured the obvious route-level wins: Flash prefill,
       direct paged prefill for chunked/prefix, scalar paged decode, SM70
       default selection, and CUDA-graph-safe decode.
     - It is not "done": the two remaining high-value areas are 64k decode
       KV-bandwidth reduction and 64k prefill direct-paged/contiguous-prefix
       efficiency. Rewriting the whole FlashAttention path is not the next
       pragmatic step; profiling and targeted long-context kernels are.

342. Original FlashQLA SM70 indexed integration regression and corrected
     default speed baseline (2026-06-12).

     Why this checkpoint exists:
     - Item 341 measured the then-current default with indexed state-cache
       prefill enabled. The user correctly flagged that the 35B 4k prefill
       result, about `8.5k tok/s`, did not match the earlier original QLA
       result near `9.6k tok/s`.
     - The problem was not that QLA was disabled. Runtime logs in the slow run
       showed the original FlashQLA-SM70 TileLang path was active, but with
       `indexed_state=True, direct_output=True`.

     Root cause and decision:
     - Standalone operator timing with a large vLLM-like state cache still
       favored indexed state-cache slightly:
       `op_flashqla_original_indexed_state_largecache_4k_slot0_20260612.json`
       measured gather/writeback `1.086 ms` vs indexed `1.057 ms`, and
       `op_flashqla_original_indexed_state_largecache_4k_slot777_20260612.json`
       measured gather/writeback `1.092 ms` vs indexed `1.063 ms`, with
       output/state max diff `0.0`.
     - Model-level timing contradicted the standalone microbenchmark: the
       integrated indexed route regressed 35B 4k prefill from the expected
       `~9.6k tok/s` range to `~8.5k tok/s`. Extra indexed tensor plumbing,
       graph/dispatcher specialization, or launch argument overhead is eating
       the standalone gain at the model level.
     - Indexed state-cache remains a diagnostic/opt-in path through
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_INDEXED_PREFILL=1`. The production default is now
       original FlashQLA-SM70 TileLang + wrapper gather/writeback +
       direct `core_attn_out` output.

     35B focused A/B evidence:
     - Slow default-indexed artifact:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k_o1_o256_qla_original_default_probe_w1_r2_20260612.json`
       logged `indexed_state=True, direct_output=True`; 4k o1 prefill was
       `0.481285 s`, or `8510.6 tok/s`.
     - Explicit original TileLang env did not change the result, proving the
       issue was not route enablement:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k_o1_o256_qla_original_explicit_probe_w1_r2_20260612.json`
       measured `8501.7 tok/s`.
     - Fixed no-indexed probe:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k_o1_o256_qla_noindexed_direct_w1_r2_20260612.json`
       logged `indexed_state=False, direct_output=True`; 4k o1 prefill was
       `0.424585 s`, or `9647 tok/s`.
     - Final no-env default verification:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k_o1_o256_qla_default_noindexed_direct_w1_r2_20260612.json`
       logged `indexed_state=False, direct_output=True`; 4k o1 prefill was
       `0.425250 s`, or `9632 tok/s`, and o256 decode was about
       `101.8 tok/s`.

     Current final no-env baseline setup:
     - GPUs: physical `0,1`, TP2.
     - Script:
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_decode.py`.
     - Case file:
       `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/qla_prefill_decode_cases_4k_16k_64k_o256_20260612.json`.
     - Common args: `dtype=half`, `quantization=awq`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `max_model_len=66000`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, `warmup=1`, `repeat=1`,
       greedy sampling with `ignore_eos=True`.
     - No QLA-specific env was exported. The artifacts record no
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL` and no
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_INDEXED_PREFILL`.

     35B-A3B-AWQ corrected default artifact:
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_default_noindexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`
     with log
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_default_noindexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.log`.

     35B-A3B-AWQ corrected default results:
     - 4k: prefill `0.424162 s` / `9656.7 tok/s`; steady decode
       `2.511600 s` for 255 tokens / `101.5 tok/s`.
     - 16k: prefill `2.288333 s` / `7159.8 tok/s`; steady decode
       `2.616180 s` for 255 tokens / `97.5 tok/s`.
     - 64k chunk16k: prefill `23.372932 s` / `2803.9 tok/s`; steady decode
       `3.499166 s` for 255 tokens / `72.9 tok/s`.

     27B-AWQ corrected default artifact:
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_default_noindexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.json`
     with log
     `bench_results/speed_baseline_20260610/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_tp2_ctx4k16k64k_o256_qla_default_noindexed_mem088_w1_r1_20260612.log`.

     27B-AWQ corrected default results:
     - 4k: prefill `2.271101 s` / `1803.5 tok/s`; steady decode
       `5.331083 s` for 255 tokens / `47.8 tok/s`.
     - 16k: prefill `11.470848 s` / `1428.3 tok/s`; steady decode
       `5.800906 s` for 255 tokens / `44.0 tok/s`.
     - 64k chunk16k: prefill `83.054073 s` / `789.1 tok/s`; steady decode
       `8.488283 s` for 255 tokens / `30.0 tok/s`.

     Comparison to item 341:
     - 35B 4k prefill recovered from `8516.9 tok/s` to `9656.7 tok/s`.
     - 35B 16k prefill improved from `6898.0 tok/s` to `7159.8 tok/s`.
     - 35B 64k prefill improved from `2720.4 tok/s` to `2803.9 tok/s`.
     - 27B changed only slightly: 4k `1784.3 -> 1803.5 tok/s`, 16k
       `1404.2 -> 1428.3 tok/s`, 64k `795.7 -> 789.1 tok/s`.
     - Decode speed is effectively unchanged for 35B and slightly lower in
       this single-repeat 27B run. The indexed toggle only targets prefill
       state-cache plumbing, not decode.

     Notes:
     - A separate plain-default probe removed the explicit
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100` argument and unset the QLA/attention
       env knobs before startup:
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_i4096_o1_plain_default_no_backend_no_qla_env_20260612.json`
       with log
       `decode_latest_qwen36_35a3b_awq_tp2_i4096_o1_plain_default_no_backend_no_qla_env_20260612.log`.
       Its engine kwargs contain no `attention_backend`, and the recorded env
       contains no `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_ATTN_V100`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_ORIGINAL_PREFILL`,
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_INDEXED_PREFILL`, or
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASHQLA_DIRECT_OUTPUT`.
     - That plain-default probe still logged
       `Using FLASH_ATTN_V100 attention backend out of potential backends`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`, and
       `Using original FlashQLA-SM70 TileLang GDN prefill path
       (indexed_state=False, direct_output=True)`. Its 4k o1 prefill was
       `0.424898 s`, or `9639.97 tok/s`. Therefore the corrected speed path is
       now the normal SM70 default, not a benchmark-only env/argument recipe.
     - The 27B run emitted one `_fused_post_conv_kernel` JIT warning during
       the first inference warmup. The measured repeats completed after that
       warning, but a future benchmark harness should warm this shape before
       starting request metrics.
     - These are speed artifacts only. They do not replace the separate
       long-output quality gate for compile graph / decode-token equality.

344. 2026-06-13 SM70 Marlin replacement op integration checkpoint.

     Scope:
     - Keep the original vLLM Marlin Python/model paths intact. On an SM70-only
       build, the original `marlin_gemm` and `moe_wna16_marlin_gemm` op names
       are registered by the SM70 Marlin implementation instead of the stock
       SM75+ implementation.
     - Add SM70 dense and MoE Marlin sources under the existing Marlin source
       directories and wire them in CMake only when CUDA arch `7.0` is present
       and no non-SM70 Marlin arch is being built. Mixed arch builds skip the
       SM70 replacement to avoid double-registering the same torch op names.
     - Python changes are limited to capability gates: Marlin linear,
       AWQMarlin, Marlin MoE experts, and Marlin support checks now accept
       SM70. No new model-level Marlin branch was added.

     Metadata compatibility:
     - The SM70 dispatch adapters consume the original vLLM Marlin metadata:
       `marlin_permute_scales` scales and packed int32 zero-points.
     - Dense and MoE dispatch now convert fp16 scales back to the logical
       SM70-kernel layout internally and unpack packed zero-points to fp16
       zero-point offsets using the same scale-perm plus AWQ/Marlin interleave
       order. This avoids adding Python-side layout conversion.
     - `use_fp32_reduce` is accepted in the replacement op signature for
       original wrapper compatibility. The current SM70 kernel path does not
       branch on it.

     Validation:
     - Build command:
       `CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-12.8 TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=7.0
       /home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210/bin/python setup.py
       build_ext --inplace`.
     - CMake logged `Building SM70 Marlin kernels for archs: 7.0` and
       `Building SM70 Marlin MOE kernels for archs: 7.0`; stock Marlin and
       Marlin MoE logged no compatible archs for this SM70-only build.
     - CUDA `_C.abi3.so` and `_moe_C.abi3.so` built and copied. Optional
       Rust `vllm-rs` still failed because the environment lacks `protoc`;
       this is unrelated to the Marlin CUDA op replacement.
     - Dense AWQ direct op with original vLLM inputs
       (`awq_marlin_repack` weight, `marlin_permute_scales` scales, packed
       `marlin_zero_points`) passed on Tesla PG503-216 SM70:
       `max_abs=0.03125`, `mean_rel=0.00033247216290420987`.
     - Real AWQMarlinMoE synthetic path using
       `awq_marlin_moe_repack`, `marlin_moe_permute_scales`, and
       `moe_awq_to_marlin_zero_points` passed fused MoE against the torch
       reference: `max_abs=6.103515625e-05`,
       `mean_abs=1.1086463928222656e-05`.

     Pitfall recorded:
     - A separate old-style `MarlinMoEWeightData.make(..., uint4)` helper path
       produced `max_abs=0.08331298828125` for the fused MoE comparison and
       about `1.0` for dense-per-expert checks. That helper uses a different
       Marlin test weight layout, not the real AWQMarlinMoE postprocess path.
       Do not use it to judge the AWQMarlinMoE SM70 replacement quality.

345. 2026-06-13 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ SM70 Marlin vs TurboMind model-speed
     checkpoint.

     Scope:
     - Same latest-tree vLLM, same Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ model, TP2 on GPUs
       `0,1`, `input_len=1024`, `output_len=256`, greedy, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, `disable_custom_all_reduce=True`.
     - Both runs used the current SM70 Flash-V100 baseline auto policy. Logs
       auto-set the compile-graph baseline knobs, including
       `VLLM_SM70_FLASH_V100_0DOT3_COMPILE_GRAPH=1`,
       `VLLM_USE_AOT_COMPILE=1`, and `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0`.

     Artifacts:
     - TurboMind baseline:
       `bench_results/marlin_sm70_compare_20260613/qwen36_35b_awq_turbomind_i1024_o256_tp2_default_20260613.json`.
       It used `--quantization awq` with `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=1`.
       Route logs confirmed `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`.
     - SM70 Marlin replacement:
       `bench_results/marlin_sm70_compare_20260613/qwen36_35b_awq_marlin_i1024_o256_tp2_default_20260613.json`.
       It used `--quantization awq_marlin` with
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_TURBOMIND=0`. Route logs confirmed
       `Using awq_marlin kernel` and `Using 'MARLIN' WNA16 MoE backend`.

     Results:
     - TurboMind: `prefill_time=0.13991382300082478 s`, equivalent to
       `7318.790795917024 tok/s`; `steady_decode_tps=97.98921063545995`;
       `decode_time=2.602327321001212 s`.
     - SM70 Marlin: `prefill_time=0.14928439100185642 s`, equivalent to
       `6859.390945884396 tok/s`; `steady_decode_tps=53.602030308360725`;
       `decode_time=4.757282485999895 s`.
     - Marlin/TurboMind speed ratio: prefill `0.9372x` (about `6.28%`
       slower), steady decode `0.5470x` (about `45.30%` slower).
     - Marlin model-load memory was lower in logs (`10.78 GiB`) than the
       TurboMind route (`16.22 GiB`), and KV cache capacity was correspondingly
       higher. This is a memory benefit, not a throughput win.

     Quality note:
     - This checkpoint is a speed comparison only. The repeated output token
       hashes differ between TurboMind and Marlin, and Marlin warmup/repeat
       hashes also differed in this single run, so this does not qualify as a
       model-quality pass for the SM70 Marlin route.

346. 2026-06-13 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP2 TurboMind custom all-reduce
     correction.

     Scope:
     - Same latest-tree vLLM and same benchmark shape as item 345 TurboMind:
       Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on GPUs `0,1`, `input_len=1024`,
       `output_len=256`, greedy, `max_model_len=4096`,
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`, Flash-V100 compile/FULL graph policy,
       and AWQ TurboMind route.
     - The item 345 TurboMind run intentionally recorded
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=True`, so hidden-state custom all-reduce was
       disabled. This correction removes that flag and verifies the normal
       production TP communication route.

     Artifact:
     - `bench_results/marlin_sm70_compare_20260613/qwen36_35b_awq_turbomind_i1024_o256_tp2_custom_ar_20260613.json`.

     Route evidence:
     - Engine kwargs record `disable_custom_all_reduce=false`.
     - Runtime logs selected `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for group `tp:0`.
     - The JSON communication policy records
       `custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=true` and
       `hidden_state_custom_allreduce_effective=true`.
     - The AWQ route still hit `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`,
       per-expert W13/W2 dispatch, and legacy single-token compact during
       graph capture.

     Results:
     - Custom all-reduce off, item 345 TurboMind:
       `prefill_time=0.13991382300082478 s`,
       `prefill_tps=7318.790795917024`,
       `steady_decode_tps=97.98921063545995`,
       `output_tps=93.27327924504792`.
     - Custom all-reduce on, this correction:
       `prefill_time=0.13739813899883302 s`,
       `prefill_tps=7452.793811193449`,
       `steady_decode_tps=109.79757714802676`,
       `output_tps=103.96830866281312`.
     - Steady decode gain from enabling production custom all-reduce:
       `+12.05068031060721%`.

     Baseline interpretation:
     - The `97.99 tok/s` number from item 345 is not the normal TP2
       production baseline because it disabled custom all-reduce.
     - For this exact TP2/no-MTP/1024x256 TurboMind口径, the current corrected
       decode baseline is `109.80 tok/s` steady decode, not `97.99 tok/s`.

347. 2026-06-13 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ TP2 TurboMind 4k prefill correction.

     Scope:
     - Latest-tree vLLM, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on GPUs `0,1`,
       `input_len=4096`, `output_len=256`, greedy,
       `max_model_len=66000`, `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, AWQ TurboMind, and production
       custom all-reduce enabled.
     - This reruns the 4k shape after item 346 clarified that item 345's
       `97.99 tok/s` decode number disabled custom all-reduce. The question
       was whether the earlier `~9.6k tok/s` 4k prefill speed had been lost
       or whether the newer `~7.45k tok/s` 1k prefill number was only a
       different input-length口径.

     Artifact:
     - `bench_results/marlin_sm70_compare_20260613/qwen36_35b_awq_turbomind_i4096_o256_tp2_custom_ar_20260613.json`.

     Route evidence:
     - Engine kwargs record `disable_custom_all_reduce=false`.
     - Runtime logs selected `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for group `tp:0`, and
       the JSON communication policy records
       `custom_all_reduce_enabled_effective=true` plus
       `hidden_state_custom_allreduce_effective=true`.
     - Baseline auto policy filled in the SM70 gates:
       GDN schedules, FlashQLA decode, Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph,
       in-memory AOT compile, and `VLLM_SM70_LM_HEAD_TOP1=0`.
