     - Measured-stage logs selected
       `Using original FlashQLA-SM70 TileLang GDN prefill path
       (indexed_state=False, direct_output=True)`, matching the corrected
       no-indexed direct-output baseline from item 342.
     - AWQ route evidence still shows `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path
       enabled`, per-expert W13/W2 dispatch, and legacy single-token compact.

     Results:
     - `prefill_time=0.4225840569997672 s`, equivalent to
       `9692.74617002945 tok/s` for 4096 prompt tokens.
     - `first_token_latency=0.424999475479126 s`.
     - `decode_time=2.294826388002548 s`.
     - `steady_decode_tps=111.11951707246835`.
     - `output_tps=94.11713308240543`.

     Interpretation:
     - The 4k prefill speed has not regressed; it is back in the historical
       `~9.6k tok/s` band from item 342.
     - The item 346 1k prefill number (`7452.793811193449 tok/s`) is a
       different input-length口径 and should not be used as evidence that
       FlashQLA/default prefill acceleration disappeared.
     - Production baseline measurements should keep custom all-reduce enabled
       by default. Do not use `--disable-custom-all-reduce` for normal speed
       baselines unless the test is explicitly isolating communication.

348. 2026-06-13 FP8 KV cache latest-tree route check for AWQ and FP8
     checkpoints.

     Scope:
     - Latest-tree vLLM, GPUs `0,1`, TP2, Flash-V100 attention, production
       custom all-reduce enabled, `input_len=4096`, `output_len=256`, greedy,
       `max_model_len=66000`, `max_num_batched_tokens=16384`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, and `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`.
     - This item checks whether the old 0.0.3 FP8 KV cache work still runs on
       the current tree for AWQ and FP8 checkpoints, and whether it materially
       improves prefill or steady decode at the current 4k/256 baseline shape.

     AWQ artifact and route evidence:
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_35b_awq_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_20260613.json`.
     - Verifier:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/verify_qwen36_35b_awq_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_20260613.json`.
     - The verifier passed
       `--require-full-flash-v100 --require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route
       --require-decode-timing --require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route`.
     - Runtime logs prove `SM70 FP8 KV cache C++ reshape_and_cache_flash path
       enabled (kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2)` and
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache decode path active
       (kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2, route=scalar_paged)`.
     - Custom all-reduce remained on: engine kwargs record
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=false` and logs selected
       `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for `tp:0`.

     AWQ speed and capacity result:
     - Auto-KV 35B-AWQ baseline from item 347:
       `prefill_time=0.4225840569997672 s`,
       `prefill_tps=9692.74617002945`,
       `steady_decode_tps=111.11951707246835`.
     - FP8-KV 35B-AWQ:
       `prefill_time=0.42376033999971696 s`,
       `prefill_tps=9665.840837568067`,
       `steady_decode_tps=109.2139627405046`.
     - Delta at this 4k/256 shape: prefill TPS `-0.27758213521127395%`,
       steady decode TPS `-1.714869162652155%`.
     - KV capacity improved from the auto-KV baseline log's
       `874,000 tokens` to `1,659,428 tokens`, about `1.90x`.

     FP8 checkpoint artifact and route evidence:
     - 27B-FP8 with explicit FP8 KV artifact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_27b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_20260613.json`.
     - 27B-FP8 auto-KV comparison artifact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_27b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_autokv_20260613.json`.
     - Verifier:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/verify_qwen36_27b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_20260613.json`.
     - The verifier passed the same Flash-V100, FP8-KV, decode-timing, and
       custom-allreduce gates as the AWQ run.
     - Runtime logs prove explicit FP8 KV is accepted with an FP8 checkpoint on
       SM70 Flash-V100, the dense route hit
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`, and FP8 KV decode used
       `route=scalar_paged`.

     FP8 checkpoint speed and capacity result:
     - 27B-FP8 auto-KV:
       `prefill_time=2.265114502999495 s`,
       `prefill_tps=1808.297106430039`,
       `steady_decode_tps=42.580777288712525`.
     - 27B-FP8 explicit FP8 KV:
       `prefill_time=2.282537292001507 s`,
       `prefill_tps=1794.4942300149433`,
       `steady_decode_tps=42.58462598570752`.
     - Delta at this 4k/256 shape: prefill TPS `-0.7633079671059528%`,
       steady decode TPS `+0.00903857853251644%`.
     - KV capacity improved from `339,750 tokens` to `649,956 tokens`, about
       `1.91x`.

     35B-FP8 limitation under the current TP2/GPU0-1 constraint:
     - The default 35B-FP8 auto-KV load failed before KV-cache benchmarking:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_35b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_autokv_20260613.log`.
       The failure is the SM70 FP8 MoE 0.0.3 dense-dequant fallback path
       running out of memory while dequantizing expert weights
       (`torch.OutOfMemoryError: Tried to allocate 512.00 MiB` with about
       `31.54 GiB` already in use on each V100).
     - Forcing the native FP8 path with explicit FP8 KV failed config
       validation:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_35b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_native_fp8e5m2kv_20260613.log`
       reports `The quantization method fp8 is not supported for the current
       GPU. Minimum capability: 75. Current capability: 70.`
     - This is a 35B-FP8 weight/MoE route problem on TP2, not evidence that
       the FP8 KV cache route itself is broken. A same-criterion 35B-FP8 FP8
       KV speed result still requires either TP4 availability or a memory-safe
       SM70 FP8 MoE/weight route.

     Interpretation:
     - FP8 KV cache is usable on the latest tree for the tested AWQ and 27B-FP8
       routes. The route gate is not the current blocker.
     - At 4k input / 256 output, FP8 KV does not produce a throughput win; the
       measured benefit is KV-cache capacity, roughly `1.9x`.
     - Do not default FP8 KV for speed based on this shape. The next useful
       speed question is long-context decode/prefill where KV bandwidth or
       cache capacity dominates, such as 16k/64k prompts or high concurrency.
     - This checkpoint is not a model-quality pass. The 27B-FP8 explicit-FP8-KV
       token hash differs from the auto-KV comparison hash, so token/logit
       equality still needs a dedicated quality gate before making FP8 KV a
       production default.

349. 2026-06-13 35B-FP8 TP2 FP8 KV cache route repaired with native SM70
     FP8 MoE default selection.

     Root cause:
     - Item 348's 35B-FP8 TP2 failure was not caused by the FP8 KV cache
       route. The default 35B-FP8 policy selected the old 0.0.3 fp16 expert
       dequant MoE fallback, which OOMed on TP2 before KV-cache benchmarking.
     - Forcing the native path in the first probe also set
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=0`, which disabled the dense SM70 FP8
       TurboMind allowance and made the global `fp8` capability check reject
       V100 as `sm70 < sm75`.
     - The working split is: keep dense FP8 TurboMind enabled via
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=1`, but disable only the MoE fp16
       dequant fallback so the existing native `Fp8SM70MoEMethod` handles the
       experts without materializing fp16 expert weights.

     Code change:
     - `vllm/config/vllm.py` now auto-sets
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=0` only when all of these are true:
       SM70 CUDA, `quantization=fp8`, MoE model, TP size `<=2`, explicit
       FP8 KV cache dtype, dense FP8 dequant/TurboMind support remains enabled,
       and the user did not explicitly set
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK`.
     - This preserves the previous TP4/auto-KV 0.0.3 fallback baseline while
       making the TP2 35B-FP8 FP8-KV route start without extra user envs.

     Validation:
     - Static check passed:
       `/home/ymzx/miniconda3/envs/vllm-0.0.5-t210/bin/python -m py_compile
       vllm/config/vllm.py`.
     - Manual control artifact, with only
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=0` added:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_35b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_native_moe_20260613.json`.
     - Default artifact, with no manual MoE fallback env:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/qwen36_35b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_default_native_moe_20260613.json`.
     - Verifier:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/verify_qwen36_35b_fp8_i4096_o256_tp2_fp8e5m2kv_default_native_moe_20260613.json`.
     - The verifier passed
       `--require-full-flash-v100 --require-sm70-fp8-kv-cache-route
       --require-decode-timing --require-sm70-custom-allreduce-route
       --require-fp8-moe-safe-route`.

     Route evidence from the default run:
     - Config log:
       `Auto-setting VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_DEQUANT_FALLBACK=0 for SM70 FP8 MoE
       with explicit FP8 KV cache on TP<=2`.
     - Dense and MoE route logs:
       `SM70 FP8 TurboMind W8A16 dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind per-expert dense path enabled`,
       `SM70 FP8 MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path enabled`, and
       `SM70 FP8 MoE single-token active-expert dense path enabled`.
     - FP8 KV logs:
       `SM70 FP8 KV cache C++ reshape_and_cache_flash path enabled
       (kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2)` and
       `FLASH_ATTN_V100 FP8 KV cache decode path active
       (kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2, route=scalar_paged)`.
     - Production TP communication remained enabled:
       `disable_custom_all_reduce=false` and `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']` for
       `tp:0`.

     Speed and capacity:
     - Default 35B-FP8 TP2 FP8-KV result, 4k input / 256 output:
       `prefill_time=1.4257779999970808 s`,
       `prefill_tps=2872.8175073597617`,
       `steady_decode_tps=63.54881823154449`,
       `output_tps=47.04624973592828`.
     - Manual-control run was the same route within noise:
       `prefill_time=1.4315601859998424 s`,
       `prefill_tps=2861.213967849516`,
       `steady_decode_tps=63.55209539079754`.
     - KV capacity is `1,676,400 tokens` at `max_model_len=66000` and
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`.

     Interpretation:
     - The 35B-FP8 TP2 FP8 KV cache route is now functionally open on the
       latest tree with the normal benchmark command; no extra MoE env is
       required.
     - The fixed issue was the TP2 weight/MoE memory cliff, not a Flash-V100
       FP8 KV cache kernel bug.
     - This is route and speed evidence, not a model-quality pass. The warmup
     and measured repeat hashes differ in the default run, so the next
     required step before production defaulting is a dedicated token/logit
     quality gate for 35B-FP8 FP8-KV against the chosen safe baseline.

350. 2026-06-13 AWQ MoE repair policy was factored into a shared SM70
     quantized-MoE router; FP8 stage-local reuse is implemented but not
     quality-accepted.

     Code change:
     - Added `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/sm70_moe_router.py`.
       It only selects the quantization-independent stage route:
       `batched`, `per_expert_dispatch`, `dense`, or `active_dense`.
       AWQ and FP8 still keep thin quant-specific adapters because their
       packed weights and scale descriptors are different.
     - `awq_sm70_moe.py` now uses the shared router while preserving the
       accepted AWQ behavior: default batched AWQ MoE still routes W13 and W2
       through `awq_moe_gemm_sm70_per_expert_dispatch_out`.
     - `fp8_sm70_moe.py` now uses the same router and adds default-off
       stage-local experimental gates:
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_W13_PER_EXPERT_DISPATCH=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_W2_PER_EXPERT_DISPATCH=1`.
       These gates call the FP8 per-expert dispatch op, not the AWQ op.
     - `benchmark_sm70_decode.py`,
       `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py`, and
       `benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` now record/test the new FP8
       W13-only and W2-only candidate lanes.

     Static validation:
     - `py_compile` passed for the new router, AWQ/FP8 adapters, env registry,
       and the affected benchmark scripts.

     Op-level evidence:
     - W13-only candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/fp8_moe_batched_w13_per_expert_dispatch_m1_m4_m8_20260613.json`.
     - W2-only candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/fp8_moe_batched_w2_per_expert_dispatch_m1_m4_m8_20260613.json`.
     - Both classify as `B-pending`, with `max_diff=0.00048828125` and no
       configured fp16-bound failure. This is not a model-quality pass.

     Model-level W13-only result:
     - Safe reference:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_native_safe_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_fp8_batched_w13dispatch_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Compare:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_fp8_native_safe_vs_batched_w13dispatch_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Same criterion: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8, TP2 on GPU0/1, full Flash-V100,
       FP8 KV `fp8_e5m2`, native FP8 MoE safe baseline vs
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` plus
       `VLLM_SM70_FP8_MOE_BATCHED_W13_PER_EXPERT_DISPATCH=1`, 4k input,
       1024 greedy output, custom all-reduce enabled.
     - Route-hit was confirmed by:
       `SM70 FP8 MoE TurboMind batched path enabled (256 experts)` and
       `SM70 FP8 MoE batched W13 using per-expert dispatch selection
       (experts=256)`.
     - Token gate failed: first mismatch at output token index `35`
       (`safe=364`, `candidate=13`), so `model_quality_gate.label` is
       `model-fail`.
     - Speed signal is real but not acceptable yet: safe generation took
       `17.69670284199674 s`; W13-only candidate took
       `11.663751992004109 s`.

     Interpretation:
     - The AWQ repair strategy can be shared as route policy, but FP8 cannot
       be accepted by simply applying AWQ's per-expert dispatch compute order.
     - Do not default the FP8 batched W13-only route. It is a useful speed
       candidate and a valid localization point, but it still causes long
       greedy output drift.

351. 2026-06-13 AWQ batched per-expert dispatch did not pass the same
     4k/1024 model-token quality gate used for the FP8 route.

     Test criterion:
     - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on GPU0/1, latest `vllm` tree,
       `--attention-backend FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `--kv-cache-dtype fp8_e5m2`,
       `--quantization awq`, 4k input, 1024 greedy output, `ignore_eos`,
       custom all-reduce enabled.
     - Safe reference used `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=0` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.
     - Candidate used `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1` and
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.

     Artifacts:
     - Safe:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_batched_perdispatch_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Compare:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_vs_batched_perdispatch_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.

     Route-hit evidence:
     - Safe path logged `SM70 AWQ MoE CUDA-graph-safe dense-stage path
       enabled`.
     - Candidate path logged `SM70 AWQ MoE TurboMind batched path enabled`,
       `SM70 AWQ MoE batched W13 using per-expert dispatch selection`, and
       `SM70 AWQ MoE batched W2 using per-expert dispatch selection`.
     - Both runs used `disable_custom_all_reduce=false` and runtime TP
       all-reduce selected `['CUSTOM', 'PYNCCL']`.

     Result:
     - Token gate failed: first mismatch at output token index `13`
       (`safe=3299`, `candidate=1788`), so `model_quality_gate.label` is
       `model-fail`.
     - Speed signal is real but not acceptable yet: safe generation took
       `16.435758664003515 s`; candidate generation took
       `11.274635998997837 s`.

     Interpretation:
     - Under this single-prompt 4k/1024 TP2 FP8-KV criterion, the current AWQ
       batched per-expert dispatch path is not quality-accepted.
     - This supersedes older route-hit-only or shorter AWQ smoke evidence for
       this exact criterion; do not use it as a default-quality pass until the
       token drift is fixed or bounded by explicit logits evidence.

352. 2026-06-13 AWQ indexed single-token decode fastpath: op-level exactness
     is fixed, but the model-level CUDA-graph quality gate is still not
     accepted. Do not repeat the 1024-token proof runs until the graph-buffer
     localization below has been used.

     Scope and artifacts:
     - Base criterion is unchanged from item 351: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2 on
       GPU0/1, Flash-V100, FP8 KV `fp8_e5m2`, 4k input, greedy output,
       custom all-reduce enabled.
     - Decode-only batched candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_batched_decodeonly_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
       Compare:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_vs_batched_decodeonly_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
       It failed at output token index `13` (`safe=3299`,
       `candidate=16629`). Split speed signal from request metrics:
       prefill `2880.08 tok/s`, decode `94.88 tok/s`.
     - Initial indexed decode candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_indexed_decode_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
       It failed at output token index `13` with the same candidate token as
       decode-only batched (`16629`) while logging
       `single-token indexed dense-stage path enabled`.
     - C++ patch changed
       `awq_moe_single_token_indexed_dense_stage_sm70_out` to pass
       `per_expert_dispatch=true` into `awq_moe_gemm_sm70_out_impl`.
       Rebuilt `_C.abi3.so` and copied it into `vllm/_C.abi3.so`.
     - Post-patch op exactness:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_m1_patterns_perdispatch_20260613.json`,
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_m1_multilayer_perdispatch_20260613.json`,
       and
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_m1_random_patterns_perdispatch_20260613.json`.
       These cover sampled MoE layers plus deterministic random expert-id
       patterns. All W13/SILU/W2 reports are bitwise exact (`max_diff=0`).
     - Post-patch indexed model candidate:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_indexed_decode_perdispatch_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
       Compare:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_vs_indexed_decode_perdispatch_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
       It still failed at output token index `13` (`safe=3299`,
       `candidate=1788`). Split speed signal from request metrics:
       prefill `2934.56 tok/s`, decode `108.58 tok/s`.
     - Current strict-safe rerun:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_current_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
       It remains in the slow exact lane with prefill `2882.33 tok/s` and
       decode `68.82 tok/s`. It differs from the older strict artifact only
       after output token index `33`, while the indexed candidate diverges at
       index `13`; therefore the indexed drift is earlier than strict-run
       variation and cannot be dismissed as baseline nondeterminism.

     Current root-cause assessment:
     - The indexed W13/W2 kernels are exact as standalone ops, including random
       expert-id ordering. The remaining model failure is therefore not a
       simple fp16 math-order mismatch in the indexed AWQ GEMM itself.
     - The suspicious code boundary is CUDA graph replay of the indexed
       grouped TurboMind descriptor/context. `dispatch_num_override=1` changes
       dispatch selection while the real launch still uses `num=top_k`,
       `expert_offsets`, and `sorted_expert_ids`/`group_idxs`. This path keeps
       the speed signal but still produces model-level token drift under the
       full Flash-V100 compile graph.
     - Do not "fix" by disabling indexed W13/W2 or by turning off batched MoE;
       that gives back the slow `~68.8 tok/s` decode lane and drops the speed
       target. The next valid diagnostic is a single 16-token graph-buffer
       localization run, not another 1024-token quality proof.

     Next diagnostic gate:
     - Use the newly added gated AWQ single-token dumps
       (`st_expert_offsets`, `st_sorted_expert_ids`, `st_inv_permuted_idx`,
       `st_w13_out`, `st_silu_out`, `st_w2_out`, `st_output`) with
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_AWQ_MOE_BUFFERS=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_DUMP_QWEN_LAYER_GRAPH_BUFFERS=1`, selected MoE layers, and
       graph step around the first divergence. Run only one strict/candidate
       pair at 16 output tokens. Use that to decide whether to fix
       prepare/metadata, W13 grouped replay, W2 grouped replay, or weighted
       reduce.

353. 2026-06-13 AWQ indexed single-token CUDA graph replay micro-gate:
     passed. This replaces the model graph-dump idea as the next decision
     boundary, because the earlier instrumented model run produced no dump
     files and also changed the strict output.

     Scope and artifact:
     - Added `--mode awq_moe_graph_replay` to
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py`. The mode forces
       `m=1`, loads one AWQ MoE layer once, captures the indexed single-token
       path once, replays multiple expert patterns through the same CUDA graph,
       and compares each replay against the strict dense single-token path.
     - Command used GPU0 only and one 35B AWQ MoE layer:
       `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`, patterns
       `random_unique:0 random_unique:1 single:7`.
     - Artifact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_cuda_graph_replay_20260613.json`.

     Result:
     - All metadata and value stages were bitwise exact under CUDA graph replay:
       `metadata_offsets`, `metadata_inv_permuted_idx`,
       `metadata_sorted_expert_ids`, `w13_gate_up`, `silu_and_mul`,
       `w2_sorted_output`, and `final_weighted_output` all reported
       `max_diff=0`, including replay patterns different from the capture
       pattern.

     Updated conclusion:
     - The indexed AWQ single-token op chain itself is graph-replay safe for
       changing `topk_ids`; the quality bug is not stale metadata inside this
       pure op chain.
     - Do not repeat the existing 1024-token full-model proof runs. They
       already show the indexed candidate fails at token index `13` while
       strict-safe drift appears only later.
     - The next useful diagnostic must move one boundary outward: compare the
       full-graph MoE call inputs and router outputs just before the AWQ MoE
       module, then compare the module output immediately after. This should be
       a single short full-model run, only after making the dump path write
       reliably without perturbing strict output.
     - The dump path was tightened accordingly: AWQ MoE dump custom-op results
       are now returned and assigned back to the live tensor, so compile/full
       graph cannot dead-code-eliminate the side-effect-free custom op. The
       outer Qwen MoE block now also has gated `moe_input` and `moe_output`
       dumps. These are all environment-gated and remain off in the default
       speed path.
     - The MoE runner graph-buffer dump path was self-tested without loading a
       model and wrote
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_dump_selftest/pid*_step0001_layer01_moe_runner_awq_selftest_shape8.pt`.
       This diagnostic plumbing check is enough; do not repeat it.

354. 2026-06-13 MTP verification-cost warmup pass: helper-kernel JIT moved out
     of the first request; this is not yet the full MTP speed recovery.

     Scope:
     - Target line remains latest-tree Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ MTP4 on physical
       GPUs 2/3, TP2, official sampling, target Flash-V100 compile/FULL graph,
       drafter `TRITON_ATTN` PIECEWISE graph, production custom all-reduce, and
       the accepted SM70 AWQ speed routes.
     - The previous cost profile showed MTP is step-cost bound, not primarily
       acceptance-collapsed: corrected no-MTP baseline is about
       `106.197 tok/s`, while MTP4 with healthy acceptance only delivered
       about `108.329 tok/s`; profiled stable per-step cost was dominated by
       target verifier plus draft/proposer work. The first warmup request also
       JIT-compiled MTP helper kernels:
       `eagle_prepare_next_token_padded_kernel`,
       `eagle_step_slot_mapping_metadata_kernel`,
       `eagle_prepare_inputs_padded_kernel`, `kernel_unified_attention`,
       `reduce_segments`, `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`, and
       `expand_kernel`.

     Code change:
     - Added `SpecDecodeBaseProposer.warmup_sm70_mtp_hotpath_kernels()`. It is
       SM70/CUDA/MTP-only, best-effort, and uses dummy tensors to compile the
       MTP prepare/metadata kernels before serving requests. It warms both the
       first-step normal sampler shape `[num_reqs, 1]` and the later rejection
       sampler shape `[num_reqs, num_spec_tokens + 1]`. It also directly warms
       the rejection-sampler `expand_kernel`. The warmup does not modify real
       request metadata, sampled tokens, draft logits, acceptance, or graph
       replay state.
     - Wired the proposer warmup into the existing default-on
       `VLLM_SM70_AUX_KERNEL_WARMUP=1` runner path, next to
       `zero_kv_blocks`, `compute_slot_mapping`, and GDN conv warmup. The log
       now includes `mtp_prepare_next_token`, `mtp_prepare_inputs`,
       `mtp_rejection_expand`, and `mtp_step_slot_mapping` when this path runs.
     - The dummy block table uses
       `ceil(max_model_len / block_size)` columns so the
       `eagle_step_slot_mapping_metadata_kernel` specialization matches the
       real request's `n_blocks_per_req` instead of accidentally warming only a
       one-column toy shape.
     - Expanded `BlockTable.warmup_slot_mapping_kernel()` from `{1, max}` to
       `{1, 2, 4, 5, 8, max}` token counts so the MTP verifier's 5-token
       step does not wait for request-time slot-mapping JIT.
     - Changed `_dummy_sampler_run()` speculative warmup from one dummy draft
       token to `self.num_spec_tokens` dummy draft tokens. This targets MTP4
       rejection/recovery warmup instead of only a toy one-token speculative
       shape.

     Verification:
     - Static checks passed:
       `PYTHONPATH=/home/ymzx/桌面/1cat-vllm/vllm conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py`.
     - `ruff check vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py vllm/v1/worker/block_table.py`
       passed.
     - Runtime smoke on physical GPUs 2/3, TP2, latest 35B-AWQ MTP4,
       `input_len=1024`, `output_len=16`, target Flash-V100 compile/FULL
       graph, drafter `TRITON_ATTN`:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_warmupjit_smoke_i1024_o16_tp2_20260613.log`.
       This confirmed the aux warmup hook ran, but the first version still
       missed the `[num_reqs, 1]` prepare-next shape.
     - Runtime smoke after adding both prepare-next shapes:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_warmupjit_smoke_i1024_o4_tp2_20260613.log`.
       `eagle_prepare_next_token_padded_kernel`,
       `eagle_step_slot_mapping_metadata_kernel`, and
       `eagle_prepare_inputs_padded_kernel` no longer appeared in request-time
       JIT warnings. Remaining warnings were `kernel_unified_attention`,
       `reduce_segments`, `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`, and `expand_kernel`.
     - Runtime smoke after expanding block-table and dummy sampler warmup:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_warmupjit_smoke2_i1024_o4_tp2_20260613.log`.
       The request-time JIT list was reduced to `kernel_unified_attention`,
       `reduce_segments`, and `expand_kernel`; `_compute_slot_mapping_kernel`
       was gone. This short `o4` smoke is a JIT-coverage check only and must
       not be used as a speed baseline.
     - The direct `mtp_rejection_expand` warmup was validated by the later
       4k/256 profile run
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_topktriton_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.log`.
       The first-request JIT warnings were reduced to
       `kernel_unified_attention` and `reduce_segments`; `expand_kernel` no
       longer appeared in the request-time JIT list.
     - A real drafter-attention warmup was tried and rejected. Artifact
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_warmjit_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`
       dropped official-sampling acceptance to about `0.56-0.57` and steady
       decode to `93.26584440945973 tok/s`. That path warmed real drafter
       attention state and is not safe. The hook was removed; only the
       metadata/helper-kernel warmup stays.

     Current conclusion and next target:
     - This removes the request-time JIT for the MTP prepare-next,
       prepare-inputs, step-metadata, and slot-mapping helper kernels from the
       validated smoke path, but it should not be counted as recovering the
       `1.5x-2x` MTP speed target by itself.
     - The next meaningful speed work remains per-speculation-step cost:
       reduce the `k+1` target verifier graph time and/or the four serial MTP
       draft forwards. In the current safe line, target Flash-V100 is already
       active; the remaining large opportunities are drafter Flash-V100 under
       graph with stable acceptance and a faster route for the unquantized MTP
       MoE/linear work.
     - Do not re-add real drafter-attention warmup as a JIT fix. Any future
       `kernel_unified_attention` / `reduce_segments` warmup must be proven
       non-mutating for drafter KV/metadata first. The next speed work should
       target per-step verifier/drafter cost instead of more warmup-only
       experiments.

355. 2026-06-13 MTP verifier sampling-cost pass: large-vocab target
     top-k/top-p filtering now avoids the PyTorch full-vocab sort for MTP
     verifier batches.

     Scope:
     - Same target line as item 354: latest Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ MTP4 on
       physical GPUs 2/3, TP2, official sampling (`temperature=1.0`,
       `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`), target Flash-V100 compile/FULL graph,
       drafter `TRITON_ATTN` PIECEWISE graph, production custom all-reduce,
       and accepted SM70 AWQ speed routes.

     Code change:
     - `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` now routes CUDA
       large-vocab fp32 top-k/top-p filtering through the existing Triton
       implementation when `batch >= 2` and `vocab >= 32768`. This targets
       the MTP verifier/rejection sampler's multi-token target logits without
       changing no-MTP single-request `batch=1` sampling.
     - The selector keeps fp16/bf16 logits on the PyTorch path because the
       Triton kernel asserts fp32 logits. This avoids a generic sampler crash
       while preserving the real MTP verifier path, which supplies fp32 target
       logits.

     Verification:
     - Static checks passed:
       `conda run -n vllm-0.0.5-t210 python -m py_compile vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`.
     - `ruff check vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py vllm/v1/spec_decode/llm_base_proposer.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
       passed.
     - GPU microbench on physical GPU 2 compared Triton/PyTorch masks for
       `vocab=248064`, `top_k=20`, `top_p=0.95`:
       `batch=1,dtype=fp32` stayed on PyTorch with `mask_equal=True` and
       `max_diff=0.0`; `batch=4,dtype=fp32` used Triton with
       `mask_equal=True`, `finite=76`, and `max_diff=0.0`;
       `batch=4,dtype=fp16` stayed on PyTorch with `mask_equal=True` and
       `max_diff=0.0`.
     - Diagnostic 4k/256 profile artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_topktriton_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_topktriton_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.log`.
       The measured repeat had acceptance length `3.953846153846154`,
       overall acceptance `0.7384615384615385`, steady decode
       `102.5197153309574 tok/s`, and prefill `0.5231085699924733 s`.
       Throughput in this artifact remains diagnostic-only because
       `VLLM_SM70_MTP_PROFILE=1` adds CUDA events.
     - Stable-window profile estimate from the cumulative-log delta between
       `calls=64` and `calls=128` shows target rejection sampling at about
       `1.429 ms/step`, down from the previous stable estimate of about
       `1.812 ms/step`. Target verifier forward remains about
       `24.225 ms/step`, and draft/proposer remains about
       `12.367 ms/step`; this pass therefore removes a real verifier cost but
       does not by itself recover the desired MTP speedup.

     Current conclusion and next target:
     - MTP acceptance is not the active blocker in this line; the latest run
       accepted about `74%` of draft tokens with `3.95` accepted-token length.
       The speed gap is step cost.
     - Official Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 MTP is serial draft by design: first forward
       from target hidden states, then one forward per additional draft token.
       That structure is not a local regression.
     - The MTP branch is unquantized because the AWQ checkpoint's
       `modules_to_not_convert` includes `mtp`. Logs show the draft MoE uses
       the upstream `TRITON Unquantized MoE backend`; FlashInfer unquantized
       MoE is not an SM70 option (`CUTLASS` needs SM90+, TRTLLM BF16 needs
       Blackwell). Dense linear projections already request the SM70 fp16
       TurboMind fast path.
     - The rejected `VLLM_SM70_UNQUANTIZED_MOE_0DOT3_FUNCTIONAL=1` run showed
       the old functional fused-experts route is not the answer: it raised
       draft cost sharply and lowered acceptance. The next implementation
     target is a safe SM70 small-batch unquantized MoE/draft-forward fast
     path, or an exact sparse rejection sampler with seeded-RNG semantics
     preserved. Do not spend more time toggling acceptance-only knobs.

356. 2026-06-13 MTP verifier AWQ MoE small-batch route rejection: the
     existing active/indexed stage candidates do not recover the missing MTP
     speed.

     Scope:
     - Latest tree, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ real MoE layer
       `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts`, physical GPU 2 only,
       SM70, `m=5`, `top_k=8`, `total_slots=40`, hidden `2048`,
       intermediate `512`. This matches the MTP4 target verifier shape
       (`1 + 4` target tokens, eight routed experts per token) for the AWQ
       MoE stages.

     Findings:
     - Current target verifier route, `awq_moe_gemm_sm70_per_expert_dispatch`
       for W13 and W2, is still the fastest existing AWQ MoE stage route for
       this shape. Measured two-stage MoE cost was about `0.35-0.38 ms/layer`
       for mixed 38-40 active experts, and about `0.10 ms/layer` for a
       single active expert.
     - Reusing `awq_moe_active_dense_stage_sm70_out` for both W13 and W2 is
       rejected: it measured about `1.32-1.42 ms/layer` for mixed routes and
       about `0.87 ms/layer` for a single active expert, which is much slower
       than the current per-expert dispatch path.
     - Temporarily widening
       `awq_moe_single_token_indexed_dense_stage_sm70_out` from `top_k<=32`
       to `top_k<=64` allowed the MTP4 `top_k=40` virtual-expert experiment
       to run, but it is also rejected for speed. It measured about
       `0.38-0.39 ms/layer` for mixed routes, slightly slower than current
       per-expert dispatch, and about `0.37 ms/layer` for the single-expert
       case. The source and rebuilt `_C.abi3.so` were restored to the
       original `top_k<=32` guard after the experiment.
     - Both rejected candidates stayed within tiny fp16-level differences
       versus the dense oracle/current path (`max_diff` up to
       `3.0517578125e-05` in the mixed-route cases), so correctness was not
       the blocker. Speed was.

     Consequence:
     - The MTP speed gap is not caused by a simple missing switch to an
       existing AWQ active/indexed MoE route. For target verifier, the
       current AWQ MoE path is already the best existing route, but its
       `m=5` cost across 40 layers still contributes roughly tens of
       milliseconds per speculative step.
     - Next work should not keep toggling existing AWQ MoE stage variants.
       The viable directions are a new SM70 small-M AWQ MoE kernel that
       improves on current per-expert dispatch for `total_slots=40`, or
       reducing other verifier/draft costs such as unquantized MTP draft MoE
       and rejection sampling without changing seeded sampling semantics.

357. 2026-06-13 AWQ indexed decode quality / TP communication localization:
     custom all-reduce and indexed MoE micro paths are exact, but full-model
     strict/indexed tokens still diverge.

     Scope:
     - Latest tree, physical GPUs 0/1 only, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2,
       `input_len=4096`, `max_tokens=1024`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`,
       Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy, no MTP.
     - Candidate route:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_DECODE_MAX_TOKENS=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_INDEXED_STAGE_FASTPATH=1`.
       Logs confirmed `single-token indexed dense-stage path enabled`,
       `single-token weighted-reduce path enabled`, Flash-V100 FP8 KV decode,
       and TP `CUSTOM + PYNCCL` communication.

     Code changes and local checks:
     - `FixFunctionalizationPass` now defunctionalizes the SM70 AWQ
       single-token W13, W2 dense-stage, compact W13, and weighted-reduce
       out-ops so torch.compile keeps their in-place mutation dependencies.
       Py-compile passed, and a manual FX fake-graph check confirmed the
       indexed W13, indexed W2, and weighted-reduce ops are replaced by the
       original in-place ops.
     - `custom_all_reduce.py` no longer returns `empty_like` during
       `CustomAllreduce.capture()` warmup when the current stream is not
       actively capturing. It computes the real eager custom all-reduce
       instead, preserving the replay path while avoiding warmup-time
       uninitialized tensors feeding persistent model state.
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_custom_all_reduce.py` gained `warmup` and
       `compile_graph` diagnostic modes. `warmup` verifies the capture-warmup
       branch; `compile_graph` traces the underlying `ca.all_reduce(...,
       registered=False)` op and captures/replays it in a CUDA graph.
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_turbomind_exactness.py` gained
       `awq_moe_compile`, which compiles and CUDA-graph replays the real
       35B AWQ single-token indexed W13 -> SiLU -> indexed W2 ->
       weighted-reduce chain against the strict dense single-token chain.

     Evidence:
     - Pure indexed MoE CUDA graph replay remains exact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_cuda_graph_replay_20260613.json`.
     - Indexed MoE `torch.compile + CUDA graph` with real
       `model.language_model.layers.1.mlp.experts` weights is exact for
       round-robin, single-expert, random, and random-unique top-k patterns:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_compile_graph_layer1_20260613.json`;
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_compile_graph_layer1_random_20260613.json`.
       All reported stages had `equal=true` and `max_diff=0.0`.
     - Custom all-reduce model-free checks are exact:
       prior graph/multi-graph exactness
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/custom_ar_tp2_graph_exactness_5120_20480_20260613.json`;
       warmup exactness
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/custom_ar_tp2_warmup_exactness_5120_20480_20260613.json`;
       new compile+graph exactness
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/custom_ar_tp2_compile_graph_exactness_4096_20480_20260613.json`.
       The new compile+graph run covered float16 sizes 4096, 8192, and
       20480 for `exact_int` and `model_like`; both TP ranks reported
       `max_diff=0.0`.
     - Full-model strict reference after the custom-AR warmup patch:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_commwarmup_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`,
       decode `62.84 tok/s`.
     - Full-model indexed candidate after the same patch:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_indexed_commwarmup_no_legacy_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`,
       decode `94.40 tok/s`.
     - Strict-vs-indexed compare still failed:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_commwarmup_vs_indexed_commwarmup_no_legacy_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`;
       first mismatch was output token index 3, strict token `271`,
       indexed token `198`.
     - Existing strict references are not perfectly stable either:
       strict-defunc vs strict-commwarmup first mismatch index 15
       (`5658` vs `29625`), and strict-current vs strict-commwarmup first
       mismatch index 15 (`14892` vs `29625`). However the indexed
       candidate diverges earlier than strict-vs-strict.
     - PYNCCL-only full-graph localization (`--disable-custom-all-reduce`)
       is not currently usable for this 35B compile/FULL graph setup. It
       selects `['PYNCCL']` for TP/EP, but the worker dies during
       memory/profiling initialization after the first prefill all-reduce and
       AWQ dense-stage route log. This failed at both
       `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` and `0.80`, so do not keep repeating
       that run as a quality probe.

     Current conclusion:
     - The actual custom all-reduce data path does not go through CPU; CPU is
       used for setup/IPC/address metadata. The CPU/debug path can still mask
       ordering or synchronization bugs, but it is not the production data
       path.
     - The indexed single-token MoE kernels, their vLLM functionalization,
       and their standalone CUDA graph replay are exact. The custom
       all-reduce kernel, capture warmup, and direct compile+graph op are also
       exact. The remaining quality problem is therefore in the full-model
       graph integration: persistent state, graph replay address/state
       binding, or an ordering dependency that only appears when the whole
       Qwen3.6-35B decode graph is stitched together.
     - Next debugging should target a boundary dump or lightweight checksum
       immediately before/after the full-model TP all-reduce and after the
       per-layer decode block inside the real graph, not more standalone MoE
       or custom-AR exactness runs.

358. 2026-06-13 35B-AWQ MTP speed recovery: MTP16 reaches the 2x decode
     target; MTP4/8 are cost-amortization limited.

     Scope:
     - Latest tree, physical GPUs 2/3 only, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2,
       code-generation 4k input / 256 output, `max_model_len=4608`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=4608`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, `ignore_eos=true`,
       warmup request excluded from score.
     - Accepted SM70 speed knobs were enabled:
       target Flash-V100, SM70 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy,
       AWQ MoE TurboMind batched GEMM, legacy single-token compact, production
       custom all-reduce, and MTP drafter Triton attention.

     Baselines and result:
     - No-MTP artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_nomtp_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `106.19741147906592 tok/s`, output
       `90.37314617490458 tok/s`, prefill `0.42853600200032815 s`.
     - MTP4 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r2_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `108.32871551803674 tok/s`, speedup `1.020x`,
       acceptance length `3.878787878787879`.
     - MTP8 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp8_code_customar_topktriton_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `125.51206096845767 tok/s`, speedup `1.182x`,
       acceptance length `5.818181818181818`.
     - MTP16 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp16_code_customar_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `211.36245046409257 tok/s`, output
       `141.64021792421613 tok/s`, prefill `0.5554938550048973 s`,
       TPOT `0.004731209341130749 s`, acceptance length `13.45`,
       overall acceptance `0.778125`, speedup `1.990x` over no-MTP.

     Cost diagnosis:
     - MTP4/8 are not primarily broken by acceptance. They are limited by
       amortization: verifier target forward, standard rejection sampling, and
       serial draft forwards/sampling still cost too much per speculative step
       unless the accepted length is high enough.
     - MTP draft MoE is full precision because the AWQ checkpoint marks
       `mtp` / `model.mtp` in `modules_to_not_convert`. The drafter therefore
       correctly avoids AWQ quantization and uses the TRITON unquantized MoE
       backend, while dense draft projections hit the SM70 fp16 TurboMind fast
       path.
     - Official non-greedy sampling (`temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`,
       `top_k=20`) means `use_local_argmax_reduction=True` does not remove
       draft full-logits/probability work. It only helps greedy draft token
       generation.

     Code change kept:
     - `vllm/model_executor/warmup/awq_sm70_warmup.py` now includes
       `speculative_config.num_speculative_tokens + 1` in dense and MoE
       AWQ warmup shapes. The MTP16 log confirms
       `dense_m=[1, 2, 4, 8, 17]` and
       `moe_tokens=[1, 2, 4, 8, 17]`. This fixes missing verifier-shape
       warmup coverage for MTP8+ without changing the numeric decode path.

     Rejected paths:
     - `disable_padded_drafter_batch=true` MTP8 control artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp8_code_customar_cpucompact_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`.
       It disabled async scheduling, lowered steady decode to
       `87.65274939591473 tok/s`, and lowered acceptance to `4.3` length /
       `0.4125` rate. CPU compact is not a valid speed or behavior fix.
     - Batch-1 Triton top-k/top-p sampler control:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_35b_a3b_awq_mtp16_code_customar_b1triton_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`.
       A microbench showed exact masks and faster isolated masking
       (`0.321 ms` vs `0.430 ms` for batch=1, vocab=151936), but the
       end-to-end stochastic output path had lower acceptance (`11.43`) and
       only `177.04391819337843 tok/s`. The sampler threshold was restored to
       the prior batch>=2 path.

     Current conclusion:
     - The latest tree can recover the requested 35B-AWQ MTP decode speed on
       the required 4k/256 official-sampling shape by using MTP16 plus the
       existing SM70 acceleration stack. This is the current speed profile.
     - MTP4/8 should not be treated as the final speed target for this 35B MoE
       shape. Further improvement should reduce the full-precision MTP drafter
       MoE cost and standard non-greedy sampler/rejection cost, but repeated
       MTP4/8 reruns are no longer useful evidence.

359. 2026-06-13 27B-AWQ MTP spot check: MTP16 hits speed but fails output
     sanity.

     Scope:
     - Latest tree, physical GPUs 2/3 only, Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ, TP2,
       code-generation 4k input / 256 output, `max_model_len=4608`,
       `max_num_batched_tokens=4608`, `max_num_seqs=1`,
       `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, `ignore_eos=true`,
       warmup request excluded from score.
     - Accepted SM70 speed knobs were enabled: target Flash-V100, GDN
       FlashQLA, SM70 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy, AWQ TurboMind dense
       path, production custom all-reduce, and MTP drafter Triton attention.

     Results:
     - No-MTP artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_nomtp_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `56.26837146205464 tok/s`, output
       `39.311130604405804 tok/s`, prefill `1.9775242490140954 s`,
       TPOT `0.017771973384272617 s`.
     - MTP16 artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp16_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `122.29271176311332 tok/s`, output
       `60.98636370117475 tok/s`, prefill `2.1090826470026514 s`,
       TPOT `0.008177102180357622 s`, acceptance length
       `11.73913043478261`, overall acceptance `0.6711956521739131`,
       speedup `2.173x` over the no-MTP steady decode baseline.

     Quality note:
     - The MTP16 speed number is not accepted as a quality-passing result. Its
       generated token prefix collapses into repeated token `4558` after the
       first token, decoding as repeated blank/newline text, while the no-MTP
       run does not show the same collapse.
     - This 27B run therefore proves route-hit and speed opportunity only. Do
       not use the `122.29 tok/s` number as an accepted 27B MTP baseline until
       the token collapse is fixed or shown to be caused by an unrelated test
       setup issue.

360. 2026-06-13 27B-AWQ becomes the MTP speed-recovery benchmark; current
     MTP4 is fixed-cost limited.

     Benchmark decision:
     - Use Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ on physical GPUs 2/3, TP2, official sampling
       (`temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, `ignore_eos=true`) as the
       primary MTP speed-recovery baseline going forward.
     - Report steady decode separately from prefill/TTFT. For tiny prompt
       checks, keep the prompt unpadded instead of repeating it to 1k/4k tokens.

     Current 27B evidence:
     - 4k code prompt, no-MTP:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_nomtp_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `56.26837146205464 tok/s`.
     - 4k code prompt, MTP4:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `86.2369945746078 tok/s`, speedup `1.532x`,
       acceptance length `3.6714285714285713`, acceptance rate
       `0.6678571428571428`.
     - Tiny code prompt (`74` input tokens), no-MTP:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_nomtp_tinycode_i74_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `56.36509077225995 tok/s`, prefill
       `0.12041010399116203 s`.
     - Tiny code prompt (`74` input tokens), MTP4:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_tinycode_i74_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`;
       steady decode `91.46294721521686 tok/s`, speedup `1.623x`,
       acceptance length `3.3376623376623376`, acceptance rate
       `0.5844155844155844`.

     Diagnosis:
     - The speed cap is not primarily prefill/context length: no-MTP steady
       decode is nearly identical for 4k and tiny prompt (`56.27` vs
       `56.37 tok/s`), and MTP4 only rises from `86.24` to `91.46 tok/s` when
       prefill is removed from the workload.
     - MTP4 acceptance is not absent. It reduces verifier steps from about
       `255` single-token decode steps to `70-77` speculative steps, but the
       expected gain is eaten by per-spec-step fixed work.
     - The MTP proposer implementation generates remaining draft tokens in a
       serial loop. Each speculative step pays multiple drafter forwards,
       metadata rebuild/update, draft sampling, target verification over
       `draft_tokens + 1` logits, and rejection sampling.
     - Qwen3.6 AWQ checkpoints mark `mtp` in `modules_to_not_convert`; the MTP
       branch is therefore correctly loaded unquantized. Dense draft
       projections request the SM70 fp16 TurboMind fast path, but the branch is
       still not an AWQ TurboMind branch.
     - Drafter graph mode is PIECEWISE by design in the current stable path.
       Forcing MTP drafter FULL graph previously collapsed acceptance, so the
       current MTP4 speed line still pays more dispatch/metadata overhead than
       the target FULL graph path.
     - Official top-k/top-p sampling keeps rejection/top-k/top-p work on the
       hot path. The code path still masks/sorts large-vocab logits for
       probabilistic rejection sampling, which is a material fixed cost for
       batch-1 MTP.

     Next optimization target:
     - Do not keep re-proving that MTP4 is only `1.5-1.6x`. The next useful
       work is to reduce per-spec-step fixed cost on 27B: profile formal
       decode steps, then attack drafter serial forward/metadata graphing,
       rejection sampler/top-k-top-p cost, and safe drafter graph capture in
       that order. The target acceptance metric is already usable; the missing
       piece is lowering verifier/drafter overhead per accepted token.

361. 2026-06-13 MTP speed recovery guardrail: official sampling is fixed, so
     the remaining acceleration work must reduce implementation cost rather
     than changing distribution knobs.

     Hard rule:
     - Keep the model's official sampling parameters fixed for accepted speed
       and quality evidence: `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, and
       `ignore_eos=true` for the current Qwen3.6 benchmarks. Do not lower
       temperature, remove top-k/top-p, switch to greedy, or otherwise change
       user-visible sampling semantics to make MTP look faster.

     Local and upstream acceleration inventory:
     - Current latest vLLM already uses `draft_sample_method="greedy"` by
       default, so Qwen MTP can avoid materializing full draft probabilities
       on the hot path. The remaining target-side official sampling and
       rejection logic still has to process `num_speculative_tokens + 1`
       logits per speculative step.
     - Current latest vLLM includes official `parallel_drafting` support for
       PARD / draft-model methods, but the native Qwen MTP path still emits
       remaining draft tokens through a serial loop. This is the largest
       structural gap if the target is 2x+ MTP speedup without sampling
       changes.
     - The current tree has fused EAGLE/MTP metadata helpers such as
       `eagle_step_update_slot_mapping_and_metadata`, so part of upstream's
       metadata overhead reduction is already present. More useful next work
       is to profile formal decode and find the remaining per-step split
       between drafter forward, metadata/graph dispatch, target verification,
       and top-k/top-p/rejection.
     - 0.0.3 had an additional MTP stochastic `token_matching` path gated by
       `VLLM_MTP_STOCHASTIC_TOKEN_MATCHING=1`. It avoids the standard
       draft-probability rejection route for eligible non-greedy sampling, but
       it is not a free default candidate until statistical equivalence and
       long-output quality are proven under the official sampling parameters.
     - FlashInfer-style top-k/top-p sampling is an upstream acceleration idea,
       but local SM70 support is not available through the standard
       FlashInfer sampler path. The SM70-compatible route is to optimize or
       specialize the existing Triton/PyTorch top-k/top-p/rejection path for
       the small-row, large-vocab MTP verification shape.

     Next implementation direction:
     - Use 27B-AWQ MTP4 as the short-cycle speed target, keep official
       sampling fixed, and add formal decode profiling only where it changes
       an implementation decision.
     - Attack the fixed cost in this order: drafter serial forward and graph
       dispatch, small-row top-k/top-p/rejection sampler cost, then safe
       drafter graph capture. Do not spend more time rerunning acceptance-only
       probes unless a code change should have moved the speed bottleneck.

362. 2026-06-13 27B-AWQ MTP4 profile: current speed is per-step-cost bound;
     the clearest concrete hotspot is MTP drafter loop metadata CPU time.

     Profile run:
     - Model: `Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ`, physical GPUs 2/3, TP2, 4k code prompt,
       256 output, MTP4, official sampling
       (`temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, `ignore_eos=true`).
     - JSON:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Log:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_profile_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.log`.
     - This run enabled `VLLM_SM70_MTP_PROFILE=1`; its decode tps is lower
       than the non-profile run because the profiler synchronizes CUDA events.
       Use it for attribution, not as the official speed baseline.

     Repeat result with profiler:
     - Warmed repeat steady decode: `82.55239271616831 tok/s`.
     - Decode time: `3.0889474139985396 s` for `255` steady decode tokens.
     - Spec metrics: `70` drafts, `280` draft tokens, `187` accepted draft
       tokens, acceptance length `3.6714285714285713`, overall acceptance rate
       `0.6678571428571428`.
     - Warmup was slower and lower-acceptance because it still hit request-time
       JIT (`_fused_post_conv_kernel`). Do not mix warmup with repeat speed.

     Derived bottleneck:
     - The non-profile 27B-AWQ no-MTP 4k/256 baseline has TPOT
       `17.771973384272617 ms/token` (`56.26837146205464 tok/s`).
     - The non-profile MTP4 run took `2.956967613004963 s` for `70`
       speculative steps, or about `42.24 ms/spec-step`.
     - With acceptance length `3.6714`, a 2x speedup over the no-MTP baseline
       would require roughly `32.6 ms/spec-step` or less. Therefore the
       current line needs about `9-10 ms/spec-step` removed at the same
       acceptance, not another acceptance-only probe.

     Profile attribution:
     - Runner profile at `calls=160`, `spec_steps=158` reports
       `target_logits=2.043 ms`, `target_rejection_sample=1.290 ms`,
       `draft_total=12.492 ms`, `bookkeeping=0.107 ms`, and
       `draft_wall_cpu=41.360 ms` averaged over all calls. The runner's
       CUDA-event fields include two large prefill calls and async scheduling,
       so they are not directly additive with wall time.
     - After subtracting the two 4k prefill target forwards, target verifier
       forward is approximately `21-22 ms/spec-step`.
     - Proposer profile at `calls=160` reports `total_gpu=12.266 ms` and
       `total_wall_cpu=30.582 ms`. The biggest anomaly is metadata/setup,
       not drafter matmul alone:
       `loop_metadata_cpu=22.717 ms`, dominated by
       `loop0_metadata_cpu=21.729 ms`; `loop1_metadata_cpu=0.453 ms` and
       `loop2_metadata_cpu=0.535 ms`.
     - Drafter forward GPU slices are comparatively small after warmup:
       `first_forward=1.267 ms`, `loop0_forward=1.074 ms`,
       `loop1_forward=0.631 ms`, `loop2_forward=0.631 ms`. Draft sampling is
       still material at about `2.04 ms` per sample point.

     Decision:
     - Current MTP4 is not primarily acceptance-limited. It is fixed-cost
       limited by a combination of 5-token target verifier forward,
       drafter serial-loop metadata/setup, and draft/target sampling.
     - The next implementation pass should first remove the loop0 metadata CPU
       stall in the native Qwen MTP proposer: cache/reuse per-layer metadata for
       the stable batch-1 MTP loop where legal, avoid per-step CPU tensor
       construction/clones, and keep draft-token dependencies on GPU.
     - If that does not recover enough, the next targets are the 5-token target
       verifier graph and small-row official top-k/top-p/rejection sampler.
       Do not treat changing sampling parameters or rerunning acceptance-only
       tests as progress.

363. 2026-06-13 AWQ indexed decode quality root update: the severe early
     divergence was a CUDA graph capture scratch-buffer lifetime bug; the
     remaining first-token disagreement is a low-margin numeric/reference
     stability issue, not standalone indexed MoE kernel corruption.

     Scope:
     - Latest tree, physical GPUs 0/1 only, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ, TP2,
       `input_len=4096`, `max_tokens=1024`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`,
       Flash-V100 0.0.3 compile/FULL graph policy, no MTP.
     - Candidate route stayed on the speed path:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_DECODE_MAX_TOKENS=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_INDEXED_STAGE_FASTPATH=1`.

     Code fix:
     - `vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_sm70_moe.py` no longer
       treats CUDA graph capture like a dummy run for MoE scratch/output
       allocation. Dummy runs still use temporary buffers, but real graph
       capture now uses the same per-layer persistent buffers as replay.
     - Rationale: FULL graph replay is address-fixed. Capturing with graph-pool
       `torch.empty` scratch let the all-layer indexed MoE graph depend on
       temporary allocation lifetime analysis across layers. That reproduced
       the severe token index 3 divergence. Using per-layer persistent buffers
       during capture removes that hidden lifetime dependency without disabling
       the indexed W13/W2 speed route.

     Evidence for the fixed severe bug:
     - Before the persistent-capture fix, all-layer indexed long decode
       diverged from strict at token index 3 in
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_commwarmup_vs_indexed_commwarmup_no_legacy_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - After the fix, the same indexed route no longer has the index 3
       corruption. Current indexed artifact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_indexed_persistent_capture_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`,
       decode `95.09 tok/s`.
     - The current strict artifact:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_safe_persistent_capture_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`,
       decode `62.76 tok/s`.
       Strict-vs-indexed now first differs at token index 13
       (`3299` vs `16629`), not index 3:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_persistent_vs_indexed_persistent_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Real-weight single-layer compile+CUDA-graph checks with router and
       metadata copy inside graph remained exact (`max_diff=0`) for random and
       random-scaled inputs:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_compile_graph_router_in_graph_layer1_random_20260613.json`;
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_compile_graph_router_in_graph_layer1_random_scaled_20260613.json`.
     - All-layer eager indexed-vs-dense sweep remained exact for W13/SILU/W2:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/awq_moe_single_token_indexed_all_layers_eager_20260613.json`.

     Evidence for the remaining index 13 classification:
     - Short `max_tokens=16` probes are not valid acceptance evidence for this
       issue. The same strict route produced different prefixes for
       `max_tokens=16` and `max_tokens=1024`; compare:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_persistent_o16_vs_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - A targeted margin/sample dump at steps 12-14 made strict and indexed
       token outputs identical for all 1024 tokens:
       `bench_results/fp8_kv_20260613/model_tokens_compare_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_margin_vs_indexed_margin_i4096_o1024_tp2_20260613.json`
       reports `equal=true`.
       This run is diagnostic only because the dump synchronizes and slows
       decode (`strict` about `50.06 tok/s`, indexed about `69.10 tok/s`).
     - In that diagnostic run, the index-13 choice had a very small top margin.
       Strict-margin step 13: `16629=18.265625`, `1788=18.21875`,
       `3299=18.140625`, top1-top2 margin `0.046875`.
       Indexed-margin step 13: `16629=18.234375`, `3299=18.09375`,
       `1788=17.890625`, top1-top2 margin `0.140625`.
     - The diagnostic sample-hidden tensors still differ numerically between
       strict-margin and indexed-margin even when tokens match:
       step 12 max/mean hidden diff `1.046875 / 0.18446`,
       step 13 `1.359375 / 0.18634`,
       step 14 `0.86328125 / 0.18199`.
       The difference is already present before the first low-margin token
       boundary and then can be amplified autoregressively.
     - Existing strict outputs are not a stable absolute token oracle:
       strict artifacts in this directory diverge among themselves at indexes
       15 or 33, and the diagnostic strict-margin run diverges from the
       current strict-persistent artifact at index 13.

     Current conclusion:
     - The fixed index-3 failure was a real kernel integration bug in CUDA
       graph capture buffer lifetime. Keep the persistent-buffer capture fix.
     - The remaining index-13 strict-vs-indexed token difference is not
       explained by standalone indexed W13/W2 kernel corruption: those kernels
       are exact when fed identical inputs, including compiled CUDA graph
       replay.
     - The remaining difference is caused by accumulated fp16/full-graph numeric
       differences between the strict per-expert route and the indexed/batched
       speed route hitting an extremely small sampler margin. A single strict
       token sequence is therefore not a sufficient quality oracle.
     - Do not go back to the old index-3 artifacts as quality evidence, and do
       not repeat short probes. Future acceptance for this route needs either
       a long-output gate with sampler-margin/logit evidence, or a stricter
       numeric boundary dump that compares hidden/logits before the sampler
       while preserving production scheduling.

364. 2026-06-13 SM70 quality acceptance policy update: stop treating every
     greedy token mismatch as the only quality oracle; require bounded
     op/layer numeric evidence plus dataset-level quality evidence.

     Reason for the policy change:
     - The latest AWQ indexed path already fixed the severe CUDA graph
       capture buffer bug. Remaining long-output token mismatches can happen
       at very small sampler margins, and even strict-safe artifacts are not a
       stable absolute token oracle.
     - A single greedy sequence is therefore a useful red-light signal, but it
       is not enough to classify a speed path as broken. The acceptance path
       must separate true compute bugs from legal fp16 schedule/reduction
       differences that do not harm model quality.

     Numeric gate:
     - Type-A exact paths still require `max_diff == 0`. This includes
       layout-only rewrites, per-expert dense bridges, metadata routing, and
       kernels that claim to preserve the same compute order.
     - Type-B fp16-bounded paths may be accepted only when every dumped
       op/layer boundary stays within the configured fp16 bound, contains no
       NaNs, has no shape/missing dump mismatch, and has no unexplained sign
       mismatch away from zero.
     - Existing MoE exactness uses `FP16_MOE_OUTPUT_BOUND = 2.0e-3`,
       `FP16_MOE_MAX_NONZERO_ULP_DIFF = 256`, and
       `FP16_MOE_SIGN_MISMATCH_ABS_BOUND = 2.0e-6`. Reuse those as the
       starting bound for W13/SILU/W2 local stage outputs unless a stricter
       per-stage bound is proven.
     - Full-layer hidden/logits diffs are not allowed to grow without
       explanation. If sampler-pre hidden/logits show large growth, localize
       the earliest boundary first and prove the local boundary is within the
       fp16 gate. Do not accept a large final hidden diff by only pointing at
       a token mismatch.

     Tooling change:
     - `benchmarks/compare_sm70_tensor_dumps.py` now supports a numeric gate:
       `--require-numeric-gate`, `--max-abs-bound`, `--mean-abs-bound`,
       `--min-cosine`, and regex-specific
       `--label-max-abs-bound PATTERN=FLOAT` /
       `--label-mean-abs-bound PATTERN=FLOAT`.
     - The gate emits `numeric-pass`, `numeric-pending`, or `numeric-fail`.
       `numeric-pending` means nonzero evidence exists without an explicit
       acceptance bound; it is not default-accepted.
     - `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` and
       `benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_serving_quality.py` now have explicit
       `--allow-token-diff-for-model-quality-gate` and
       `--allow-sampler-argmax-diff-for-model-quality-gate` switches. These
       switches make token/argmax differences warning evidence instead of a
       hard fail, but only when explicit numeric or dataset-level gates are
       also being used.

     Example numeric compare command:
     ```bash
     python benchmarks/compare_sm70_tensor_dumps.py \
       /path/to/strict_dumps /path/to/speed_dumps \
       --require-numeric-gate \
       --label-max-abs-bound 'w13_gate_up|silu_and_mul|w2_sorted_output=0.002' \
       --label-mean-abs-bound 'w13_gate_up|silu_and_mul|w2_sorted_output=0.0002' \
       --json-out bench_results/.../tensor_numeric_gate.json
     ```

     Dataset/model quality gate:
     - Run the same model, TP size, GPU set, max length, KV dtype, sampling
       parameters, and prompt set for strict-safe and speed routes. The strict
       output is a baseline for dataset score/logprob, not a bitwise token
       oracle.
     - Minimum smoke set before default-enabling a speed path:
       fixed SM70 prompt set with logprobs, GSM8K small subset for math
       accuracy, a code/instruction prompt set for structured generation, and
       MRCR or another long-context retrieval set when the change can affect
       long-context behavior.
     - For small smoke runs, treat any accuracy drop larger than one sample or
       any systematic invalid-output increase as a fail. For larger runs,
       tighten this to task-specific score deltas after the strict baseline is
       recorded.
     - Token exactness remains recorded in reports, but default acceptance is:
       numeric gate passed, model/dataset score did not regress, and no
       high-margin sampler/logit contradiction is found.

     First dataset commands to run against each strict/speed server:
     ```bash
     # Fixed prompt/logprob smoke.
     python benchmarks/benchmark_sm70_serving_quality.py \
       --base-url http://127.0.0.1:8000 \
       --model /home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ \
       --prompts-json benchmarks/sm70_quality_prompts_qwen36.json \
       --max-tokens 256 \
       --logprobs 5 \
       --prompt-logprobs 5 \
       --ignore-eos \
       --out bench_results/.../serving_quality.json

     # Math accuracy smoke; increase num-questions after the route passes smoke.
     python tests/evals/gsm8k/gsm8k_eval.py \
       --host http://127.0.0.1 \
       --port 8000 \
       --num-questions 32 \
       --num-shots 5 \
       --max-tokens 256 \
       --temperature 0.0 \
       --seed 42 \
       --save-results bench_results/.../gsm8k_32.json

     # Long-context retrieval smoke; tune max-prompt-tokens to the server len.
     python tests/evals/mrcr/mrcr_eval.py \
       --host http://127.0.0.1 \
       --port 8000 \
       --num-samples 12 \
       --needles 2 4 8 \
       --max-prompt-tokens 4096 \
       --max-tokens 512 \
       --temperature 0.0 \
       --seed 42 \
       --save-results bench_results/.../mrcr_12.json
     ```

365. 2026-06-13 First strict-vs-indexed dataset/model quality smoke for the
     35B-AWQ indexed MoE speed route.

     Route and server scope:
     - Model: `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-AWQ`.
     - GPUs: physical 0/1, TP2.
     - Server args held fixed across strict and indexed:
       `dtype=float16`, `quantization=awq`, `kv_cache_dtype=fp8_e5m2`,
       `max_model_len=8192`, `max_num_batched_tokens=4096`,
       `max_num_seqs=1`, `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88`,
       `attention_backend=FLASH_ATTN_V100`, `disable_custom_all_reduce=false`,
       `disable_cascade_attn=true`, `language_model_only=true`,
       `skip_mm_profiling=true`, `max_logprobs=20`.
     - Strict route env:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=0`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.
     - Indexed speed route env:
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_GEMM=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_BATCHED_DECODE_MAX_TOKENS=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_MOE_SINGLE_TOKEN_INDEXED_STAGE_FASTPATH=1`,
       `VLLM_SM70_AWQ_MOE_LEGACY_SINGLE_TOKEN_COMPACT=0`.
     - Both routes kept the Flash-V100 compile/FULL graph policy and GDN
       schedule envs used by the previous 1024-token artifacts.

     Tooling updates used by this run:
     - `compare_sm70_tensor_dumps.py` now has a configurable numeric gate with
       label-specific bounds.
     - `benchmark_sm70_model_tokens.py` and
       `benchmark_sm70_serving_quality.py` now allow token/argmax differences
       to be warning evidence under explicit model/dataset gates.
     - `benchmark_sm70_serving_quality.py` also allows missing sampler-logits
       dumps as warning evidence for serving/dataset comparisons where logits
       dumps are intentionally absent.
     - `benchmark_sm70_serving_quality.py` was fixed to pass
       `kv_cache_dtype` into `_sm70_attention_policy()`.

     Fixed prompt/logprob smoke:
     - Strict artifact:
       `bench_results/quality_gate_20260613/serving_quality_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_iq8_o256_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Indexed artifact:
       `bench_results/quality_gate_20260613/serving_quality_qwen36_35b_awq_indexed_iq8_o256_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Gate artifact:
       `bench_results/quality_gate_20260613/serving_quality_gate_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_vs_indexed_iq8_o256_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Result: `model_quality_gate.label = model-pass`,
       `token_equal = true`, `max_prompt_logprob_diff = 0.0`,
       `max_output_logprob_diff = 0.0`,
       `max_prompt_top_logprob_diff = 0.0`,
       `max_output_top_logprob_diff = 0.0`,
       `max_prompt_perplexity_abs_diff = 0.0`.
     - Elapsed wall time for the same 8 prompts:
       strict `32.9704 s`, indexed `24.4786 s`.

     GSM8K 32-question smoke:
     - Strict artifact:
       `bench_results/quality_gate_20260613/gsm8k_qwen36_35b_awq_strict_n32_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Indexed artifact:
       `bench_results/quality_gate_20260613/gsm8k_qwen36_35b_awq_indexed_n32_tp2_20260613.json`.
     - Strict: accuracy `0.03125`, invalid rate `0.03125`,
       output throughput `54.9839 tok/s`, total output tokens `8192`.
     - Indexed: accuracy `0.0`, invalid rate `0.03125`,
       output throughput `78.3329 tok/s`, total output tokens `8192`.
     - Interpretation: this is a one-sample accuracy difference on a 32-sample
       smoke. It does not exceed the new small-smoke failure rule
       ("larger than one sample" fails), but it is not enough to claim final
       dataset quality is fully proven. Run a larger GSM8K subset before
       default-enable acceptance if this route depends on dataset evidence.

     Long-context dataset status:
     - MRCR did not produce a valid score in this run. The first attempt found
       missing `datasets`; after installing it into `vllm-0.0.5-t210`, MRCR
       streaming/sample loading made no visible progress for over two minutes
       and was stopped to avoid wasting the benchmark window.
     - A local long-context retrieval prompt attempt with prompt length about
       `5152` tokens and `prompt_logprobs=1` caused OOM during prompt-logprob
       full-vocab all-gather (`1.89 GiB` allocation) and killed the strict
       server. This is an evaluation request/memory issue, not evidence of an
       indexed MoE quality failure.
     - Next long-context smoke should either disable `prompt_logprobs` for long
       prompts or keep prompt length under the memory-safe prompt-logprob
       boundary.

     Current acceptance status:
     - Fixed prompt/logprob serving gate passed exactly.
     - GSM8K small smoke is not a hard fail, but remains too small for final
       quality acceptance.
     - Long-context dataset evidence remains pending.

366. 2026-06-13 Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ MTP4 acceptance-rate fix: official
     non-greedy sampling must use probabilistic draft sampling, not greedy
     one-hot draft tokens.

     Root cause:
     - The current vLLM native MTP proposer defaults to
       `draft_sample_method="greedy"`. Under official Qwen sampling
       (`temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`), this means the drafter
       returns no draft probability tensor.
     - The rejection sampler then treats the draft token as a one-hot proposal
       (`q=1` for that token). Acceptance is therefore approximately limited by
       the target probability of the greedy draft token, which explains the
       observed 60-70% acceptance range.
     - This was not an MTP weight-loading failure and not a target Flash-V100
       issue. It was a sampling/proposal mismatch in the official stochastic
       sampling path.

     Fix:
     - For SM70 MTP defaults,
       `_maybe_apply_sm70_mtp_defaults()` now sets
       `speculative_config.draft_sample_method="probabilistic"` when the user
       did not pass an explicit value.
     - The same default block also pins the MTP drafter attention backend to
       `TRITON_ATTN`. This affects only the drafter. The target model still
       selects `FLASH_ATTN_V100` in the measured run.
     - The default respects explicit user configuration and can be disabled
       with `VLLM_1CAT_DISABLE_SM70_MTP_DEFAULTS=1`.

     Evidence, physical V100 GPUs 2/3 only, latest tree,
     `/home/ymzx/models/Qwen3.6-27B-AWQ`, TP2, 4k code prompt, 256 output,
     official sampling, custom all-reduce enabled, target Flash-V100, drafter
     Triton attention:
     - Old greedy-default artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`
       repeat steady decode `86.2369945746078 tok/s`, decode
       `2.956967613004963 s`, TPOT `0.011595951423548875 s`, acceptance
       length `3.6714285714285713`, overall acceptance
       `0.6678571428571428`, per-position acceptance
       `0.8714285714/0.7142857143/0.5714285714/0.5142857142`.
     - Manual probabilistic control artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_prob_draft_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`
       repeat steady decode `109.26522927221468 tok/s`, decode
       `2.3337707859900547 s`, TPOT `0.009152042298000214 s`, acceptance
       length `4.709090909090909`, overall acceptance
       `0.9272727272727272`, per-position acceptance
       `0.9636363636/0.9636363636/0.9090909091/0.8727272727`.
     - Post-fix default artifact:
       `bench_results/sm70_migration_20260613/decode_latest_qwen36_27b_awq_mtp4_default_prob_draft_code_customar_i4096_o256_w1_r1_tp2_20260613.json`
